This dynamic environment provides enough cold gas to trigger the activity of the black hole at the centre of NGC 1316, whose rapid flickering on timescales of 10's of Myr has produced the complex radio continuum morphology of Fornax A maccagni2020 and has impacted the distribution and kinematics of gas within this galaxy's stellar body maccagni2021 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Fornax A as an FR-I whose Fermi LAT emission is lobe-dominated, with weak core contribution and a small Doppler factor.

quotes.quote: Fornax A is the second FR-I whose ray emission can be observed from the lobe by Fermi LAT ack16 . ack16 reported its GeV band behavior using 6.1 years of Fermi LAT data, and concluded that its ray emission originates in the lobes and the core emission contributes the total emission no more than 14 . Its radio 5GHz core flux density (0.05 Jy) also accounts for a small fraction of the total flux density (72 Jy) di14 . Our Doppler factor estimation for Fornax A ( _ =0.97 ) is also small, supporting that the core emission is not boosted by its jet.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1316 as a 1-3 Gyr merger remnant with disturbed gas-and-dust morphology; its globular-cluster system includes bright intermediate-color clusters with spectra indicating 3 Gyr ages.

quotes.quote: An interesting comparison is the cases of NGC 1316 and NGC 1052. Both are thought to be 1--3 Gyr old merger remnants. NGC 1316 has a very disturbed morphology with extensive gas and dust.
The NGC 1316 GC system reveals several GCs at intermediate colour and bright magnitudes of 18.5 < V < 21.5. The brighter three are confirmed by spectra to be 3 Gyrs old from Goudfrooij et al. (2001).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1316 as a well-known merger remnant whose new VLT kinematics reach larger radii, reveal central enhanced dispersion, and show random motions dominating to large radii.

quotes.quote: NGC 1316 (FORNAX A) For this well-known merger remnant, Figure fig:comp1 reveals generally good agreement with the published results, although the new data reach to very much larger radii. The new data indicate a rather higher dispersion than the points at the largest radii of the previous measurements, but since these points were derived from the lowest S/N data in the older observations, the conflict is probably not significant. The more extended velocity dispersion profile from the new data for the first time reveals a seemingly-distinct feature of enhanced dispersion within the central 15 arcsec . In this galaxy, as might be expected in a merger, random motions dominate out to large radii in the new data, bringing into question whether this galaxy should really be classified as an S0 system.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1316 as roughly Z 0.01 with an older inner stellar population, linking its radial age change to known merger-remnant status.

quotes.quote: NGC 1316 -- A Z 0.01 is found from models, with age t > 8 Gyr. The inner annuli seem to be populated by a rather old (t 13 ) stellar system with respect to the outer annuli (t 8). Such radial change of the stellar age would be also supported by the fact that this galaxy is a known merger remnant goudfrooij01 .

sources.summary: The paper lists FORNAX as a nearby high-flux radio galaxy at 16.9 Mpc and considers it near Source B, though probably not physically associated.

quotes.quote: For radio galaxies, those giving the highest radio fluxes at earth are, in order of increasing distance from earth: CEN-–A (NGC 5128) at 4.9 Mpc VIRGO – A (NGC 4486, M87) at 16.8 Mpc and FORNAX (NGC 1316, ARP 154) at 16.9 Mpc 'Source B' In the list just given, the first two have been mentioned already. FORNAX is seen to be not far from the 'Source B' but probably too far to be physically associated. However, it is in the FORNAX cluster and this has galaxies extending across to l, b : 200 ^ , 40^ .

sources.summary: The paper contrasts Fornax A with M87, describing it as similarly luminous but with an unusually small SMBH and low globular-cluster specific frequency.

quotes.quote: Compare the galaxies M87 (NGC 4486) and Fornax A (NGC 1316), which have similar luminosities ( M_V=-22.7 and 22.8 respectively). M87 has an SMBH mass that is larger than expected for its dispersion by 0.20 dex, while Fornax A contains an unusually small SMBH, by 0.42 dex. M87 has a specific frequency S_N = 12.2 that is a factor of 3 larger than the average value for our sample of S_N = 4.0 , while Fornax A is characterized by an unusually small value, S_N = 0.9 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses Fornax A as a very extended southern radio source whose weak core is absent from AT20G, so WMAP 23 GHz maps were used for integrated polarisation.

quotes.quote: Fornax A is one of the closest and most extended sources in the Southern Hemisphere, with two lobes extended over a region more than 50 arcmin wide. Its weak core has a flux density at 20 GHz much below the AT20G survey 10 mJy detection limit, and for this reason it is not included in the AT20G catalogue. WMAP was able to integrate over the source area only at 23 GHz, which we included in our analysis.

sources.summary: The atlas gives Fornax A's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: Fornax A is a FR I-like radio source coinciding with the peculiar galaxy NGC 1316 at a distance of D= 19 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median). It contains a LINER-like nucleus bregman_o_2005 and features large diffuse radio lobes. There are various indications for Fornax A being a merger remnant schweizer_optical_1980,goudfrooij_star_2001 with powerful nuclear activity until relatively recently iyomoto_declined_1998 .

sources.summary: The paper says Fornax A lacks convincing extended gamma-ray emission, but may be worth investigating because the closest 3FGL source is offset from its core.

quotes.quote: There is no convincing case of extended emission in other radio galaxies with relatively large radio extensions, such as Cen B kat13 , NGC 6251 tak12 , and Fornax A. Fornax A may be a good case to investigate this emission che07,geo08 . The closest 3FGL source is offset from the Fornax A core by 0 15, while the 95 contour distance is 0 092 (see Figure fig:ForA for a VLA 1.5 GHz image).

sources.summary: The paper says FCC 21 is the only galaxy whose CDF the coeval model fails to mimic and connects it to merger features, intermediate-age GCs, tails, loops, arms, and central dust.

quotes.quote: FCC 21 (NGC 1316). This is the only galaxy for which our coeval model does not mimic the CDF. The galaxy is lenticular and well known for its recent merger feature 1980ApJ...237..303S,2001A&A...376..837H and the presence of intermediate-age GCs 2001MNRAS.322..643G,2018MNRAS.479..478S . The tidal tails, loops, and arms are extended out to 54 and lots of dust structures are visible in the central region 1980ApJ...237..303S .

sources.summary: The paper treats Fornax A as an influential foreground and calibration source: its transit affected antenna sampling, its PRISim model used clean components, and the model flux was 541.7 Jy at 180 MHz.

quotes.quote: After LST binning, most baselines have samples from between 30 and the full 64 days in each frequency/time bin; baselines associated with antennas 3 and 16 contain bins with as few as 10 days sampled during the transit of Fornax A ( 3 hours in LST).

The sky model used by PRISim includes a GSM diffuse model ( deoliveira2008 ), point sources from the GLEAM sky survey with flux density >1 Jy at 150 MHz wayth:2015,hurley_walker2017 , a model of Pictor A created from GLEAM, and a model of Fornax A created by using clean components derived from the deconvolution techniques described in (Personal communication, Carroll \ Byrne 2018). This Fornax A model has a total flux of 541.7 Jy at 180 MHz, consistent with the low frequency observations assuming a spectral index of 0.8 mckinely_et_al2015 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 1316 as a Fornax BCG and the center of a southwest merging group whose outskirts and surface-brightness structure trace cluster assembly.

quotes.quote: With FDS we i) mapped the surface brightness around the BCGs NGC 1399 and NGC 1316 out to an unprecedented distance of about 200 kpc ( R 6R_e ) and down to _g 29-31 mag/arcsec ^2 Iodice2016,Iodice2017 ; ii) traced the spatial distribution of candidate GCs inside a large fraction of the cluster core Dabrusco2016,Cantiello2018 ; iii) studied the galaxy outskirts, detected ICL and faint ( _g 28-30 mag/arcsec ^2 ) features in the intra-cluster region in the core of the cluster Iodice2016, Iodice2017b, Iodice2019, Raj2019 and in the outskirts of NGC 1316 Iodice2017 ; iv) provided the largest size and magnitude limited catalogue of dwarf galaxies in the cluster Venhola2017,Venhola2018 .

By combining the structural properties of the galaxies from FDS imaging with the spectroscopic data from F3D (i.e., morphology, colours, kinematics, and stellar population) in the two-dimensional projected phase-space, the cluster shows three well-defined groups of galaxies: the core , the north-south clump and the infalling galaxies (see Fig. fig:Fornax_groups ), apart from the southwest merging group centred on NGC 1316 Drinkwater2001 . Galaxies in each group have different light and colour distributions, kinematics, and stellar populations properties Iodice2019a .

sources.summary: NGC 1316 is the group BGG, a giant radio-galaxy merger remnant with extended stellar loops and streams from a past gas-rich merger.

quotes.quote: The brightest group galaxy (BGG), NGC 1316, is a peculiar early-type galaxy with a stellar mass of 6 10 ^ 11 Iodice2017 . NGC 1316 is a giant radio galaxy Ekers1983, Formalont1989, McKinley2015, Maccagni2020 , known merger remnant, and the brightest galaxy in the Fornax cluster volume (even brighter than the brightest cluster galaxy NGC 1399). There are a number of extended stellar loops and streams in NGC 1316 that are a result of a 10:1 merger that occurred 1 -- 3 Gyr ago, between a massive early-type galaxy and a gas-rich, late-type galaxy Schweizer1980, Mackie1998, Goudfrooij2001, Iodice2017, Serra2019 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses Fornax A as a bright, complex MWA EoR foreground source whose shapelet model substantially reduces residual foreground power.

quotes.quote: Most recently, Line:2020 modelled Fornax A, one of the brightest and most complex sources located within the MWA EoR main observing fields, using shapelets and MWA data at multiple resolutions. When testing the shapelet model of Fornax A, Line:2020 , found that the residuals in their set of real MWA test data were dominated by systematics unrelated to Fornax A alone. However, when using a simulated MWA data set, the residual power in the EoR PS after foreground removal was reduced by two orders of magnitude at all angular scales, just by improving the Fornax A model.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1316 as the giant elliptical host of Fornax A, with S-shaped recent radio outburst emission and multiple generations of X-ray cavities.

quotes.quote: NGC 1316 The giant elliptical galaxy, NGC 1316, hosts a well-known strong radio source Fornax A located at the outskirts of the Fornax cluster. Radio emission in the higher resolution BA configuration image extends from the northwest to southeast in the form of an oblong letter 'S' and represents the most recent of its AGN outbursts with the south-eastern extension also visible in the C configuration image (Figure fig:chandra_vla_b a), both previously published; fomalont1989, maccagni2020a,maccagni2021b . \ Several generations of X-ray cavities are visible in the XMM-Newton and Chandra images lanz2010 .

sources.summary: The paper treats For A as a nearby powerful AGN that can improve Auger AGN-catalog correlations and whose flux is enhanced in the Prim magnetic-field model.

quotes.quote: The radio galaxies, Centaurus A (Cen A, NGC 5128), Virgo A (Vir A, M87), and Fornax A (For A, NGC 1316) are the closest and most powerful nearby AGNs van2012radio,cavagnolo2010 .
It has been suggested that For A can increase the correlation of the Auger data with ray AGN catalogs matthews2018fornax,de_Oliveira_2022 .
The enhancement happens to M82, NGC 4945, NGC 2903, NGC 2146, and For A.

sources.summary: The review identifies Fornax A's lobes as breakthrough IC/3K detections, relatively clean of thermal contamination, and yielding magnetic-field estimates of a few microgauss.

quotes.quote: The recent breakthrough came from the dual detections of IC/3K from the lobes of Fornax A: spatially with the ROSAT PSPC (Feigelson et al. 1995) and spectrally with ASCA (Kaneda et. al. 1995).
For relics and radio lobes such as Fornax A, we do not have to contend with excessive thermal emission and can get a fairly clean measurement if IC/3K intensity and distribution.

Magnetic field strengths of a few G have been derived from equipartition calculations for the Coma radio halo, from the IC/3K estimates for the lobes of Fornax A, and from some estimates for clusters from Faraday rotation.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC1316 as underpredicted by 4-7 orders of magnitude, describes it as a disturbed early-type elliptical, and finds the adjusted model within about one order.

quotes.quote: Sources that were under-predicted 4-7 orders of magnitude are: NGC1291, NGC1316, NGC1553, NGC4261, NGC4438, NGC4594, NGC4697 and IC4296.

NGC1291 is an early type spiral, NGC1316 is disturbed early type elliptical, NGC 1553 has a spiral feature passing threw a center, NGC 4261 core is surrounded by a disk of gas and dust, NGC4438 has an outflow bubbles which also may indicate angular momentum to dominate the accretion flow, NGC4594 is a spiral galaxy, the last two sourced NGC 4697 and IC4296 are elliptical radio galaxies.

Other sources (NGC1291, NGC1316, NGC4438, NGC4472, M87, NGC4594, NGC4697, IC1459, IC4296) differ from their observational counterparts of about 1 order of magnitude, lots of them even less than order of magnitude (see Table 1.)

gmw_00000102.name: H 0557-385
gmw_00000102.overview: H 0557-385 is a Seyfert 1.2 galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770] hosted within a highly-inclined early-type galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770]. The active nucleus exhibits complex intrinsic X-ray absorption [arXiv:astro-ph/0310248, arXiv:astro-ph/0603699], a soft excess [arXiv:astro-ph/0310248], and a neutral iron emission line [arXiv:astro-ph/0310248, arXiv:astro-ph/0603699].

sources.summary: The paper finds that H0557-385 varies above 10 keV, needs complex intrinsic absorption and a soft excess, and shows a neutral iron line typical of Seyfert 1 galaxies.

quotes.quote: H0557-385 (IRAS F05563-3820) Also H0557-385 has been observed twice by BeppoSAX (December 2000 and January 2001) and found at a similar flux level below 10 keV; above this energy the flux changed by about a factor of 2 in just over a month. Therefore the two observations were analyzed individually to search for possible spectral differences particularly at high energies.

This source requires both intrinsic absorption and a soft excess component. The absorber is complex with a cold and a warm column density, both fully covering the source. The cold absorber is of the order of 1.1 0.3 10 ^ 21 cm ^ 2 in both observations while the warm absorber is N _ H^* =8.6^ +1.2 _ 0.9 10 ^ 21 cm ^ 2 with =5.9 ^ +7.8 _ 4.8 erg cm s ^ 1 in the first observation and N _ H^* =6.5^ +1.1 _ 0.9 10 ^ 21 cm^ 2 with =4.3 ^ +4.5 _ 3.2 erg cm s^ 1 in the second one.

An iron line is detected in each measurement with high significance ( 99 confidence level): the line is compatible with being neutral and has an equivalent width value typical of Seyfert 1 galaxies (EW= 120-150 eV).

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3A 0557-383 as a Seyfert 1 galaxy with complex low-energy absorption and an iron K-shell line, adopting Turner et al.’s ionized-gas model.

quotes.quote: 3A 0557-383 The hard X-ray source 3A 0557-383 was identified with a Seyfert 1 galaxy. Turner1996 reported the complex absorption below 2 keV of the X-ray spectrum from an ASCA observation. They found a good fit with either 96 of the source covered by a column of 3.1 10 ^ 22 cm ^ 2 of low-ionization gas or full covering by a column of 3.6 10 ^ 22 cm ^ 2 of highly ionized gas, and an neutral iron K-shell emission line with equivalent width 300 eV. We used the latter model (Model D of Turner1996 ).

sources.summary: The atlas gives H 0557-385's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: H 0557-385 is a Sy 1.2 object veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 in the highly-inclined early-type galaxy LEDA 75476 at a redshift of z= 0.0339 ( D 144 Mpc) that belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. This object was observed with /IRAC, IRS and MIPS where it appears as a nearly unresolved nuclear MIR source without any extended host emission. The PBCD IRAC 8.0 m image is partly saturated and is not used.

gmw_00000103.name: H 1143-182
gmw_00000103.overview: H 1143-182 is a low-inclination spiral host galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770] classified as a Seyfert 1.5 active galaxy [arXiv:1112.0540, arXiv:1310.2770]. Its near-infrared spectrum is dominated by broad permitted lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0605463], and its nuclear region exhibits a blurred reflection component [arXiv:1105.3065].

sources.summary: The paper highlights 2MASX J11454045-1827149 as a Sy 1.5 with a low 2MASS-derived black-hole mass and suspiciously high Eddington ratio needing H-width follow-up.

quotes.quote: Additionally, we note that the mass of the Sy 1.5 2MASX J11454045-1827149, derived from the 2MASS K-band photometry, is low ( M/M_ = 6.2 ), requiring a very high accretion rate ( L_ bol /L_ Edd = 3.5 ). We have obtained optical spectroscopy of the H- region of this source from the CTIO SOAR telescope and are in the process of reducing the data. An alternative mass estimate will be included in Winter et al., in prep.

For one source, 2MASX J11454045-1827149, which does not have a measurement from alternative methods, the Eddington ratio is suspiciously high ( > 1 ). As discussed above, we will determine a mass estimate based on the H- width (Winter et al., in prep).

sources.summary: The atlas treats H 1143-182 as an individually observed active or starburst galaxy, summarizing its prior classification and near-infrared continuum, emission-line, and absorption properties.

quotes.quote: H 1143-182 . This classical Seyfert 1 galaxy has been studied mostly in the X-rays and UV region. The NIR spectrum is dominated by broad permitted lines of H i , He i , O i , and Fe ii , with intrinsic FWHM of 3800 . The NLR spectrum is rather weak with only [ S iii ] , [ S ix ] , [ Si x ] , and [ Si vi ] detected. No narrow components of the permitted lines were identified.

sources.summary: The paper detects blurred reflection in HE 1143-1800 with Model SRC but not Model ION, and its emissivity index helps explain why Model N07 missed reflection.

quotes.quote: A significant detection of a blurred reflection component is made in 7/11 observations at a confidence level of 95 : MCG+8-11-11, Mrk 6, Mrk 110, NGC 7469(1), NGC 2110, HE 1143-1800 and NGC 5506(1).
NGC 2110, HE 1143-1800 and NGC 5506(1) are the three observations that give a significant detection of the blurred reflection with Model SRC, but not with Model ION.
For NGC 2110, MCG+8-11-11, Mrk 110 and HE 1143-1800, the uncertainty of the emissivity index is not compatible with q = 3 , which explains why reflection could not be detected with Model N07.

sources.summary: The atlas gives H 1143-182's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: H 1143-182 ( z = 0.0329 : D 144 Mpc) was discovered as an AGN in X-rays by . It is classified as a Sy 1.5 veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 , and belongs to the 9 month BAT AGN sample. The host galaxy is the low-inclination spiral galaxy LEDA 88639.

gmw_00000104.name: Hydra A
gmw_00000104.overview: Hydra A is a giant elliptical brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:2005.10252, arXiv:2403.03974] and a Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxy [arXiv:1304.0989] hosting a LINER nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770]. The system features a rotating edge-on molecular disk [arXiv:1902.09227, arXiv:2403.03974] and precessing radio jets that have inflated large-scale lobes and X-ray cavities [arXiv:1809.04050, arXiv:2112.09056].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PGC026269 with Hydra A, reports new IFU observations, finds rotating but decoupled stellar and gas components, and links optical line ratios to AGN photoionisation.

quotes.quote: PGC026269 This galaxy coincides with the well-known luminous radio counterpart Hydra A. Abell 0780 is a poor cluster with an associated cooling flow nebulae. IFU observations of this galaxy has not been presented in the literature before this study. The stellar line-of-sight velocity (from the IFU data) is blue-and redshifted by 175 km s ^ 1 which is slightly higher than that derived from long slit spectroscopy (51 20 km s ^ 1 Paper 1). We plot the kinematics of the stellar and gaseous components in Figures fig:PGC026kinematics and fig:PGC026kinematics2 .

Both the stellar and gaseous components show clear rotation in Figures fig:PGC026kinematics and fig:PGC026kinematics2 , although it also seems to be decoupled. This system bears resemblance to the BCG NCG 3311 in Abell 1060, studied by Edwards et al. \ (2009) which also showed striking rotation. The rotation of the warm gas agrees with the long-slit observations by McDonald et al. \ (2012).

sources.summary: The paper treats Hydra A as a cluster-centre FRI comparison case, using published X-ray profiles and radio maps to show pressure behavior similar to 3C 31.

quotes.quote: Although the majority of this paper focuses on 3C 31, using our new X-ray data, we also carried out a pressure comparison for Hydra A as a preliminary test of whether our findings are likely to apply widely to FRI radio galaxies. For Hydra A, we did not reanalyse the archival Chandra and XMM-Newton observations, but made use of previously published gas density and temperature profiles. For the gas density profile we used the double beta model of wise07 , normalised to the density profile published by david01 . We interpolated over the (projected) temperature profile of david01 to obtain a cluster pressure profile over the radial ranges of interest, which is also shown in Fig. press1 .

For Hydra A we used the 330-MHz map of laing04 for the outer lobes with a resolution of 15 arcsec, and to image the inner structure with sufficient resolution for our geometric measurements we made a map using A and B configuration archival VLA data at 1.4 GHz e.g. taylor90 with resolution of 1.4 arcsec. For 3C 31, roughly 20 regions per jet were used to measure the radio flux density and jet geometry. For Hydra A around 20 regions were used to study the northern jet.

For Hydra A, we simply calculated an internal pressure profile for the existing radio bins, under the same assumptions. This profile is shown in Fig. press1 , illustrating a strong qualitative similarity to the behaviour of the 3C 31 jets.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA molecular absorption observations of the brightest cluster galaxy Hydra-A, linking its compact AGN sightline to clumpy molecular clouds near the galaxy center.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We present Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array observations of the brightest cluster galaxy Hydra-A, a nearby ( z=0.054 ) giant elliptical galaxy with powerful and extended radio jets. The observations reveal CO(1-0), CO(2-1), ^ 13 CO(2-1), CN(2-1), SiO(5-4), HCO ^ + (1-0), HCO ^ + (2-1), HCN(1-0), HCN(2-1), HNC(1-0) and H _ 2 CO(3-2) absorption lines against the galaxy's bright and compact active galactic nucleus. These absorption features are due to at least 12 individual molecular clouds which lie close to the centre of the galaxy and have velocities of approximately 50 to +10 relative to its recession velocity, where positive values correspond to inward motion. The absorption profiles are evidence of a clumpy interstellar medium within brightest cluster galaxies composed of clouds with similar column densities, velocity dispersions and excitation temperatures to those found at radii of several kpc in the Milky Way.

We present ALMA observations of CO, ^ 13 CO, CN, SiO, HCO ^ + , HCN, HNC and H _ 2 CO molecular absorption lines seen against the bright radio core of Hydra-A. Their narrow velocity dispersions (typically 1 km s ^ 1 ) are similar to those seen molecular cloud complexes of the Milky Way and indicate that the observations are tracing individual clouds of cold molecular gas. The molecular gas clouds typically have excitation temperatures of 5 - 10 K, diameters of 1 - 10 pc and masses of a few tens to a few thousands of M_ . The precise origins and locations of the absorbing molecular clouds within Hydra-A are difficult to constrain, though they are most likely to be within the inner few kpc of the disc where the column densities of molecular gas are highest.

sources.summary: The paper treats Hydra A/3C218 as an extended radio source in Abell 780, models its cooling-flow environment, and analyzes lobe RM data, focusing on the cleaner north lobe.

quotes.quote: Hydra A (or 3C218) is an extended extragalactic radio source located at a redshift of 0.0538 1991trcb.book.....D in the cluster Abell 780 of richness class 0. However, we will refer to it hereafter as Hydra A cluster. Detailed X-ray studies have been performed on it e.g. 1997ApJ...481..660I, 1998MNRAS.298..416P, 2001ApJ...557..546D . For the derivation of the electron density profile parameter, we relied on the work by 1999ApJ...517..627M done for ROSAT PSPC data while using the deprojection of X-ray surface brightness profiles as described in the Appendix A of pfrommerensslin . Since Hydra A is known to exhibit a strong cooling flow as observed in the X-ray studies, we assumed a double profile defined as n_ e () = [ n_ e1 ^2 (0)(1+(/r_ c1 )^2)^ 3 +n_ e2 ^2 (0)(1+(/r_ c2 )^2)^ 3 ] ^ 1/2 .

The source consists of two lobes, a northern and a southern one. Typical RM values in the north lobe are in the range between -1000 rad m ^ 2 and +3300 rad m ^ 2 whereas in the south lobe also values of down to -12000 rad m ^ 2 were observed. We concentrated our analysis on the north lobe of this source because the signal-to-noise ratio in the data of the south lobe does not seem sufficient enough for our purposes.

sources.summary: The paper describes Hydra A as a central two-lobed polarized radio source with strong Faraday rotation and uses its lobes to test Pacman rotation-measure mapping.

quotes.quote: The polarised radio source Hydra A, which is in the centre of the Abell cluster 780, also known as the Hydra A cluster, is located at a redshift of 0.0538 1991trcb.book.....D . The source Hydra A shows an extended, two-sided radio lobe. Detailed X-ray studies have been performed on this cluster e.g. 1997ApJ...481..660I, 1998MNRAS.298..416P, 2001ApJ...557..546D . They revealed a strong cooling core in the cluster centre. The Faraday rotation structure was observed and analysed by 1993ApJ...416..554T . They reported RM values ranging between 1 000 rad m ^ 2 and +3 300 rad m ^ 2 in the north lobe and values down to 12 000 rad m ^ 2 in the south lobe.

In this section, we concentrate on the north lobe of Hydra A and discuss the south lobe separately in Sect.  sec:hydrasouth . The south lobe is more depolarised, leading to a lower signal-to-noise than in the north lobe. The north lobe is a good example of how to treat noise in the data, while the south lobe gives a good opportunity to discuss the limitations and the strength of our algorithm Pacman .

sources.summary: The paper investigates Hydra A as the nearest cluster-scale AGN outburst system, reporting gamma-ray non-detections that constrain particle populations in its radio lobes and bubbles.

quotes.quote: Hydra A, the closest galaxy cluster hosting a cluster scale AGN outburst, located at a redshift of 0.0538, is investigated for being a gamma-ray emitter with the High Energy Stereoscopic System (H.E.S.S.) array and the Fermi Large Area Telescope ( Fermi LAT).

The non-detection of Hydra A in gamma-rays has important implications on the particle populations and physical conditions inside the bubbles in this system. For the case of bubbles mainly supported by hadronic cosmic rays, the most favorable scenario, that involves full mixing between cosmic rays and embedding medium, can be excluded. However, hadronic cosmic rays still remain a viable pressure support agent to sustain the bubbles against the thermal pressure of the ambient medium. The largest population of highly-energetic electrons which are relevant for inverse-Compton gamma-ray production is found in the youngest inner lobes of Hydra A.

The Hydra A system (Abell 0780) at RA(J2000) = 9 ^ h 18 ^ m 05.7 ^ s and Dec(J2000) = -12 05 44 at a redshift of 0.0538 is the closest known galaxy cluster which hosts a cluster-scale AGN outburst nulsen2005b . It features several cavities with a total expansion work pV of 4 10^ 60 erg done on the ICM. Thus the total energy required, depending on the equation of state of the main pressure agent, is (0.8 - 1.6) 10^ 61 erg which were deposited in the last few 10 ^8 years in the surroundings wise2007 . Hydra A also features low-frequency radio lobes extending to almost 4 \ from the cluster center lane2004 .

sources.summary: The paper uses Chandra data to show Hydra A transports metal-enriched gas from the central galaxy along radio jets, enhancing intracluster metallicity out to 120 kpc.

quotes.quote: Using deep Chandra observations of the Hydra A galaxy cluster, we examine the metallicity structure near the central galaxy and along its powerful radio source. We show that the metallicity of the intracluster medium is enhanced by up to 0.2 dex along the radio jets and lobes compared to the metallicity of the undisturbed gas. The enhancements extend from a radius of 20 kpc from the central galaxy to a distance of 120 kpc.

Evidently, Hydra A's powerful radio source is able to redistribute metal-enriched, low entropy gas throughout the core of the galaxy cluster. Hydra A has undergone a major AGN outburst that has transported 2 - 7 10 ^7 M _ of metal-enriched gas out of the BCG along the radio jets. The gas has been enhanced by up to 0.2 dex in metallicity compared to the ambient gas out to distances as large as 120 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper presents Hydra A as a feedback system with X-ray cavities, radio jets, line-emitting gas, and apparent polarization explained by noise rather than a detection.

quotes.quote: Hydra A: displays the now classical feedback situation (McNamara et al. 2000) with X-ray cavities unarguably at the locations of the outwardly propagating radio jets. The optical line emitting gas spans the region between the radio core and radio knots, shares the S-symmetry of the radio emission and overlaps with the radio emission only at the edges of the knots (Baum et al. 1988). Our long slit, in p.a. 20^ , runs approximately in the direction of the radio source, orthogonal to an edge-on dust disk, Fig. 4.

The polarized flux data, Fig.8, show elevated levels of apparent polarization at the position of the dust lane, and also excess emission at the positions of H + [ NII ] and [ SII ] 6717, 6731 . However, as shown in Fig. 14, this also is a consequence of the positive definite nature of the polarized flux in the presence of higher noise due to Poisson counting statistics. The derived position angle is quite close to that of the dust lane, but again the uncertainties are too large to allow us to conclude we have a definitive measurement of polarization, formally only 1.25 for H on the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper describes Hydra A as a well-studied precessing radio source whose simulations and jet geometry imply a 1 Myr precession period and subparsec binary separation.

quotes.quote: Hydra A This well studied radio source has a large-scale S-symmetry Taylea90 . An extended 3D simulation study of the jet-environment interaction for this particular source, where source geometry and precession period has been varied, has recently determined the precession period to 1 Myr Nawea16a . This is also compatible with a Gowea82 model of precessing jets with relativistic aberration (Fig fig:Hydracomp and Table t:hyapars ). As argued above for similar precession periods, this precession is most likely related to geodetic precession due to a binary supermassive black hole. The precession period together with the black hole mass of (5 4) 10^8 M_ Rafea06 limits the binary separation to a maximum of 0.2 pc (eq. eq:geoprecsep ).
The parsec-scale jet is obviously misaligned with the large-scale jet, as expected for a precessing source.

sources.summary: Hydra-A is described as a well-studied molecular absorption system with a massive edge-on gas disc and absorption from clouds aligned with the continuum.

quotes.quote: Hydra-A has a 5 kpc wide edge-on molecular gas disc with a total mass of 3.1 0.2 10^ 9 M _ , a smooth velocity gradient and a velocity range of approximately 700 km/s Rose2019a . Lower angular resolution CO(2-1) observations from IRAM place the mass at 2.26 0.29 10^ 9 M _ Hamer2014 . The molecular gas with velocities matched to the absorption is centred on the radio core (Fig. fig:main_fig_1 ) and extended by a few hundred parsecs along the blueshifted edge of the disc.

Hydra-A is by far the best studied absorption system of this kind, and this matches the observations of Rose2020 . They identified at least 12 molecular clouds which are responsible for Hydra-A's absorption. The absorbing clouds are also at the peak of the emission profile -- the most likely velocity at which we would detect clouds lying in the disc.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS0915-11 as a powerful local FRI radio source whose SPIRE fluxes are confused by a companion, while recent star formation may boost its thermal infrared component.

quotes.quote: Reduced PACS and SPIRE images of PKS0915-11 can be seen in Figure Fig:PKS0915 . This object appears brighter than its nearby companion in the PACS bands; however, the companion becomes relatively brighter from 100 to 160 . It is clear that the companion then dominates in the longer wavelength SPIRE images.

Of the two WLRG/FRI objects that are not dominated by non-thermal core emission at all far-IR wavelengths, PKS0915-11 (Hydra A) is particularly interesting: despite its FRI radio morphology, it is one of the most powerful radio sources in the local Universe. Also, unlike most WLRG/FRI sources, it has been identified to have a strong recent star formation component dicken12 . Therefore, it is possible that star formation heating of dust boosts the thermal component of its SED, so that the non-thermal emission does not dominate the infrared SED as for the other WLRG/FRI in the sample.

sources.summary: The paper reports a weak shock front from an AGN outburst in Hydra A, linking radio lobe cavities, cluster-scale shock energy, cooling-flow feedback, and preheating.

quotes.quote: Here we report the discovery of a weak shock front generated by an AGN outburst in the Hydra A Cluster. Hydra A has well known radio lobe cavities mwn00,dnm01,ndm02 , which surround the 1.4 GHz inner radio lobes and extend to 40'' (40 kpc) from the cluster center. By contrast, the weak shock front discussed here extends up to 6' from the cluster center, surrounding the much larger low frequency radio lobes lct04 and representing a much more energetic phenomenon.

In section sec:struct we discuss the main features of the new deep image of Hydra A and in section sec:shock we discuss the evidence for the shock front and estimate the energy of the outburst that drove it. We assume flat CDM, with H_0 = 70 \ km \ s^ 1 \ Mpc^ 1 and _ m = 0.3 , giving a scale of 1.05 \ kpc \ arcsec^ 1 at the redshift of Hydra A, z = 0.0538 . The outburst in Hydra A is three orders of magnitude more energetic than that which produced the 14 kpc shock in M87 10^ 58 erg, fnh04 .

sources.summary: The paper models Hydra A as a cluster-scale AGN outburst where hadronic cosmic rays inflate X-ray bubbles, power radio lobes, and may produce gamma rays near telescope sensitivity limits.

quotes.quote: One system featuring such an outburst is Hydra A. High energy particles which are injected by AGNs in clusters of galaxies can produce gamma rays through various processes (see for a recent review). Inelastic collisions between cosmic ray protons and target nuclei in form of the thermal intra-cluster medium (ICM) can result in emission of gamma rays through ^0 decay , . Cosmic microwave background (CMB) photons can be up scattered by high energy electrons to gamma-ray energies in inverse compton processes , , . In this contribution we investigate a scenario were the bubbles in the X-ray gas of Hydra A are expanded by hadronic cosmic rays and produce gamma rays due to inelastic collisions with nuclei of the thermal plasma and subsequent ^0 decay.

In case of Hydra A the total energy of the outburst is close to 10 ^ 61 erg. Composition of the non-thermal particles inside the lobes: The age of the outburst in the Hydra A system was estimated to be in the order of 10 ^8 years.
In case of the Hydra A system the ratio between the density of thermal plasma inside and outside the radio lobes is less than 0.3 .

sources.summary: The paper observes Hydra A as a symmetric two-sided FRI radio galaxy at z=0.054, finding dramatic spectral-index and curvature increases along its jets, especially northward.

quotes.quote: Hydra A The radio structure of Hydra A has been studied by Taylor90 Dwarakanath95 and Lane04 ; extended regions of very steep spectrum emission are seen to the north and south of the emission in Figure HydraAContFig . This source is a symmetric two-sided FRI radio galaxy with a weak, strongly self absorbed core (position C). Hydra A is at a redshift of z=0.054 Dwarakanath95 giving a projected linear size of 1.1 kpc per arc-second; the source is visible in Figures HydraACleanFig and HydraAContFig to approximately 45 kpc from the nucleus. Magnetic field estimates of Taylor90 from minimum pressure arguments range from about 10 to 70 micro-gauss in various features giving estimated radiative lifetimes of the 90 GHz emitting electrons in the range 0.3 to 5 Myr. In this galaxy, both the spectral index and curvature increase dramatically along the visible portion of the jets, especially in the north.

sources.summary: The paper treats Hydra A as a Chandra-observed FR I radio galaxy embedded in the Hydra cluster, using its surrounding magnetic field and X-ray spectrum to constrain ALP-induced irregularities.

quotes.quote: One of the best candidate sources is Hydra A, a Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxy located at redshift z = 0.0538 that has been observed by the Chandra Observatory . It is centered on a corona of hot thermal electrons emitting in X-rays by radiative cooling.

The magnetic field profile surrounding Hydra A has been extensively studied using Faraday rotation maps of the polarization of the radio emission of the active galactic nucleus lobes due to the propagation through the magnetized electron plasma 1993ApJ...416..554T,2003A&A...412..373V,2005A&A...434...67V,2008MNRAS.391..521L,2011A&A...529A..13K .

The search for irregularities in the X-ray spectrum of Hydra A measured by Chandra leads to a constraint at the 95 CL on the ALP coupling to two photons of g_ a < 8.3 10^ 12 GeV ^ 1 for ALP masses below 7 10^ 12 eV . In this study, the key point is the use of a measured profile of the magnetic field, determined from Faraday rotation maps.

sources.summary: The paper uses Hydra A as the Parkes flux calibrator, noting its known 1.4 GHz flux density and spectral index, radio lobes, and possible contribution to calibration bias.

quotes.quote: Additionally we observed an absolute flux density calibrator, the radio galaxy Hydra A, once every week of observations.

For the polarisation calibration we used the ideal feed assumption and for the absolute flux density calibration we used the radio galaxy Hydra A (3C218). While there are small discrepancies in published measurements, which could arise by different pointing positions along the two radio lobes, its flux density at 1.4 GHz and spectral index is well known, see Table tab:Calibrators . We adopted the value reported by 1977Baars .
That could be a result of a decrease in the flux density of Hydra A, or a slight offset in telescope pointing -- the spectral index of Hydra A changes rapidly across its extent 2004Lane .

sources.summary: The paper uses Hydra A as the archetypal disk-dominated central galaxy, with an edge-on molecular disk and ordered rotation perpendicular to the radio jet axis.

quotes.quote: Whilst nearby Hydra A clearly hosts an edge-on molecular gas disk ( Rose19 ), the molecular emission peaks at the centre of the majority of our more distant targets are not resolved (eg. A1835, Phoenix).

By comparison, in A262 and Hydra A, the majority of the molecular gas is resolved in a rotating circumnuclear gas disk (eg. Prandoni07,Hatch07 ) and only 10 extends to larger radii. Disk-dominated sources are rare in our sample ( 15 ) with only these two clear examples.
The archetype Hydra A hosts an edge-on disk, 5 across. IFU observations of the ionized and warm molecular hydrogen show rotation in a plane perpendicular to the radio jet axis ( Hamer14 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses Hydra-A radio-galaxy observations to validate MWA SEFD simulations, describing the observing setup, beam pointing, single-source sky model, and extended-source flux model.

quotes.quote: The relative difference SEFD _I^ obs defined by Eq. eqn:SEFD_I_error was measured using MWA observations tracking Hydra-A radio-galaxy recorded in a standard observing mode at the central frequency 154.88 MHz. On 2014-12-26 between 16:05:42 and 05:13:42 UTC, the MWA recorded 23 112 s observations centred on the Hydra-A radio-galaxy at a position (Az,ZA) = (80.7 , 45.8 ) and (Az,ZA) = (290.7 , 32.4 ) at the start and end of the observations respectively. For the purpose of a comparison of SEFD _I between the data and simulations, 52 s of the first 112 s observation (obsID 1103645160 referred later as ObsA), which started at 2014-12-26 16:05:42 UTC with Hydra-A at (Az,ZA) = (80.7 , 45.8 ), were analysed.

The same observation ObsA was used for calibration. It was sufficient to use a single-source sky model composed of Hydra-A alone, which was the dominant source in the observed field. However, due to the extended structure of Hydra-A, which is resolved by the longest baselines of the MWA Phase 1, it was necessary to use its model by 2014MNRAS.440..327L converted to the required multi-component format by gleamx https://github.com/nhurleywalker/GLEAM-X-pipeline .

sources.summary: Hydra A is modeled as a significant local source, especially at the highest energies, and can become a dominant UHECR source in expanded source samples.

quotes.quote: Surprisingly, also Centaurus A contributes only a negligible fraction to the total UHECR intensity, especially at high energies, while the most significant local sources on average are Virgo A, Fornax A, Pictor A, and Hydra A. The latter two are important in particular at the highest energies, E 100 EeV . The relative dominance between those four sources depends significantly on the EGMF strength and their lifetimes.
We can conclude that a local source sample consisting of either Hydra A and Pictor A or Virgo A/ 3C 270 and Fornax A is sufficient to explain the data.

Including more local sources---in particular at Galactic longitudes between 90 \ and 180 --leads to a multitude of possible scenarios where, e.g., Hydra A, Pictor A or Centaurus B can become the dominating UHECR sources without improving the fit significantly.

sources.summary: The paper reports that 0915--11 has a point-source plus extended X-ray profile, low Eddington ratio, and ambiguous but possible radiatively inefficient accretion depending on the observation.

quotes.quote: The object 0915--11 was fit well by a PSF plus -model out to 25 arcseconds. Past 25 arcseconds the source appears to require another -model, but we could not get good statistics on a second -model---perhaps because the field of view was not large enough.

Even the \ observation of 0915--11, with its high X--ray luminosity, has L_ bol /L_ Edd 10^ 3 . This is a model--independent indication of Radiatively Inefficient Accretion Flow (RIAF). Using the results of our spatial analysis, specifically the gas density profile obtained by the de--projection of the surface brightness profile, we calculated M _ Bondi , which serves as a rough estimate of accretion onto the black hole. With M _ Bondi and L_ bol we can use the formula L_ bol = M _ Bondi ^2 to make an estimate of the radiative efficiency of accretion . We find values on the order of 10^ 5 10^ 6 , except for the object 0915--11, which using the \ observation yields =0.01 , and using the \ observation yields =0.09 . Those objects with <<0.1 are very likely to have inefficient accretion, but even 0915--11, based on the \ observation, is a candidate. However, the \ observation makes the accretion scenario for 0915--11 ambiguous.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PGC026269 as Hydra A and a notable BCG, with moderate rotation, an increasing velocity-dispersion profile, and low central dispersion.

quotes.quote: PGC026269: Also known as Hydra A. The radial kinematic profiles of this galaxy reveal rotation of the order of 51 km s ^ 1 along the major axis, as well as an increasing velocity dispersion profile.
Five out of 41 BCGs (ESO349-010, ESO444-046, ESO552-020, NGC3311, PGC026269) were found to have a positive velocity dispersion gradient.

The only notable BCG data point that lies significantly above the dashed line is for the galaxy PGC026269, which possesses moderate rotation (51 km s ^ 1 ) and surprisingly low central velocity dispersion (222 km s ^ 1 ). However, the ellipticity of this galaxy is zero and it is not rotationally supported.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Hydra A's large-scale bubble, finding no significant inverse-Compton power-law component and deriving a 99 percent upper limit on 1 keV lobe emission.

quotes.quote: Hydra A For Hydra A we extracted spectra for and around the large-scale bubble seen in low-frequency radio observations (Fig. \ picture ), as this seemed to us to be the region of the source that was most likely to be bright in inverse-Compton emission compared to its surroundings. The best-fitting temperatures for the off-lobe, cluster regions around 3.6 keV and abundances around 0.30 solar (Table results ) are in reasonably good agreement with the properties of the ICM at this distance from the nucleus obtained by Simionescu \ (2009) using a more complex thermal model. Fixing the temperature and abundance for the lobe region and allowing the normalization of the power law to vary, we obtain a power-law normalization consistent with zero within the errors derived from the fit for both values of N_ H . Using our xspec simulations, we derive a 99 per cent confidence upper limit on the power-law normalization corresponding to 39 nJy at 1 keV for the NRAO N_ H values: the values from the alternative N_ H value are similar.

sources.summary: The paper cites Hydra A as an AGN-feedback system with cool gas around radio lobes, outer cavities filled by low-frequency radio emission, and metal-rich gas redistributed along jets.

quotes.quote: Cool gas is seen surrounding the inner radio lobes of, for example, Hydra A , Perseus A , 3C 317 in Abell 2052 , and B2 0149+35 in Abell 262 .

Examples of systems with outer cavities are Perseus , Abell 2597 , Abell 262 , Abell 2052 , and Hydra A . In all of these cases, when the cavities seemed devoid of radio emission at higher frequencies, lower frequency radio emission was found to extend farther from the cluster center to fill the outer cavities.

Analysis of deep Chandra observations of the Hydra A cluster reveals higher metallicity gas along the radio jets and lobes, indicating that the gas is being redistributed from the center (where the gas metallicity is typically higher) to larger radii in the cluster .

sources.summary: Hydra A is treated as a luminous radio galaxy whose VHE and HE upper limits constrain its extended lobes, favoring near equipartition and limiting hadronic cosmic-ray support.

quotes.quote: This included upper limits for the VHE emission of a luminous radio galaxy Hydra A located at the center of a rich cluster of galaxies . These limits, together with the analogous ones in the HE regime, allow to constrain the energetics and the content of the extended lobes formed in the system due to the interactions of relativistic jets with the intracluster medium. In particular, it was found that the extended lobes in Hydra A have to be close to the equipartition condition u_e/u_B 1 , and cannot be fully supported by hadronic cosmic rays, as otherwise the derived ray upper limits would be violated. However, ultrarelativistic protons can still remain a viable pressure support agent to sustain the lobes against the thermal pressure of the ambient medium.

sources.summary: The paper compares precessing relativistic jet simulations with Hydra A, finding parameters that reproduce the northern jet curvature, knot pattern, plume transition, and gentle bow-shock heating.

quotes.quote: We present three dimensional relativistic hydrodynamical simulations of a precessing jet interacting with the intracluster medium and compare the simulated jet structure with the observed structure of the Hydra A northern jet. For the simulations, we use jet parameters obtained in the parameter space study of the first paper in this series and probe different values for the precession period and precession angle. We find that for a precession period P 1 Myr and a precession angle 20^ the model reproduces i) the curvature of the jet, ii) the correct number of bright knots within 20 kpc at approximately correct locations, and iii) the turbulent transition of the jet to a plume. The Mach number of the advancing bow shock 1.85 is indicative of gentle cluster atmosphere heating during the early stages of the AGN's activity.

sources.summary: Hydra A is described as a powerful central radio source in A780, with complex 500 kpc radio morphology, active inner jets, cavities, shocks, and AGN-driven lobes regulating ICM cooling.

quotes.quote: A780 shows many small-scale substructures related to the AGN-ICM interaction in the center of the cluster. In fact, A780 is better known for the powerful radio source, Hydra A (3C 218), emanating from its central, brightest galaxy ( matthews1964 ). The radio source has a complex morphology that extends nearly 8 ( 500 kpc) in the north-south direction, slightly NNE-SSW ( lane2004 ), while active jets have been detected in the central 50 kpc region ( taylor1990 ). Chandra X-ray observations have revealed a multicavity system, a large-scale shock, an X-ray bright filament near the cluster center, and a spiral like structure in the ICM ( mcnamara2000 , nulsen2005 ; wise2007 ; lagana2010 ). According to general consensus, the giant radio lobes, triggered by AGN jets interact with the surrounding ICM and regulate the cooling flow in Hydra A.

sources.summary: The paper describes Hydra-A as a giant elliptical BCG with an edge-on dust and molecular disk, powerful radio lobes, X-ray cavities, and well-studied molecular absorption clouds.

quotes.quote: Hydra-A Galaxy and environment Hydra-A is a giant elliptical galaxy with an edge-on disk of dust and molecular gas which lies at the centre of an X-ray bright cluster Hamer2014 . At 200 GHz, powerful radio lobes propagate 40 kpc to the north and south from the centre Taylor1990 , but at 1 GHz these extend to over 500 kpc in the north and 250 kpc in the south Lane2004, Baidoo2023 . The radio lobes are surrounded by cavities in the X-ray emitting gas of the intracluster medium created by repeated AGN outbursts McNamara2000, Wise2006 .
Hydra-A has a 3.1 0.2 10^ 9 M _ edge-on molecular disk with a very smooth velocity gradient and a central velocity matching that of of the stellar absorption lines of the radio core Rose2019a .

sources.summary: The atlas gives Hydra A's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: Hydra A is a FR I radio source coinciding with the early-type galaxy MCG-2-24-7 at a redshift of z= 0.0549 ( D 240 Mpc) with a LINER nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . The first MIR detection of Hydra A was with /MIPS shi_far-infrared_2005 . Our photometric measurement of the MIPS 24 m matches their published fluxes.

sources.summary: The paper models Hydra-A with about 33 starbursts separated by roughly 0.2 Gyr and groups it among BCGs requiring periodic starbursts.

quotes.quote: As an example, in case of Hydra-A, the best fitting combination is given by =180 Myr and =33 .
Hence our results show that the ``young'' stellar component in Hydra-A comprises about 33 starbursts separated roughly by about 0.2 Gyr, the youngest being 0.2 Gyr old and the oldest being 6 Gyr old.

While ZwCl 3146, A 1795, A 1835 and RXC J1504 seem to require continuous star formation, NGC 1275, PKS 0745, Hydra-A, A 1068 and A 2597 seem to require periodic starbursts with the interval ranging between 20 Myr and 200 Myr.

sources.summary: The paper notes Hydra A's prominent molecular disk causes line-frequency absorption at the AGN, so its inner 160 pc is excluded and east/west spectra are measured separately.

quotes.quote: Hydra A has a prominent disk and substantial absorption due to the disk is seen at the line frequency at the position of the AGN ( ; see figure fig:image ). Thus, we excluded the innermost 160 pc, and we extracted and examined the spectra in the eastern and western regions of the AGN separately (figure fig:spec ). The parenthesized FWHM values shown in table tab:result are the flux averages of the two gaussian components except for Hydra A, for which it is the flux average of the east and west values.

gmw_00000105.name: IC 883
gmw_00000105.overview: IC 883 is a luminous infrared galaxy undergoing a major merger [arXiv:1411.0361, arXiv:1701.07025], featuring two large tidal tails [arXiv:1111.3293, arXiv:1701.07025] and an unsettled rotating molecular gas disk [arXiv:1411.0361, arXiv:1410.5560]. It is classified as a starburst-active galactic nucleus composite [arXiv:1701.07025, arXiv:1807.04298] with a parsec-scale radio jet [arXiv:1810.02371, arXiv:1811.09281] and optical LINER characteristics [arXiv:0901.0704, arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The paper presents Arp 193 as a tailed LIRG/LINER whose mid-infrared and molecular-line properties favor spatially extended starburst activity rather than a compact AGN-dominated nucleus.

quotes.quote: Arp 193 (z=0.023) is a LIRG (L _ IR = 10 ^ 11.6 L _ ) with a long narrow tail along the south-eastern direction rot04 . It is classified optically as a LINER vei95 , showing no obvious optical AGN signatures. The mid-infrared 8--20 m dust emission is dominated by a spatially-extended component with no prominent compact core soi01 . The emission surface brightness is 2 10 ^ 12 L _ soi01 , which is within the range of starburst phenomena. The HCN(1--0) and HCO ^ + (1--0) fluxes of Arp 193, measured with single-dish radio telescopes, are high sol92,gra08 .

pap07 found that the HCN(4--3) to HCN(1--0) flux ratio of Arp 193 is much lower than that of other LIRGs. The weak high-excitation HCN(4--3) line may suggest that the dominant energy source is a spatially-extended diffuse one, rather than a compact high-emission surface-brightness one. So far, no obvious AGN signatures have been seen in the Arp 193 observational data veg08 .

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 883 as a LIRG major merger with nuclear activity and star formation, multi-knot radio structure, prior H I emission, and blended absorption from unsettled rotating cold gas.

quotes.quote: IC 883 - ARP 193 - UGC 8387 The host galaxy of this radio source is undergoing a major merger. The galaxy is a luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) where L_ IR =4.7 10^ 11 L_ at z=0.0233 Sanders2003 . The radio source has been observed with e-Merlin (beam size =165.23 88.35 mas) and VLBI e-EVN (beam size =9.20 6.36 mas)  Romero2012 . The radio source consists of 4 knots and extends for about 750 pc. The innermost 100 pc of the galaxy show both nuclear activity and star formation.

The resolution of our observations does not allow the disentanglement of different absorption components. Hence, the \ line in our observations is blended, spanning the same velocity range of the \ seen in emission, and of the molecular gas. The morphology of the absorption line, along with the overall properties of the cold gas detected in emission, suggests that in this galaxy the cold gas is rotating in a disk, which is unsettled because of the ongoing merger event.

sources.summary: The paper presents multiwavelength observations of IC 883 as a late-stage LIRG merger rich in clusters and tidal tails, with starburst-dominated nuclear X-rays plus evidence for a weak AGN.

quotes.quote: New optical Hubble Space Telescope (HST) , Spitzer Space Telescope , GALEX , and Chandra observations of the single-nucleus, luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) merger IC 883 are presented. The galaxy is a member of the Great Observatories All-sky LIRG Survey (GOALS), and is of particular interest for a detailed examination of a luminous late-stage merger due to the richness of the optically-visible star clusters and the extended nature of the nuclear X-ray, mid-IR, CO and radio emission. In the HST ACS images, the galaxy is shown to contain 156 optically visible star clusters distributed throughout the nuclear regions and tidal tails of the merger, with a majority of visible clusters residing in an arc 3--7 kpc from the position of the mid-infrared core of the galaxy.

The X-ray hardness ratio and the L_ X(2-10 keV) / L_ FIR(40-120 m) are both consistent with star formation being the major contributor to the hard nuclear X-ray emission from IC 883. However, the large equivalent width of the Si XIII emission line may be due, in part, to contributions from AGN photoionized gas.

The high ionization [ Ne V ] 14.32 m emission line is also detected. The emission is further evidence that an AGN is present -- it is determined that the [ Ne V ] luminosity of IC 883 is too high to be due to solely to supernova remnants.

sources.summary: The paper maps Arp 193 in CO at subarcsecond resolution, identifying it as a nearby luminous infrared merger with extended emission, resolved molecular disk kinematics, dense gas, and possible weak AGN signatures.

quotes.quote: With an interest in ultraluminous and luminous infrared galaxies (U/LIRGs; see 6.1), we chose Arp 193 (also known as IC 0883, UGC 08387, VV 821, IRAS F13182+3424, and NVSS J132035+340822) as the best test case for C-PACS observations. Unlike the closest ULIRG Arp 220, which does not have an appropriate calibrator within 12 degrees, Arp 193 has a nearby bright quasar (1310+323, 2.8 ^ away) suitable for phase calibration and C-PACS atmospheric calibration according to our findings in the first part of this paper. Our new maps of Arp 193 improve on the previously highest resolution millimeter maps by hereafter, DS98 DownesSolomon98 by a factor of 3 in angular resolution in the ^ 12 CO(2-1) line. Arp 193 is nearby z=0.023 Richter+94 , has extended emission, and has been studied extensively at multiple wavelengths.

We demonstrate C-PACS in typical use by applying it to the observations of the nearby very luminous infrared galaxy Arp 193 in ^ 12 CO(2-1) at a linear resolution of 70 pc ( 0.12 0.18 ), 3 times better than previously published molecular maps of this galaxy. We resolve the molecular disk rotation kinematics and the molecular gas distribution and measure the gas surface densities and masses on 90 pc scales. We find that molecular gas constitutes 30 of the dynamical mass in the inner 700 pc of this object with a surface density 10^4 M _ pc ^ 2 ; we compare these properties to those of the starburst region of NGC 253.

sources.summary: The paper presents IC 883 as a nearby LIRG merger with tidal tails, strong star formation, and direct radio evidence for a low-luminosity AGN with a young GPS-like nucleus.

quotes.quote: IC 883 is a luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) classified as a starburst-active galactic nucleus (AGN) composite. In a previous study we detected a low-luminosity AGN (LLAGN) radio candidate. Here we report on our radio follow-up at three frequencies which provides direct and unequivocal evidence of the AGN activity in IC 883. Our analysis of archival X-ray data, together with the detection of a transient radio source with luminosity typical of bright supernovae, give further evidence of the ongoing star formation activity, which dominates the energetics of the system.

IC 883, also known as UGC 8387, I Zw 056 and Arp 193, is one of the closest ( D=100 Mpc) LIRGs, with an IR luminosity of 4.7 10^ 11 sanders03 . The system consists of a 4 kpc rotating ring of molecular gas and dust observed edge-on, and two large tidal tails (one protruding orthogonally from the centre and another extending along the main body towards the South), which indicate the occurrence of previous merger episodes e.g., smith95,downes98,scoville00 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies UGC 08387 as a radio AGN because its 8.44 GHz map shows an FRI-like jet, a bulge-matched radio center, outward steepening, and a NeV AGN detection.

quotes.quote: UGC 08387 The map at 8.44 GHz reveals a jet-like radio structure, FRI-like fr74 . The centre of radio emission matches the HST bulge position. The map shows a distribution of flat values which are steepening outwards, getting higher than 1.1. We classify this as a radio-AGN. Although the 6.2 m PAH EQW suggests this is a SB, we get a [ NeV ] detection telling us this object contains an AGN.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1320 + 3408 as a LIRG major merger and starburst-AGN composite with core-jet radio morphology and a three-component absorption profile.

quotes.quote: J1320 + 3408 This major merger (also known as IC 883, UGC 8387, Arp 193) consists of a LIRG that is classified as a starburst-AGN composite. The radio source has a core-jet morphology at sub-parsec scales romero2017 . Detection of \ 1 \ absorption towards the radio source has been reported previously heckman1983,clemens2004,gereb2015 . Our GMRT absorption spectrum is consistent with the results reported in the literature. We find that the absorption profile is best fitted with three Gaussian components.

sources.summary: UGC 08387 is classified as an AGN from [Ne v], shows a soft X-ray nebula likely from an outflow, and has radio evidence for a low-luminosity AGN and pc-scale jet.

quotes.quote: [ 45 ] UGC 08387 : This sources meets our [ Ne v ] 14.32 m line selection criterion, and is thus classified as an AGN, though there is no hint of its presence in the X-ray Chandra data. As already seen in IwaSan2011 , a soft X-ray nebulae extends perpendicular to the plane of the galaxy; most likely associated with a galactic-scale outflow. Previous evidence of the AGN presence has come from detection of compact radio sources at mili-arcsec resolution e.g. LonSmi1993,ParCon2010 , which RomPer2012 attributed to the presence of various supernovae in coexistence with a low-luminosity AGN. Using VLBI data, RomAlb2017 provide evidence for the presence of a pc-scale radio jet.

sources.summary: UGC 08387, or IC 883, is identified as an AGN host with a parsec-scale radio jet; incomplete OSIRIS mosaicking still shows possible excited gas on the molecular disk outskirts.

quotes.quote: UGC 08387 UGC 08387, or IC 883, has been identified as an AGN host by the detection of the line from IRS spectra . A parsec-scale radio jet detected using VLBI further suggests that the nucleus plays host to AGN activity . Though the mosaicking of the nucleus of this galaxy is unfortunately incomplete as mentioned in Paper I due to observing conditions, we included the data analysis here for completeness. Even though the central AGN is not pictured within the frame, we see a hint of what may be low-velocity, excited on the outskirts of the molecular gas disk. Due possibly to heating by AGN photoionization, the / map may indicate a layer of gas heated by the Seyfert nucleus worthy of future follow-up observations perpendicular to the gas disk.

sources.summary: The paper notes IC 883 has a LINER optical spectrum, but finds starburst models can fit its line ratios and MIR dust emission in a compact galaxy.

quotes.quote: IC 883 The optical spectrum of IC 883 is classified as that of a LINER by Veilleux et al. (1995). However, we could fit the few line ratios presented in Table 8 with an averaged sum of starburst models, as for the other galaxies of the sample. Nevertheless, checking the results of composite models presented by Contini Viegas (2001b) which refers to AGN, we found that the observed line ratios could be also explained by a model with \ = 100 , 0 \ = 100 3, and \ = 10 ^9 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 eV ^ 1 at 1 Ryd. However, such a model cannot explain the emission by dust in the MIR, because \ is too low.
As the galaxy is more compact than the former ones it was reasonable to consider that the clouds with different \ correspond to the same starburst age.

sources.summary: The survey describes Arp 193 as a LINER LIRG with extended radio, near-IR, and mid-IR emission and no compact radio source.

quotes.quote: Arp 193 (IC 883, VV 821, UGC 8387, I Zw 56) v95 classify the optical spectrum as LINER. 8.4 GHz images from the VLA show disk-like extended emission with no sign of a compact source c91 . rush96 give an upper limit on the soft X-ray luminosity. Near and mid-infrared images also show disk-like extended emission sco00,s01 .

sources.summary: The atlas gives IC 883's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: IC 883 is an infrared-luminous galaxy with a peculiar morphology at a redshift of z= 0.0233 ( D 101 Mpc), which has an active nucleus optically classified either as LINER kim_optical_1995,veilleux_optical_1995 or as AGN/starburst composite yuan_role_2010 . We treat this object as AGN/starburst composite, and because there is no strong evidence for an AGN from other wavelengths, we treat it as an uncertain AGN (but see below). IC 883 was already studied at high angular resolution with Keck/LWS soifer_high-resolution_2001 in four N band filters.

gmw_00000106.name: IC 1459
gmw_00000106.overview: IC 1459 is a giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0508263, arXiv:1310.2770] featuring a rapidly counter-rotating stellar core [arXiv:2104.09600] and a LINER active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1611.00857, arXiv:1310.2770]. It exhibits a disturbed morphology with stellar shells [arXiv:1611.00857, arXiv:1912.07452], tidal tails [arXiv:1912.07452], and a surrounding 250-kiloparsec neutral hydrogen cloud complex [arXiv:2002.07312].

sources.summary: The paper maps IC1459 as a dusty massive elliptical with disturbed morphology, possible merger accretion, LINER/GPS radio activity, dust lanes, spiral-arm-like features, and radial metallicity structure.

quotes.quote: IC 1459 IC1459 is a massive elliptical galaxy E3 (M _V = -21.19, D = 16.5 h ^ 1 Mpc) that belongs to a group of mostly spiral galaxies group number 15 of huchra82 . It has a counter-rotating stellar core franx88 with a maximum rotation of about 170 km s ^ 1 at 2'' (0.15 kpc). The stellar mass of the counter-rotating core is about 10 ^ 10 M _ franx88 and the outer region velocity goes up to 45 km/s but in opposite direction. Stellar shells at tens of kpc from the center and other peculiar isophotes were observed by and quantified by . This disturbed morphology has led to the hypothesis that IC1459 accreted some external material in the past, probably from a merger cappellari02 .

IC1459 hosts an active galactic nucleus characterized by two strong symmetric radio jets about 1 Jy at 1.4 GHz; slee94,ekers89,tingay15 , whose activity may have been triggered by the same event that gave the galaxy its peculiar morphology and kinematics. IC1459 has a strong LINER optical spectrum phillips86 . However Chandra observations of the supermassive black hole (SMBH) of IC 1459 show a weak X-ray source L _X = 8 10 ^ 40 ergs.s ^ 1 , 0.3-8 keV, fabbiano03 with a flux much lower than expected from a 2 10 ^9 M _ black hole 10x lower than normal radio loud galaxy, cappellari02 .

sources.summary: The paper studies IC 1459 as a massive early-type central group galaxy with a counter-rotating core; it finds non-disk ionized gas, shock/AGN contributions, and late-stage gas accretion.

quotes.quote: IC 1459 is an early-type galaxy (ETG) with a rapidly counter-rotating stellar core, and is the central galaxy in a gas-rich group of spirals. In this work, we investigate the abundant ionized gas in IC 1459 and present new stellar orbital models to connect its complex array of observed properties and build a more complete picture of its evolution. Using the Multi-Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE), the optical integral field unit (IFU) on the Very Large Telescope (VLT), we examine the gas and stellar properties of IC 1459 to decipher the origin and powering mechanism of the galaxy's ionized gas. We detect ionized gas in a non-disk-like structure rotating in the opposite sense to the central stars. Using emission-line flux ratios and velocity dispersion from full-spectral fitting, we find two kinematically distinct regions of shocked emission-line gas in IC 1459, which we distinguished using narrow ( 155 km s ^ 1 ) and broad ( > 155 km s ^ 1 ) profiles.

Our results imply that the emission-line gas in IC 1459 has a different origin than that of its counter-rotating stellar component. We propose that the ionized gas is from late-stage accretion of gas from the group environment, which occurred long after the formation of the central stellar component. We find that shock heating and AGN activity are both ionizing mechanisms in IC 1459 but that the dominant excitation mechanism is by post-asymptotic giant branch stars from its old stellar population.

sources.summary: The paper reports broad nuclear lines in IC 1459, fits multiple oxygen-doublet components, and links its broad components with evidence for outflowing gas.

quotes.quote: In NGC 1052, NGC 2217 and IC 1459, a broad is also seen in their spectra. In these cases, we fitted the \ line with the same set of Gaussian functions used to describe the line profile, i.e. we assumed that all Gaussian components of the line have the same kinematics as the Gaussian components found for the line. The fitted profiles for the \ lines of NGC 1052, NGC 2217 and IC 1459 are shown in Fig. B2 of Appendix B, together with the fitted profile of NGC 4958 taken from 2019MNRAS.486.1138R .

In addition, we fitted the profiles of the 6300, 6363 lines of NGC 2217, NGC 5044 and IC 1459, since these galaxies also present visible broad components in this doublet. For NGC 2217 and NGC 5044, we fitted a sum of two Gaussian functions per line, while for IC 1459 a sum of three Gaussian functions per line was necessary for the profile of the doublet.

For both NGC 2217 and IC 1459, the broad components are narrower and have lower radial velocity values than the broad features detected in . One possible interpretation for these components in the 6300, 6363 doublet is related to nuclear outflows, since shocks may enhance the emission 2006agna.book.....O,2008ApJS..178...20A . Indeed, all three objects present pieces of evidence of outflowing gas, as stated by IFS data 2015MNRAS.451.3728R, 2017MNRAS.470.1703D, 2017ApJS..232...11T .

sources.summary: The paper models IC 1459 as a giant elliptical with a counter-rotating stellar core, radial outer anisotropies, typical mass-to-light ratio, and no dynamically important dark halo inside 3 effective radii.

quotes.quote: IC 1459 is a giant E3 elliptical galaxy. Its absolute blue magnitude is -20.52, heliocentric radial velocity 1691 \ (taken from NED database and in agreement with our observations). \ It covers 5.2 3.8 arcmin on the sky (RC3).

(i) IC 1459 has a counter--rotating stellar core and its h_4 has in the outer parts values that are significantly different from zero implying the existence of radial anisotropies. We could not obtain the simultaneous fit to the velocity and velocity dispersion (for the major axis) which means that we are faced with a situation where the 2I approach is not completely satisfactory and that the motion of stars in this galaxy requires three integrals. However, study of the 2I modelling of the velocity dispersion permits us to draw conclusions about the constant mass--to--light ratio in this galaxy: we infer that in the B -band it is 5 < M/L_B < 10 (in solar units, for the Hubble constant of 70 km \ s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 ). Because of the fact that the velocity dispersion shows a decreasing trend compatible with a given constant mass--to--light ratio we conclude that up to 3 R_e ( 100 arcsec 11.7 kpc) there is no need for the dark matter halo, or at least, the contribution of the dark matter within this radius is dynamically unimportant.

sources.summary: The paper describes the central early-type IC 1459 galaxy as morphologically disturbed, associated with nearby low-column-density H I, and hosting an AGN with symmetric radio jets.

quotes.quote: IC 1459 is a loose galaxy group hosting ten bright galaxies of mostly late morphological type brough2006 . It is named after its central, luminous early-type member, which has a distance of 29 Mpc blakeslee2001,tonry2001 . The group is part of the larger-scale Grus cluster -- a loose over-density hosting mostly spirals aaronson1981 .

Interestingly, data taken with both the Very Large Array walsh1990 and the ATCA oosterloo1999 reveal the presence of low-column-density ( <10^ 20 cm ^ 2 ), intra-group \ near the central galaxy IC 1459. This gas may have been lost by one of the spirals as it travelled across the group and could be related to the disturbed appearance of the early-type galaxy itself -- it exhibits twisted isophotes williams1979 , a counter-rotating stellar core franx1989 and low-surface-brightness spiral-like features in deep optical imaging see http://ftp.aao.gov.au/images/deep_html/i1459_gr_d.html malin1985 . This galaxy also hosts an active galactic nucleus charactertised by two symmetric radio jets ( 1 Jy at 1.4 GHz; tingay2015 ), whose activity may have been triggered by the same events that gave the galaxy its peculiar morphology and kinematics.

sources.summary: The paper presents IC 1459 as the group BGG, mapping its extended stellar envelope, shells, tidal tails, color structure, and evidence for ongoing accretion.

quotes.quote: IC 1459 is the biggest and the reddest galaxy of the group, with R_ e ,g 23.9 kpc and g- = 0.84 mag (Table tab:mtotre ). By fitting the light distribution we found that the outer stellar envelope starts to dominates the light for R 5 arcmin (see Sec. subsec:1459ld and Tab. tab:fit1d1459 ). The enlarged region of the VST mosaic around IC 1459 in the g-band surface brightness levels (see Fig. fig:1459SB ) highlights the structure of the IC 1459 outer envelope (i.e. at R 5 arcmin). There are shells in the North-East and South-West regions and prominent sharp edges on the west side. We detected an elongated tidal tail on the south, and thick arc-like tail protruding on the West side of the small group member IC 5264.
In conclusion, the faint features detected in the IC 1459's envelope provide evidence of the on-going accretion process on the BGG.

sources.summary: The paper presents VLBI imaging of PKS 2254 367 in IC 1459, identifying it as a nearby GPS source with symmetric, low-luminosity parsec-scale jets.

quotes.quote: We present the first multi-frequency VLBI images of PKS 2254 367, a Giga-hertz-Peaked Spectrum (GPS) radio source hosted by the nearby galaxy IC 1459 ( D =20.5 Mpc). PKS 2254 367 and the radio source in NGC 1052 (PKS 0238 084; D =17.2 Mpc) are the two closest GPS radio sources to us, far closer than the next closest example, PKS 1718 649 ( D=59 Mpc).

With a spacing between observing epochs of only 12 days, obviously no motion has been detected in PKS 2254 367 but the opposing jets appear highly symmetric with regard to their surface brightnesses, indicating that they are in the plane of the sky and/or not significantly relativistic.

In PKS 2254 367, the jets have a projected extent of approximately 8 pc. The integrated monochromatic luminosity at 5 GHz of PKS 2254 367 is 7 10^ 22 W Hz ^ 1 , and the compact radio source in NGC 1052 has a very similar luminosity, 5 10^ 22 W Hz ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper records IC 1459's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: IC 1459: We correct the M/L for G.E., scaled it from R to V- band using the (V-R) color from Hyperleda and adjust it to the distance of KormendyHo2013 . The galaxy is a core elliptical Rusli2013b , despite the classification of KormendyHo2013 . We use the profile of Rusli2013b and derive R_e=18.6'' , while their n=7.6 Core-Sersic fit profile has R_e=45.4'' (see Fig. fig_extrap ). The difference is driven by the extrapolation. The same applies to the result of Sani2011 who measure R_e=53.3'' , and Laesker2014 , who quote R_e=38.4'' for the 'classical' fit and R_e=51.9'' for the best fit.

sources.summary: The paper says IC 1459 has asymmetric LOSVD profiles from a cold disk-like component and that GMOS radial-velocity, dispersion, and h3 profiles are consistent with earlier long-slit data.

quotes.quote: These asymmetric profiles have already been detected for IC 1459 by 1988ApJ...327L..55F . Indeed, these authors interpreted this result as a kinematically cold disclike component that is embedded in a kinematically hot structure, although they described the LOSVD profile as a sum of two Gaussian functions. In NGC 1404, the anti-correlation between h_3 and the radial velocity is probably a consequence of the KDC also being embedded in the spheroidal structure of this object. Comparison of the GMOS-IFU results with other data sets Comparison with long-slit data In order to check the consistency of our results, we compared the stellar kinematics extracted from the GMOS data cubes with other data sets presented in the literature. The kinematics of the galaxy IC 1459 were previously analysed by 2002ApJ...578..787C using long-slit data from the HST and from ground-based spectroscopy. In Fig. perfil_comparacao_ic1459 , we show 1D profiles of the radial velocity, velocity dispersion and h_3 moment (only ground-based results) along the major-axis of IC 1459 extracted from the GMOS data cube and from both long-slit spectrographs. One may see that the results from GMOS seem to be consistent with the data presented by 2002ApJ...578..787C . The differences are probably caused by the fact that these measurements have distinct spatial resolutions.
