sources.summary: The paper presents IC 1459 as a giant elliptical with a radio core, counter-rotating stellar core, dust-lane gas disk, and gas-dynamical black-hole mass below stellar-dynamical expectations.

quotes.quote: An example of this approach is provided by the E3 galaxy IC 1459. This giant elliptical hosts a compact nuclear radio source, has a counter-rotating stellar core ( 10'' ), a shallow cusp in the luminosity profile, and a blue central point source . FOS kinematics of the emission-line gas associated with the nuclear dust-lane reveals a disk in rapid rotation (Figure fig:i1459 ), with significant velocity dispersion. Detailed models for the gas motions which include the effects of turbulence show that M_ BH 2.5 10^8 M_ , which is a factor of ten smaller than the mass suggested by axisymmetric f(E, L_z) models of the ground-based stellar kinematics along the major axis. A general stellar dynamical model that incorporates major-axis STIS spectroscopy and ground-based spectroscopy along four position angles is under construction.

sources.summary: The paper excludes IC 1459 from its black-hole relation sample because its peculiar disturbed elliptical morphology, tidal features, shells, counter-rotating core, and uncertain SMBH mass make its structure unreliable.

quotes.quote: Despite our attempt to include all of the lowest and highest mass SMBHs, as these data points can have substantial weight on any fitted relation, we have not included the peculiar elliptical galaxy IC 1459 due to uncertainty on both its SMBH mass and its S ersic index n . This galaxy displays clear signs of past interaction, evidenced by stellar tidal tails (Malin 1985) and stellar shells and ripples at large radii (Forbes Reitzel 1995). Due to its unrelaxed, disturbed morphology, its light-profile is not well fitted with an R^ 1/n model. It also possesses a fast counter-rotating stellar core. While the stellar dynamics of the core suggest a SMBH mass of 2.6 10^9 M_ , the gas dynamics reveal the mass could be as low as 3.5 10^8 M_ (Cappellari et al. \ 2002).

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 1459 as an elliptical galaxy with a hard X-ray AGN component whose central point source complicates spectral analysis and temperature-structure interpretation.

quotes.quote: IC 1459, z=0.0057 IC 1459 is a well studied elliptical galaxy with a known hard X-ray component suggestive of an AGN (Matsumoto et al. 1997). Recent Chandra observations of this system reveal that the nuclear X-ray source has a luminosity L _x = 8 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 in the 0.3--8 keV band and a slope =1.88 0.09 (Fabbiano et al. 2003). In carrying out the spectral analysis we have accounted for the presence of the AGN in IC 1459 by introducing a variable slope power law component with a variable absorption in our spectral fits for the central region. The presence of the bright point-source at the center of the group complicates the spectral analysis. We find a significant temperature variation 3^ distance from the center, rising from 0.3 0.1 to 1.0 0.2 keV, with the hotter temperature observed to the north-west and south-east of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper models a small central light excess in IC 1459, uses single-component dynamical mass-to-light values, and finds its mass deficit just below the black hole mass.

quotes.quote: Inside 0.7 , the surface-brightness profile of IC 1459 seems to show a small excess of light. We model this nuclear excess as an extra Gaussian component in the center on top of the \ function, which after PSF convolution provides a good fit to the data.

For the galaxies IC 1459, NGC 3379, NGC 4261, and NGC 4374 and the three objects with dubious black hole masses (see below) the \ values come from single-component dynamical modeling (i.e., without dark matter; see text).
In three galaxies (IC 1459, NGC 3842 and NGC 5813), the mass deficit is just below the black hole mass.

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for IC1459, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: IC1459 (Fig. fig:IC1459 ) does not show any clear sign of having a complex morphology, but the residual image from a single-component model reveals significant deviations from a 2D- \ profile. Apart from a clear core, which we accordingly mask for improved modeling, IC1459 exhibits a relatively strong isophotal twist ( 15 ), which might indicate that the galaxy is triaxial. Despite the central light deficit, the central surface brightness is notably higher than what we observe in other cored ellipcticals, and the residuals barely benefit from core-masking.

sources.summary: The review uses IC 1459 as a cautionary example where ionized-gas kinematics suggested a dark central mass, but detailed STIS data showed irregular motion unsuitable for disk modeling.

quotes.quote: The case of IC 1459 serves as a cautionary tale. FOS observations at 6 positions within 0 3 of the nucleus revealed a steep central velocity gradient in the ionized gas, and disk model fits implied the presence of a central dark mass of (1-4) 10^8 \ (Verdoes Kleijn \ 2000). More recently, STIS observations have mapped out the circumnuclear kinematics in much greater detail and found an irregular and asymmetric velocity field that cannot be interpreted in terms of flat disk models; a stellar-dynamical analysis finds = (2.6 1.1) 10^9 \ (Cappellari \ 2002). Thus, the ionized gas fails to be a useful probe of the gravitational potential in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The atlas gives IC 1459's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: IC 1459 is a giant elliptical galaxy at a distance of D= 30.3 Mpc ( z=0.006 ; blakeslee_synthesis_2001 ) with a moderately radio-loud LINER nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . The detection of a compact flat-spectrum radio core verifies the AGN nature of IC 1459 slee_parsecscale_1994 . Its stellar core is counter-rotating, which indicates a past merger franx_counterrotating_1988 , and hosts one of the most massive black holes of the nearby galaxy population ( M_ BH /M_ = 9 0.4 ; cappellari_counterrotating_2002 ).

sources.summary: The review describes IC 1459 as an elliptical galaxy with a large surrounding H I cloud complex and tidal debris extending at least 250 kpc through its group.

quotes.quote: IC 1459 . --- Saponara et al. (2018), using the Australia Telescope Compact Array (ATCA), detect a large H i cloud complex near the elliptical galaxy IC 1459 (HIPASS J2257--36) as well as further H i debris in the wider surroundings. The total extent of these tidal debris, extending from the north-east of the spiral galaxy NGC 7418A (HIPASS J2256--36a) to the north-east of IC 1459 is at least 250 kpc (M _ HI 10^9 M _ ). The IC 1459 galaxy group was our first WALLABY science target for H i observations with an early array of only six PAF-equipped ASKAP antennas.

sources.summary: The paper studies IC 1459 as a peculiar elliptical with shells, counter-rotating core, strong radio/blue nucleus, LINER-like nuclear region, and a black hole inferred from HST gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: = The peculiar elliptical galaxy IC 1459 ( M_V = -21.19 , D = 16.5 h^ 1 ) has a fast counterrotating stellar core, stellar shells and ripples, a blue nuclear point source and strong radio core emission.
The nuclear region of IC 1459 has line ratios typical of the LINER class (see e.g., Heckman 1980, Osterbrock 1989 for the definition of LINERS).
The truth is likely to be found somewhere between these extremes, and we therefore conclude that IC 1459 has a BH with mass in the range = 1 -- 4 10^8 .

gmw_00000107.name: IC 3639
gmw_00000107.overview: IC 3639 is a face-on, barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770, arXiv:astro-ph/0012035] that is interacting within a triple system [arXiv:astro-ph/0411405]. It hosts a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770] with a resolved nuclear starburst [arXiv:astro-ph/0001104] and heavy Compton-thick obscuration [arXiv:0902.2777].

sources.summary: The paper presents Tol 1238-36.4 as a face-on spiral in AM 1238-362 whose HII-region luminosity function is shifted toward high luminosities, likely because of interaction-triggered star formation.

quotes.quote: AM 1238-362 is a fairly tight triple system composed by two nearly face-on (ESO 381-G009 and Tol 1238-36.4) and an edge-on (ESO 381-G006) spiral galaxies (Fig. AM_a ). A detailed study of the morphological and spectrophotometric properties of the two face-on galaxies has been presented in Temporin et al. (2003a), where the interaction signatures and star formation properties have been discussed in the framework of the evolutionary history of the system. The two galaxies exhibit enhanced star formation activity, and the radial and azimuthal distribution of their H emission show a tendency for both disks to concentrate the star formation activity on the side facing the companion (see Temporin et al. 2003a, Fig. 20).

AM 1238-362. The distribution of H ii regions across the disks of the two face-on members seems related to the interaction. Their H ii region LFs, presented here for the first time, considerably differ from one another, although the size distributions do not. The shift of the LF of Tol 1238-36.4 towards high luminosities is possibly a consequence of the interaction process.

sources.summary: The paper includes IC 3639 in the subset of Seyfert 2 nuclei whose UV morphology indicates a spatially resolved nuclear starburst and obscured Seyfert 1 nucleus.

quotes.quote: In four of the targets (Mrk 477, NGC 7130, NGC 5135 and IC 3639) the morphology suggests that they harbor a nuclear starburst (Figure 1). In these cases, the UV continuum is spatially resolved, showing sub-arcsec structure. The effective radius (50-200 pc) of the UV emission is similar to the size of the NLR. At the position where HST+WFPC2 (F606W) suggests that the nucleus is located, the UV images show very little emission (less than 15 of the total). This result suggests that even at UV wavelengths the S1 nucleus is still obscured.

sources.summary: IC 3639 is described as heavily Compton-thick, with depleted high-energy flux and extended soft X-ray contamination; optical-line proxies set its intrinsic power.

quotes.quote: IC 3639 The intrinsic high-energy flux from this source is likely to be completely depleted due to heavy Compton-thick obscuration with N_ H >10^ 25 cm ^ 2 e.g., risaliti99 . Analysis is complicated by the fact that most of the <10 keV X-ray flux observed is likely related to extended emission ghosh07, guainazzi05 . Using instead the 5007 emission line ( F_ OIII =3.4 10^ 12 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 from lumsden01 , corrected for extinction) as a proxy for the intrinsic power (in conjunction with the :X-ray relation from panessa06 ) gives L_ 2-10 4 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 . This also matches older \ measurements quoted by guainazzi05 , if we correct for extinction using the prescription of bassani99 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes X-ray spectra of TOLOLO 1238-364, describing its best-fit components, Fe K emission or flux behavior, and comparison with previous modeling.

quotes.quote: TOLOLO 1238-364 The Chandra spectrum was best fit by an absorbed power law with a thermal component and a Gaussian to accommodate the Fe K emission. The data were binned by a minimum of 2 counts and we therefore employed the C-statistic. tololo1238 fit this spectrum with an absorbed power law plus thermal brehmsstrahlung model after binning by a minimum of 20 counts which washes out the Fe K feature.

sources.summary: The atlas gives IC 3639's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: IC 3639 is a face-on spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0109 ( D 49.5 Mpc) with an AGN and a recent nuclear starburst gonzalez_delgado_ultraviolet-optical_1998,gonzalez_delgado_nuclear_2001 . The AGN is optically classified as a Sy 2 kewley_optical_2001 with polarized broad lines being detected heisler_visibility_1997 . According to , the AGN is dominating the emission, and we do not treat IC 3639 as AGN/starburst composite.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 3639 as a barred galaxy paired with ESO 381-G009, with resolved soft X-rays and a relatively weak scattered contribution in its complex model.

quotes.quote: The soft X-rays are resolved in Mrk 1073 and IC 3639, so the most likely spectral model for both is thermal emission.

In the case of IC 3639, although statistical improvement of the complex model (Model 4) over the preferred Model 3 is not significant, we learn from it that the scattered contribution in this galaxy is relatively weak.
IC 3639 IC 3639 has a bar Mul97 and is paired with ESO 381-G009.

gmw_00000108.name: IC 4329A
gmw_00000108.overview: IC 4329A is an edge-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:1810.10732] that hosts a bright, highly variable Seyfert 1.2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1604.03260, arXiv:2305.13028]. The galaxy forms an interacting pair with IC 4329 [arXiv:1810.10732] and exhibits nuclear millimeter emission likely originating from its corona [arXiv:2309.02776, arXiv:2403.19524].

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes IC 4329A XMM-Newton data, finding the previously reported Fe K absorption feature after applying the Laha et al. model.

quotes.quote: IC 4329A Laha et al. (2014) report the analysis of the XMM-Newton observation of IC 4329A (OBSID 0147440101) performed in 2003. They claim the non-detection of an absorption line at the observed energy of E = 7.57 0.03  keV previously reported by Tombesi et al. (2010a). We note that this absorption line was initially reported by Markowitz, Reeves Braito (2006), who performed a detailed broad-band analysis of the same XMM-Newton dataset.

We re-analyze the data applying Laha et al. (2014) model. We consider a neutral absorbed ( N_H = 3.8 10^ 21  cm ^ 2 ) power-law continuum with 1.8 and two black-body components with temperatures kT = 46 eV and kT = 286 eV to model the soft excess (see their Table 5). We consider also a pexmon component with R = 1.67 and an emission line at E = 6.87 keV.

From the ratios and the contour plots in Fig. 1 we note the presence of an absorption feature at the observed energy of E 7.57 keV. Indeed, this absorption line with rest-frame energy E = 7.70^ +0.02 _ 0.03  keV, intensity I = (-11.8 2.5) 10^ 6  ph s ^ 1  cm ^ 2 and EW = 15^ +4 _ 3  eV is required at 99.99 ( ^2/ = 20.4/2 ).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Suzaku observations of IC4329A, finding variable soft power-law emission, reflection, and a harder less-variable primary component previously unrecognized in this Seyfert I galaxy.

quotes.quote: The bright and highly-variable Seyfert I Active Galactic Nucleus, IC4329A, was observed with Suzaku 5 times in 2007 August with intervals of 5 days, and a net exposure of 24--31 ks each. Another longer observation was carried out in 2012 August with a net exposure of 118 ks. In the 6 observations, the source was detected in 2--45 keV with average 2--10 keV fluxes of ( 0.67--1.2 ) 10^ 10 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . The intensity changed by a factor of 2 among the 5 observations in 2007, and 1.5 within the 2012 observation. Difference spectra among these observations revealed that the variability of IC4329A was carried mainly by a power-law component with a photon index 2.0 .

However, in addition to this primary component and the associated reflection, the broad-band Suzaku data required another, harder, and less variable component with 1.4 . The presence of this new continuum was also confirmed by analyzing the same 6 data sets through the spectral decomposition technique developed by Noda2013a . This 1.4 continuum is considered to be a new primary component that has not been recognized in the spectra of IC4329A so far, although it was recently identified in those of several other Seyfert I galaxies (Noda et al. Noda2013a , Noda2014 ).

sources.summary: The paper observes IC 4329A with ALMA/VLA/ATCA and models a compact mm excess as coronal synchrotron emission near its Seyfert nucleus.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The emission center of the IC 4329A nucleus is located at = 13h49m19.26170s 0.00262^ , = -30^ 18 ^ 34.22145 ^ 0.00264^ (J2000) at 150 GHz.

quotes.quote: IC 4329A is a southern Seyfert 1.2 galaxy at z=0.0161 wil91 whose central black hole mass is 1.2 10^8 M_ nik04,mar09 . The host galaxy is an edge-on spiral galaxy in a pair with IC 4329 separated by 3 arcmin. IC 4329A is one of the brightest X-ray Seyferts and shows a typical spectrum of radio-quiet Seyferts in the hard X-ray band zdz94,mad95,zdz96,brenneman2014a .

The mm excess is clearly visible in the cm--mm spectrum, which determines the properties of the coronae. The measured ALMA spectrum of IC 4329A exhibits flattening below 100 GHz, while calibrators' spectra exhibit simple power-law shape (Figure. fig:calibrator ). This indicates SSA in a very compact region in the order of several tens of the Schwarzschild radius. The measured spectra are well reproduced by the assumed models without time variability. The coronal properties of IC 4329A are estimated as R=39 10 r_s with B=9 6 G and p=2.9 0.9 based on our model fitting.

sources.summary: IC 4329A is described as an edge-on dusty AGN whose broad-line spectrum and red H-beta shoulder have remained broadly stable across nearly 50 years.

quotes.quote: The AGN nature of IC 4329A, an edge-on galaxy with a striking dust lane (see, e.g. ), was established in 1972 ( ). The discovery paper highlights the strong broad-line spectrum, with Balmer line zero intensity widths of up to 13000 km.s ^ 1 . It furthermore records an H to-H ratio of over 8, a clear sign of significant nuclear reddening and extinction. The high Balmer-line flux ratios were confirmed in many later studies (e.g., ), although it is now also clear that the intrinsic ratio may be somewhat greater than was initially assumed, meaning that the intrinsic nuclear luminosity is not high enough to warrant classification as a quasar. The discovery spectrum also revealed a secondary peak (red shoulder) of the broad H , corresponding to a rest wavelength of 4900 , and a recession velocity of roughly 2300 km.s ^ 1 relative to the primary H peak.

Figure fig2 displays the spectrum most recently obtained for IC 4329A. It does not differ markedly from the other spectra published over the past almost 50 years. Notably, the redshifted second peak of the H emission line is still clearly distinguishable, implying that its presence is associated with a stable nuclear configuration that the AGN returns to frequently. It is pointed out that there are spectra where the red peak appears absent (e.g. ).

sources.summary: The paper studies IC 4329A as a nearby Seyfert 1.2 AGN, separates UV host and nuclear emission, and finds its intrinsic UV continuum fits disk models only beyond 80-150 gravitational radii.

quotes.quote: We present a study of far and near ultraviolet emission from the accretion disk in a powerful Seyfert 1 galaxy IC 4329A using observations performed with the Ultraviolet Imaging Telescope (UVIT) onboard . These data provide the highest spatial resolution and deepest images of IC 4329A in the far and near UV bands acquired to date. The excellent spatial resolution of the UVIT data has allowed us to accurately separate the extended emission from the host galaxy and the AGN emission in the far and near UV bands.

IC 4329A is a nearby Seyfert 1.2 AGN 2006A&A...455..773V at a redshift of z = 0.016054 1991AJ....101...57W . It is the 2nd brightest type 1 AGN after NGC 4151 in the Swift/BAT catalog 2013ApJS..207...19B . The AGN resides in an edge-on galaxy with a dust lane passing through the nucleus. Based on a detailed multi-wavelength spectral study 2018A&A...619A..20M estimated an accretion rate of 10-20 of the Eddington rate given its black hole mass in the range of 1-2 10^8 .
We find that the intrinsic UV continuum is fully consistent with the standard disk models, but only if the disk emits from distances larger than 80-150 gravitational radii.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes IC 4329A with IXPE, XMM-Newton, and NuSTAR, finding tentative X-ray polarization aligned with the radio jet and constraints on coronal geometry.

quotes.quote: We present an X-ray spectro-polarimetric analysis of the bright Seyfert galaxy IC 4329A. The Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer ( IXPE ) observed the source for 500 ks, supported by XMM-Newton ( 60 ks) and NuSTAR ( 80 ks) exposures. We detect polarisation in the 2--8 keV band with 2.97 confidence. We report a polarisation degree of 3.3 1.1 per cent and a polarisation angle of 78 10 (errors are 1 confidence) .

IC 4329A (redshift z=0.0161 , Willmer1991 ) is a Seyfert Type 1.2 galaxy Veron-Cetty2006 , meaning that its optical spectrum includes both broad and narrow emission lines and its X-ray spectrum is relatively un-obscured . This indicates in the unification scheme Urry1995 that the inclination angle is small enough for our sight line to the supermassive black hole to not be blocked by the dusty torus , such that we see X-rays directly from the accretion flow and broad optical lines from gas rapidly orbiting the black hole (the broad line region) . A recent optical reverberation campaign Bentz2023 further backed up this hypothesis and provided a black hole mass measurement of M 7 10^7 M _ , broadly consistent with earlier estimates e.g. Gonzalez-Martin2012 . An X-ray reverberation feature consistent with this mass has been observed from the source Kara2016 . IC 4329A was selected for an observation primarily because it is one of the brightest AGN in the X-ray sky, with a 2--10 keV flux in the range F_ 2-10 (0.1-1.8) 10^ 10 \ e.g., Beckmann2006 .

sources.summary: The paper reports a coordinated X-ray monitoring campaign of the Seyfert IC 4329A, finding modest intra-observation variability, day-scale softer-when-brighter changes, persistent soft excess, weak reflection, and wind absorption.

quotes.quote: We present the results of a joint and campaign on the active galactic nucleus (AGN) IC 4329A, consisting of 9 20 ks observations, and 5 20 ks observations within nine days, performed in August 2021. Within each observation, the AGN is not very variable, with the fractional variability never exceeding 5 .

In all observations, a soft excess is clearly present. Consistently with previous observations, the X-ray spectra of the source exhibit a cut-off energy between 140 and 250 keV, constant within the error in the different observations. We detected a narrow iron line consistent with being constant during the monitoring, and likely originating in a distant neutral medium.

We do not find evidence of a broad component of the iron line, suggesting that the inner disk does not produce strong reflection. We find that the reflection component is weak ( R_ max =0.009 0.002 ). We also found the presence of a warm absorber component together with an ultra-fast outflow.

sources.summary: IC 4329A is described as a type 1 Seyfert with extensive X-ray and RM study; GRAVITY resolves its thick BLR and finds a misaligned host-galaxy disc.

quotes.quote: IC 4329A is a type 1 Seyfert galaxy that is well-observed in X-ray e.g., Madejski1995, Delvaille1978, Piro1990, Nandra1994 . Several RM campaigns were implemented to measure the BLR size and BH mass of IC 4329A. For instance, Winge1996 presented the first RM data for IC 4329A, which was reanalysed by Peterson2004 .

The inclination angle is i 54 ^ ; similar to Bentz2023 , the high inclination angle of IC 4329A suggests that the AGN system and its host galaxy disc are misaligned significantly. The opening angle is _0 54 ^ , indicating that the BLR is thick. We resolved the BLR of IC 4329A, with a BLR radius of log R_ BLR [ ld ] = 1.13 ^ +0.10 _ 0.23 and a BH mass of log M_ BH [ M_ ] = 7.15 ^ +0.38 _ 0.26 . The inferred BLR inner radius is log R_ BLR, min [ ld ] = 0.64^ +0.27 _ 0.28 . Bentz2023 recently published results of a new RM campaign for IC 4329A which spanned about 5 months during 2022.

sources.summary: IC4329A is the paper’s robust blueshifted-absorption case near 7.5 keV, favoring a high-velocity Fe XXVI interpretation but requiring repeat confirmation.

quotes.quote: Blueshifted absorption features We detect absorption features at around 7.5 keV in two objects, IC4329A and NGC 4151. These features are difficult to interpret, as if there is only a single line the identification will always be ambiguous. Unless substantial velocity shifts are invoked, the most plausible identification with Fe K , but this should always be accompanied by a much stronger K features.

If, on the other hand, we identify this as a K feature the most likely identification is with the H-like ion Fe xxvi , as only a single feature is evident. In this case a very large blushift of order 0.1c must be invoked. Similar features have been reported previously, with the interpretation of a very fast outflow being preferred (Chartas, Brandt Gallagher 2003; Pounds et al. 2003; Reeves, O'Brien Ward 2003; Braito et al. 2007). Taking our two cases, there is some ambiguity about the feature in NGC 4151, which may in reality be an iron K-edge, rather than an absorption line. Thus the only apparently robust case is IC4329A, the feature in which has already been reported by Markowitz et al. (2005).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Suzaku observations of the bright Seyfert 1 galaxy IC 4329A, finding narrow and broad Fe K components and relativistic reflection from the inner accretion disk.

quotes.quote: We present an analysis of five Suzaku observations of the bright Seyfert1 galaxy IC 4329A. The broad energy band and high signal-to-noise ratio of the data give new constraints on the iron K line profile and its relationship with the Compton hump at higher energies. The Fe K bandpass is dominated by a narrow core (EW=57 _ 3 ^ +3 eV) at 6.4 keV consistent with neutral material. Using a physically-motivated model, our analysis also reveals the presence of a broad Iron K line (EW=124 _ 11 ^ +11 eV), most likely produced in the inner part of the accretion disk and blurred by general relativistic effects.

We have analyzed 5 Suzaku observations in order to determine the nature of the Fe K emission and its relation to the Compton Hump in the Seyfert 1 galaxy IC 4329A. The analysis of the iron line shows a core with an energy that is consistent with an origin in neutral reflection form distant material. Once this narrow component of the line is fitted, broad residuals in the Fe K band appear, but these are only clearly evident when all datasets are combined (Fig. 1gaussXIS ): they are not clearly visible in the single data/model ratio of each observation (Fig. power ).

sources.summary: One Suzaku observation of IC 4329A lacks a relativistic line but shows strong high-energy reflection, while other observations show both components, challenging simple reflection interpretations.

quotes.quote: The opposite situation pertains to one observation of IC 4329A (Obs. ID: 702113020). In this spectrum the relativistic line was not detected, with a tight upper limit, while a strong reflection continuum is observed at high energies. This is very puzzling, especially when considered in context with the other observations of IC 4329A with Suzaku . These do show both a relativistic line and a reflection continuum, with strengths that are typically consistent with each other.

However, the other observations of IC 4329A have Fe abundance close to solar. A variation of the Fe abundances is highly unlikely, especially if we consider the short time scale between each observation ( 1 week). These observations of IC 4329A therefore provide a challenge to the prevailing reflection paradigm.

sources.summary: The paper models IC 4329A as a nearby reddened Seyfert/quasar whose edge-on host, dust lane, AGN torus, and wind contain distinct dust components.

quotes.quote: IC 4329A is bright nearby AGN at redshift z = 0.016054 Will91 . It has been described as `an extreme Seyfert galaxy' Disn73 and `the nearest quasar' Wils79 , based on the spectroscopy of its broad optical emission lines. However, it is technically classified as a Seyfert 1.2 by Vero06 . The host galaxy of the AGN is highly inclined (i.e. edge-on), with the observed ratio of minor to major axis b/a = 0.28 deVa91 .

In this paper we have carried out broad-band continuum modelling, together with X-ray and IR spectroscopy, to study dust in . By examining our results from reddening, X-ray absorption and IR emission by dust, and comparing with previous polarisation studies, we can construct a physical picture of dust in . We argue that the derived information from each of the above analyses points to the presence of two distinct components of dust in : one in the ISM of the host galaxy and its associated dust lane, and the other a nuclear component in the AGN torus with likely association to the wind.

sources.summary: The paper uses IC 4329A as an observed Seyfert benchmark: ALMA radio synchrotron constrains its non-thermal coronal electrons, and modeled gamma-ray/neutrino spectra are normalized to its Swift/BAT flux.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig:electron_spectrum shows the steady-state non-thermal electron spectrum obtained for the injection spectral index of p_ inj =2.0 together with the observationally determined electron spectral distribution for IC 4329A Inoue2018 . alma observed non-thermal synchrotron radiation between 90.5 GHz and 231 GHz which corresponds to the electron Lorentz factors between 50 and 80, respectively. The corresponding region is shown as the shaded region in the Fig. fig:electron_spectrum . For the calculation of the steady-state spectrum, we set =10^8M_ , r_c = 40 , B=10 G, kT_e=100 keV, =1.1 , and _g=30 . The synthetic electron distribution obtained for p_ inj =2.0 nicely reproduces the observationally determined electron spectrum in the energy range constrained by the observations.

The former and the latter roughly corresponds to NGC 4151 and IC 4329A, respectively. NGC 4151 is the brightest Seyfert in the X-ray sky Oh2018 . For the comparison, the overall fluxes of both panels are renormalized to match with the Swift /BAT flux of NGC 4151 and IC 4329A, respectively, at 14-195 keV Oh2018 . We note that we do not calculate the detailed X-ray spectra of each objects, which is beyond the scope of this paper.

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 4329A as an individually modeled Seyfert source, noting Fe-line residuals, broad and narrow Fe detections, absorption/outflow evidence, and reflection fits with lamp-post geometry.

quotes.quote: Fig_residual , we show the residuals of IC 4329A before and after including the redshifted Gaussian component. The broad Fe lines were detected in the sources IC 4329A 2007MNRAS.382..194N,1997ApJ...477..602N , Mrk 766 2007MNRAS.382..194N,1997ApJ...477..602N , MCG-6-30-15 1995Natur.375..659T,2002MNRAS.335L...1F,2001MNRAS.328L..27W,2007PASJ...59S.315M,2007MNRAS.382..194N , Mrk 1040 1995MNRAS.276.1311R , Mrk 335 2011MNRAS.411.2353P , Ark 120 2007MNRAS.382..194N,2011MNRAS.410.1251N and Fairall 9 1997ApJ...477..602N based on the data from previous observatories. Further, an additional zguass was included to check the presence of the narrow ( _ nrw =0.001 keV) Fe K _ emission line ( E_ nrw =6.4 keV) due to neutral reflection. The detection of narrow Fe emission lines by Chandra HETG observations was already reported for the sources Fairall 9, MCG-06-30-15 ( 2004ApJ...604...63Y,2002ApJ...570L..47L ), IC 4329A 2004ApJ...608..157M , Ark 120 2016ApJ...832...45N , Mrk 590 2007A&A...470...73L and 3C 382 2007ApJ...664...88G .

They observed single or multiple Fe K-shell absorption lines in IC 4329A ( E_ rest 7.69 keV, width =100 eV), Ark 120 ( E_ rest 9.18 keV, width =10 eV), Mrk 766 ( E_ rest 7.28 keV, width =100 eV; E_ rest 7.63 keV, width =10 eV) and no significant absorption was detected in MCG-6-30-15, Mrk 110, Fairall 9, Mkn 335, Mrk 590 and Ark 564. In a further study, presented detailed modelling of these highly ionised absorbers using xstar and obtained outflow velocities of 0.097 for IC 4329A, 0.306 for Ark 120 and 0.082 (0.088c) for Mrk 766. also found a narrow absorption feature in the XMM-Newton spectrum of IC 4329A, around 7.7 keV, which is an evidence for a high velocity ( 0.1 ) outflow in the source. performed a search for the Fe K absorption lines in the Suzaku spectra of AGN.

We fitted the spectra of the sources IC 4329A and Swift J2127.4+5654 with relxilllp and obtained the source height in units of gravitational radius R_ g . The height of the corona in Swift J2127.4+5654 is found to be 39(50) R_ g for a=0 (0.998) and in the case of IC 4329A the parameter is poorly constrained and has an upper limit of 23(22) R_ g .

sources.summary: The paper presents joint ALMA and X-ray monitoring of IC 4329A, finding compact unresolved millimeter emission, strong daily variability, and evidence favoring synchrotron emission from the X-ray corona.

quotes.quote: We present the first joint ALMA ( 100 GHz) and X-ray ( / / ; 2--10 keV) observations of the unobscured RQ AGN, IC 4329A ( z = 0.016 ). The time-averaged mm-to-X-ray flux ratio aligns with recently established trends for larger samples Kawamuro22,ricci23 , but with a tighter scatter ( 0.1 dex) compared to previous studies. However, there is no significant correlation on timescales of less than 20 days. The compact mm emission exhibits a spectral index of 0.23 0.18 , remains unresolved with a 13 pc upper limit, and shows no jet signatures.

Notably, the mm flux density varies significantly (factor of 3) within 4 days, exceeding the contemporaneous X-ray variability (37 vs. 18 ) and showing the largest mm variations ever detected in RQ AGN over daily timescales. The high amplitude variability rules out scenarios of heated dust and thermal free--free emission, pointing toward a synchrotron origin for the mm radiation in a source of 1 light day size. While the exact source is not yet certain, an X-ray corona scenario emerges as the most plausible compared to a scaled-down jet or outflow-driven shocks.

sources.summary: The review uses IC 4329A as the best-determined broad-band Seyfert 1 case, describing ROSAT/CGRO/Ginga modeling, mild absorption, and viable thermal or nonthermal spectral fits.

quotes.quote: The only Seyfert 1 galaxy for which the broad band X- and ray spectrum is well determined is IC 4329A (Madejski \ 1995) being the second brightest Seyfert galaxy in the 2-10 keV range. Simultaneous ROSAT and CGRO OSSE, as well as nonsimultaneous Ginga data are shown in Figure fig1 ( left panel ). The observations were modelled by Madejski \ (1995), Zdziarski \ (1994), and Magdziarz Zdziarski (1995).

A small column depth of neutral material about = 3 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 along the line of sight causes absorption below 2 keV. The average X/ spectrum for four Seyfert 1s using nonsimultaneous Ginga and OSSE data shows similar properties as the spectrum for IC 4329A (Zdziarski \ 1995).

This question might be answered if the spectrum above a few hundred keV was known with certainty, but the best available spectrum of the brightest typical Seyfert 1, IC 4329A, does not have sufficient signal to noise at such energies. The spectrum of IC 4329A can be fit with both thermal and nonthermal models (Zdziarski \ 1994).

sources.summary: The paper uses IC 4329A as a bright Seyfert example, analyzing years of hard X-ray data and finding spectral variability including a changing high-energy cutoff.

quotes.quote: As an example, the spectral variability of the bright Seyfert 1 galaxy IC 4329A has been investigated in the 20--100 keV band.

In the left panel of Figure spe , the uncorrelated variations of the 18--60 and 60--100 keV fluxes suggest a spectral change of the source during the 6 years of INTEGRAL observations. Indeed, a high-energy cut-off at E_ = 39 17 keV , signature of thermal Comptonisation processes, can be found in the August 2003 ISGRI spectrum, whereas no sign of curvature was detectable in July 2003 (right panel of Fig. spe ). During the same period (August 2003) the broad component of the 6.4 keV Fe K line has been found to show a hint of variability on hour time scale . This hard X-ray spectral change could indicate an increase of the electron plasma temperature, moving the cut-off towards higher energies, where it was not detectable by ISGRI in the July 2003 observation.
As an example of spectral variability studies with INTEGRAL , we have analysed IBIS/ISGRI data spanning more than 5 years of the bright Seyfert 1 galaxy IC 4329A.

sources.summary: The paper models IC 4329A as a nearby bright Seyfert example whose AGN corona could produce observable submillimeter synchrotron emission, especially with non-thermal electrons.

quotes.quote: As an example, we consider IC 4329A which is a nearby bright Seyfert 1.2 galaxy in the southern hemisphere. IC 4329A locates at the distance of redshift z=0.0161 wil91 and its central supermassive black hole mass M_ 1.20 10^8M_ del10 . Recent joint NuSTAR / Suzaku X-ray observation determined the properties of the corona as k_ B T_e=50_ 3 ^ +6 keV with _ T =2.34_ 0.11 ^ +0.16 in the case of spherical geometry bre14 . Following section sec:for , we can evaluate the expected synchrotron emission from IC 4329A with these latest observational information.

Figure. fig:all shows the expected radio emission from the corona of IC 4329A for different magnetic field strength ( B=10^2 G and 10^3 G) and for different electron populations (thermal electrons only and thermal + non-thermal electrons). We also show the sensitivity limit of ALMA cycle-2 with an integration of 60 seconds http://alma.mtk.nao.ac.jp/e/index.html for comparison. The electron density in the corona is so high that most of radio emission is absorbed by ambient electrons. However, emission at higher frequency can survive from synchrotron self-absorption. If the corona contains thermal electrons only, the expected flux is far below the ALMA sensitivity limit. If non-thermal electrons exist together with thermal electrons in the corona, the predicted flux is higher than the ALMA sensitivity limit and the jet component. This is because the observable emission are emitted by high energy non-thermal electrons. Therefore, detection of synchrotron emission by ALMA would allow us to investigate not only the properties of magnetic fields in the corona but also its non-thermal content. Importantly, future ALMA observations will be able to see both of the corona and the jet. Hereinafter, we focus on models with thermal + non-thermal electrons. Figure. fig:range shows the detectable parameter ranges of magnetic field B and the fraction of non-thermal component with ALMA. Here we take into account the galactic dust, dust torus, and jet contamination. The triangle point represents the value of =4 with which AGNs can explain the cosmic MeV gamma-ray background radiation and B=10^3 G at which magnetic energy is in energy equipartition with gas energy in the disk. The ALMA sensitivity allows us to investigate down to 0.02 for B=10^3 G which is limited by the contamination of the jet emission. And we can investigate the coronal magnetic field strength down to B=50 G, corresponding to 0.3 of gas energy in the disk, with =4 . Thus, wide range of B and can be tested with ALMA. In other words, we can strongly constrain the coronal properties even if ALMA does not see the coronal synchrotron emission. In this letter, we have investigated the method to probe the magnetic field and non-thermal contents in AGN coronae. Future ALMA sub-mm observations of nearby bright AGNs such as IC 4329A will be able to probe these new physical information of the corona and test the magnetic-reconnection heated corona scenario liu02 , although it is difficult to investigate the case with a pure thermal corona. For the case of IC 4329A, we can investigate wide range of the magnetic field strength and the energy fraction of non-thermal electrons.

sources.summary: The paper applies its hot-accretion-flow model to IC 4329A, treating the source as a Seyfert 1.2 at z=0.0161 with a massive black hole and edge-on spiral host.

quotes.quote: We applied our model to the source IC 4329A, confirming earlier results which showed evidence of nonthermal particles in the corona.

is a bright X-ray Seyfert 1.2 galaxy The class Seyfert 1.2 is used to describe objects with relatively weaker narrow H components, intermediate between Seyfert 1.0 and 1.5 veron2006 . veron2006 at z = 0.0161 ( willmer1991 ; equivalent to a luminosity distance of 69.61 Mpc, assuming the cosmological parameters H_0 = 70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _ = 0.7 , and _ m = 0.3 ). The mass of the central black hole is estimated in 1.2 10^8 M_ markowitz2009,delacalle2010 . The host galaxy of the AGN is an edge-on spiral galaxy; the inclination of the disk of the host galaxy with respect to the axes of the AGN is thought to be the result of the interaction between and the companion galaxy IC 4329 at 3 arcmin of separation Wolstencroft1995 . The hard X-ray spectrum of the AGN is standard from a radio-quiet Seyfert, that is, a power-law resulting from the Compton up-scattering of the optically thick disk photons by the hot plasma of the RIAF or "corona".

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 4329A as an AGN outlier with a small bulge mass, large [OIII]-derived velocity dispersion, extended radio structure, and likely non-virial line broadening.

quotes.quote: The galactic bulge mass of IC 4329A, which clearly deviates from other AGN in the Figure 1 in Nelson (2000), is one of the smallest in the Wandel (1999) sample of about 10^ 10.6 solar mass. However, the bulge velocity dispersion derived from the [ OIII ] width of IC 4329A is one of the largest. The small bulge mass but large bulge velocity in this object compared with others in the Wandel (1999) sample is in contradiction with the Faber-Jackson relation.

The high resolution radio map of IC 4329A consists of a compact core and with extended component to several kpc (Unger et al. 1987). If the extended component is the radio jet, then the large [ OIII ] width can be due to the non-virial component (Nelson Whittle 1996). This object will also be removed from the sample in the following analysis.

sources.summary: The paper fits IC 4329a's hard X-ray Seyfert spectrum with an anisotropic illumination model, deriving a nearly edge-on inclination and noting evidence for partial obscuration and reddening.

quotes.quote: We found values of corresponding to : 81 for IC 4329a , and NGC 4151 , and 67 for MCG 8-11-11 . These large inclination angles reflect the small amount of reflection in these objects. Using Fig.  rdemu , one can check that these angles are in good agreement with the previously derived R parameter values : 1.03 ( =60 ), 1.64 ( =0 ) and 0.43 ( =65 ), respectively for IC 4329a , MCG 8-11-11 and NGC 4151 .

In the case of IC 4329a , a polarimetric study (Wolstencroft et al. ), assuming that the polarization arises from scattering on the inner edge of the optically thick torus, shows that IC 4329a is unlikely to be seen pole-on. Rather, it suggests that our line of sight goes through the edge of the torus so that the central nucleus is partially obscured. This would be consistent with a large inclination if the torus is geometrically thin and, on the other hand, it would also explain the large column density obtained in the best fits --almost 10 times the galactic column density (see also Walter Fink ).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes a deep NuSTAR observation of IC 4329A, measuring its high-energy cutoff and constraining coronal temperature and optical depth with Comptonization models.

quotes.quote: We present an analysis of a 160 NuSTAR observation of the nearby bright Seyfert galaxy IC 4329A. The high-quality broadband spectrum enables us to separate the effects of distant reflection from the direct coronal continuum, and to therefore accurately measure the high-energy cutoff to be E_ cut =178^ +74 _ 40 . The coronal emission arises from accretion disk photons Compton up-scattered by a thermal plasma, with the spectral index and cutoff being due to a combination of the finite plasma temperature and optical depth. Applying standard Comptonization models, we measure both physical properties independently using the best signal-to-noise obtained to date in an AGN over the 3-79 band. We derive kT_ e =37^ +7 _ 6 with =1.25^ +0.20 _ 0.10 assuming a slab geometry for the plasma, and kT_ e =33^ +6 _ 6 with =3.41^ +0.58 _ 0.38 for a spherical geometry, with both having an equivalent goodness-of-fit.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4329A as heavily absorbed, compares four previously reported warm absorber components, and detects three similar components in this analysis.

quotes.quote: IC 4329A - studied the same dataset. The source is heavily absorbed. The slope =1.71 . There are four components of WA, =-1.37 , =0.56 , =1.92 , and =2.70 . We detected three WA components, which are nearly similar to these.

sources.summary: The paper classifies IC 4329A as a non-outflow source in its CCD edge analysis, while noting prior outflow classifications, compatible upper limits, and lower-energy warm-absorber residuals.

quotes.quote: The remaining four (IC 4329A, NGC 5548, 3C 382, and 3C 390.3) are categorized as having outflows in 1997MNRAS.286..513R and non-outflow sources in our study. The upper limits on the optical depths for IC 4329A and NGC 5548 are within the range of values for sources with detected outflows, though the ^2 values are low. Further, Chandra X-ray grating observations of these sources show ionized absorption is present 2005AA...432..453S,2005A&A...434..569S . The residuals to our base model show evidence of potential warm absorber signatures in the spectra of IC 4329A and NGC 5548, but at lower energies ( < 0.7 keV) than probed with the O 7 and O 8 edges.

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 4329A as an inclined Seyfert 1 with no silicate absorption, no near-IR excess, a nearly featureless spectrum, and a narrow-torus high-inclination fit.

quotes.quote: The only exception appears to be IC 4329A, which is a highly inclined system, but does not show silicate absorption. Deo et al. (2009) concluded for this galaxy that it is likely that our line of sight does not intersect any dense clouds in the host galaxy.
It is worth noting that all the Seyfert 1s except IC 4329A show a slight excess of emission in the near-IR, above the median torus+AGN model fits, which might be attributed to hot dust.
Indeed, it is possible to have a type 1 viewed at a relatively high i provided that the width of the angular size of the torus is small (for a given N_0 ), as is the case for NGC 4151 and IC 4329A.

sources.summary: IC 4329A is treated with an Fe K FWHM upper limit, and its inferred Fe K limit remains very close to the dust-consistent boundary.

quotes.quote: IC 4329A is also treated as upper limit on v_ FWHM .
For 9 of the 13 AGN, the measurements or limits on \ are fully consistent with . These are NGC 3783, NGC 4151, NGC 4593, NGC 5548, Mrk 509, 3C 273, IRAS 13349+2438, NGC 4051 and IC 4329A.
IC 4329A is the only source in which the limit to \ apparently sits very close to (but still consistent with) .

sources.summary: The paper excludes IC 4329A from the SED sample because its edge-on spiral host contaminates and reddens the optical/UV continuum, preventing a plausible diskpn fit.

quotes.quote: IC 4329A Inspection of images from NED reveal that the host galaxy for this AGN is an edge-on spiral galaxy. As a result, the optical and UV continuum will be heavily contaminated with light from the galaxy and reddened by the pronounced dust lane visible in the optical images. The OM points for this object do not produce a plausible fit to the diskpn model at all, and since the mass estimate from has very large errors, we exclude this object from our SED sample altogether.

sources.summary: The paper notes IC4329A as a high-inclination galaxy showing silicate emission, with its narrow-line region visible above the inclined host disk and little dense intervening dust.

quotes.quote: A few high-inclination galaxies ( IC4329A) do not show silicate absorption, but rather silicate emission. We checked Hubble Space Telescope images of IC4329A and note that the narrow-line region of this object is clearly visible in the F533N (narrow-band optical filter around [ O III ] 5007 ) filter above the inclined host galaxy disk. Our line of sight to the central source in IC4329A likely does not intersect any dense dust clouds in the host interstellar medium.

sources.summary: The paper lists IC 4329A(1) among observations without broad iron-line evidence, similar to small-reflection-strength cases, and viewed edge-on in Model SRI.

quotes.quote: The three narrow observations that do not show evidence for a broad iron line component with the ionised and/or blurred iron line models are: Mrk 590, IC 4329A(1) and NGC 5548(1).
Mrk 590 and IC 4329A(1) are similar to Mrk 6 and NGC 5506(1) in that they are best-fit with a small reflection strength and an emissivity index close to q=3 .
However, while Mrk 590 has a moderate disc inclination, our view of the disc in IC 4329A(1) is edge-on in Model SRI.

sources.summary: The atlas gives IC 4329A's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: IC 4329A is an edge-on spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0161 ( D 70.6 Mpc) with a Sy 1.2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 that belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. According to its high luminosity, IC 4329A is the nearest quasar-like object wilson_ic_1979 . The nucleus is partly obscured by a prominent dust lane, but the X-ray properties indicate additional absorption closer to the AGN steenbrugge_xmm-newton_2005 .

sources.summary: The paper includes IC 4329a among X-ray-bright Seyferts with no significant LAT signal, models its Suzaku/NuSTAR states, and compares its luminosity ratio with NGC 4151.

quotes.quote: NGC 5548 and IC 4329a The likelihood analysis of the Fermi /LAT data does not reveal any significant signal around these two X-ray-bright Seyfert galaxies (in both TS < 1 ) and therefore we computed the ULs given in Table tab:gamma .
For IC 4329a, we show in Fig. \ fig:1 a the parameters of the power-law fits from Brenneman et al. \ (2014) for lower and higher flux states during Suzaku and NuSTAR observations in 2012.
In addition, both sources have a similar L_ / L_ X ratio to NGC 4151 (for IC 4329a it is even lower).

sources.summary: The appendix says IC 4329A shows lower variability amplitudes but strong energy-range alignment, agreement with prior work, and larger uncertainties from its shorter exposure.

quotes.quote: IC 4329A shows much lower amplitudes of variability but strong overall alignment through the whole energy range. It is also in agreement with the results of Tombesi10 and Markowitz06 . A relatively low _ net compared to other sources with good evidence for UFOs stems from the shorter total exposure time of 113 ks, leading to larger statistical uncertainties.

sources.summary: The paper finds prominent nuclear and patchy ALMA emission in IC 4329A and calls for better radio data to compare with the mm-wave image.

quotes.quote: 66: IC 4329A In our image at a resolution of 0 1, nuclear emission is prominent, and patchy components are seen. Radio (8.4 GHz) emission was imaged with a beam of 0 3 0 7 by and significant radio emission with 4.9 mJy beam ^ 1 exists around the nuclear mm-wave emission. However, for further discussion, as the radio image is very noisy, better radio images with higher sensitivities and also higher spatial resolutions are desired to be compared with the ALMA one.

gmw_00000109.name: IC 4518W
gmw_00000109.overview: IC 4518W is a spiral luminous infrared galaxy hosting a Seyfert 2 nucleus [arXiv:1804.07311, arXiv:astro-ph/0610616]. It is the western member of an interacting pair [arXiv:1310.2770], featuring a compact, unresolved mid-infrared nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0610616, arXiv:astro-ph/0612577] with a minor star-formation contribution [arXiv:1804.07311].

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4518A as a type 2 Seyfert and Compton-thin source with prior broadband studies, X-ray variability, and a best-fitting Compton-thick torus reflector.

quotes.quote: IC 4518A This galaxy was optically classified as a type 2 Seyfert galaxy 2009zaw and it is classified as a Compton-thin source 1999bassani, 2008derosa, 2015lore . 2008derosa presented a 0.2–200 keV broad-band study of this galaxy with INTEGRAL , XMM-Newton , Chandra and ASCA to investigate the continuum shape and the absorbing/reflecting medium properties. They fitted the reflection model and found that in this object the presence of the reflection component above 10 keV is statistically required by the data. However, they found the best fit value larger than 1 that they suggested that could be related with the geometry of the reflector, which should be more complex than that used in their work. 2015lore studied XMM–Newton data of this galaxy and found variations in an eight-day period, that correspond to a flux variation of 40 (41 ) in the soft (0.5 - 2 keV) (hard - 5 - 10.0 keV) energy band. for this work, and data were not simultaneous, thus we explored possible spectral variability.

Using PEXMON we found R _ f =3.41 _ 1.10 ^ 6.17 . Using borus02 we find a Compton-thick reflector torus with (N_ H,refl ) =24.59 _ 24.11 ^ 25.16 , covering more than 39 of the sky. Using XILLVER we found a model consistent with <1.73 and R _ f >2.94.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC 4518W as a Seyfert 2 with hard X-ray emission lines, including neutral iron and a possible highly ionized iron contribution.

quotes.quote: IC 4518W It is a Seyfert 2 galaxy. Its \ and INTEGRAL observations are described by deRosa2008 . This is the only galaxy in the sample in which we detect two prominent emission lines in the hard X-ray spectrum, one at 6.39 0.03 keV and a weaker emission line at 7.1 ^ +0.1 _ 0.2 (Table tbl_feka ). The former is compatible with \ emission from neutral Fe. The latter may be \ produced by highly ionized iron, Fe K or these lines blended.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4518A as a type 2 Seyfert in an interacting pair, Compton-thin source, with variability tied to the nuclear continuum.

quotes.quote: IC 4518A This galaxy was optically classified as a type 2 Seyfert galaxy zaw2009 . The 2MASS image shows two interacting galaxies (see Appendix multiimages ). It is a Compton thin source bassani1999, derosa2008 . It was observed twice with XMM -Newton in 2006. Variability analyses were not found in the literature.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC 4518A as part of a merging system and finds a tail-like mm-wave core structure roughly parallel to an elongated MIR component.

quotes.quote: 75: IC 4518A This is a member of a merging system with IC 4518B. The prominent core emission, which appears to have a tail-like structure with a PA of 200 -210 , is seen in our data at a 0 2 resolution. To our knowledge, no high-resolution radio images have been published, while showed a 1.4 GHz image at a resolution 10 . As data taken at comparable resolutions, showed 12 m and 18 m images at resolutions of 0 4--0 5. The author revealed an elongated MIR component with a PA of 17 \ (blue dotted arrow), which is almost parallel to the mm-wave tail.

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 4518W as a local LIRG/Seyfert 2 with compact unresolved nuclear mid-infrared emission, no PAH emission in the nucleus, and asymmetric off-nuclear line emission.

quotes.quote: The 3 LIRGs host an optically identified AGN (NGC 5135 and IC 4518W are Seyfert (Sy) 2s, whereas the NGC 7130 has been classified as Sy 2 or LINER). From our T-ReCS and NICMOS imaging data we have detected unresolved nuclear emission in NGC 5135 and IC 4518W, implying sizes of < 40-51 pc, respectively, whereas the mid-IR nuclear emission of NGC 7130 appears resolved with a size of 190 pc (see Alonso-Herrero et al. 2006b).

The Sy nuclei of NGC 5135 and IC 4518W do not show PAH emission as found for other Sy nuclei with high spatial resolution mid-IR spectroscopy (e.g., Circinus Roche et al. 2006 and NGC 1068 Mason et al. 2006). This supports the scenario that the carriers are evaporated in the presence of a hard ionization field such as an AGN.

The spatial distributions show that is clearly present in off-nuclear regions associated with the AGN with an asymmetric distribution, especially in the case of IC 4518W (Fig. 3), as also found for Circinus (Roche et al. 2006).

sources.summary: The appendix describes IC 4518W as a spiral Sy2 galaxy with minor subarcsecond star-formation contribution and [SIV] emission north of the nucleus, possibly related to the NLR.

quotes.quote: IC 4518W is a spiral galaxy with a Sy2 nucleus Veron10 . Diaz10 and Asmus14 found that the SF contribution at subarcsecond resolution is probably minor in its nucleus. Diaz10 found [ SIV ] line emission at 265 pc toward the north of the nucleus. They suggested that this emission could be related with the NLR.

sources.summary: The paper fits IC 4518A optical narrow lines, uses its line ratios to classify it as Seyfert 2, and measures a single-Gaussian [OIII] flux.

quotes.quote: IC 4518A : We fit for the narrow lines in the optical spectrum of IC 4518A (courtesy of N. Masetti). The log( [ OIII ] /H ) and log( [ NII ] /H ) line ratios (courtesy of J. Moustakas), 0.839 and -0.199 respectively, place it in the Seyfert 2 region of the BPT diagram. We fit a single Gaussian to the [ OIII ] line and obtain a flux of F [ OIII ] = 1.20 10 ^ 13 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The atlas gives IC 4518W's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: IC 4518W is the western galaxy of an interacting pair with a nuclear separation of 38 at a redshift of z= 0.0163 ( D 70.2 Mpc) hosting a Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . It was observed with /IRAC, IRS and MIPS, where a compact MIR nucleus without any host emission was detected. Note that IC 4518E is also detected and extended in the IRAC and MIPS images.

sources.summary: The paper presents IC 4518W as a LIRG with an unresolved Seyfert 2 nucleus that dominates its mid-infrared emission and supplies about half of the IC 4518W+E system emission.

quotes.quote: For the unresolved sources (nuclei of NGC 5135 and IC 4518W) the aperture corrections to include all the flux from the point source were computed from standard star observations. To compare our T-ReCS data with other galaxies (see 3.3), we converted the T-ReCS N band flux densities to IRAC 8 m ones, convolving Spitzer /IRS spectra with the appropriate filter transmissions. For NGC 5135 and NGC 7130 we found f_ ( IRAC 8 m )/f_ ( N-band ) = 0.86 and 0.76, respectively. These might be average values as spatial variations of the MIR spectral properties of the central regions have been observed in nearby galaxies (e.g., Smith et al. 2004). For the Seyfert (Sy) nuclei of NGC 5135 and IC 4518W, and using a 'generic' IRS Sy2 template (Mrk 3, Weedman et al. 2005), we obtained f_ ( IRAC 8 m )/f_ ( N-band ) = 0.56 .

The MIR emission of IC 4518W is dominated by the Sy2 nucleus which accounts for up to 75 (Table 1) of the N band flux density of this galaxy and approximately half of the MIR emission of the system (IC 4518W+E).

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 4518W as a local LIRG with an unresolved Sy2 nucleus that appears to dominate the galaxy's mid-infrared emission.

quotes.quote: Three LIRGs (NGC 5135, IC 4518W, and NGC 7130) were observed with the N broad-band filter (central wavelength _ =10.36 m) and NGC 3256 with the Si-2 narrow-band filter ( _ =8.74 m).

The Sy2 nuclei of NGC 5135 and IC 4518W appear unresolved in the T-ReCs images, in agreement with the detection of nuclear point sources in the higher spatial resolution ( 0.15 ) NICMOS images (see AAH06).
The Seyfert nucleus of IC 4518W appears to be dominate the mid-IR emission, whereas for NGC 5135 and NGC 7130 the Seyfert nuclei contribute 20 or less of the N band emission within the central 10 .

gmw_00000110.name: IC 5063
gmw_00000110.overview: IC 5063 is a nearby lenticular Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1910.04912] that contains a central gas disk [arXiv:1811.07935] and large-scale dust lanes [arXiv:1910.04912, arXiv:2309.02167]. Its active nucleus drives a multiphase galactic wind [arXiv:1803.00740, arXiv:1811.07935] and hosts a low-power radio jet that directly accelerates the surrounding molecular gas [arXiv:2202.05222].

sources.summary: The paper uses ALMA CO data to show IC 5063 has a fast multiphase molecular outflow along its radio jet, likely driven by jet-cloud interaction.

quotes.quote: We use high-resolution (0.5 arcsec) CO(2-1) observations performed with the Atacama Large Millimetre/submillimetre Array to trace the kinematics of the molecular gas in the Seyfert 2 galaxy IC 5063 . The data reveal that the kinematics of the gas is very complex. A fast outflow of molecular gas extends along the entire radio jet ( 1 kpc), with the highest outflow velocities about 0.5 kpc from the nucleus, at the location of the brighter hot-spot in the W lobe. The ALMA data show that a massive, fast outflow with velocities up to 650 \ of cold molecular gas is present, in addition to one detected earlier in warm H _2 , \ and ionised gas. All phases of the gas outflow show similar kinematics.

IC 5063 appears to be one of the best examples of the multi-phase nature of AGN-driven outflows. Both the central AGN and the radio jet could energetically drive the outflow. However, the characteristics of the outflowing gas point to the radio jet being the main driver.

sources.summary: The paper fits IC 5063 as a radio-loud type 2 Seyfert whose absorption lets the data reject low torus columns and tightly constrain the reprocessing component.

quotes.quote: IC 5063 Partly due to higher line-of-sight absorption in comparison with 3C 390.3 and NGC 2110, the \ data for IC 5063 have the constraining power to reject a part of the parameter space on statistical grounds, despite lower photon statistics. As the third pair of panels in fig:sweep shows with the lack of \ constraints for <10^ 23 , no model with >5 can be found for a lower torus column density. ^2 as a function of \ has a minimum ( ^2/ d.o.f. =259.6/253 ) around =23.9 . With \ fixed at this value, >0.77 and <0.62 . This model is shown in the upper right panels of fig:eeuf . Fitting for the torus column density, we find that it is very well constrained, =23.95 0.07 , and that there is almost no degeneracy with the covering factor.

Unlike the cases of 3C 390.3 and NGC 2110, the contours in the - \ plane are elongated along the axes, predominantly vertically. Constraints on \ and \ are therefore no different than those obtained with \ fixed. With a high \ and \ near the CT threshold, the reprocessed component contributes 20 of the flux in the 10--50 keV band.

sources.summary: The paper uses IC 5063 as a nearby type-2 Seyfert test case, describing its lenticular host with dust lanes and fitting NuSTAR and Spitzer spectra to constrain a shared X-ray/mid-IR torus.

quotes.quote: For this work, we selected the type-2 Seyfert IC 5063 as a test object. This AGN is located at the center of a nearly lenticular galaxy at 46 Mpc Alonso-Herrero11 . This galaxy contains a disc with large-scale dust lanes Morganti98 , possibly resulting from a merger Morganti98 . According to Ichikawa15 the bolometric luminosity of IC 5063 is 3.38 10^ 44 erg s^ 1 .

We considered Spitzer /IRS and NuSTAR spectra for this analysis. We combined the radiative transfer code borus02 at X-ray Balokovic18 to describe the X-ray reflection and Smooth Fritz06 , Clumpy NenkovaB08 , or CAT3D-wind Hoenig17 models to describe mid-IR AGN dust to create a set of baseline models. We found that the combination of the borus02 and Smooth models is the best choice to fit the spectra from both wavelengths of IC 5063. Moreover, all the parameters of the dusty torus can be constrained if the X-ray and mid-IR inclination and half-opening angles are linked to the same value (bS2 baseline model).

sources.summary: The paper reports that IC 5063 has extended coronal gas on both sides of the AGN, elevated excitation outside the nucleus, and radio-jet-linked shock excitation.

quotes.quote: The sample employed in this work is composed of five local Seyfert 2 AGN ( z < 0.02 , where z is the redshift), widely known for displaying a low-luminosity radio-jet and a narrow line region (NLR) characterized by a bi-conical structure, previously mapped through the [ O iii ] 5007 emission. This is the case of the Circinus Galaxy, NGC 5728, IC 5063, NGC 3393 and NGC 5643. They all display [ Fe vii ] 6087 in the nuclear spectrum and a NLR that extends to at least 2 kpc from the AGN.

In IC 5063, an increase of the gas excitation is evident outside the nuclear region, at 500 pc NW and SE of the AGN. The strong relationship with the radio jet is evident in Figure fig:gals . Radio emission from the jet traces cavities that enhances [ Fe vii ] /H , suggesting a role in the excitation of these hot spots. The values of that ratio, between 10 ^ 2 to 10 ^ 1 , cannot be easily explained by photoionization from the central source. Instead, shock models of are able to reproduce the observed gas excitation, along the region where the coronal gas is detected.

sources.summary: The paper studies IC 5063 as a nearby Type 2 Seyfert with a gas disk, radio lobes, and multi-phase outflows, linking the outflows to jet-ISM interactions and shock compression.

quotes.quote: IC 5063 is a nearby ( z =0.01131) early-type galaxy with a Type 2 Seyfert nucleus Danziger1981, Inglis1993 . The galaxy hosts a gaseous disk that is detected out to a radius of 28kpc in HI emission Morganti1998 , is associated with prominent dust lanes, and may be the result of a merger Morganti1998 . The inner parts of this disk (within a few kpc) are also detected in cold CO Morganti2015 and warm H _2 molecular gas Tadhunter2014 , and [ OIII ] emission lines from warm, ionised gas are detected in HST images along the edges of the dust lane to a radius of 10 kpc Morganti1998 . IC 5063 has a radio power at the upper end of the range for Seyfert galaxies (P _ 1.4 GHz =3 10^ 23 WHz ^ 1 ), and the radio emission is concentrated in a triple-structure parallel to the dust lanes, which consists of a nucleus and two radio lobes 2 arcsec ( 0.5 kpc) on either side of the nucleus to the south east (SE) and north west (NW) (Figure fig: ic5063_hst_17ghz ).

Observations have revealed fast ( V 700 km s ^ 1 ) outflows spatially associated with the NW radio lobe across a range of gas phases: warm ionised Morganti2007, Sharp2010, Congiu2017, Venturi2021 ; neutral Morganti1998, Oosterloo2000 ; warm molecular Tadhunter2014 and cold molecular Morganti2013, Morganti2015, Dasyra2016, Oosterloo2017 . In addition, IC 5063 has been the target of X-ray observations Vignali1997, Tazaki2011, Travascio2021 . IC 5063 is unusual in the sense that its radio jets are propagating in the plane of its disk Morganti1998, Oosterloo2000, Morganti2015, Mukherjee2018 . Considering that the outflows in the galaxy are spatially associated with the bright radio features, particularly at the NW radio lobe, it is thought that jet-ISM interactions are the main outflow acceleration mechanism in the galaxy, with these interactions being particularly strong due to the jet propagating in the denser ISM in the disk Tadhunter2014, Morganti2015 .

sources.summary: The paper presents the first optical polarimetric map of IC 5063, linking its radio-loud Seyfert nucleus, jets, winds, dust lane, and ISM interactions.

quotes.quote: We also present, for the first time, an optical polarimetric map of the radio-loud AGN IC 5063 and we examine the complex interactions between the AGN outflows and the surrounding interstellar medium (ISM).

IC 5063 appears to show a lot of complex interactions between the jets, the AGN winds and the local galactic material. Thus, we divided the HST/FOC map in six distinct regions. These regions are listed in Tab. tab:IC5063_int_regions and delimited in Fig. fig:IC5063_map_regions . These specific regions are individually explored through the polarization information made available by the HST/FOC observation and correlated with observations at other wavelengths: In Fig. fig:IC5063_radio , we show the superposition of our HST/FOC map with the 18 GHz ATCA map provided by Morganti1998 .

Region 4: Dust lane On the northern part of the HST/FOC map, we can see a clear cut in logarithmic scale on the flux intensity map. We know from Morganti1998 , in particular, their Fig. 4, that this obscured region in optical corresponds to the extension of the dust lane, which is confirmed by the WFPC2 infrared map (Fig. fig:IC5063_ir ). Using the spatial capabilities of the FOC, we can isolate and integrate the polarization from this dusty region.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC5063 as an S0 Seyfert 2 and narrow-line radio galaxy with scattered broad H emission, a red unresolved nucleus, absorbed X-ray spectrum, and flux variability.

quotes.quote: IC5063 IC5063 is an S0 galaxy (z=0.011) presenting a typical Seyfert-2 galaxy spectrum (Colina, Sparks Macchetto 1991). Scattered broad H_ emission has been detected by Inglis et al. (1993). HST NICMOS observations show a very red unresolved point source (Kulkarni et al. 1998). IC5063 has a radio power at 1.4 GHz two orders of magnitude greater than that of typical Seyfert galaxies and thus it can be classified as a Narrow-Line Radio galaxy as well (Ulvestad Wilson 1984). A observation showed a power-law photon index of =1.5 and N_ H 2.5 10^ 23 (Smith Done 1996).

Turner et al. (1997) presented two observations of IC5063. The spectrum is in good agreement with the observations. IC5063 shows short-term X-ray flux variability at the 90 per cent confidence level (Turner et al. 1997).

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 5063 as a well-known Seyfert early-type galaxy with AGN-dominated hot dust, PAH emission, molecular lines, and high-ionization MIR lines.

quotes.quote: The MIR spectra of NGC 1052, considered a prototypical LINER see e.g. Ho08 , and of IC 5063, a well known Seyfert (see for references on-line notes in R05) are shown in Fig. Fig9 . The continuum of both galaxies is dominated by hot dust, most probably coming from an AGN torus. PAH emission features are also visible; in particular, NGC 1052 shows both the 7.6 and 11.3 m PAH complexes, while it is possible to distinguish only the 7.6 m feature in IC 5063.

H _2 0-0 S(1) and S(3) molecular emission lines are present in both spectra. Ionic forbidden emission lines of [ Ar II ] , [ Ne II ] and [ Ne III ] , [ S III ] and [ O IV ] are revealed in both spectra. High-ionization [ S IV ] and [ Ne V ] emission lines are revealed only in IC 5063. We classify the MIR spectra of these two ETGs as Class--4 .

sources.summary: IC 5063 is presented as a Seyfert 2 galaxy with fast neutral-hydrogen, cold, and warm gas outflows plus a UV blue ring-like structure possibly tied to accretion.

quotes.quote: IC 5063, at the bottom of Figure fig1 , is a well known Seyfert 2 see Veron06 . It was the first galaxy in which a fast neutral hydrogen outflow was discovered see and reference therein Morganti07 . Morganti et al. have also found evidence of cold and warm gas outflows and noticed that the outflow rate, associated to the HI, is in the same range as for ``mild'' starburst driven superwinds in ULIRGs. Although limited to the inner kpc region the observed outflows may have sufficient kinetic power to have a significant impact on the evolution of the interstellar medium of IC 5063. In A10 we show that in IC 5063 a Seyfert like ionization extends out to our largest sample radius, _e /2, i.e. about 3 kpc (H _0 =75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ).

Figure fig1 shows the complex, outer, blue ring-like structure detected in UV in IC 5063. The origin of such structure may be connected with an accretion episode as the complex dust-lane crossing the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper classifies IC 5063 as a narrow-line Seyfert 2 radio galaxy with Compton-thin absorption, concave hard X-ray spectra, diffuse soft X-rays, and possible jet contribution.

quotes.quote: IC 5063: Classified as narrow line Sy 2 radio galaxies and the Suzaku data are studied by 2011ApJ...738...70T . There is a Nustar data but not published. 2012ApJ...748..130M also studied the source in a sample of Sy 2 sources which have exhibited broad Fe K line in reflected spectra. The source is classified as Compton thin by 2012ApJ...748..130M . Absorbed source with a concave spectra in the hard X-rays and diffuse soft X-ray emission.
An absorbed powerlaw and a blackbody for the diffuse soft X-ray emission could fit the data. There could be X-ray contribution from radio jets.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 5063 as a nearby S0 Seyfert with a low-power radio jet that drives multiphase outflow and produces extended perpendicular ionized-gas line broadening.

quotes.quote: IC 5063 is an S0 early-type galaxy residing at a distance of 46 Mpc from Earth (1 '' 220 pc). With a radio power of P_ 1.4 GHz = 3 10^ 23 W Hz ^ 1 ( Tadhunter:2014aa ), it is one of the brightest Seyfert galaxies in the radio, although it is still radio quiet. IC 5063 hosts a radio jet limited to the inner 1 kpc of the galaxy along its major axis ( 0.5 kpc per side), which drives an outflow of neutral atomic ( Morganti:1998aa , Oosterloo:2000aa ), molecular ( Morganti:2013aa , Tadhunter:2014aa , Morganti:2015aa , Dasyra:2016aa , Oosterloo:2017aa ), and ionised gas ( Morganti:2007aa , Dasyra:2015aa , Congiu:2017aa ) on the same scales along the jet direction by creating a cocoon of swept off gas.

The jet is known to lie close to the plane of the disc, as clear signs of jet-interstellar medium (ISM) interaction are observed along the entire jet in HI ( Oosterloo:2000aa ), CO ( Morganti:2015aa ) and H _2 ( Tadhunter:2014aa ). In Figs. fig:maps_ic5063_1 and fig:maps_ic5063_2 we report our MUSE flux and kinematic maps for IC 5063, respectively.

The map shows an elongated region of enhanced line width (up to 800 km/s at its centre; see also Lopez-Coba:2020aa ) perpendicular to the radio jet, AGN ionisation cones, X-ray emission, and ionised and molecular outflow. This perpendicular enhanced line width is also very extended, spanning about 7 kpc ( 3.5 kpc per side), while the radio jet only extends over the central 1 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper includes IC 5063 among nearby radio galaxies with fast outflows and reports broad, resolved H2 emission showing turbulent warm molecular gas associated with jet energy deposition.

quotes.quote: We present mid-IR Spitzer spectroscopy of 8 nearby radio-galaxies where fast outflows of ionized and atomic gas were detected by previous optical and radio observations. Our main results and conclusions are the following: In all of the sources, we detect high equivalent width H _2 line emission from warm (100-5000 K) molecular gas, implying warm H _2 masses ranging from 10^8 to 10^ 10 M _ . The observed H _2 to-PAH luminosity ratios are all above the values predicted from photo-ionization models, and we suggest that the H _2 emission is associated with the dissipation of a fraction of the kinetic energy provided by the radio jet.

All the sources, except OQ 208, have at least one broad H _2 line with intrinsic FWHM > 430 km s ^ 1 . 3C 293, 3C 305, 3C 459, 4C 12.50, and IC 5063 show two or more resolved H _2 lines. These results show that the warm H _2 gas is very turbulent in most of the sources.

sources.summary: IC5063 is a post-merger elliptical with a Seyfert 2 nucleus, symmetric barrel-shaped ENLR, radio nucleus and lobes, broad outflowing H I absorption, and no H II regions.

quotes.quote: IC 5063: A post-merger Elliptical, this galaxy containing a Seyfert 2 nucleus, it shows a spectacular, symmetric ENLR extending across the WiFeS field. Barrel-shaped with a bright linear core in the inner in the inner regions, it develops horns with an opening angle of 30 in the outer parts. Observed with HST, the inner region forms a fine knotty ionisation cone in \ with an opening angle the same as in the outer regions Schmitt03a . A small (4 arcsec; 860pc) double-lobe plus nucleus radio structure has been resolved at 8.6GHz by Morganti98 , who also detected a very broad outflowing ( 700 km s ^ 1 ) HI absorption against the strong nuclear continuum source. The broad-line nucleus was detected in polarised light by Lumsden04 .
No \ regions are visible.

sources.summary: IC 5063 is presented as a Seyfert 2 with a radio-jet-driven galactic wind, a biconical ENLR, complex nuclear line profiles, and shock-contaminated nuclear parameter estimates.

quotes.quote: IC 5063: A well-studied galaxy exhibiting both Seyfert 2 excitation and a radio jet that powers a spectacular galactic wind e.g. Sharp_BlandHawthorn_2010_winds, Tadhunter_2014_Nature_IC5063, Morganti_2015_IC5063, Dasyra_2015_IC_5063_SINFONI . The biconical, outflowing ENLR has a large opening angle of 90 \ and extends at least 30 \ across the WiFeS field of view. The nuclear line profiles are extremely complex with multiple independent components. There is a hidden broad-line region revealed by spectropolarimetry Lumsden_2004_hiddenBLR .

In this galaxy we measure all four parameters to be lower in the nucleus than in adjacent regions. This is likely caused by the large-scale galactic wind, which is evident in the three Gaussian components required to fit the nuclear emission lines (with widths up to _v 350 km s ^ 1 and offsets between components of over 200 km s ^ 1 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses IC 5063 as a comparison AGN where radio-jet interactions disturb molecular gas, elevate CO line ratios, and produce high-excitation, extreme-kinematic gas along the jet.

quotes.quote: A similar case is IC 5063 ( Dasyra16,Oosterloo17 ), where the CO gas that is kinematically disturbed by the AGN has very high values for . Modelling the various line ratios in IC 5063 suggested that the disturbed gas has densities and kinetic temperatures similar to those observed in NGC 1068. Also in the case of IC 5063 this is very likely the result of the AGN dumping large amounts of energy in the ISM surrounding the AGN.

Another case for which detailed information is available is IC 5063 Morganti15,Oosterloo17 , where the close morphological match between the radio jet and the region of molecular gas with elevated line ratios and extreme kinematics is a strong indication that the interaction between the radio plasma with the ISM is the main mechanism for disturbing the gas and for affecting its excitation. In HE 1353--1917 and IC 5063, the highest excitation and the most extreme kinematics do not occur at the core, but along the entire jet where it appears to be interacting strongly with the ISM. In addition, the fact that in HE 1353--1917 and IC 5063 the orientation of the jet is such that the jet moves in the plane of the gas disc, while in \ the jet is not aligned with the disc, may play a role in explaining the differences.

sources.summary: IC 5063 is described as a Sy2 nucleus in a merger-remnant host with a dust lane, strong radio luminosity, unresolved mid-IR nucleus, and high torus obscuration.
