quotes.quote: IC 5063. This Sy2 nucleus is hosted by a merger remnant classified as an elliptical galaxy Colina91 . An obscuring dust lane partially covers the eastern side of the galaxy. A peculiarity of this source is its strong radio luminosity, which is two orders of magnitude larger than the typical values for Seyfert galaxies Colina91 . From an N-band adquisition image of IC 5063 obtained with T-ReCS/Gemini-South, Young07 tentatively found the nucleus of this galaxy slightly resolved.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 5063 as a Seyfert galaxy with a neutral-hydrogen outflow and 15 kpc X-shaped ionized structure, arguing Thomson scattering can reproduce its morphology and supports hollow quasar outflows.

quotes.quote: The morphology of the outflow in IC 5063 Located in the southern hemisphere, IC 5063 ( z = 0.011, Morganti2007 ) is a Seyfert-galaxy that is famous to be the first AGN where a fast outflow of neutral hydrogen was detected Morganti1998 . Among other characteristics, its Hubble Space Telescope image shows an ionised structure extending out to 15 kpc in a remarkable, X-shaped morphology Morganti2003 . However, spectroscopic and spectropolarimetric images of solid double cone models Wolf1999,Marin2012a show that the scattering region re-emits uniformly to the irradiation source and the peculiar behaviour of IC 5063 cannot be visually explained using uniform or fragmented polar winds.

We checked that the X-shape morphology is also reproduced in total flux, a straightforward conclusion as Thomson scattering is wavelength-independent. the X-shaped morphology of IC 5063, as well as the bright flux spot in the AGN inner regions detected by Morganti2003 . It indicates that Thomson scattering is still a dominant mechanism at large distances from the central source of IC 5063 and it tends to support the idea that quasar outflows are hollow structures.

sources.summary: The paper studies IC 5063 as a Seyfert 2 galaxy whose ENLR gas shows velocity-dependent ionization, possible high-velocity shock contribution, and geometry consistent with a hollow bicone aligned with the disk.

quotes.quote: We studied the properties of the gas of the extended narrow line region (ENLR) of two Seyfert 2 galaxies: IC 5063 and NGC 7212. We analysed high resolution spectra to investigate how the main properties of this region depend on the gas velocity. We divided the emission lines in velocity bins and we calculated several line ratios. Diagnostic diagrams and SUMA composite models (photo-ionization + shocks), show that in both galaxies there might be evidence of shocks significantly contributing in the gas ionization at high V , even though photo-ionization from the active nucleus remains the main ionization mechanism. In IC 5063 the ionization parameter depends on V and its trend might be explained assuming an hollow bi-conical shape for the ENLR, with one of the edges aligned with the galaxy disk.

In the CN, N0 and N1 regions of IC 5063 the ionization parameter decreases of about one order of magnitude between V=-600 and V=500 kms , which means that the blueshifted gas is irradiated directly by the AGN, while the redshifted gas is ionized by an attenuated continuum. In the S0, S1 and S2 regions U increases in the opposite direction, from V=-400 to V=200 . The velocity range is quite narrow because at higher V the effects of the extinction correction dominates with respect to the real behaviour of the ionization parameter. The ionization increase with V is clearly confirmed by the ( [ O III ] / ) vs V plot. This behaviour of the ionization parameter might be explained assuming a hollow bi-conical shape of the ENLR with one of the edges aligned with the galaxy disk.

sources.summary: IC 5063 is presented as a Seyfert 2 and the first clear H I outflow case, with blueshifted absorption located by VLBI at a bright radio hot spot.

quotes.quote: The first object where an \ outflow was discovered is the Seyfert 2 galaxy IC 5063 Morganti98 . This object is one of the few Seyfert galaxies with relatively strong radio continuum. The radio morphology shows a core and two lobes, one of which is ending in a particularly radio bright hot spot, about 500 pc from the nucleus. In this galaxy, highly blueshifted absorption ( 700 , see Fig. \ fig:IC5063ATCA ) was detected using the ATCA. The spatial resolution of these ATCA data were not sufficient to determine the location of the outflow, but later VLBI observations allowed to establish that the location of the fastest part of the outflow is coincident with the bright hot spot mentioned above, around 0.5 kpc from the nucleus Oosterloo00 .

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 5063 as the best-studied jet-driven outflow case, where ALMA shows a fast molecular outflow, altered gas conditions, and a clumpy high-pressure medium.

quotes.quote: The best studied example of a jet-driven outflow is IC 5063. This is also due to the geometry of the system, with the radio jet expanding into the large gas disk. ALMA observations show that in the immediate vicinity of the radio jet, a fast outflow (up to 800 ), is occurring (see Morganti15 and Fig. fig3 ). The radio jet is also affecting the physical conditions of the molecular gas, as derived from observations of multiple transitions ( Oosterloo17 ; Fig. fig3 ). The line ratios suggest that the outflowing gas is optically thin with a mass of a few 10^6 \ and a mass outflow rate 10 .

sources.summary: The review presents IC 5063 as a Seyfert 2 where a low-power radio jet disturbs multiphase gas, drives a fast molecular outflow, and changes gas conditions.

quotes.quote: One of the objects where the effects of the radio jet on the molecular gas has been studied in details is the Seyfert 2 galaxy IC 5063. This object is also one of the clearest examples of low-power jet (this source would be classified as radio-quiet) disturbing the kinematics of all the phases of the gas see for an overview Tadhunter14,Morganti15 . The ALMA observations have confirmed the presence of molecular gas with disturbed kinematics across the entire region co-spatial with the radio emission Morganti15 . The observations of a number of CO transitions have shown that in the immediate vicinity of the radio jet, a fast outflow, with velocities up to 800 , is occurring.
In addition to this, the interaction is also affecting the physical conditions of the gas Dasyra16,Oosterloo17 .

sources.summary: The paper uses IC 5063 as a Seyfert 2 example where low-power radio jets disturb ionized, molecular, and H I gas, producing a clear jet-induced outflow.

quotes.quote: The best illustration of this is the case of IC 5063, a Seyfert 2 galaxy, with strong emission lines. This galaxy hosts low radio power jets ( P_ 1.4 GHz 10^ 23.4 ). Despite the low radio power, they provide one of the clearest examples of jet-induced outflows, where the radio plasma is disturbing the kinematics of all the phases of the gas see for an overview Tadhunter14,Morganti15 . The region co-spatial with the radio emission is where the most kinematically disturbed ionised, molecular and \ gas is located (with velocities deviating up to 600 \ from regular rotation). This jet-ISM interaction is also affecting the physical conditions of the gas Oosterloo17 .

sources.summary: The paper groups IC 5063 with Sy2 sources showing X-ray absorption variability, high covering factors, and intermediate/high opening angles, preferring a thick clumpy gas-and-dust torus intercepted along the line of sight.

quotes.quote: Panel 5 of Figure fig:cartoon_Sy2 (e.g. NGC 4507, NGC 1052, NGC 4939, and IC 5063): Sy2 sources with absorption variability at X-rays, high covering factors, and half opening angles at both X-rays and mid-IR consistent with intermediate or high values. A clumpy distribution of both gas and dust is preferred for this group of objects. All the properties described above can be explained with a geometrically-thick torus where dust and gas is distributed in clumps. The gas is probably located at both the outer dusty and inner dust-free regions, the latter producing the observed absorption variability. Indeed the inclination angles constrained for these sources are consistent with these clumps being intercepted by the LOS.

sources.summary: The paper uses IC 5063 as a nearby Seyfert 2 comparison, describing its edge-on disk, radio-jet-driven multiphase outflows, AGN-photoionised warm gas, and post-shock cooling sequence.

quotes.quote: Finally, using the results for the nearby Seyfert 2 IC 5063 presented in Holden2022 along with the results for NGC 1068 and NGC 4151 that we present here, we can begin to construct a sample of nearby Seyferts with spatially-resolved, detailed studies of their NLR outflows. IC 5063 is a nearby ( z=0.01131 ) early-type Seyfert 2 galaxy that is seen close to edge-on, with a radio jet propagating almost in the plane of the disk which drives fast ( v_ out 700 km s ^ 1 ) outflows Morganti1998, Oosterloo2000, Morganti2015, Mukherjee2018, Holden2022 . These outflows are seen in multiple gas phases, including warm ionised Morganti2007, Sharp2010, Congiu2017, Venturi2021, Holden2022 ; neutral Morganti1998, Oosterloo2000 ; warm molecular Tadhunter2014, Holden2022 and cold molecular Morganti2013, Morganti2015, Dasyra2016, Oosterloo2017 .

In Holden2022 , we presented evidence that both the outflowing and quiescent warm ionised gas in IC 5063 has dominant AGN-photoionisation --- even though the outflows show clear signatures of shock acceleration --- and that the different outflow phases may represent a post-shock cooling sequence. We interpreted this situation as the pre-shock gas being AGN-photoionised, and the closest post-shock gas to the AGN kept in an ionised state by photoionisation.

sources.summary: The paper finds three linearly aligned mm-wave components in IC 5063, with spectral indices favoring nonthermal origins for the outer components.

quotes.quote: 89: IC 5063 Our ALMA image produced at a 0 4 resolution shows three compact components: one at the central AGN position ( a ) and the other two to the SE and NW ( b and ). They are almost linearly aligned at an angle of 115 , which is similar to that found for linear structures at frequencies of 10--20 GHz. The mm-wave components were already discussed by with a different ALMA data at 230 GHz. Using 17 GHz radio data from together, the authors constrained the spectral indices for the SE, NW, and central components to be 0.87 0.02, 0.93 0.01, and 0.33 0.01. The resolutions of the used 17 GHz and 230 GHz data were 0 4--0 5, comparable to ours.

sources.summary: The review highlights IC 5063 as a nearby lenticular radio-source host where ALMA shows jet-driven molecular outflow, high gas velocities, altered excitation, and clumpy gas.

quotes.quote: A remarkable case in the context of this review is the one of the nearby ( z=0.011341 ) lenticular galaxy IC 5063, hosting the young (i.e. t_ age 1 Myr) core-double lobe radio source PKS 2048-57 and observed at high resolution with ALMA in different molecular gas transitions see Morganti15a,Dasyra16,Oosterloo17 . These observations demonstrate a prominent alteration of the molecular gas kinematics in the regions co-spatial with the radio lobes, with anomalously high gas velocities (up to 650 km s ^ 1 ) that can be unequivocally explained as a jet-driven outflow with a mass rate of 12 M _ [ see Figure fig:CO_jet , left panel ] . Molecular line ratios are also powerful tools to investigate the physical conditions of the gas in various environments, as different molecules, isotopologues, and transitions of the same species trace different gas components within the same galaxy. The ratios of the various molecular transitions observed in IC 5063 allow to demonstrate that the molecular gas physics is modified around the radio source, varying from optically-thick and sub-thermally excited in the unperturbed gas to optically-thin with a high excitation temperature ( >50 K) in the regions affected by the jet-driven outflow. Signs of gas fragmentation are also found, suggesting that the outflowing molecular gas is clumpy.

sources.summary: The paper finds IC 5063 has dust-obscured asymmetric near-UV emission, with ionized-gas subtraction removing much of the southeast emission but leaving extended southwest light and clumps.

quotes.quote: IC 5063 (Sy2) Most of the emission to the southeast is removed with f =0.20, but in order to get rid of the northern filamentary structure, the scaling factor has to be increased to 0.25-0.30. Some negative residuals appear in the subtracted image (top right panel of Fig. fig:all3_01 ). Rather than a change in the ionisation parameter, we suspect that these residuals may be due to dust obscuration, affecting much more the near-UV image than the [ OIII ] one. This object is heavily obscured by dust, which can explain the axial asymmetry of the emission.

sources.summary: The paper presents IC 5063 as the best-studied case where radio jets reproduce disturbed molecular gas across a 1 kpc radio source and alter CO excitation through shocks.

quotes.quote: In the best studied case, IC 5063 Morganti15,Oosterloo17 , we have detected kinematically disturbed molecular gas along the full extent of the radio source (about 1 kpc). The comparison between observations and simulations shows that the radio jets can produce the observed distribution and kinematics of the molecular gas ( Mukherjee18b ).

This has been seen using multiple CO transitions in the cases of IC 5063 (see Fig. \ fig2 ) and PKS 1549--79, see and respectively. The line ratios of the CO transitions indicate that the gas in the region affected by the interaction with the radio plasma has different excitation and/or optical thickness, as result of the impact from strong shocks.

sources.summary: The paper cites IC 5063 as a Seyfert 2 with ENLR density measurements, nuclear Xshooter constraints, and complex jet-ISM emission-line profiles.

quotes.quote: Using the Magellan Echellette spectrograph mounted on one of the Magellan Telescope, Co17 succeeded in measuring the radial behavior of the \ doublet ratio within the ENLR of two Seyfert 2s, IC 5063 and NGC 7212. The densities they inferred were all below 10^4 , except at the nucleus on the north side of IC 5063. Using the VLT Xshooter spectrograph, Ho23 obtained deep observations of IC 5063 where the \ doublet measured in the nucleus implied a density in _ 10 of 3.79 ^ +0.18 _ 0.19 .

Emission line fitting procedure Most galaxies in our sample exhibit complex emission line profiles due to AGN-driven winds and outflows, or strong jet-ISM interactions, as in the case of IC 5063 Mo15 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes X-ray spectra of IC 5063, describing its best-fit components, Fe K emission or flux behavior, and comparison with previous modeling.

quotes.quote: IC 5063 The Chandra spectrum was moderately affected by pileup, 14 according to the jdpileup model in Sherpa . We therefore included a pileup model in the XSpec spectral fits, allowing only the grade migration parameter ( ) to be free, and obtained an value of 0.37; excluded the pileup component when calculating the observed 2 - 10 keV flux. The jdpileup model indicated no pileup in the local 4 - 8 keV spectral fit, so it was modeled without a pileup component in XSpec.

sources.summary: The paper includes IC 5063 among Seyfert torus fits, noting nuclear dust features, compatible silicate/SED fits, and moderate apparent silicate depth like MCG 5 23-16.

quotes.quote: In our sample, Circinus, IC 5063, NGC 5506, NGC 7582, NGC 2110, NGC 7172, and possibly NGC 7674 show dust features in the nuclear (central 1-2 ) regions (see e.g., Quillen et al. 1999; Maiolino et al. 2000; Martini et al. 2003).
These galaxies are all the pure Seyfert 1s (IC 4329A, NGC 3227, NGC 4151 and NGC 7469), and NGC 1068, IC 5063, MCG 5 23-16, and NGC 7674.

As an illustration of the complicated behavior of the silicate feature in terms of the torus model parameters, we can see that galaxies of the same type and with similar apparent depths of the 9.7 m silicate feature (e.g., type 1s IC 4329A and NGC 7469, and type 2s MCG 5 23-16 and IC 5063, see H onig et al. 2010) have different fitted values of N_0 and _V (see Table 5, and Appendix A).

sources.summary: The atlas gives IC 5063's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: IC 5063 is a peculiar galaxy with both spiral and elliptical properties at a redshift of z= 0.0113 ( D 45.3 Mpc) with a Sy 2 nucleus kewley_optical_2001 with polarized broad lines being detected lumsden_spectropolarimetry_2004 . It belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample, is luminous at radio wavelengths and possesses an extended NLR (PA 65 ; morganti_radio_1998,morganti_radio_1999,schmitt_hubble_2003 ). propose IC 5063 to be the remnant of a recent merger, while speculate that the nuclear obscuration might be caused by foreground dust lanes.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 5063 as a peculiar Sy2 galaxy with spiral and elliptical properties, possible recent-merger origin, and nuclear obscuration attributed to foreground dust lanes.

quotes.quote: IC 5063 is a peculiar galaxy with both spiral and elliptical properties with a Sy2 nucleus Kewley01 . Colina91 proposed that IC 5063 is a remnant of a recent merger, while Martini03 speculated that the nuclear obscuration might be caused by foreground dust lanes. We did not find records of SF in other works at the scales traced by our observations.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 5063 as a massive early-type Seyfert with a central gas disc, radio jet, double ionization cone, and a prominent jet-driven multiphase outflow.

quotes.quote: IC 5063 (Seyfert 2 - D_ L 45.3 Mpc - 1" 219.6 pc) is a massive early-type galaxy with a central gas disc and a radio jet that propagates for several hundred parsec before it extends beyond the disc plane morganti1998 . An extended ( 20 kpc) dominated double ionisation cone with an X morphology is observed colina1991 , ubiquitously dominated by photoionisation from the AGN sharp2010 . In this galaxy there is one of the most spectacular examples of jet-driven outflow, with similar features in the ionised, neutral atomic, and molecular phase, since the radio plasma jet is expanding into a clumpy gaseous medium, possibly creating a cocoon of shocked gas which is pushed away from the jet axis morganti1998,sharp2010,tadhunter2014,dasyra2015,oosterloo2017 .

sources.summary: The paper uses IC 5063 as a comparison where high-resolution observations localize a cold-gas outflow driven by a low-luminosity radio jet, though AGN radiation may contribute.

quotes.quote: The best example of the impact of a low-luminosity radio jet on the molecular gas has been observed in the Seyfert 2 galaxy IC 5063 . Here the jet-ISM interaction has been `caught in action' by the high-spatial resolution observations that enabled the localisation of the cold-gas outflow which showed that radio jets could be the main driver of the outflow. However, in this case, the optical AGN is powerful and hence it is not possible to completely exclude the effect of the strong nuclear radiation on the outflow.

sources.summary: The paper reports extended high-ionization gas outflow in IC 5063, where coronal-line emission reaches up to 1.2 kpc and likely traces jet-inflated shells.

quotes.quote: We report the first characterization of an extended outflow of high ionized gas in the Circinus Galaxy and in IC 5063 (Figure fig:collage ) by means of the CLs [ Fe vii ] 6087 \ and [ Fe x ] 6375 . In both cases, the [ Fe vii ] emission is located within the ionization cone already detected in the [ O iii ] 5007 \ line and is found to extend up to a distance of 700 pc (Circinus) and 1.2 kpc (IC 5063) from the centre. Correspondingly, the [ Fe x ] 6375 \ emission extends up to 400 pc (Circinus) and 700 pc (IC 5063).

gmw_00000111.name: III Zw 35N
gmw_00000111.overview: III Zw 35N is a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0507436, arXiv:1810.02371] that forms a closely interacting double system with a southern companion [arXiv:0803.1832, arXiv:1810.02371]. The galaxy hosts a composite starburst and active nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770] containing a clumpy, rotating circumnuclear ring that produces OH megamaser emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0507436, arXiv:0801.2937].

sources.summary: The paper models IIIZw35 as a luminous infrared galaxy whose OH megamaser emission arises in a narrow, clumpy, rotating circumnuclear starburst ring rather than separate compact and diffuse maser phases.

quotes.quote: IIIZw35 is a Luminous Infra-red Galaxy ( L_ FIR = 11.3 ) at a distance of 110 Mpc (assuming H _0 = 75 Mpc ^ 1 , ( , hereafter C90)) which gives a linear scale of 0.5 pc mas ^ 1 . From optical spectroscopy it has been classified as a borderline LINER/Seyfert 2 (C90). The galaxy lies on the well known radio-FIR correlation and both the radio continuum and IR emission are consistent with being powered by a starburst with a star formation rate (SFR) of 19 yr ^ 1 (P01). Both the 1667 MHz and weaker 1665 MHz megamaser emission were discovered by .

Overall Geometry In this paper we fit the IIIZw35 observations by an inclined axisymmetric model in which both OH clouds and continuum emission coexist within circumnuclear rings (see Figure fi:figure2 ). To reconcile such a symmetric geometry with the observed East-West asymmetry in both line and continuum emission (see Figure fi:figure1 a) our model also includes a bi-cone of free-free absorption which covers the eastern side of the source. This obscuration defines the maser ring orientation requiring the eastern side to be the most distant.
In contrast, the mechanism discussed in this paper explains both types of maser structures using a single phase of low opacity clouds within a thin circumnuclear ring.

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS F01417 + 1651 as III Zw 35, a double Seyfert 2 LIRG with OH megamaser emission and a clumpy rotating starburst ring magnetic-field interpretation.

quotes.quote: IRAS F01417 + 1651 : This LIRG is most commonly known as III Zw 35 III Zw 35 and has an optical heliocentric redshift of z = 0.0274 . It is a double galaxy system and is classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy. staveley-smithccpu87 present a single-dish spectrum from the Jodrell Bank Mk1A 76 m telescope showing emission from the 1667 MHz transition at a velocity of 8262 km s ^ 1 with a peak flux density of 240 mJy and a total velocity extent of 270 km s ^ 1 at the 10 flux density level. The line profile can easily be seen to have at least three components.

Both the Gaussian and B( ) analyses suggest that an azimuthal magnetic field is embedded within this starburst ring such that the field points towards us at the southernmost tangent point and away from us at the northernmost tangent point. parracep05 estimate that the OHM clouds would be magnetically confined by a magnetic field of order 10 mG.

sources.summary: The paper reobserves IRAS F01417+1651/III Zw 35, finding a brighter 1667 MHz feature but unchanged Stokes V behavior, Gaussian magnetic-field fits, and field reversal.

quotes.quote: IRAS F01417+1651 (III Zw 35): The shape of features in the Stokes I spectrum remain similar in the more recent observations, as shown in Figure fig:01417 , but the entire 1667 MHz feature appears to be moderately brighter in 2008--2009 than it was in 2006. The peak flux density feature, located at 1622.8 MHz, brightened by 40 mJy, a fractional change of 8 . There are no clear changes between the old and more recent Stokes V spectra.

The individual Gaussian components used for fitting the new spectrum, and the magnetic field values associated with each, are in good agreement with those of R08. In addition, the same field reversal is observed. Despite brightening, the structure of the OHM in IRAS F01417+1651 appears relatively unchanged.

sources.summary: The paper models IIIZw35 as a starburst galaxy with OH megamaser emission from clumpy unsaturated gas in a thin ring, requiring external continuum seed photons and free-free absorption.

quotes.quote: We present a model for the OH megamaser emission in the starburst galaxy IIIZw35. The observed diffuse and compact OH maser components in this source are explained by a single phase of unsaturated clumpy gas distributed in a thin ring structure and amplifying background continuum. We emphasize the importance of clumpiness in the OH masing medium, an effect that has not been fully appreciated previously. The model explains why multiple bright spots are seen only at the ring tangents while smoother emission is found elsewhere.

Both the observed velocity gradients and the line to continuum ratios around the ring enquire a geometry where most of the seed photons come from a continuum emission which lies outside the OH ring. To explain both the OH and continuum brightness, free-free absorbing gas is required along the ring axis to partially absorb the far side of the ring.

sources.summary: The paper describes IIIZw35 as an OH megamaser whose diffuse and compact emission are explained by a rotating clumpy maser ring, with compact emission at tangent points of greatest overlap.

quotes.quote: pihl observed IIIZw35 using intermediate and high resolution, and detected both diffuse and compact emission. They proposed a model based on a clumpy maser medium located in a rotating ring. This model was extensively improved by parra05 , who used Monte Carlo simulations to account for the overlap of individual maser clouds. The clumpy maser model gives results that are in excellent agreement with the observed spectra and with interferometric observations on all angular scales. The compact maser emission arises at the tangents to the ring where cloud overlap is greatest in both position and velocity. The model of IIIZw35 finds best agreement with observations using maser clouds having a size \ .7 pc, magnitude of (1667 ) \ 1.5 and velocity dispersion of \ 20 .

sources.summary: III Zw 035 is an unresolved double system in which radio, FIR, and IRAC evidence make the northern component dominant and responsible for the assigned IRAS flux.

quotes.quote: [ 64 ] III Zw 035 : This closely interacting double system is completely unresolved in both Herschel and MIPS images used to derive the contribution of each galaxy into the IRAS source, as shown in Table table:IRfrac . However, ChaSta1990 mention that the majority of the radio continuum (and also probably FIR) emission originates in the northern galaxy. High angular resolution radio continuum observations from BarLer2017 indicate the northern component is the most compact source of the brightest and closest ULIRGs from the GOALS sample, while they don't detect the southern component at 33 GHz. Also, when considering IRAC channel 1 to 4 images (at 3.6, 4.5 ,5.8 and 8.0 m ), it is possible to see that the northern source clearly dominates and the southern source fades with increasing wavelength. Thus, we assign a contribution to the IRAS flux of 100 to the northern source.

sources.summary: The paper presents III Zw 35/IRAS F01417+1651 as an OH megamaser whose compact emission, ring geometry, and clumpy molecular-gas modeling support radiative pumping plus geometric effects.

quotes.quote: Two more OHMs, III Zw 35 and IRAS F17207--0014, were observed by Diamond1999 to have compact maser emission without corresponding compact background radio continuum features.

Observations of III Zw 35 by Pihlstrom2001 found that the OH emission occurred in a ring, and concluded that radiative pumping and geometric effects could together explain the diffuse and compact maser emission. Parra2005a performed more detailed modeling of III Zw 35 that validated the results of Pihlstrom2001 , and they further suggested that such a model seemed to qualitatively explain other OHMs.

The model assumptions in LE08 drew upon the conclusions of Parra2005a , who performed detailed modeling of the emission in IRAS F01417+1651, and concluded that the source could be well explained by a clumpy ring of molecular gas in which masing clouds have typical sizes of 1 pc, densities of 10^4 , and turbulent line widths of 20 .

sources.summary: The paper describes III Zw 35 as a compact OH megamaser source modeled as a clumpy ring, similar to IRAS F17207--0014, with evidence for magnetic field reversal across its torus.

quotes.quote: Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) observations of three nearby OHMs reveal emission on scales of 1--10 pc (Arp 220: Lonsdale1998 ; III Zw 35: Diamond1999, Pihlstrom2001 ; IRAS F17207--0014: Diamond1999, Momjian2006 ).

However, Parra2005a geometrically modelled III Zw 35, a source with compact features, and explained its emission in terms of a clumpy ring, in which masing clouds had typical densities 3 10^3 . Momjian2006 observed IRAS F17207--0014 and found masing characteristics with remarkable similarity to those of III Zw 35, and they suggested that a model similar to that of Parra2005a could explain the emission they observed.
In III Zw 35, for which we find / 3 , Robishaw2008 saw evidence for a magnetic field reversal on opposite sides of the R 20 pc torus in which masing occurs.

aliases_in_paper: IRAS 01418+1651 III Zw 35 is a close pair of galaxies (10 \ separation, PA 20 ). The northern galaxy,
sources.summary: The atlas gives III Zw 35N's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: III Zw 35N, is an infrared-luminous galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0277 ( D 121 Mpc) hosting an AGN, and a OH mega-maser chapman_oh_1986,chapman_combined_1990 . The AGN is optically classified either as Sy 2 veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 or LINER veilleux_optical_1995 or as borderline Sy 2/H II yuan_role_2010 . Intense star formation appears to be present in III Zw 35N pihlstrom_evn_2001,hattori_study_2004 , and thus we treat this object as AGN/starburst composite.

sources.summary: The paper classifies IRAS F01417+1651 as a starburst-like compact radio source, arguing its brightness temperature is below the AGN threshold and edge inversions are not significant.

quotes.quote: IRAS F01417+1651 This is a COM radio source with flat range of values and we classify it as a SB. To be an AGN, the brightness temperature should be above 10 ^ 5 10 ^ 6 K , but this object has a value of 3 10^ 4 K. Note: the inverted values of are located at the edges of the maps, and because the errors are of the order of 0.2, we don't take them into consideration.

sources.summary: III Zw 035 is a LIRG with strong minor-axis emission, non-Keplerian kinematics, enhanced velocity dispersion, and a resolved shock-excited molecular outflow.

quotes.quote: III Zw 035 III Zw 035 is a LIRG that shows strong extension in emission along the minor axis of the galaxy coupled with non-Keplerian kinematics and enhanced velocity dispersion in the same region. The 100mas scale data show a hint of this extension in the maps, but the molecular outflow is best resolved in the 35mas scale maps in Figure fig:6panel . The peak is not only displaced from that of the K band continuum, but is shown to be shock-excited in a coherent region in the / map.

gmw_00000112.name: IRAS 00188-0856
gmw_00000112.overview: IRAS 00188-0856 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:0802.1666, arXiv:1310.2770] classified optically as a LINER or an AGN-starburst composite [arXiv:1310.2770]. Although its optical morphology is undisturbed and shows no traces of merger activity [arXiv:2101.12058], it exhibits strong water ice absorption [arXiv:0802.1666] and radio properties that suggest the presence of an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2101.12058].

sources.summary: The paper uses IRAS 00188-0856 as a well-confirmed ULIRG ice-absorption case and as a template for modeling far-infrared ice emission effects on submillimeter redshift estimates.

quotes.quote: The spectrum of Arp 220 may be consistent with a contribution of 12.5 m libration mode absorption, supportive of the identification of ice absorption in this source but, until recently ( idm ; guido ; masa06 ), the only well confirmed case of ice absorption among ULIRGs in their sample was that of IRAS 00188-0856 where ice absorption at 3 m confirms the identification ( masa ). See for further well confirmed ice identifications. To illustrate this we present Fig. 1 which employs a slightly detailed model to explain the absorption features observed between 2.7 and 12.4 m.

It has not been possible to fit the entire 3 m absorption without using ice. Thus, the absorption at 6 m also is partly attributed to water ice. With these data, 12.5 m water ice absorption, while included in the model, does not seem to be required so that one may conclude that two features, and particularly the degeneracy breaking short wavelength edge of the 3 m absorption contribute to the fairly robust identification of ice in IRAS 00188-0856.

sources.summary: The paper highlights IRAS 00188 0856 as optically undisturbed but radio-AGN-like, with a large nonthermal fraction, high-frequency spectral break, and no optical merger traces.

quotes.quote: IRAS 00188 0856, whose optical morphology is not disturbed, shows a large nonthermal fraction and a spectral break at high frequency, which can be explained by synchrotron ageing of nonthermal plasma emitted from AGNs.

In particular, large nonthermal fractions (Figure fig:spectra ) are found in IRAS 00091 0738 and IRAS 00188 0856, which are probable candidates for sources with radio AGNs. Although at 30 GHz the presence of a predominant thermal component is found for typical ULIRGs 2017ApJ...843..117B , the AGN candidates reported here might suggest a significant contribution of a nonthermal component at the frequency. Therefore, as suggested by 2011ApJ...739L..25L , the detection of spectral breaks can still be a way to distinguish AGNs from starbursts. We note that, while an instantaneous injection model is preferred if multifrequency images with the sufficiently high spatial resolution are available e.g. 2006ApJ...648..148N , a continuous injection model is supposed for unresolved sources, whose break does not disappear even if several components of different spectral ages are superposed e.g. 1999A&A...345..769M,2003PASA...20...19M . In addition to the above, some authors have made other arguments. 2010MNRAS.405..887C has proposed that the ionisation loss that gradually flattens radio spectra at lower frequencies explained the spectral break 2006ApJ...645..186T . Nevertheless, some of their sample at the same time show a highly steep spectral index of < -1 at high frequency 2008A&A...477...95C , which still requires the synchrotron ageing of nonthermal plasma as found in AGNs. On another hand, a consideration made by 2013ApJ...777...58M has not only explained the spectral break but also the highly steep spectral index. As referred in Section sec:sf , they have reported that as the merger stage of merging galaxy pairs progresses radio bridges and tidal tails of ongoing mergers start to show a steep spectral index at high frequency, which is caused by rapid synchrotron ageing in a magnetised medium produced by the dynamical interaction. This scenario could be the case for IRAS 00091 0738 and IRAS 01298 0744, whose tidal tails are optically confirmed by 2002ApJS..143..315V . In contrast, the optical image of IRAS 00188 0856 provided by the same authors does not show any signs of tidal tails or disturbed morphology. In contrast, the optical image of IRAS 00188 0856 provided by the same authors does not show any signs of tidal tails or disturbed morphology. We do not find any traces of merger activity at least in the optical image and thus another explanation like an AGN is still considerable for IRAS 00188 0856.

sources.summary: The atlas gives IRAS 00188-0856's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: IRAS 00188-0856 is an ultra-luminous infrared galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.1284 ( D 574 Mpc) with an optical nuclear classification as a LINER kim_optical_1995,veilleux_optical_1995,veilleux_new_1999 or as borderline Sy 2/starburst yuan_role_2010 . Here, we treat it as AGN/starburst composite. It was observed with /IRAC and IRS and appears nearly unresolved in all corresponding images.

gmw_00000113.name: IRAS 01003-2238
gmw_00000113.overview: IRAS 01003-2238 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770] with conflicting classifications, including a starburst-dominated H II region [arXiv:1103.3212] and an active galactic nucleus (AGN) [arXiv:0907.4370] hosting a Seyfert 2 nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770]. The galaxy exhibits 100-kpc scale extended radio emission [arXiv:2101.12058] and has undergone a major optical flare interpreted as a candidate tidal disruption event [arXiv:2104.08317].

sources.summary: IRAS 01003 2238 has ambiguous optical classification, modest mid-IR absorption, soft X-rays consistent with starburst emission, and no direct AGN detection below 10 keV.

quotes.quote: IRAS 01003 2238 This source is usually classified as H ii region in the optical (Veilleux et al. 1999), yet there are also some conflicting claims about its possible Seyfert 2 nature (Allen et al. 1991; Yuan, Kewley Sanders 2010). Although detected, absorption features in the mid-IR are not dramatic in both depth and shape, and PAH emission is clearly distinguished on top of a very steep continuum. The X-ray spectrum is described with a power law plus a mekal component, yielding a C stat/d.o.f. = 21.3/35 . The spectrum is very soft (Fig. f3 ), and is characterized by a mekal temperature of kT = 0.66^ +0.31 _ 0.21 keV and a power law photon index of 2.9^ +0.4 _ 0.3 , suggesting again a sole SB origin. The observed flux is broadly consistent with such interpretation ( 2 10^ 14 and 5 10^ 15 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 in the 0.5--2 and 2--10 keV energy range).

An upper limit to the 2--10 keV AGN contribution has been computed as above, resulting in 1.3 10^ 14 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 . Hence, similar considerations can be drawn in this case: no direct AGN emission is detected below 10 keV, hinting at a Compton-thick circumnuclear environment. The lack of any AGN signature even in the form of a reflected spectrum implies a complete covering of the X-ray absorber.

sources.summary: The paper studies ULIRG F01004-2237 after a 2010 flare, interpreting 2018 emission-line variability as a compact outflow light echo and debating TDE versus AGN origins.

quotes.quote: Here we present a VLT/Xshooter spectrum of the nucleus of the ULIRG F01004-2237 that was taken in August 2018, about 8 yr after a major flare in its integrated optical emission. We interpret these results in terms of a light echo from the outflowing warm gas: the direct emission from the flaring event is continuing to fade, but due to light travel time effects we are only now observing the impact of the flare on the emission from the extended outflow region. Unless the outflow is confined to a small range of angles close to our line of sight, these observations imply that the outflow must be relatively compact ( < 50 pc). In terms of the nature of the flare event, we speculate that the properties of the F01004-2237 flare may be the result of a tidal disruption event (TDE) occurring in an object with pre-existing AGN activity.

Based on the detection of this flare and the strong He emission lines, which are sometimes observed in TDEs, we identified F01004-2237 as a TDE tadhunter17 . In particular, whereas in most TDE candidates the TDE-related features fade away and become invisible on a timescale of 1 yr or less, in F01004-2237 the broad He features were still present in our 2015 spectrum, approximately 5 yr after the original flare. Recently, trakhtenbrot19 have proposed an alternative explanation for the variability of F01004-2237 in terms of AGN activity. Here we present the analysis of a deep VLT/Xshooter spectrum of F01004-2237 taken in August 2018 -- about 8 yr after the original flare -- that has a direct bearing on our understanding of both its AGN outflows, and the nature of its flaring activity.

sources.summary: The paper reports a mid-infrared echo from a TDE candidate in ULIRG F01004-2237, finding delayed WISE brightening, high MIR luminosity, a parsec-scale dust torus, and large warm-dust mass.

quotes.quote: We present the mid-infrared (MIR) light curves (LCs) of a tidal disruption event (TDE) candidate in the center of a nearby ultraluminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG) F01004-2237 using archival WISE and NEOWISE data from 2010 to 2016. At the peak of the optical flare, F01004-2237 was IR quiescent. About three years later, its MIR fluxes have shown a steady increase, rising by 1.34 and 1.04 mag in 3.4 and 4.6 m up to the end of 2016. The host-subtracted MIR peak luminosity is 2-3 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 .

Fitting the MIR LCs using our dust model, we infer a dust torus of the size of a few parsecs at some inclined angle. The derived dust temperatures range from 590-850 K, and the warm dust mass is 7 M_ . Such a large mass implies that the dust cannot be newly formed.

sources.summary: The paper monitors F01004 2237, an AGN-hosting ULIRG, and argues its evolving helium lines remain consistent with a candidate tidal disruption event.

quotes.quote: We present results of spectroscopic monitoring observations of the Ultra-Luminous Infra Red Galaxy F01004 2237. This galaxy was observed to undergo changes in its optical spectrum, detected by comparing a spectrum from 2015 with one from 2000. These changes were coincident with photometric brightening. The main changes detected in the optical spectrum are enhanced \ 4686 emission and the appearance of \ 3898, 5876 emission lines.

The favoured interpretation of these changes was that of a tidal disruption event (TDE) happening in 2010. However, subsequent work suggested that these changes are caused by another hitherto unknown reason related to variations in the accretion rate in the active galactic nucleus (AGN). Our optical spectroscopic monitoring observations show that the evolution of the He lines is in line with the evolution seen in TDEs and opposite of what observed from reverberation mapping studies of AGNs, renewing the discussion on the interpretation of the flare as a TDE.

sources.summary: The paper notes conflicting classifications for IRAS 01003--2238, but its continuum-dominated Spitzer spectrum and weak PAH contribution imply an AGN-dominated ULIRG despite non-detections of high-ionization tracers.

quotes.quote: For the fourth galaxy, IRAS 01003--2238, conflicting classifications exist. Several studies have concluded that this galaxy may be starburst-powered armus88,rigopoulou99,lutz99,wilman99 , whereas classify this galaxy as Seyfert-II and refer to it as a QSO. Its Spitzer-IRS spectrum is continuum-dominated class 1A; spoon07 with a 6.2 m PAH equivalent width more than ten times lower than for starburst galaxies. From the PAH luminosity we calculate a contribution of the starburst to the infrared luminosity of 10--20 peeters04 . This would imply that IRAS 01003--2238 is AGN-dominated The non-detection of the 14.32 m [ Ne v ] line and of the near-infrared AGN tracer 1.962 m [ Si vi ] veilleux97,dannerbauer05 is not exceptional among AGN-dominated ULIRGs.

sources.summary: The paper reports 100 kpc extended radio emission around IRAS 01004 2237, with two-sided morphology, no extended X-rays, and a structure suggestive of AGN activity.

quotes.quote: In addition, we find 100-kpc scale extended emission associated with IRAS 01004 2237. The two-sided morphology and absence of extended X-ray emission suggest that this system is not induced by a merger in a cluster but originates from AGN activity.

IRAS 01004 2237 shows extended features across 50 arcsec, which corresponds to the projected linear size of 100 kpc (Figure fig:im-IRAS01004 ). A few arcseconds away from the northeast component, we find a faint point source in an optical image provided by the Pan-STARRS 2016arXiv161205560C whose colour suggests a galaxy at 0.6<z<1.0 cf. 2015ApJ...803..105P , while the southwest one has no counterpart, which should be associated with IRAS 01004 2237 itself.

sources.summary: The atlas gives IRAS 01003-2238's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: IRAS 01003-2238 is classified as an ultra-luminous infrared galaxy and a Wolf-Rayet galaxy indicating a recent starburst armus_detection_1988 . It has a redshift of z= 0.1178 ( D 523 Mpc) and hosts a Sy 2 nucleus, which appears to dominate over the starburst yuan_role_2010 . IRAS 01003-2238 was observed with /IRAC and IRS and appears nearly unresolved in the IRAC 5.8 m image.

gmw_00000114.name: IRAS 04103-2838
gmw_00000114.overview: IRAS 04103-2838 is a disturbed ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770] at a redshift of z=0.1175 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It is optically classified as a LINER [arXiv:0907.1236, arXiv:1310.2770] and hosts a Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0907.1236, arXiv:1310.2770] that contributes approximately six percent of its bolometric luminosity [arXiv:0907.1236].

sources.summary: The paper classifies IRAS 04103 2838 as a warm LINER ULIRG with a 6 percent AGN contribution, Compton-thick iron K evidence, and a non-Seyfert Ne VI detection.

quotes.quote: IRAS 04103 2838 is optically classified as a LINER and is a warm ULIRG according to its IRAS colours ( f_ 25 /f_ 60 =0.30 ). We are able to unveil an AGN component at 5--8 m, whose contribution to the bolometric luminosity is estimated as 6 per cent. The presence of an AGN is confirmed by the detection of a broad K line at 6.4 keV, during a recent XMM-Newton observation (Teng et al. 2008). Due to the large equivalent width ( 1.65 keV) of this iron feature the AGN is supposed to be Compton-thick, anyway it does not seem to dominate the total energy output of the source.

Due to its ionization energy of 126 eV this feature is connected to the hardest nuclear activity, and indeed it is convincingly observed also in other three Seyfert galaxies (IRAS 05189 2524, IRAS 12072 0444 and IRAS 13454 2956) plus IRAS 04103 2838. Fluxes and equivalent widths are listed in Tab. t4 , and are in good agreement with the values provided by Veilleux et al. (2009) for their positive detections. We note that our identification of this line also in IRAS 04103 2838 represents the first detection involving a non-Seyfert ULIRG.

sources.summary: The atlas gives IRAS 04103-2838's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: IRAS 04103-2838 is a disturbed ultra-luminous infrared galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.1175 with an active nucleus classified optically either as H II or LINER veilleux_optical_1999,veilleux_new_1999 but also as Sy 2 yuan_role_2010 . The presence of a Compton-thick AGN was demonstrated through deep X-ray observations teng_xmm-newton_2008 , confirming the type II nature of this source. IRAS 04103-2838 was observed with /IRS in LR staring mode.

gmw_00000115.name: IRAS 05189-2524
gmw_00000115.overview: IRAS 05189-2524 is an advanced major merger [arXiv:1404.6470, arXiv:2202.04019] classified as an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:2202.04019, arXiv:2309.02776] and a Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1510.04453, arXiv:1310.2770]. The system features a compact core with curved tidal tails [arXiv:2202.04019] and hosts an active galactic nucleus that dominates its infrared emission [arXiv:1510.04453, arXiv:1612.08181].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 05189--2524 as an advanced merger with AGN-dominated infrared emission, bright ULIRG X-rays, and variable absorption changing its Compton-thick classification.

quotes.quote: IRAS 05189--2524 is an advanced--stage merger with a single nucleus. Optically classified as a Seyfert 2, near-infrared spectroscopy reveals the presence of a hidden broad line region via broad Paschen vei99a, vei99b . Continuum and emission-line diagnostics from Spitzer spectra indicate that the infrared luminosity of this source is dominated by an AGN vei09a . In the X-ray band, IRAS 05189--2524 is one of the brightest ULIRGs on the sky.

Historic XMM-Newton and Chandra data imply a 2--10 keV X-ray continuum with a luminosity of 10 ^ 43 erg s ^ 1 . The source was observed by Suzaku in 2006, at which time its 2--10 keV flux appears to have dropped by a factor of 30 and the Fe line became more prominent. Its 0.5--2 keV flux appeared unchanged. The target was undetected by Suzaku PIN teng09 , and those data were unable to distinguish whether this change in observed flux was due to a change in the intrinsic AGN luminosity or to a change in the thickness of the absorbing column.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 05189 2524 as an AGN-dominated late-stage merger/Seyfert with OH P-Cygni profiles, compact moderate-velocity outflow, and a more extended high-velocity component.

quotes.quote: IRAS 05189 2524 : This is an AGN-dominated ULIRG, with _ AGN ranging from 0.6 to 1 using the six diagnostics in . It has a single identifiable nucleus, and is proposed to be a late-stage merger system due to the observed tidal tails in the optical san88 . Its optical classification is Sy 2, but it shows broad ( 2000 ) emission near-IR lines (Pa , Pa , He) and is thus classified as Sy 1 in the near-IR sev00 . found from blueshifted [ Ne iii ] and [ Ne v ] mid-IR emission lines detected in this and other ULIRGs that the gas ionization increase with increasing blueshift. While the ground-state OH119 and OH79 profiles show well defined P-Cygni line shapes, the excited OH84 and OH65 profiles show strong absorption at systemic velocities (GA15).

At moderate blueshifted velocities ( 200 ), OH79 has an outflowing counterpart in both OH84 and OH65 (Fig. ciioh65prof ), indicating a compact component with N_ H 10^ 23 \ and momentum flux ( P _ out 1.5 10^ 35 dyn) somewhat lower than that provided by the AGN ( 3 10^ 35 dyn), suggesting a momentum driven shell (Figs. testradpw and pboost b). OH119 shows a flat absorption profile between 200 and 600 \ at 10 level, indicating that the high-velocity gas, not detected in OH84 or OH65, is more extended and with a low covering factor. With 340 pc, the high-velocity component can be roughly explained with N_ H 4 10^ 22 , yielding P _ out 5 10^ 35 dyn and thus requiring moderate momentum boost.

sources.summary: The paper classifies IRAS F05189 2524 as a ULIRG and advanced major-merger product with a compact core, curved tails, high-spin SMBH evidence, and a bright WISE transient.

quotes.quote: Transient in IRAS F05189 2524 IRAS F05189 2524 has coordinates RA= 05^ h 23^ m 03^ s .936 , Dec= 25^ 19 00 .08 and is located at a distance of 180.9 12.7 Mpc. Assuming this distance, the galaxy has log(L _ IR /L _ )= 12.17 and is therefore classified as a ULIRG. The morphology of the galaxy, showing a compact core along with two curved tail structures, indicates an advanced major merger product Sanders1988,Veilleux2002 .

There is evidence from the X-ray spectrum of the galaxy that the SMBH has a high spin, with some of the models presented in Xu2017 finding a dimensional spin parameter > 0.9, although others favour a lower value. The WISE light curves for this galaxy show that after a period of decline, a bright transient occurred between MJD 57080.6 and 57275.9 (27 February 2015 to 10 September 2015). The transient peaked on MJD 57441.0, implying a rise time of 0.5-1 year. The transient declines relatively rapidly for a year, and then more slowly. Recent observations show flux densities close to the level observed immediately before the outburst, although with some small fluctuations, implying the galaxy nucleus may have returned to a `quiescent' level after 4.5 years.

sources.summary: SMART fits IRAS 05189-2524 as a warm ULIRG with an edge-on obscured AGN, polar dust, and evidence for a recent dust-obscured tidal disruption event.

quotes.quote: IRAS 05189-2524 is generally believed to be a `warm' ULIRG and to contain an obscured AGN with its torus viewed almost edge-on. This galaxy was recognized from the mid-90s as a ULIRG that has similar characteristics to the prototypical Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068. It was found in particular by to show broad lines in polarized flux. found that a fit with polar dust provides a better fit to the SED of IRAS 05189-2524 compared to a fit without polar dust. found evidence that IRAS 05189-2524 recently experienced a dust obscured tidal disruption event (TDE) with an IR echo from polar dust.
We find that a fit with SMART with an obscured AGN and polar dust provides a good fit to the SED. We find that the polar dust in IRAS 05189-2524 has a temperature of 1050.6^ 28.7 _ 23.2 K.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS F05189-2524 as a mid-infrared AGN and likely Seyfert, but says its radio map appears starburst-like despite possible young-AGN or jet-forming emission.

quotes.quote: IRAS F05189-2524 This object has a [ NeV ] detection making it a mid-IR AGN. Similarly the 6.2 m PAH EQW suggests an AGN. The optical classification suggests a Seyfert galaxy, probably of type 2.

Based on the map and the histogram, this is a radio-SB, since the map gives values of that are flat. The SB is also suggested by it's brightness temperature ( 2 10^ 4 K; Table tab:Tb ). The map in this case does not help us identify the AGN in this ULIRG, although it could be a young AGN and we could be seeing emission from a jet that is being formed.

sources.summary: The paper models IRAS 05189-2524 with absorbed power-law and thermal X-ray components, finds no iron line, and interprets the hard spectrum as a Compton-thin type 2 AGN.

quotes.quote: IRAS 05189-2524: This source was observed by ASCA for 40000 seconds. The spectrum is well fitted by a two-component model, consisting of a powerlaw with photon index = 1.89^ +0.35 _ 0.34 absorbed by a column density of N_H = 4.7^ +1.4 _ 1.1 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 , and a thermal component with kT=0.88 ^ +0.89 _ 0.35 . The iron line is not detected.

From the non-detection we estimate an upper limit to the equivalent width of 235 eV. The measured 2-10 keV flux is 5.3 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . In summary, the E >2 keV spectrum is typical of a Compton--thin type 2 AGN, while at lower energies an extra thermal component is present, that can be either associated to the AGN itself or due to starburst activity.

sources.summary: The paper contrasts IRAS 05189-2524 with IRAS 17208-0014, describing it as a cold ULIRG whose low CO excitation resembles quiescent Milky Way molecular clouds and could be missed in high-J CO searches.

quotes.quote: The two ULIRGs ( L_ IR > 10^ 12 L_ ) IRAS 17208-0014 and IRAS 05189-2524 have been found to have very different CO line excitation (Figure 4) despite similar IR continuum and CO J=1--0 line luminosities and similar S_ 60 m /S_ 100 m ratios. For IRAS 17208-0014 the CO brightness temperature ratios measured are typical for the high-excitation star forming molecular gas found in many such galaxies, but those found for the ``cold'' IRAS 05189-2524 are surprisingly typical of the quiescent molecular clouds found in the Milky Way disk (Table 2).

The CO emission in IRAS 05189-2524 on the other hand is compatible with gas of n 100 cm^ 3 , T_k 15 K , and K_ vir 1 , typical of the quiescent and mostly self-gravitating molecular clouds found in the Galactic disk immersed in a FUV radiation field of G_ 1 (Habing units). In such conditions high-J CO lines are expected to be very faint with CO(J+1-J)/(1-0) 0.01 for J+1 4 for the bulk of the molecular gas. This is much lower than that expected from CO SLEDs of other ULIRGs such as Mrk 231 or Arp 220 or indeed most high redshift objects ( ), with only the extremely red object HR 10 coming close (Figure 3). Thus starburst galaxies at high resdshifts with similar CO SLEDs to that of IRAS 05189-2524 would remain undetected by typical high-J CO observations with today's radio telescopes, even after scaling their CO line fluxes by their 10 times higher IR luminosities.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 05189-2524 as a compact late-stage merger and type 2 Seyfert whose L-band spectrum indicates an unobscured AGN plus starburst activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS 05189-2524 IRAS 05189-2524 has a compact structure, typical of a final merging stage. Only a single nucleus is visible in the optical images, and the only evidence for merging is a faint tidal tail (Veilleux, Kim Sanders 2002). According to optical spectroscopic data, IRAS 05189-2524 has been classified as a type 2 Seyfert galaxy (Veilleux et al. 1999).

In our sample we can distinguish nuclei with an unobscured AGN (low EW_ 3.3 , flat continuum and absence of strong absorption) in IRAS 05189-2524, IRAS 23060+0505 and the brighter nucleus of NGC 6240, and sources with a heavily absorbed AGN (significant EW_ 3.3 , steep inverted continuum with strong dust and gas absorptions) in IRAS 19254-7245, IRAS 20551-4250 and the fainter nucleus of NGC 6240. The detection of PAH emission line points to the presence of starburst activity in all sources.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 05189 2524 as an ultraluminous infrared merger and reports strong diffuse mm-wave emission around its central peak.

quotes.quote: 26: IRAS 05189 2524 This is an ultraluminous IR galaxy and is considered to be in the final stage of a galaxy merger e.g., ric17b,Ric21b . Around the central peak emission, diffuse emission within a radius of 0 1 is revealed at a resolution of 0 04. The encompassing area contains emission of 4 mJy, of which 1.3 mJy beam ^ 1 is ascribed to the peak emission. Thus, the contribution of the diffuse component is strong. Although there were few images taken at comparable resolutions, to our knowledge, NIR (2.2 m and 3.8 m) images at resolutions of 0 1--0 2 were obtained by , who utilized a camera of Subaru/IRCS in an adaptive-optics mode.

sources.summary: The paper says the BeppoSAX spectrum of IRAS 05189-2524 does not require reflection; model B agrees with prior work, though poor statistics leave a 20 keV excess uncertain.

quotes.quote: IRAS 05189-2524: The BeppoSAX observation of this source was analyzed in detail by. Severgnini et al. 2000 The reflected component is not required from the fit ( ^2=2 with one more free parameter). The fit obtained with model B is in agreement with that of Severgnini et al. (2000). The poor statistics ( ^2_ =1.2) is mainly due to an excess in the PDS data around 20 keV. The statistics is however not good enough to understand whether this excess is real or not.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 05189 2524 as an extreme ULIRG and summarizes its morphology, infrared/AGN indicators, and role among high FIR-to-CO systems in the local ULIRG sample.

quotes.quote: IRAS 05189 2524. The optical image of this z=0.043 ULIRG suggests that it is in a late stage of a merger with a highly obscured, compact single nucleus farrah03 . It is one of the six ULIRGs in the IRAS Bright Galaxy Sample with the warmest f_ 25 /f_ 60 color sanders88a . Presence of both an obscured AGN young96 and a buried starburst dudley99 is suggested by a Sy2 spectrum veilleux95 and an X-ray spectrum characteristic of a Compton thin AGN and a thermal component risaliti00,lutz04 . By modeling the observed SED, farrah03 conclude that >30 of the total IR luminosity is contributed by the AGN.

sources.summary: IRASF 05189--2524 is identified as the AGN-dominated member of the Seyfert LIRG sample, an advanced major merger whose SINFONI emission is unresolved within the aperture.

quotes.quote: All of them host an active nucleus (AGN), although the AGN dominates the energy output of the galaxy only for IRASF 05189--2524.
Three of the galaxies (NGC 34, IRASF 05189--2524, and UGC 05101) are advanced major mergers.

The SINFONI FoV for the IRASF 05189-2524 observations is 3 3 \ (2.5 2.5 kpc). This source is unresolved in the SINFONI data (FWHM 0.3 ), and therefore we expect that most of the flux is included in our measurements.

sources.summary: The paper includes IRAS 05189+2524 in its QSO wind comparison, estimating black-hole mass, bolometric luminosity, UFO properties, and global CO/OH/Na outflow energetics.

quotes.quote: IRAS 05189+2524 . We estimate the black hole mass from the M_ BH relation KH13 , using as a range the two values of determined by 137 Dasyra+06 and 265 Rothberg+13 . L_ BOL is taken from Veilleux+13 , while the UFO velocity and column density from Smith+19 . The galaxy-scale outflow is seen via different tracers: CO Lutz+20 , OH Gonzalez-Alfonso+17 and Na Rupke+17 . The OH and Na phases dominate the energetics, and we sum them for the purpose of determining the global outflow properties.

sources.summary: The paper classifies IRAS F05189-2524 as lower-excitation in its data, notes prior high-J excitation, and cites shocks as important for its higher-J CO excitation conditions.

quotes.quote: This effect is seen in IRAS F05189-2524, IRAS F12112+0305, IRAS 17208-0014, IRAS F20551-4250, and IRAS F23128-5919.

And indeed, some of the sources belonging to the `lower-excitation' group under our criteria, have been observed to have high-excitation CO SLEDs when probing CO( J>7 ) lines (e.g., IRAS 09022-3615, IRAS F05189-2524, IRAS 17208-0014) . showed that, while there are sources for which a single-component CO SLED fit is sufficient, the CO ladder of IRAS 09022-3615 (classified as a warm ULIRG in their study, and included also in our work) requires three excitation components in order to reproduce the observed CO fluxes up to J_ up =13 : (i) a cold (47 K) component peaking at J_ up 3 , (ii) a warm (205 K) component peaking at J_ up 6 , and (iii) a third hot (415 K) component that dominates at high- J transitions, peaking around J_ up 11 .

Sources IRAS 17208-0014 and IRAS F05189-2524 are classified as Class III objects in their study, while in this work they both lie in our lower-excitation group. According to , shocks play an important role in setting the higher- J CO excitation conditions of IRAS F05189-2524.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes X-ray spectra of F05189-2524, describing its best-fit components, Fe K emission or flux behavior, and comparison with previous modeling.

quotes.quote: F05189-2524 Since the spectral parameters were consistent between the XMM-Newton and Chandra observations, other than a lower constant multiplicative factor for the Chandra spectra, we fit these spectra simultaneously to constrain the 2 - 10 keV flux. However, we only report the flux from the Chandra observation as we have demonstrated that extended emission contaminates the XMM-Newton field of view. The spectra were best fit by a double absorbed power law plus a thermal component, though the temperature was not constrained.

sources.summary: The atlas gives IRAS 05189-2524's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: IRAS 05189-2524 is possibly a late stage merger system of two spirals sanders_ultraluminous_1988 and is classified as an ultra-luminous infrared galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0426 ( D 181 Mpc) hosting a Sy 2 nucleus veilleux_optical_1995 with polarized broad emission lines young_polarimetry_1996 . After its discovery with , this object was observed with Palomar 5 m carico_iras_1988 , MMT maiolino_new_1995 , klaas_infrared_2001,ramos_almeida_mid-infrared_2007 . The first high-angular resolution N band images were obtained with Keck/MIRLIN in 1998 soifer_high_2000 , Palomar 5 m/MIRLIN in 1999 gorjian_10_2004 and ESO 3.6 m/TIMMI2 in 2002 siebenmorgen_mid-infrared_2004 .

gmw_00000116.name: IRAS 08572+3915
gmw_00000116.overview: IRAS 08572+3915 is an interacting double-nucleus ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1510.04453, arXiv:1911.06326] hosting a heavily dust-obscured, energetically dominant active galactic nucleus in its northwestern core [arXiv:1612.08181, arXiv:2108.04838]. This system exhibits deep silicate absorption [arXiv:2211.07758], minimal polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon emission [arXiv:1806.05198], and fast molecular outflows [arXiv:1809.07906, arXiv:2101.11645].

sources.summary: IRAS 08572+3915 is presented as a double-nucleus ULIRG with AGN-dominated infrared output, weak X-ray detections, and extreme intrinsic X-ray weakness tied to outflows.

quotes.quote: IRAS 08572+3915 is a double nucleus ULIRG whose northwestern nucleus is thought to host an AGN. Its optical spectrum is intermediate between a LINER and Seyfert 2 vei95, vei99a . Continuum and emission-line diagnostics from Spitzer spectra indicate that the infrared luminosity of this source is dominated by an AGN vei09a . No significant X-ray detection of this source has been reported, but Chandra data show a detection of a few counts. Using the hardness ratio between the 0.5--2 and 2--8 keV Chandra bands and assuming a power-law spectrum with =1.8 , teng09 estimated a 0.5--10 keV flux of 3 10^ 14 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , corresponding to a luminosity of 2 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 .

This disagreement between infrared and X-ray diagnostics is particularly large for Mrk 231 and IRAS 08572+3915: while Spitzer diagnostics find that these two sources are heavily dominated by AGN, both are very underluminous in the X-rays. The ratio of intrinsic 2-10 keV luminosity to bolometric luminosity for Mrk 231 is only 0.03 , and the ratio for IRAS 08572+3915 is even lower (assuming no intrinsic absorption.) teng14 established that Mrk 231 is intrinsically X-ray weak, likely related to the powerful wind detected in this broad-absorption line (BAL) quasar.

sources.summary: The MOX paper describes IRAS F08572+3915 as a double-nucleus ULIRG, Compton-thick and X-ray weak, with low-count X-ray data and CO-detected molecular outflows.

quotes.quote: IRAS F08572+3915: This is a double nucleus ULIRG. The source has been identified as Compton thick 2010ApJ...725.1848T , and the previous studies have estimated an absorption column density of 10^ 25 . The powerlaw photon index =-0.43 is not constrained due to low counts. The authors classified this as a weak ULIRG. This source has also been studied by 2011A&A...529A.106I using observations.

Nustar hard X-ray studies were carried out by 2015ApJ...814...56T and the authors conclude that the source is X-ray weak and could not be detected in any of the energy bands. The molecular outflows in this source has been detected using IRAM PDBI telescope with the CO(1-0) emission line 2014A&A...562A..21C . In our analysis, we found that this source has very low counts and hence HR method was used to calculate the 2-10 luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper studies IRAS 08572+3915 NW, a dust-obscured ULIRG AGN, using CO rovibrational absorption to decompose torus components and infer clumpy inflowing/outflowing gas.

quotes.quote: The observation target in this work, which is IRAS 08572+3915 (hereafter referred to as IRAS08), is a ULIRG whose infrared luminosity is L(8 - 1000 m )/L_ =12.09 Sanders1988a . Its redshift determined by the CO ( J=1 0 ) emission line is z=0.0583 Evans2002 . IRAS08 has two NIR cores with a separation of 5 4=5.6 kpc Scoville2000 in the northwest (NW) and southeast (SE), and approximately 80 of mid-infrared luminosity comes from the NW core Soifer2000 .

In this work, we focus on the NW core. IRAS08 NW is classified as an AGN based on the lack of the polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon (PAH) emission feature at 3.3 m , and it is likely to be heavily dust obscured based on the absorption feature of the carbonaceous dust at 3.4 m Imanishi2000,Doi2019 and the silicate dust at 9.7 m of _ Si,9.7 5.2 Dudley1997 . Moreover, this AGN is expected to be Compton thick ( N_ H 10^ 24 cm^ 2 ) based on a small number of hard X-ray counts ( 10 ) and a very small ratio of the hard X-ray luminosity to the infrared luminosity Iwasawa2011 .

Hence, we performed herein the velocity decomposition of the CO rovibrational absorption lines in the ULIRG IRAS 08572+3915 NW with a higher-resolution spectrum ( R 10 , 000 ) than the previous studies to probe the dynamical and physical properties and the spatial distribution of each component. We report the results in this paper. This is the first velocity decomposition study of the CO rovibrational absorption lines in AGNs.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 08572+3915 as a deeply obscured, interacting local ULIRG where smooth torus models best reproduce its deep silicate absorption and AGN dominance.

quotes.quote: proposed that the ULIRG IRAS 08572+3915 is the most luminous IR galaxy in the local ( z<0.2 ) Universe. It is an example of an interacting ULIRG, which exhibits one of the deepest silicate absorption features observed in a galaxy. proposed that the deep silicate absorption feature is due to the fact that the AGN torus in this object is observed almost edge-on. The huge luminosity of this object, which approaches or exceeds 10^ 13 L_ , is due to the large anisotropy correction.

To explore further the properties of this system, we fit the SED with all four combinations of AGN radiative transfer models. It is interesting that the smooth models fit much better than the two-phase models. The last two in particular do not fit the deep silicate absorption feature of this galaxy and predict that the object is starburst-dominated. According to the fits of the smooth models, which perform much better, this is an AGN-dominated object.

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS 08572+3915 as a bright ULIRG and strong buried-AGN candidate with small silicate-depth ratios, weak PAH contamination, and supporting infrared absorption evidence.

quotes.quote: However, for IRAS 08572+3915, a bright ULIRG with a small _ 18 ' / _ 9.7 ' ratio (Table 4), spo06 independently found the signature of a strong dust temperature gradient, based on the fact that the observed optical depths of crystalline silicate dust absorption at _ rest 11 m, 16 m, 18.5 m, 23 m, and 28 m are systematically weaker at the longer wavelengths than expected (their Figure 2 bottom). A similar trend was found also in the average spectrum of ULIRGs with _ 9.7 ' > 2 (Spoon et al. 2006; their Figure 5). We thus believe that the small _ 18 ' / _ 9.7 ' ratios observed in several ULIRGs are most naturally explained by strong dust temperature gradients.

IRAS 08572+3915, 12127 1412, 17044+6720 show 3--4 m spectra with very small PAH contaminations. The _ 3.4 values are 0.8, 0.35, and 0.15 for IRAS 08572+3915, 12127 1412, 17044+6720, respectively idm06 . The _ 3.4 value reflects the column density of bare carbonaceous dust (without an ice mantle) pen94,ima96,raw03 . In the Galaxy, the _ 9.7 ' / _ 3.4 ratio is surprisingly similar in different directions ( _ 9.7 ' / _ 3.4 = 14--17; Roche Aitken 1984, 1985; Pendleton et al. 1994). Thus, a _ 9.7 ' / _ 3.4 ratio substantially smaller than 14--17 can be a signature of a strong dust temperature gradient.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 08572+3915 as an AGN-dominated double-nucleus source with compact OH outflow components, high-velocity absorption, and an OH65 P-Cygni profile requiring substantial momentum.

quotes.quote: IRAS 08572+3915 : This is an AGN-dominated double-nucleus source with CO detected only in the NW nucleus eva02 , and with no trace of PAH emission at 3.3 m ima08 . It shows strong absorption in the blue wings of OH119, OH79, and OH84 out to 1000 , and somewhat more tentatively in OH65. At blueshifted velocities, OH84 is almost as strong as OH119 (Fig. ohblue ), indicating a compact outflow. There are also hints of the presence of a non-outflowing (i.e., peaking at systemic velocities) component, but it is spectrally diluted by the prominent wings. It is the only source in our sample that shows a P-Cygni profile in the highly excited OH65 doublet.

Our best-fit model for the blueshifted wing requires 2 components for the outflow, though both are very compact with similar 110 pc. The lower velocity ( 200-800 ) component, with N_ H 1.5 10^ 23 , and the high velocity ( 800-1100 ) component, with N_ H 8 10^ 22 , both have similar P _ out (1.3-1.5) 10^ 36 dyn, about 3 times the momentum supplied by the dominant AGN and the starburst ( 5 10^ 35 dyn).

sources.summary: The paper identifies the main northwest component, gives its CO peak position, notes asymmetric westward emission, and measures a slightly elongated disk with CO flux and molecular gas mass.

quotes.quote: The main galaxy in the system (located in the north-west quadrant of Figure fig:hst_co ) has previously been observed and detected in CO(1-0) line emission rhc_solomon97,rhc_evans02,rhc_cicone14 , although with a sensitivity and spatial resolution poorer than the observations presented here. Figure fig:hst_co (left) shows the distribution of the CO emission on top an HST (F814W) image. An elliptical Gaussian fit to the uv table of the A+B+C+D observations gives the peak of emission at R.A. 09:00:25.38 and DEC. + 39:03:54.2. This position coincides within 0.1 of the radio center at 8.44 GHz found by rhc_condon91 . The galaxy's emission is not perfectly symmetric around this point, but is more extended towards the West.

The best elliptical Gaussian fit has an intrinsic major FWHM of 0.61 0.2 and an intrinsic minor FWHM of 0.54 0.2'' , corresponding to 0.74 by 0.65 kpc, with a P.A. of 10 10^ . The disk is thus slightly elongated towards the north/north-west, but because the length of the major and minor axes only differ by a little, the P.A. is not used here to constrain the orientation and inclination of the disk.

sources.summary: The paper models IRAS F08572+3915 as a dust-obscured ULIRG whose massive, fast molecular outflow can be reproduced by radiation pressure with AGN luminosity decay.

quotes.quote: We first compare the radiation pressure-driven outflow model with observations of a particular individual source: IRAS F08572+3915. This system is a key example of a local dust-obscured ultra-luminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG), with AGN luminosity of L 4 10^ 45 erg/s. A molecular outflow was previously detected in CO observations, with an average velocity of 800 km/s Cicone_et_2014 , and the corresponding outflow energetics are reported in Fluetsch_et_2019 . New high angular resolution CO observations spatially resolve the molecular outflow, which has an outflowing mass of M 3 10^8 M_ and reaches a velocity of v 1200 km/s around 1 kpc Herrera-Camus_et_2020 . The associated mass outflow rate is M 350 M_ /yr , while the outflow momentum rate and kinetic power are p 3 10^ 36 dyn and E _k 2 10^ 44 erg/s, respectively.

Here we examine whether the outflow energetics observed in IRAS F08572+3915 can be explained in terms of radiation pressure on dust. The available observational values of the source Herrera-Camus_et_2020 are adopted as input parameters of the radiation-driven outflow model.

sources.summary: The paper adds unpublished molecular-line data for IRAS 08572 + 3915, handles an oversized Cycle 0 beam by assuming compact J=4-3 emission, and finds hotter ALMA dense gas than older CO modeling.

quotes.quote: However, the J=4--3 data for HCN, HCO ^ + , and HNC for IRAS 06035 7102, and HNC J=4--3 data for IRAS 08572 + 3915 were not shown in the previous publications. For IRAS 08572 + 3915, the beam size of J=4--3 data taken in Cycle 0 is significantly larger than the adopted value (Table tab:beam ). However, we assume that a dominant fraction of the observed J=4--3 emission originates from the adopted beam, because of the following reasons. (1) The J=4--3 emission is generally more spatially compact than the lower J-transition lines owing to the higher critical density in the former shi15 . (2) Changing originally small beam-sized data to unnecessarily large beam sizes increases the noise in units of mJy beam ^ 1 , and thus the scatter of the spectral data becomes large.

For the three ULIRGs, IRAS 08572 + 3915, IRAS 12112 + 0305, and IRAS 22491 1808, molecular gas temperature was estimated to be 60--150 K, based on large beam-sized CO multiple J-transition line observations and RADEX modeling pap12a . Our ALMA HCN and HCO ^ + line study provides significantly higher temperature for nuclear molecular gas in all the three ULIRGs (see Figure fig:ULIRGfit and Table tab:bestfit ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 08572 as a low-redshift ULIRG with CO absorption, the clearest crystalline-silicate detection, deep silicate absorption, and no common PAH features.

quotes.quote: Recent groundbased high-resolution M-band spectroscopy reveals the CO band to be also present in the spectrum of the low-redshift ULIRG IRAS 08572 (Geballe et al., in preparation). We have detected significant substructure in the deep silicate absorption features towards a sample of strongly obscured ULIRG nuclei. The absorption features appear at 11, 16, 19, 23 and 28 m and are best revealed by a spline fit to the amorphous silicate absorption component. We identify the residuals with crystalline silicates, most likely forsterite (Mg _2 SiO _4 ).

The clearest detection is offered by the spectrum of IRAS 08572, shown in detail in figure fig:08572_panel . This spectrum combines the deepest silicate feature in our sample with the complete absence of all commonly present PAH emission features.

For a detailed study of the absorption features contributing to the 6 m absorption complex we refer to Keane et al. (in preparation) and for a full analysis of the origin of the hydrocarbon bands in the spectrum of IRAS 08572 to Pendleton et al. (in preparation).

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 08572+3915 as a heavily obscured ULIRG hosting a powerful AGN, with OH outflow terminal velocities above 1000 km/s like Mrk 231.

quotes.quote: For this first study of outflow signatures in our data we use a sub-sample that is mainly constrained by the observing schedule of Herschel, but that covers a broad range of AGN and starburst activity, including a starburst template (NGC 253), a cold, starburst dominated ULIRG (IRAS 17208-0014,) warm ULIRGs (S25/S60 > 0.1) and/or ULIRGs with strong AGN contributions (Mrk 231, IRAS 13120-5453, IRAS 14378-3651), and a heavily obscured ULIRG (IRAS 08572+3915), which hosts a powerful AGN (e.g. Veilleux et al. 2009, V09).

Figure Fig1 shows the (continuum normalized) OH 79 m line spectra for all objects. For NGC 253 we show the central spatial pixel only. The Mrk 231 spectrum is taken from Fischer et al. (2010) and is repeated here for completeness. In all cases we detect P-Cygni profiles typical of outflows, with blue shifted absorption and red shifted emission features, of more than 1000 km/s in some cases.
In significant contrast to this the terminal velocities of the OH outflow in the two AGN-dominated ULIRGs (Mrk 231 and IRAS 08572+3915) are well above 1000 km/s.

sources.summary: The paper confirms IRAS 08572+3915 as intrinsically hyperluminous, explains the higher luminosity through torus anisotropy, notes large outflows, and describes it as a deep-silicate ULIRG.

quotes.quote: With the more detailed analysis presented in this paper, which includes the addition of the emission of the host galaxy, we confirm the result obtained by efs14 for IRAS 08572+3915 namely that it is intrinsically a hyperluminous infrared galaxy. The predicted higher luminosity is due to the anisotropic emission of the torus.
