The only way to avoid this conclusion is if the AGN torus were more `spherical' than predicted in our model but more spherical models approaching a torus covering factor of 90 are included in our AGN torus libraries. There is also clear evidence for large outflows in this ULIRG which suggest a non-spherical geometry spo13,rup13,gonz17 .

Attempts to fit the SED of IRAS 08572+3915 with a foreground screen model have also not been successful levenson07 . IRAS 08572+3915 is one of the first ULIRGs which will be targets of JWST so we will be able to elucidate further the nature of this interesting system in the near future.

sources.summary: The paper estimates IRAS F08572+3915 has a detached, redshifted northern outflow component with low energy rate, weak ionized gas, and fossil-like lopsidedness.

quotes.quote: IRAS F08572+3915 has an outflow component offset by about R = 6 kpc to the north from the nucleus, redshifted by v 900 km s ^ 1 Fig. 2 in Herrera-Camus2020A&A . These authors interpret it as a fossil remnant of an earlier episode of nuclear activity. Using the total gas mass in the component as given in that paper, M_ out 5 10^7 , we estimate the mass flow rate as M_ out v/R 7.6 yr ^ 1 , which leads to an energy rate of E 2 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 , almost two orders of magnitude lower than the main outflow.

The low mass flow rate can be explained by the small solid angle of the outflow. An ionized outflow exists in the system, but its mass rate is more than three orders of magnitude lower than the molecular one. The lopsidedness, detachment, weakness and minuscule ionized component all agree with the interpretation of this being a fossil.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 08572+3915 as a dynamically young merging system with a buried northwest AGN, high AGN fraction, low star formation, and a heavily obscured nucleus.

quotes.quote: IRAS 08572+3915: classified as dynamically young system formed by two disks' galaxies which are in the process of merging Arribas2000 , this system shows a presence of a buried AGN in the north-west galaxy Rupke13 . This source presents the highest AGN fraction and the lowest SFR in our sample see also Efstathiou14 . Moreover, this source hosts a very obscured nucleus, which is thick even in the mid-IR at the 9.7 m spectral feature, and shows large water and ice opacities Stierwalt14 .

We further note that is its CIAGLE estimated dust luminosity from starformation is low (=10 ^ 10.19 L _ ). This is because of the obscured AGN Efstathiou14 with a high AGN fraction =99 , responsible for most of the observed dust luminosity (=10 ^ 12.09 L _ ; torus luminosity from CIGALE). Moreover, our radio modeling shows a very high thermal fraction=86 for this source which must be due to absoroption of ionizing photons by dust (see Section sec:TF ).

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 08572+3915 as a double-nucleus ULIRG whose NW nucleus is energetically dominant, optically LINER-like, and strongly favored as a buried AGN.

quotes.quote: IRAS 08572+3915 (z = 0.058) is a ULIRG (L _ IR 10 ^ 12.1 L _ ) consisting of two nuclei (NW and SE) with a separation of 5 '' sco00,kim02 . The NW nucleus (IRAS 08572+3915NW) is believed to be energetically dominant soi00 and is therefore our primary interest. It is classified optically as a LINER vei99 . It is one of the strong buried AGN candidates because (1) the energy source is suggested to be very compact and is more centrally concentrated than the nuclear dust, as expected for a buried AGN dud97,soi00,idm06,ima07 , and (2) the polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAH) emission (the starburst indicator) is extremely weak imd00,idm06,spo06,arm07,ima07 . It is thus a particularly interesting target for investigating whether the HCN(1--0) to HCO ^ + (1--0) brightness-temperature ratio is similar to those found in AGN-dominated nuclei or starburst-dominated galaxies koh05 .

sources.summary: The paper includes IRAS 08572 + 3915 among HCN-flux-excess ULIRGs with isotopologue ALMA data, a deep H13CN detection, and molecular ratios linked to buried AGN behavior.

quotes.quote: In addition to these lines, the isotopologue H ^ 13 CN J=3--2 ( _ rest = 259.012 GHz), H ^ 13 CO ^ + J=3--2 ( _ rest = 260.255 GHz), and HN ^ 13 C J=3--2 ( _ rest = 261.263 GHz) line data were taken independently for three ULIRGs, which show HCN flux excesses and modestly bright molecular emission lines (i.e., IRAS 08572 + 3915, IRAS 12112 + 0305, and IRAS 22491 1808) in our ALMA Cycle 4 program 2016.1.00051.S (PI = M. Imanishi). The details of these isotopologue observations are tabulated also in Table 2. The primary aim was to estimate possible flux attenuation by line opacity (not dust extinction) for HCN, HCO ^ + , and HNC, based on a comparison of HCN-to-H ^ 13 CN, HCO ^ + to-H ^ 13 CO ^ + , and HNC-to-HN ^ 13 C flux ratios at J=3--2 jim17,ima17 , to test the high HCN abundance scenario in HCN-flux-enhanced AGN-containing ULIRGs, and to obtain the intrinsic flux ratios among HCN, HCO ^ + , and HNC in this small sample.
For IRAS 08572 + 3915, the flux upper limit in Cycle 2 data (Table 16) is higher than the detected flux in deep Cycle 4 data (Table 8).

We see a trend that ULIRGs with optically identified Seyfert-type AGNs (e.g., G: Superantennae, I: PKS 1345 + 12) distribute at the right side with higher HCN-to-HNC flux ratios than those with optically elusive, but infrared-identified buried AGNs (e.g., C: IRAS 20551 4250, D: IRAS 08572 + 3915, H: IRAS 15250 + 3609; J: IRAS 06035 7102, L: IRAS 12127 1412) vei99 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NOEMA CO(1-0) observations of IRAS F08572+3915, a low-redshift ULIRG of two interacting spirals, to constrain its multiphase AGN-driven molecular outflow.

quotes.quote: In that spirit, in this paper we present deep NOEMA CO(1-0) line observations of the molecular gas in the outflow in the ultra-luminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG) IRAS F08572+3915. These new observations, which achieve an angular resolution a factor of 2 better compared to previous studies rhc_cicone14 , improve our constraints on the size, geometry and velocity structure of the outflow, leading to a more reliable measurement of the outflow energetics. Combined with estimates of the outflow properties from other phases (warm molecular, ionized, and atomic), here we present one of the few multi-phase views available of an AGN-driven outflow for additional examples see rhc_veilleux13,rhc_tombesi15,rhc_feruglio15,rhc_tombesi17,rhc_rupke17 .

IRAS F08572+3915 is a low redshift ( z=0.0582 ) ULIRG L_ IR ,8-1000 m =1.4 10^ 12 L_ ; rhc_veilleux13 composed of two interacting spiral galaxies with a separation of about 5 kpc. Figure fig:hst_co shows an HST (F814W) image of the interacting pair and the circumgalactic material around them.

sources.summary: The paper uses IRAS 08572+3915 to illustrate a deep ULIRG silicate profile with narrow 11, 16, 19, and 23 micron residual features attributed to crystalline silicates.

quotes.quote: The resulting optical depth profiles for the twelve sources in our sample have apparent 10 m optical depths ranging from 2.1 for IRAS 17068+4027 to 4.2 for IRAS 08572+3915, with a mean value of 2.5 (see Figure fig3 and Table tab2 ).

The third panel of Figure fig2 shows the fit to the silicate profile of the ULIRG IRAS 08572+3915. The red curve smoothly fits the maximum depth of the broad 18 m feature, while ignoring the strong, narrow substructure around 11, 16, 19 and 23 m. After subtracting the spline-interpolated amorphous component from the optical depth spectrum, the features at 11, 16, 19 and 23 m show up in the fit residual spectrum, clearly above the levels expected for artifacts introduced by the fitting procedure.
A few sources (e.g. IRAS 06301--7934, IRAS 23129+2548) further show a weak feature at 27--28 m and only one source, IRAS 08572+3915, a strong feature at 11 m (see also Figure fig2 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS F08572+3915 as AGN-dominated in bolometric luminosity and classifies it as a radio AGN because its core has inverted radio spectral-index values consistent with a face-on jet.

quotes.quote: IRAS F08572+3915 This object shows a compact core at 8.44 GHz with several diffuse point-like structures in the surrounding area. show that this object contains an AGN that dominates the bolometric luminosity of the galaxy. From the map we see that the core emission gives an inverted , getting flatter outwards. Because values of at the core of radio emission are inverted, suggesting emission from a face-on jet, we classify this object as a radio-AGN.

sources.summary: IRAS 08572 + 3915 is described as a double-nucleus ULIRG/LINER or Seyfert 2 whose northwest nucleus likely hosts an energetically dominant buried AGN.

quotes.quote: IRAS 08572 + 3915 ( z = 0.0580) is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG; L _ IR 10 ^ 12 L _ ) with L _ IR 10 ^ 12.1 L _ (Table 1), classified optically as a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region (LINER) galaxy vei99 or a Seyfert 2 yua10 . It shows a merging double nuclear morphology (northwestern [ NW ] and southeastern [ SE ] ) with a separation of 5 arcsec sco00,kim02,ima14a . The NW nucleus (IRAS 08572+3915 NW) is argued to contain an energetically dominant buried AGN, based on infrared spectroscopic and imaging energy diagnostic methods dud97,imd00,soi00,spo06,ima06a,arm07,ima07b,vei09,nar10 , and so has been selected as a target to scrutinize elusive buried AGNs through various methods ima07a,ten09,ten15 . We conducted ALMA Cycle 0 observations of IRAS 08572 + 3915 at HCN/HCO ^ + /HNC J=4--3 lines and reported their molecular line flux ratios ima14b ; detailed descriptions about IRAS 08572 + 3915 can be found in the paper cited.

sources.summary: The paper detects only the north-west component of IRAS 08572+3915 and interprets its flat radio spectrum and thermal-fraction mismatch as evidence for a synchrotron-dominated obscured AGN.

quotes.quote: IRAS 08572+3915 : We only detect the north west (NW) component of this system. The NW component is optically classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy and is suspected to have a highly obscured AGN e.g., Iwasawa11, Rupke13 . The flat spectrum observed in Figure fig:fig2 suggests this is a flat spectrum AGN, which was also suggested by Condon91 based on 1.49 and 8.44 GHz continuum observations. The high thermal fraction predicted from the IR luminosity only indicates that the IR emission is mostly dominated by an AGN instead of star formation, and that the 33 GHz emission is dominated by synchrotron instead.

sources.summary: The paper uses IRAS 08572+3915 as a low-PAH ULIRG template to calibrate crystalline-forsterite optical-depth corrections and tests that correction in its silicate-feature spectral fitting.

quotes.quote: Hence we estimate N_ cry for all the objects in our sample by calculating CF_ I08572 _ cry ( 23 m )/ _ cry ( 11 m ) , where CF_ I08572 is defined as _ cry, I08572 (11 m )/ _ cry, I08572 (23 m ) and obtained from the spectral decomposition of the 10 m silicate feature of IRAS 08572+3915 which shows little PAH emission. From figure 4 in Spoon2006 , the mean and standard deviation of the (16 m )/ (23 m ) are found to be 1.71 and 0.46, respectively, and the value of (16 m )/ (23 m ) for IRAS 08572+3915 is 1.46. We investigated the effect of the difference between 1.71 and 1.46 on the 5.3--12 m spectral fitting, assuming N_ cry 1.71/1.46 times larger.

(3) We obtained CF_ I08572 (defined in the previous subsection) by fitting the 5.3--12 m spectrum of IRAS 08572+3915 with equation eq:model where free parameters are the mass column density of crystalline forsterite, N_ cry , as well as the parameters in Table tab:para .

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 08572+3915 as a local luminous ULIRG outlier with deep silicate absorption, minimal PAH emission, and an obscured composite AGN/starburst power source.

quotes.quote: Previous studies suggest that IRAS 08572+3915, which displays deep silicate absorption and minimal PAH emission Spoon2007 , is the most luminous ULIRG in the local ( z < 0.1 ) universe and is thought to be an AGN-dominated galaxy with its torus viewed almost edge-on Efstathiou2014 ; IRAS 18030+0705 displays the strongest PAH emission of all ULIRGs in the sample Spoon2007 ; IRAS 13218+0552 is a known quasar with extreme outflows Lipari2003 ; and finally IRAS 00397-1312 is the most luminous galaxy in the sample Farrah2009 , showing deep silicate absorption and weak PAH features as in the case of IRAS 08572+3915.

Specifically, ULIRG IRAS 08572+3915 at z=0.058 is located very low at the vertical (PC5) axis of the 3D Feature space and at the same time is closer to galaxies identified to display AGN/starburst composite spectrum. This suggests that it can posses composite AGN/starburst power source, but dust obscures it in a drastic way. However, deep obscuration by dust may not be the only factor that makes IRAS 08572+3915 an outlier because Arp 220 which also shows evidence of deep obscuration is not an outlier.

sources.summary: IRAS 08572+3915 is treated as an interacting-pair ULIRG; the paper finds H ii-like extranuclear ionization and says its velocity-dispersion range is too small to show the shock correlation.

quotes.quote: The sample of galaxies consists of six low- z ULIRGs (see general properties in table misulirgs ) covering a relative wide range of dynamical states of the merging process. Three of the galaxies (IRAS 08572+3915, IRAS 12112+0305, and IRAS 14348 1447) are interacting pairs separated by projected distances of up to 6 kpc, while the rest of the galaxies (IRAS 15206+3342, IRAS 15250+3609, and IRAS 17208 0014) are more evolved, single nucleus ULIRGs, some with a light profile and overall kinematics close to that of intermediate mass ellipticals (e.g. IRAS 17208 0014, Genzel et al. 2001). The two-dimensional kinematic properties (velocity field and velocity dispersion) have been studied in detail before using integral field optical spectroscopy (see Colina et al. 2005 and references therein).

According to this ratio, LINER-like emission is found in the extended extranuclear regions in three systems: IRAS 14348 1447 , IRAS 15250+3609 , and IRAS 17208 0014 . Contrary, for IRAS 08572+3915 , IRAS 15206+3342 and IRAS 12112+0305 this line ratio suggests a dominant H ii like ionization.

Individually, two systems, IRAS 12112+0305 and IRAS 14348 1447, show a clear correlation in the three line ratios. For three of the remaining systems, IRAS 08572+3915,IRAS 15206+3342 and IRAS 15250+3609, the range in velocity dispersion is relatively small to show the correlation.

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS 08572+3915 as the extreme ULIRG silicate-absorption case, requiring a deeply embedded nuclear source in optically and geometrically thick material.

quotes.quote: The most extreme example, IRAS 08572+3915, has S_ sil = -4.0 Spo06 .

As Figures fig:strength and fig:specind together illustrate, models with p=1 , moderate thickness ( R_ out a few hundred R_ in ), and moderately large optical depth ( _V a few hundred) agree with both the observed mid-IR color and the large magnitude S_ sil of IRAS 08572+3915.

Comparing the observations of IRAS 08572+3915 with the simulated SED of the preferred solution, in which _ V = 200 , p =1 , and R_ out = 300R_ in , the 14 and 30 measurements yield L_ 12 = 1.5 and 1.1, respectively. Both values are comparable to the luminosity derived from the IRAS fluxes, L_ 12 = 1.4 , at a distance of 250 Mpc.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 08572 + 3915 as an extreme ULIRG and summarizes its morphology, infrared/AGN indicators, and role among high FIR-to-CO systems in the local ULIRG sample.

quotes.quote: IRAS 08572 + 3915. This z=0.059 ULIRG shows two cores which are well separated by 6 kpc. Both the stellar nuclei are dominated by H emission cam05 . It is also a well-known warm object ( f_ 25 /f_ 60 =0.23 ) with a strong evidence for an AGN soifer00,farrah03 .

sources.summary: IRAS 08572+3915 is treated as an extreme ULIRG with marginal Chandra detection, no Suzaku high-energy emission, very high gas column, and template type-2 quasar status.

quotes.quote: IRAS 08572+3915, instead, has been investigated with all the current X-ray observatories: it exclusively reveals a very marginal detection with Chandra , but no high-energy emission is seen with Suzaku (Teng et al. 2009). CO measurements imply for this source a gas column up to 3 10^ 25 cm ^ 2 (Evans et al. 2002). As a consequence, both these galaxies can be considered as a template type 2 quasar in the local Universe.

sources.summary: The atlas gives IRAS 08572+3915's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: IRAS 08572+3915 is a galaxy pair with two overlapping spirals with a separation of 6 ( 6 kpc) sanders_ultraluminous_1988 . The north-western galaxy is an ultra-luminous infrared galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0584 ( D 254 Mpc) with an active nucleus classified optically either as H II veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 , LINER kim_optical_1995,veilleux_optical_1995,veilleux_new_1999 , or Sy 2/starburst composite yuan_role_2010 . We adopt the latter classification.

sources.summary: F08572+3915 has the highest OH and Na velocities, the fastest CO outflow in the sample, and a revised broad [C II] FWHM that weakens a correlation.

quotes.quote: F08572+3915 has by far the highest velocity in OH and Na, and the second highest velocity in [ CII ] .
F08572+3915 has the fastest CO outflow of the sample, with wings peaking around 800 km s ^ 1 and +900 km s ^ 1 .
Most importantly, the [ CII ] _ broad FWHM of F08572+3915 decreases from 1586 km s ^ 1 to 898 km s ^ 1 , which weakens the correlation between [ CII ] _ broad FWHM and Na I D v_ 50 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 08572+3915 as a ULIRG whose very steep mid-infrared spectrum implies an extremely powerful AGN dominating roughly ninety percent of its infrared luminosity.

quotes.quote: 2010, , 140, 1868 Group I: The Late-Stage Merger Sample IRAS 08572+3915 : IRAS 08572+3915 is a ULIRG. Its very steep spectrum from 2 to 20 \ implies an extremely powerful AGN, which approaches 91 of the total IR luminosity coming from the galaxy. This is consistent with efs14 , who also found an AGN luminosity fraction around 0.9, and dal14 , who estimated an AGN contribution of 85 .

sources.summary: The paper presents IRAS F08572+3915 as an AGN-dominated LIRG whose AKARI/Spitzer spectrum lacks PAH emission and instead shows hot-dust continuum plus absorption features.

quotes.quote: The one is a starburst-dominated galaxy (CGCG 436-030; upper panel) and the other is an AGN-dominated galaxy (IRAS F08572+3915; lower panel). As expected, the starburst CGCG 436-030 shows pronounced PAH emission features, which are absent in IRAS F08572+3915. Instead, IRAS F08572+3915 has a steeply rising continuum emission contributed by hot and warm dust emission as well as strong absorption features.

gmw_00000117.name: IRAS 09149-6206
gmw_00000117.overview: IRAS 09149-6206 is a nearby radio-quiet Seyfert-I active galaxy [arXiv:2009.10734] located at a low Galactic latitude [arXiv:1310.2770]. The nucleus hosts a supermassive black hole [arXiv:2009.10734] surrounded by a rotating, disk-like broad-line region [arXiv:2311.03590] and an asymmetric distribution of hot dust [arXiv:2401.07676].

sources.summary: The paper resolves IRAS 09149 6206 with VLTI/GRAVITY, measuring its broad-line region offset, velocity gradient, radius, and central black-hole mass in an active galaxy nucleus.

quotes.quote: We present new near-infrared VLTI/GRAVITY interferometric spectra that spatially resolve the broad Br emission line in the nucleus of the active galaxy IRAS 09149 6206. We use these data to measure the size of the broad line region (BLR) and estimate the mass of the central black hole. Using an improved phase calibration method that reduces the differential phase uncertainty to 0.05 \ per baseline across the spectrum, we detect a differential phase signal that reaches a maximum of 0.5 between the line and continuum. This represents an offset of 120 as (0.14 pc) between the BLR and the centroid of the hot dust distribution traced by the 2.3 m continuum.

A clear velocity gradient, almost perpendicular to the offset, is traced by the reconstructed photocentres of the spectral channels of the Br line. We infer the radius of the BLR to be 65 as (0.075 pc), which is consistent with the radius--luminosity relation of nearby active galactic nuclei derived based on the time lag of the H line from reverberation mapping campaigns. Our dynamical modelling indicates the black hole mass is 1 10^8 M_ , which is a little below, but consistent with, the standard M_ BH - _* relation.

sources.summary: The paper presents IRAS 09149--6206 as a nearby radio-quiet Seyfert-I with complex X-ray absorption and reflection, constraining its SMBH mass, high spin, and modest outflow velocities.

quotes.quote: \ is a nearby ( z = 0.0573) radio-quiet Seyfert-I active galaxy ( Perez89, Cram92 ). Although it is X-ray bright, detected as part of the hard X-ray surveys undertaken with the BAT and ISGRI instruments ( BAT9m, 3ISGRI ) on board the \ (hereafter ; SWIFT ) and the \ observatory ( INTEGRAL ), it has received relatively little dedicated observational attention to date; prior to this work it has only been the target of a short 16 ks observation with \ ( XMM ), and a series of snapshot observations with the \ XRT. These observations imply the presence of a moderately absorbed AGN, with \ 10^ 22 \ (when fit with a neutral absorber; Malizia07, Winter09, Vasudevan10 ).

Combining X-ray timing and spectroscopy, we have been able to fully characterise the supermassive black hole in the type I Seyfert galaxy , a complex source that has received relatively little observational attention to date. We find the mass of the black hole to be M_ BH /M_ = 8.0 0.6 (primarily from X-ray timing constraints on the PSD break frequency provided by ) and the spin of the black hole to be a^* = 0.94^ +0.02 _ 0.07 (from broadband X-ray spectroscopic constraints on the relativistic reflection from the inner disc, combining , \ and ).

sources.summary: The paper includes IRAS 09149-6206's published GRAVITY BLR measurements in the R-L relation, notes its continuum-phase behavior from hot-dust asymmetry, and calculates its virial factor.

quotes.quote: To start, we have the BLR radius and BH mass measurements of our 4 targets, and adding the measurements of the 3 previously published targets (IRAS 09149-6206 from GRAVITY2020a , 3C 273 from GRAVITY2018 , and NGC 3783 from GRAVITY2021a ), gives us 7 AGNs to plot in the R-L space, now spanning a wide range of luminosity (log L_ (5100 ) 43.0 - 46.5). We fit these data points with a power-law relation similar to that of Bentz2013 : log \ (R_ BLR / ld ) = K + \ log ( L_ /10^ 44 \ erg \ s ^ 1 ).

Such a global offset is observed in IRAS 09149-6206 and NGC 3783 primarily due to the asymmetry of the hot dust emission GRAVITY2020a, GRAVITY2020b . More details about the derivation of Eqn. eqn:visphi_cont are shown in Appendix B of . Fig. fig:visphi_4agns shows the differential phase spectra of our 4 targets averaged for each baseline. Most of the baselines of the targets show signals in their differential phase curve that are either negative (Mrk 509 and PDS 456) or positive (Mrk 1239 and IC 4329A) and mostly coincide with the peak of their line profiles. As for IRAS 09149-6206 GRAVITY2020a , this indicates that their differential phase spectra show signatures of a continuum phase.
The virial factor of 3C 273 was taken from GRAVITY2018 , while the virial factors of NGC 3783 and IRAS 09149-6206 are calculated from the posterior distributions of their parameters.

sources.summary: The paper notes IRAS 09149-6206 as a low-efficiency infrared-selected source whose black-hole mass estimates differ greatly, strongly affecting its inferred accretion rate.

quotes.quote: We also note the very low efficiency IRAS 09149-6206. This source was discovered due to its IR emission, but has not been studied in great detail. We used the mass value by , which is M_ BH = 7 10^ 7 M_ but from the K band its mass is M_ BH = 3 10^ 9 M_ ( vasudevan09 ) which is a very large difference, and moves the source to the upper right region of Fig. eff_iras (the two extreme values are plotted as red squares). The mass value is most likely somewhere in between the two values mentioned above, the lower value adopted gives an extremely high mass accretion rate compared to any other source in our sample.

sources.summary: The paper cites GRAVITY observations of IRAS 09149-6206, describing a rotating disk-like BLR and Br-line measurements of BLR radius and black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: Very high resolution interferometry observations of 3C 273, IRAS 09149-6206, and NGC 3783 have been obtained with the GRAVITY instrument using the European Southern Observatory Very Large Telescope Interferometer (VLTI). These data suggest that all 3 AGN have a rotating, disk-like BLR. From the broad Paschen- line of the quasar 3C 273, Sturm2018SpatiallyScale inferred R_ BLR =0.12 0.03 pc and M_ = 2.6 1.1 10^8 M_ . Amorim2020The091496206 spatially resolved the broad Br line in IRAS 09149-6206 and inferred R_ BLR =0.075 pc and M_ 10^8 M _ . More recently, Amorim2021TheRegion used VLTI data to map broad Br in NGC 3783 and found a BLR with a radial distribution of clouds within R_ BLR 0.014 pc and then in Amorim2021A3783 reported M_ =2.54^ +0.90 _ 0.72 10^7 M _ . For all 3 objects, the BLR measurements obtained from GRAVITY are consistent with the R-L relation and reverberation mapping studies of those same AGN.

sources.summary: The atlas gives IRAS 09149-6206's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: IRAS 09149-6206 is a Seyfert galaxy at a low Galactic latitude and a redshift of z= 0.0573 ( D 249 Mpc) hosting a Sy 1 nucleus perez_iras_1989 . It was observed with /IRAC, IRS and MIPS and appears slightly resolved in all corresponding images. Our IRAC 5.8 and 8.0 m photometry matches the values published in .

gmw_00000118.name: IRAS 11095-0238
gmw_00000118.overview: IRAS 11095-0238 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy at a redshift of z=0.1066 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It likely represents the late stage of a merger with two nuclei [arXiv:1310.2770], displaying strong evidence of buried nuclear activity [arXiv:1103.3212] and a composite AGN-starburst or LINER classification [arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS 11095-0238 as an OH119 and OH79 silicate-correlation outlier, with OH119 emission despite the fourth strongest silicate absorption in the sample.

quotes.quote: It is important to note that this relationship can be strengthened by removing one outlier, IRAS 11095-0238. We identify this galaxy as an outlier because OH119 is in emission even though it has the fourth strongest silicate absorption in the sample ( S_ sil = 3.28 ). Except for Mrk 1066 ( S_ sil = 1.01 ) no other galaxy shows EW(OH119) past a silicate strength of 1.00 . Excluding IRAS 11095-0238, increases the strength of the fit: = 0.65, p = 0.00003.

However, removing the two largest outliers, IRAS 11095-0238 (similar reasons as for OH119) and MCG-01-24-012 (due to extreme EW emission, +0.242), increases the strength of the relationship, and we now find a significant correlation ( = 0.54, p = 0.002) between the two observables.

sources.summary: IRAS 11095 0238 is grouped with buried-activity ULIRGs and classified as a weak X-ray ULIRG whose limited Chandra counts require hardness-ratio analysis.

quotes.quote: The remaining four sources in our sample similarly display strong evidence for buried nuclear activity at mid-IR wavelengths, with spectral trends that are well represented by the ones shown in Fig. f1 . At high energies, IRAS 00091 0738 and IRAS 11095 0238 have been classified as weak X-ray ULIRGs by Teng Veilleux (2010): the small count rate does not allow traditional spectral fitting, and the only possible analysis is based on hardness ratio methods. We have reviewed these 15 ks Chandra observations to obtain a measure of the received 2--10 keV flux, and found that it is consistent with a SB origin of only less than 15 and 30 per cent, respectively, of the far-IR emission (see Table t2 ), assuming that no X-ray contribution from the AGN is detected.

sources.summary: The atlas gives IRAS 11095-0238's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: IRAS 11095-0238 is an ultra-luminous infrared galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.1066 ( D 480 Mpc) which might represent the late stage of a merger with two nuclei separated by 0.5 ( 0.9 kpc; PA 45 duc_southern_1997,bushouse_ultraluminous_2002 ). Optically the object is classified either as LINER veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 or as AGN/starburst composite yuan_role_2010 . We conservatively treat this object as uncertain AGN/starburst composite in the absence of multiwavelength evidence for the presence of an AGN.

gmw_00000119.name: IRAS 13349+2438
gmw_00000119.overview: IRAS 13349+2438 is an infrared-luminous, radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:1301.3148, arXiv:astro-ph/9803094] with a spiral-like host galaxy [arXiv:1301.3148] and a possible companion that may have triggered tidal interactions [arXiv:1301.3148]. The active nucleus exhibits rapid X-ray variability [arXiv:astro-ph/9803094] and hosts a radially stratified, outflowing wind with multiple warm absorber zones [arXiv:1807.06310, arXiv:2008.05965].

sources.summary: The paper finds IRAS 13349+2438 has discrepant CO profiles, missed CO(2-1) flux may be possible, and its CO redshift is offset from the optical estimate.

quotes.quote: IRAS 13349+2438 The CO(1-0) spectrum presents a double-horn profile, while the CO(2-1) emission does not. A plausible explanation is that it is a result of the difference in the beam size. In particular, the beam of the IRAM 30m for the CO(2-1) is about half the optical diameter of the galaxy and the CO(2-1) emission may not be fully encompassed with one beam. Therefore, a significant fraction of the CO(2-1) can potentially be missed by the IRAM 30m. However, we cannot exclude that the gas close to the centre is more excited and presents brighter CO(2-1) emission than the outskirt region.

We note that the two peaks in the CO(1-0) spectrum of IRAS 13349 are redshifted compared with the optically estimated redshift ( z_ opt =0.107641 based on the H , H and [ O 3 ] lines; Kim_1995 ). Using the profile of the CO(1-0) emission, we find a new systemic velocity (corresponding to the centre of the double-peak) redshifted by 240 km s^ 1 from the nominal frequency. This corresponds to a redshifted frequency of 103.98596 GHz. We thus get a CO-estimated redshift z_ CO =0.10852 , which corresponds to a luminosity distance of 502.4 Mpc.

sources.summary: The paper models IRAS 13349+2438 as a quasar with a proposed dusty warm absorber, testing ROSAT spectra and finding that dusty absorption can fit if the intrinsic spectrum is steep.

quotes.quote: IRAS 13349+2438 This quasar received a lot of attention, recently. A detailed optical study was presented by Wills et al. (1992). In X-rays, the presence of a dusty warm absorber was suggested (Brandt et al. 1996). Brinkmann et al. (1996) detected changes in the spectrum as compared to the earlier data; the warm-absorption features remained present (Brandt et al. 1997). Here, we apply the model of a dusty warm absorber to the X-ray spectrum.

Although repeatedly suggested, such a model has not been fit previously (for first results see Komossa 1998). Given the potentially strong modifications of the X-ray absorption spectrum in the presence of dust, it is important to scrutinize whether a dusty warm absorber is consistent with the observed X-ray spectrum. Since some strong features of dusty warm absorbers appear outside the sensitivity range, data are best suited for this purpose; we used the two pointed PSPC observations of Jan. 1992 and Dec. 1992 (P-2 hereafter).

sources.summary: The paper characterizes IRAS 13349+2438 as an infrared-luminous quasar with spiral-like host, possible companion interaction, obscuration, polarized emission, and a massive black hole.

quotes.quote: Based on HET optical spectra of [ O iii ] , we refine the redshift of 13349 to be z = 0.10853 . The weakness of the [ O iii ] in combination with strong Fe ii in the HET spectra reveal extreme Eigenvector-1 characteristics in 13349, but the 2468 width of the \ line argues against a narrow-line Seyfert 1 classification; on average, IR, optical and UV spectra show 13349 to be a typical QSO. Independent estimates based on the \ line width and fits to the 13349 SED both give a black hole mass of M_ BH = 10^ 9   .

IRAS 13349+2438, hereafter `` 13349'', ( z=0.10764 - ir13349-z ; updated here to z = 0.108530 based on the 3 higher resolution HET spectra reported in this paper) is a prototype infrared-luminous quasar with a bolometric luminosity of 2 10^ 46  erg s ^ 1 ir13349:discovery . Images of the host galaxy and nearby environment show the galaxy to be spiral-like, with a possible companion at 5 \ along the minor axis (e.g., hutchings:90 ). Evidence for tidal structure suggests that the object may have interacted with the companion, and this could supply gas and dust to the nucleus, fueling quasar activity and enhancing nuclear obscuration and scattering. Indeed, 13349 has a broad-emission line optical spectrum that becomes heavily reddened at shorter wavelengths, and exhibits high optical continuum and emission-line polarisation hereafter W92 wills92 .

sources.summary: The paper describes this well-studied radio-quiet NLS1 as intermediate morphology, with extended emission, flat spectral indexes, a compact core, high brightness temperature, and possible beamed jet.

quotes.quote: J1337+2423 IRAS 13349+2438 is a well-studied radio-quiet NLS1. The map of Fig. fig:J1337p2423 shows an intermediate morphology, with an extended emission at P.A. 86 ^ reaching up to 2 arcsec (7.1 kpc). Both the in-band spectral indexes are flat, just like the broad-band spectral index. The unresolved core has a size of 560 235 pc.
The brightness temperature, with respect to other radio-quiet sources, is high, 72000 K. Given these properties, we cannot rule out that this source harbors a low-power relativistic beamed jet.

sources.summary: The paper highlights IRAS 13349 + 2438 as an infrared-bright radio-quiet quasar with high polarization, unusual radio properties, and rapid X-ray variability resembling blazar behavior.

quotes.quote: The first source IRAS 13349 + 2438 was initially detected through its strong infrared emission (Beichman et al. 1986) and has been classified as a radio-quiet, infrared bright quasar with a value of R=1.9 . It has a redshift of z=0.107 and a high polarisation which rises from 1.4 at 2.2 m (K-band) to 8 at 0.36 m (U-band). Wills et al. (1992) found no variability of the polarisation or flux density on timescales from days to months and discussed a bipolar geometry to account for its polarisation and other properties. VLA observations revealed an unresolved source with an unusual radio spectrum with a maximum flux density of 7 mJy at a frequency of 5 GHz .

Recent ASCA observations of IRAS 13349 + 2438 discovered for the first time rapid x-ray variability or blazar like behaviour in a RQQSO. The source displayed intensity variations on two separate occasions by factors of two on timescales of only a few hours without any significant spectral changes (Brinkmann et al. 1996). There is also some evidence in the x-ray data for even more rapid variability.

sources.summary: The paper includes IRAS 13349+2438 as an NLSy1-like quasar and tests dusty warm-absorber models, finding that a steep intrinsic power law with optical-reddening column can fit the ROSAT spectrum.

quotes.quote: We use the definition of NLSy1 `loosely', i.e., we include the quasar IRAS 13349+2438 which shares some similarities with NLSy1s (Brandt et al. 1996, B96 hereafter). Recently, evidence has accumulated that some warm absorbers contain significant amounts of dust (B96, Komossa Fink 1996, Reynolds 1997, Komossa Fink 1997b-d, Reynolds et al. 1997, Leighly et al. 1997, Komossa Bade 1998, Komossa 1998). The possibility of a dusty warm absorber in IRAS 13349 was suggested by B96 to explain the lack of excess X-ray cold absorption despite strong optical reddening (for follow-up observations see Brinkmann et al. 1996, Brandt et al. 1997).

Here, we apply the model of a dusty warm absorber to the X-ray spectrum of IRAS 13349. Although repeatedly suggested, such a model has not been fit previously (for some results see Komossa 1998). Given the potentially strong modifications of the X-ray absorption spectrum in the presence of dust, it is important to scrutinize whether a dusty warm absorber is consistent with the observed X-ray spectrum.

In fact, if we allow for a steeper intrinsic powerlaw spectrum, with 2.9 much steeper than the value, a dusty warm absorber with N_ w =N_ opt , the value derived from optical reddening, fits the spectrum well ( ^2_ red = 1.2, Tab. 1).

sources.summary: The paper uses IRAS 13349+2438 as a Seyfert 1 case study to test how soft X-ray excess models affect inferred warm absorber properties.

quotes.quote: Here we investigate the effect of the assumed physical model for the soft-excess on the flux of the unobserved part of the spectrum and its effect on the WA properties. We perform a case study using the observations of the bright Seyfert 1 galaxy IRAS 13349+2438 with WA features. The two different physical models for the soft excess: blurred Compton reflection from an ionised disk, and, optically thick thermal Comptonisation of the disk photons, predict different fluxes in the unobserved energy range. However the current X-ray data quality does not allow us to distinguish between them using derived WA parameters. This, in turn, implies that it is difficult to determine the origin of the soft-excess emission using the warm absorber features.

sources.summary: IRAS 13349+2438 uniquely shows anti-correlated low and high energy variability, a positive high-energy-suppressed second term, and possible relativistic reflection with warm absorption.

quotes.quote: IRAS 13349+2438 This source the only source in our sample where the primary component shows a significant anticorrelation between low and high energies. This first term appears to be qualitatively very similar to the pivoting terms found in the objects described in mcg6objects , showing the break at 1 keV, with a possible second break around 7 keV. However, there is no PC corresponding to the normalisation of the primary emission. The second term is consistent with being entirely positive, and is largely suppressed at high energies. This is very similar to the first order term found in NGC 4395, and the component produced in simulations of an absorber that varies in covering fraction (see simsection ) and indeed this source does show a strong warm absorber Sako01 . IRAS 13349+2438 also shows a marginally significant soft lag 90 confidence, DeMarco13 , which supports the interpretation of the first component as a pivoting power law component in the presence of relativistic reflection, however the absence of detected variability in the primary emission remains a mystery.

sources.summary: The paper presents XMM-Newton RGS observations of IRAS 13349+2438, finding broad highly ionized absorption lines, a cooler iron UTA feature, no clear absorption edges, and at least two absorbing regions.

quotes.quote: The luminous infrared-loud quasar IRAS 13349+2438 was observed with the XMM-Newton Observatory as part of the Performance Verification program. The spectrum obtained by the Reflection Grating Spectrometer (RGS) exhibits broad ( v 1400 km s ^ 1 FW H M ) absorption lines from highly ionized elements including hydrogen- and helium-like carbon, nitrogen, oxygen, and neon, and several iron L-shell ions ( Fe xvii -- xx ). Also shown in the spectrum is the first astrophysical detection of a broad absorption feature around = 16 - 17 \ identified as an unresolved transition array (UTA) of 2p - 3d inner-shell absorption by iron M-shell ions in a much cooler medium; a feature that might be misidentified as an O vii edge when observed with moderate resolution spectrometers. No absorption edges are clearly detected in the spectrum. We demonstrate that the RGS spectrum of IRAS 13349+2438 exhibits absorption lines from at least two distinct regions, one of which is tentatively associated with the medium that produces the optical/UV reddening.

sources.summary: The paper models IRAS 13349+2438 as a quasar whose X-ray spectrum may contain a dusty warm absorber, testing dust effects against ROSAT PSPC data.

quotes.quote: IRAS 13349+2438 This quasar received a lot of attention, recently. In X-rays, the presence of a dusty warm absorber was suggested (Brandt et al. 1996). Here, we fit a model that explicitly includes the presence of dust to the X-ray spectrum. Although repeatedly suggested, such a model has not been applied previously (for some results see Komossa Fink 1998). Given the potentially strong modifications of the X-ray absorption spectrum in the presence of dust, it is important to scrutinize whether a dusty warm absorber is consistent with the observed X-ray spectrum.

sources.summary: IRAS 13349+2438 uses SED-based mass constraints, belongs to the dust-consistent Fe K group, and has Fe K detection in only one observation.

quotes.quote: This was the case for all sources except IRAS 13349+2438 which is derived from constraints on modeling its spectral energy distribution.
For 9 of the 13 AGN, the measurements or limits on \ are fully consistent with . These are NGC 3783, NGC 4151, NGC 4593, NGC 5548, Mrk 509, 3C 273, IRAS 13349+2438, NGC 4051 and IC 4329A.

However, note that for two sources from our sample (3C 273 and IRAS 13349+2438), the \ line is detected in only one observation and it is unknown whether this is due to intrinsic and strong variability of the line.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes X-ray spectra of the low-redshift quasar IRAS 13349+2438, finding two warm absorber zones and a newly identified ultra-fast outflow dominating kinetic power.

quotes.quote: We present an analysis of \ spectra of the low-redshift quasar IRAS 13349+2438. The RGS spectrum shows a large number of absorption lines from two zones of warm absorption, with velocities of 600 km s ^ 1 , as noted by previous authors. Additionally, we find robust evidence from multiple Ly absorption lines for a previously undiscovered ultra-fast zone of absorption, with an outflow velocity of -0.13 0.01 . The warm absorbers and ultra-fast outflow have similar mass outflow rates, around 40 of the Eddington accretion rate, but the kinetic power is dominated by the high velocity gas, which has a power of 4 of the Eddington luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper reports joint X-ray observations of IRAS 13349+2438, finding multiple blueshifted absorption components that imply a radially stratified quasar wind.

quotes.quote: We present joint \ and \ observations of the bright, variable quasar IRAS 13349+2438. This combined dataset shows two clear iron absorption lines at 8 and 9 keV, which are most likely associated with two layers of mildly relativistic blueshifted absorption, with velocities of 0.14c and 0.27c . We also find strong evidence for a series of Ly absorption lines at intermediate energies in a stacked \ EPIC-pn spectrum, at the same blueshift as the lower velocity iron feature. This is consistent with a scenario where an outflowing wind is radially stratified, so faster, higher ionization material is observed closer to the black hole, and cooler, slower material is seen from streamlines at larger radii.

sources.summary: The paper cites IRAS 13349+2438 as an X-ray bright QSO whose warm-absorber transitions were confirmed and blueshifted, indicating outflowing host-galaxy plasma.

quotes.quote: For example, the X-ray bright QSO IRAS 13349+2438 ( z=0.10764 ) and the Seyfert-1 galaxy (e.g. MCG-6-30-15 at z=0.007749 ) observed extensively with ROSAT Brandt96 and ASCA Brandt97 gave indications of absorption features in the sub-keV regime. Their Chandra and XMM-Newton spectra confirmed this fact and exhibited a wealth of transitions such as , - , - , , - , and while successfully identifying almost all charge states of Fe and O in some cases hereafter HBK07 Behar03,HBK07 . Furthermore, these observations showed the various transitions to be blueshifted relative to the host galaxy, indicating that the warm absorber plasma is, in fact, outflowing.

sources.summary: The atlas gives IRAS 13349+2438's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: IRAS 13349+2438 is a quasar and narrow-line Sy 1 galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.1076 ( D 483 Mpc; see lee_ionized_2013 for a dedicated comprehensive multiwavelength study). It was the first radio-quiet quasar discovered in the infrared by , who observed this object with Palomar 5 m/BOLO1 in 1985 in the N band. In 2000, IRAS 13349+2438 was observed with Palomar 5 m/MIRLIN gorjian_10_2004 , followed by /IRC in 2005 and /IRAC in 2009.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 13349+2438 as a borderline type-2 AGN, noting conflicting line-width evidence and spectropolarimetry suggesting the sightline intercepts equatorial dusty material.

quotes.quote: The case of IRAS 13349+2438 is somewhat similar to the previous object. The H line width measured by Lee2013 favours a type-2 classification, an argument in contradiction with Brandt1996 who found that IRAS 13349+2438 shares many properties with narrow-line Seyfert 1s, though they noted that this object must have a peculiar geometry. Such statement is supported by the spectropolarimetric observations realized by Wills1992 , who have shown that the observer's line of sight is probably intercepting part of the equatorial dusty material, classifying IRAS 13349+2438 as a borderline type-2 AGN.

gmw_00000120.name: IRAS 15250+3609
gmw_00000120.overview: IRAS 15250+3609 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1901.06723] and major-merger candidate [arXiv:1101.0682] featuring a deeply enshrouded nucleus [arXiv:1101.0682] and a 27-kiloparsec tidal ring [arXiv:1101.0682]. The system is classified as a LINER-starburst composite [arXiv:1609.01291, arXiv:1310.2770] hosting a buried active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1609.01291] and a massive molecular gas outflow [arXiv:1803.02408].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 15250+3609 as a ULIRG whose HCN-vib feature is blended with an HCO+ outflow signature; OH and HCN absorption indicate a highly obscured galaxy, so its HCN-vib luminosity is an upper limit.

quotes.quote: In the case of IRAS 15250+3609, the HCN-vib line is likely blended with a potential outflow signature in the nearby HCO ^ + line. Here, we have attributed the entire emission feature to the HCN-vib line, and the value given should be considered an upper limit to the HCN-vib luminosity.

The ULIRG IRAS 15250+3609 was observed in the HCO ^ + J=3 - 2 line by ima16a . They interpreted a peak close to the expected position of the HCN-vib line as an outflow signature in the HCO ^ + line. Their interpretation is supported by the fact that similar features are seen in the HCN J=3 - 2 and J=4 - 3 lines ima16a,ima18 .

Furthermore, IRAS 15250+3609 has a high equivalent width in the OH 65 m doublet gon15 , indicating that the galaxy is highly obscured, as well as an HCN absorption at 14 m lah07 . Here, we have attributed the feature as completely due to HCN-vib emission, but it is likely that it is indeed blended with an HCO ^ + outflow signature and the value given here should be considered an upper limit to the HCN-vib luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS 15250+3609 as a distant ULIRG major-merger candidate with a tidal ring and enshrouded nucleus, but reports no p-H3O+ detection.

quotes.quote: IRAS 15250+3609 is a relatively distant ultraluminous galaxy - probably a major merger with a dominant bright nucleus. A tidal feature appears to emerge from the southwest side of the nucleus and turns around on the eastern side to create an enormous closed ring, 27 kpc in diameter. The optical spectrum is a composite of H II and LINER features veilleux99 . IRAS 15250 exhibits deep silicate absorption features spoon06 indicating a deeply enshrouded nucleus. Our JCMT beam covers the whole optical galaxy.
p- \ line emission was detected towards IC 342, NGC 4418, NGC 253, NGC 1068 and NGC 6240, while Arp 299 and IRAS 15250 were not detected.

sources.summary: The paper reports P Cygni HCO+ profiles in IRAS 15250 + 3609, interpreting blueshifted absorption as molecular outflowing material and estimating a high molecular hydrogen column.

quotes.quote: P Cygni profiles are detected in the HCO ^ + J=4--3 and J=3--2 lines toward IRAS 15250 + 3609, with an estimated molecular outflow rate of 250--750 M _ yr ^ 1 .

IRAS 15250 + 3609 shows a broad negative absorption feature at the 0.4--0.5 GHz higher frequency (= blueshifted) side of the HCO ^ + J=4--3 emission line in Figure 8c. A similar broad negative absorption feature was detected at the higher frequency (blueshifted) side of the HCO ^ + J=3--2 emission line ima16c . These absorption profiles are naturally interpreted as outflowing material, in which blueshifted gas in front of the nucleus absorbs the background nuclear continuum emission.

Adopting the HCO ^ + to-H _ 2 abundance ratio of 10 ^ 8 gre09 , the molecular H _ 2 column density of the blueshifted outflowing gas in IRAS 15250 + 3609 is estimated to be N _ H2 > 6 10 ^ 21 (cm ^ 2 ).

sources.summary: The paper places IRAS 15250 among the more deeply absorbed ULIRG spectra, with increasing silicate and hydrocarbon absorption, weak PAH emission, and little unconcealed star formation.

quotes.quote: The water ice feature deepens and the importance of PAH emission decreases moving down from Mrk 273 to IRAS 15250. At the same time, the depth of the 10 and 18 m silicate features increases and hydrocarbons absorption bands at 6.85 and 7.25 m become apparent. Further note how from IRAS 18030 to IRAS 15250 the characteristic PAH emission feature at 7.7 m gradually gets replaced by a broad absorbed-continuum peak at 8 m.

The same emission features are also weakly present in the spectrum of IRAS 15250, most notably the PAH emission feature at 6.22 m. Evidently, the contribution of unconcealed star formation in IRAS 15250 is far smaller than in Arp 220.

sources.summary: IRAS 15250 + 3609 is presented as a ULIRG/LINER or AGN-starburst composite with several infrared indicators of a luminous buried AGN.

quotes.quote: IRAS 15250 + 3609 ( z = 0.0552) is a ULIRG with L _ IR 10 ^ 12.0 L _ (Table 1). It is classified optically as a LINER vei95 or an AGN-starburst composite yua10 . The presence of a luminous buried AGN is suggested from (1) a steeply rising continuum at > 3.5 m, most likely due to AGN-heated hot dust emission ima10 , (2) low equivalent widths of PAH emission features at > 5 m spo02,nar10,ima11b,sti13 , and (3) a high emission surface brightness ima11b . nar10 estimated that 50 of the bolometric luminosity of IRAS 15250 + 3609 is explained by the buried AGN.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 15250+3609 as a local ULIRG with redshifted OH absorption indicating gas funneling toward the central core, alongside outflow-related spectral components.

quotes.quote: In the sample of sources observed with Herschel /PACS, there is one local ULIRG with prominent redshifted absorption in the OH doublets, IRAS 15250+3609 ( L_ IR 10^ 12 , GA17), probably indicating massive funneling of gas toward the central galaxy core as a consequence of merging. The spectrum of HCO ^+ 3-2 at submillimeter wavelengths also shows a redshifted absorption feature, but the profile is dominated by redshifted emission and blueshifted absorption of the continuum characteristic of an outflow ima16 While the far-IR is mostly sensitive to the gas in front of the continuum source, blocking to some extent the emission from behind, the submillimeter traces the gas in front and behind the continuum more evenly, which may explain the observed differences. . The redshifted OH119 absorption in this source is shown with R=1000 in Fig. inflows . The emission feature at negative velocities is expected to be associated with outflowing gas, but the redshifted absorption represents a massive inflow.

sources.summary: IRAS 15250+3609 is a more evolved single-nucleus ULIRG; the paper finds LINER-like extranuclear emission and limited velocity-dispersion range in the shock-correlation test.

quotes.quote: The sample of galaxies consists of six low- z ULIRGs (see general properties in table misulirgs ) covering a relative wide range of dynamical states of the merging process. Three of the galaxies (IRAS 08572+3915, IRAS 12112+0305, and IRAS 14348 1447) are interacting pairs separated by projected distances of up to 6 kpc, while the rest of the galaxies (IRAS 15206+3342, IRAS 15250+3609, and IRAS 17208 0014) are more evolved, single nucleus ULIRGs, some with a light profile and overall kinematics close to that of intermediate mass ellipticals (e.g. IRAS 17208 0014, Genzel et al. 2001). The two-dimensional kinematic properties (velocity field and velocity dispersion) have been studied in detail before using integral field optical spectroscopy (see Colina et al. 2005 and references therein).

According to this ratio, LINER-like emission is found in the extended extranuclear regions in three systems: IRAS 14348 1447 , IRAS 15250+3609 , and IRAS 17208 0014 . Contrary, for IRAS 08572+3915 , IRAS 15206+3342 and IRAS 12112+0305 this line ratio suggests a dominant H ii like ionization.

Individually, two systems, IRAS 12112+0305 and IRAS 14348 1447, show a clear correlation in the three line ratios. For three of the remaining systems, IRAS 08572+3915,IRAS 15206+3342 and IRAS 15250+3609, the range in velocity dispersion is relatively small to show the correlation.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 15250 + 3609 as an extreme ULIRG and summarizes its morphology, infrared/AGN indicators, and role among high FIR-to-CO systems in the local ULIRG sample.

quotes.quote: IRAS 15250 + 3609. This z=0.055 ULIRG shows a ring-like structure around a spheroid in the center scoville00 with a much dimmer nucleus 0.7 arcsec away from the center farrah03 . lutz99 have classified it as a starburst/LINER based on its mid- IR spectrum. However, in farrah03 's SED study, this object has been found with one of the largest AGN contribution ( L_ AGN /L_ IR 0.6 ) among the sample.

sources.summary: The atlas gives IRAS 15250+3609's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: IRAS 15250+3609 is a peculiar ultra-luminous infrared galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0552 ( D 238 Mpc) with an active nucleus optically classified as a composite LINER and H II kim_optical_1995,veilleux_optical_1995,veilleux_new_1999 and AGN/starburst composite farrah_starburst_2003,yuan_role_2010 . We conservatively treat this object as uncertain AGN/starburst composite in the absence of multiwavelength evidence for the presence of an AGN. A putative second, fainter nucleus 0.7 \ ( 0.8 kpc) south-east is visible in the NICMOS images scoville_nicmos_2000 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 15250+3608 as near the Eddington limit and classifies it as a composite source where an AGN may coexist with a strong starburst.

quotes.quote: IRAS 15250+3608 : This systems is one of the sources emitting at, or close to, the Eddington limit. The optical and mid-IR diagnostics classify this galaxy as a composite source. The fact that it is close to the Eddington limit, agrees with the potential coexistence of an AGN and a strong starburst.

sources.summary: The authors note that IRAS F15250+3608 lacks detected stellar or gas rotation in Paper I and may contain shocked nuclear gas with high velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: IRAS F15250+3608 IRAS F15250+3608 is the only galaxy in Paper I for which we did not see rotation in either stars or gas. In this case, the differential velocity map should not be interpreted in a similar manner as the others but has been included for completeness. There is potentially shocked gas near the nucleus reaching velocity dispersion upward of 150 km s ^ 1 , which could be worth follow-up observations to confirm.

gmw_00000121.name: I Zw 1
gmw_00000121.overview: I Zw 1 is a nearby narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:0705.2383] and quasar [arXiv:0705.2383, arXiv:1911.04800] hosted by a face-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:0705.2383, arXiv:2210.16764]. It is interacting with a small companion galaxy to its west [arXiv:0705.2383, arXiv:2210.16764], which has triggered star formation [arXiv:0705.2383] and created arm-like tidal tails [arXiv:2210.16764].

sources.summary: The paper presents I Zw 1 as a nearby NLS1/QSO with a spiral host, signs of minor-merger interaction, blue western-disk star formation, and nuclear near-infrared line measurements.

quotes.quote: I Zw 1 is considered as the prototypical narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy (NLS1) and as one of the closest quasi-stellar objects (QSOs). With a clear spiral host and a small companion galaxy to the west, I Zw 1 is a possible example of minor-merger-related nuclear activity. This study investigates possible signs of a relation between merger process, star formation activity, and AGN properties in the case of I Zw 1. The morphology of I Zw 1 and nearby sources is investigated via high-resolution NIR images. Color trends in the host galaxy of I Zw 1 are discussed by means of optical-to-NIR color composites.

The data support the scenario of a tidal interaction between I Zw 1 and the small companion galaxy to the west. A concentration of blue color in the western part of the I Zw 1 host galaxy might be the manifestation of merger-induced star formation activity. Previous findings that the likely companion has an old evolved stellar population are substantiated by the new data.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes IZw1 as an AGN with asymmetric polarization relative to Mrk 985, deriving inclination-related parameters, turbulence, and broad-line-region magnetic-field strengths.

quotes.quote: Now let consider the AGN IZw1. This object has p_c 0.67 , p_ line (left) 0.7 , p_ line (centre) 0.2 and p_ line (right) 0.9 . The form of the polarization spectrum is slightly more asymmetric than in Mrk 985.

Using general formulas (33)--(35) we find the following estimates: g_c 1, a g_ line 0.52, b_ g_ line 3.905, p_ line ( ) g_ line 3.18 . As in the case of Mrk 985, we found, that g_ line 1 and p_c( ) 2.68 . This corresponds to 0.44 .

As a result, the small scale turbulence parameter C 0.08 . As in the previous cases, using Eq.(11) and the value =0.44 we can estimate the magnetic field strength for IZw 1: B_ =3.42 G and B_ =12.7 G.

sources.summary: The paper uses I Zwicky 1 as a reflection-dominant narrow-line Seyfert 1 test case, finding consistent inner-disk inclination measurements even from single-epoch data with strong reflection features.

quotes.quote: The prototypical narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy I Zwicky 1 has a complicated soft X-ray absorption structure and a strong Fe K line ding2022 , which is thought to be evidence of strong relativistic disk reflection . The warm absorbers (WAs) in the soft X-rays, a combination of fast outflows and corresponding shocked circumnuclear gas ding2022 , evolve over long timescales. Changes in ionization may be driven by the different densities of the clumps crossing the observer's line-of-sight, in which the ``skin'' layer facing the source has a higher ionization state costantini2007,silva2018 . I Zwicky 1 was observed once in 2020 by NuSTAR in a 5 day-long exposure and twice by XMM-Newton, each with a 70 ks exposure (PI: D. R. Wilkins). Because of the strong variability, we divide the NuSTAR data into five epochs according to their overlap with the XMM-Newton observations as in see our Table tab:izw_spec_divid ding2022 .

For I Zwicky 1 ( R_f > 1 ), the inclination measurements from different cases and different epochs only vary a little with confidence intervals mostly overlapping, especially for the two simultaneous epochs, whereas the inclination measurements of 3C 382 ( R_f 0.5 ) fluctuate between different combinations of data. We note that combining more data improves the fits more significantly for 3C 382 than for I Zwicky 1. That said, although case 5 is intuitively the most ideal one among the five cases, the na i ve expectation that more data give better performance does not necessarily hold. We have shown that applying RELXILL to single-epoch data that exhibit strong reflection signal (I Zwicky 1) suffices to offer satisfactory results with errors of only several degrees. Yet, for data with weak reflection features like those in the single epochs of 3C 382, the fits can yield dubious results with measured inclination angles varying by tens of degrees.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes I Zw 1 as a QSO host and transition object, finding merger evidence, a two-armed host with a western companion, molecular gas, and circumnuclear starburst sites.

quotes.quote: The likely merger process and the properties of the stellar populations in the I Zw 1 host galaxy are analyzed on the basis of multi-wavelength observations With the ISAAC camera at the Very Large Telescope (VLT/UT1) of the European Southern Observatory (ESO), Chile (Paranal), with the interferometer of the Berkeley-Illinois-Maryland Association (BIMA), USA (Hat Creek/California), and with the IRAM Plateau de Bure Interferometer (PdBI), France and N-body simulations. The data give a consistent picture of I Zw 1, with properties between those of ultra-luminous infrared galaxies (ULIRGs) and QSOs as displayed by transition objects in the evolutionary sequence of active galaxies.

The J-band image of I Zw 1 (Fig. fig:1 ), obtained with ISAAC at a median seeing of 0.6'' ( 0.7 kpc), provides a detailed view of the host galaxy with its two-armed spiral, its likely western companion galaxy, and the northern foreground star. The ongoing merger process with the companion is indicated by the tidal bridge towards the companion and by the tidal tail and the elongated shape of the companion . The parameter space of this interaction is investigated with the help of restricted stellar-dynamical N-body simulations.

The circum-nuclear ^ 12 CO(1-0) emission in I Zw 1, observed with the BIMA interferometer at an angular resolution of 0.7'' ( 0.8 kpc), has a ring-like structure with a radius of about 1.5 kpc (see ). The comparison of the BIMA ^ 12 CO(1-0) measurements with PdBI ^ 12 CO(2-1) data indicates that the molecular gas in the disk region is cold or subthermally excited while the gas in the circum-nuclear region is warm and optically thick. The large molecular clouds in the ring-like structure are most probably the sites of the massive starburst activity discussed in , , and Sect. sec:2.2 .

sources.summary: The paper models I Zw 1 as a prototype narrow-line Seyfert 1 with a steep X-ray continuum, weak soft excess, ionized-disk reflection, and strong flare-driven hardening.

quotes.quote: We present results based on an observation of the prototype narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy I Zw 1 performed in June 2002. The 0.6-12 spectrum is well described by a steep power-law ( 2.3 ) and a weak soft blackbody component ( kT 200 ) below 2 . The soft X-ray excess emission is featureless and contributes only 10 to the total X-ray emission in the 0.6-2 band. There are tentative evidences for an iron iron K line and an iron K edge, both from high ionized He-like iron. The 0.6-12 continuum of I Zw 1 can be explained in terms of a blackbody ( kT 40 ), thermal comptonization and its reflection off the surface of an ionized accretion disk.

X-ray emission from I Zw 1 was highly variabile during the observation. I Zw 1 showed a large, symmetric X-ray flare during which the X-ray luminosity increased by L 6 10^ 43 erg s^ 1 in an interval of 2800 only. The X-ray spectral variability was unusual during the flare.

sources.summary: The paper describes I ZWI as a prototype NLS1 whose broad [OIII] emission varied over a decade, implying a transient emission-line region and low-ionization narrow-line region.

quotes.quote: The object, I ZWI, is the prototype of NLS1 and was discovered by Zwicky(1971). In this paper we present two new spectra of I ZWI taken in 2001 and 2002. The variations of the [ OIII ] broad component are examined by comparing them with the spectrum taken by Boroson Green(1992, hereafter BG92).

Assuming a Keplerian motion in emission region, the material emitting broad [ OIII ] is possibly located in TLR, between BLR and NLR. A positive relation between the FeII emission and H (or continuum) flux is identified in the object I ZWI. Whereases, the parameter RFe marginally decreases with ionizing continuum. In I ZWI, the inferred flux ratio of narrow component of [ OIII ] to narrow component of H is 1.7 rather than the generally adopted value( 5-10 ). Such low value suggests that there is a low ionized NLR in I ZWI.

sources.summary: The paper studies I Zwicky 1 as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy whose X-ray spectral variability changes across a flux dip, suggesting a two-component corona and possible compact jet base.

quotes.quote: The short-term spectral variability of the narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy I Zwicky 1 ( ) as observed in an 85 \ observation is discussed in detail. \ shows distinct modes of variability prior to and after a flux dip in the broad-band light curve. Before the dip the variability can be described as arising from changes in shape and normalisation of the spectral components. Only changes in normalisation are manifested after the dip. The change in the mode of behaviour occurs on dynamically short timescales in .

The data suggest that the accretion-disc corona in \ could have two components that are co-existing. The first, a uniform, physically diffuse plasma responsible for the ``typical'' long-term (e.g. years) behaviour; and a second compact, centrally located component causing the rapid flux and spectral changes. This compact component could be the base of a short or aborted jet as sometimes proposed for radio-quiet active galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper says IZw1 has two warm-absorber phases with similar column densities, high outflow velocities, UV contribution from the lower-ionization phase, and non-equilibrium absorber phases.

quotes.quote: Two warm absorber phases are also found in the spectrum of IZw1 by costantini07 . The two absorbers, which have ionization parameters of log 0 and log 2.5 , have similar column densities of 1.3 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 . The lower ionization phase has an outflow velocity of 1700 km s ^ 1 , and the higher of 800 km s ^ 1 ; implying far higher outflow velocities than those in the Arakelian 564 absorbers. The warm absorber phases in IZw1 are similar in ionization to our Phases 1 and 3; the lower ionization phase of the two is contributing significantly to the UV spectrum, as seen in Arakelian 564.

The shape of the thermal stability graph in IZw1 is also very similar to that seen here (Fig. 564_preseq ) in that there is no unstable region. costantini07 suggest that this could be due to the steep soft X-ray spectra displayed by many NLS1s. They also find that, like ours, their warm absorber phases are not in pressure equilibrium and so would require, for example, magnetic confinement in order to co-exist.

sources.summary: I Zw 1 is discussed as a Seyfert 1/PG QSO with PAH-free nuclear infrared emission, narrow molecular lines, and ALMA-detected continuum and HCN/HCO+ emission.

quotes.quote: I Zw 1 (z=0.0611) is included in the Palomar-Green quasi-stellar objects (PG QSO) catalog and is classified optically as a Seyfert 1 sch83 . ima11a performed infrared 3--4 m ( L band) 1 6-wide slit spectroscopy and did not detect a 3.3 m PAH emission feature in the nuclear region of I Zw 1. The observed nuclear infrared 3--4 m flux is high among PAH non-detected AGNs. No detectable 11.3 m PAH emission feature is found at the nucleus bur13 . Single-dish CO J=1--0 and HCN J=1--0 observations of I Zw 1 with the IRAM 30-m telescope show narrow molecular line emission with full width at 10 intensity of < 400 km s ^ 1 eva06 .

The HCN J=3--2 and HCO ^ + J=3--2 emission lines are clearly detected at the nuclear regions of NGC 7469 and I Zw 1. The HCN-to-HCO ^ + J=3--2 flux ratios are 1.11 0.06 and 1.24 0.08 for the NGC 7469 and I Zw 1 nuclei, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper reports the first I Zwicky 1 reverberation campaign, measuring an H-line lag, black hole mass, super-Eddington accretion rate, and unusually low black-hole-to-bulge mass ratio.

quotes.quote: We report results of the first reverberation mapping campaign of I Zwicky 1 during 2014 2016 , which showed unambiguous reverberations of the broad H line emission to the varying optical continuum. From analysis using several methods, we obtain a reverberation lag of _ H =37.2^ +4.5 _ 4.9 days. Taking a virial factor of f_ _ BLR =1 , we find a black hole mass of =9.30_ 1.38 ^ +1.26 10^6 from the mean spectra. The accretion rate is estimated to be 203.9_ 65.8 ^ +61.0 L_ Edd ^ 2 , suggesting a super-Eddington accretor, where L_ Edd is the Eddington luminosity and is the speed of light.

By decomposing Hubble Space Telescope images, we find that the stellar mass of the bulge of its host galaxy is (M_ bulge / ) = 10.92 0.07 . This leads to a black hole to bulge mass ratio of 10^ 4 , which is significantly smaller than that of classical bulges and elliptical galaxies. After subtracting the host contamination from the observed luminosity, we find that I Zw 1 follows the empirical R_ BLR L_ 5100 ^ 1/2 relation.

sources.summary: ALMA resolves I ZW 1's nuclear CO emission, finds spiral-arm-like structures, and confirms a southeast-to-northwest velocity gradient consistent with earlier CO mapping.

quotes.quote: The CO(1 0) imaging observations of I ZW 1 were obtained with the IRAM Plateau de Bure Interferometer (PdBI) and the BIMA millimeter interferometer at 1 .9 and 0 .7 angular resolution schinnerer98, staguhn04 , respectively. Compared with these observations, our ALMA observations at 0 .6 resolution resolve the emission in the nuclear region with improved uv coverage and angular resolution. In contrast to the BIMA observations, the ALMA observations do not show any particular spatial structure in the nuclear region. This is similar to the spatial distribution of CO(2 1) emission obtained with PdBI at 0 .9 resolution staguhn04 . We detect two spiral arm-like structures to the west and the east of the nucleus in the ALMA CO map respectively, consistent with that of the PdBI CO(1 0) observations at 1 .9 resolution schinnerer98 .
For I ZW 1, the velocity gradient is obvious in the southeast to northwest direction, from about +190 to 190 . This is consistent with the velocity map presented in schinnerer98 .

sources.summary: The paper models I Zw 1 as a prototypical NLS1, using its spectrum, SED, optical-UV luminosity, bolometric luminosity, and ionizing photon estimate in BLR photoionization work.

quotes.quote: The template has been obtained in a very simple way by removing lines that are not in the PG 0050+124 ( I Zw 1 ) spectrum. The result of their procedure was a spectrum representing only permitted emission in I Zw 1 (after removing Balmer lines, [ OIII ] 4959, 5007 lines, [ NII ] 5755, blend of Na I D and He I 5876, two intense [ ] lines at 5158 and 5273 ). The use of I Zw 1 was motivated by the fact that emission lines in this source were very narrow and their subtraction did not affect much a broad wavelength range.

We utilize the spectral energy distribution (SED) for the nearby (z= 0.061) Narrow Line Seyfert 1 (NLS1), I Zw 1 The I Zw 1 ionizing continuum shape is obtained from http://vizier.u-strasbg.fr/vizier/sed/ Vizier photometric viewer .

The sublimation radius is estimated using the integrated optical-UV luminosity for I Zw 1 . This optical-UV luminosity is the manifestation for an accretion disk emission and can be used as an approximate of the source's bolometric luminosity. The bolometric luminosity of I Zw 1 is L _ bol 4.32 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 . This is obtained by applying the bolometric correction prescription from netzer2019 to I Zw 1 's optical monochromatic luminosity, L_ 5100 3.48 10^ 44 erg s^ 1 persson1988 .

sources.summary: The paper models I Zw 1 as a prototypical Type-1 narrow-line Seyfert galaxy, using its continuum, luminosity, reverberation radius, and metallicity indicators to test BLR line emission.

quotes.quote: Using an incident continuum for I Zw 1 a prototypical Type-1 narrow-line Seyfert galaxy, our basic setup is able to recover the line ratios for the optical (i.e. ) and for the NIR (i.e. ) in agreement to the observed estimates. Although, the pairs of ( , ) that reproduce the conforming line ratios, unfortunately, do not relate to agreeable line EWs. We thus propose a way to mitigate this issue - the low ionization line (LIL) region of the BLR cloud doesn't see the same continuum seen by a distant observer that is emanated from the accretion disk, rather it sees a filtered version of the original continuum which brings the radial sizes in agreement with the reverberation mapped estimates for the extension of the BLR.

The I Zw 1 ionizing continuum shape is obtained from http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/byname?objname=I 20Zw 201 hconst=67.8 omegam=0.308 omegav=0.692 wmap=4 corr_z=1 NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database . The bolometric luminosity of I Zw 1 is L _ bol 4.32 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 . This is obtained by applying the bolometric correction prescription from : k_ Bol = L_ 5100 10^ 42 ^d where, =40, d=-0.2 and L_ 5100 is measured in erg s ^ 1 . For I Zw 1 , L_ 5100 3.48 10^ 44 erg s^ 1 persson1988 .

sources.summary: The paper treats PG 0050+124 as the best-resolved quasar host, with complex CO visibility structure, an extended tidal arm, and a compact core plus spiral arms.

quotes.quote: As an example, the averaged real visibility as a function of uv distance of PG 0050+124 is shown in Figure fig:uvfit . As the best-resolved object in our sample, the simple one-component model does not fit the data perfectly. This is likely due to the complexity of the extended tidal arm to the northwest of the galaxy (Figure fig:outflow_ex b; see below).

The only exception is PG 0050+124, whose ACA-derived size is 50 higher. J. Molina et al. fit a S e rsic (1968) profile to the intensity maps and found S e rsic indices 1 (close to a Gaussian profile), and so our measured sizes are directly comparable. The high-resolution CO map of PG 0050+124 reveals a compact core plus two spiral arms. The size from the ACA uv data is likely affected by the spiral arms, in particular the more extended one to the northwest.

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 0050+124 as a close NLS1 merger host with tidal tails, strong optical-to-X-ray variability, and marginal clumpy VLBA radio structure lacking a clear AGN core.

quotes.quote: PG 0050+124 (I Zwicky 001, Mrk 1502, UGC 00545) is one of the closest z=0.06 , 1999ApJS..121..287H,2009ApJS..184..398H narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy. Its host galaxy is an almost face-on spiral with a fainter companion to the west and two arm-like tidal tails formed by the galaxy merger 1999A&A...349..735Z . PG 0050+124 is intriguing for its extreme variability: its X-ray flux has varied by more than an order of magnitude over the past decade 2012ApJS..199...28G,2018MNRAS.478.2557G ; it is one of the most variable RQQ from optical-to-UV bands with change of brightness by a factor of 2 1998ApJ...501...82P,2012ApJS..199...28G , but no significant variability found in radio bands.

In 2022arXiv220801488A , PG 0050+124 shows a resolved structure along the east-west direction in the 1.4-GHz VLBA image, but only marginal detection of a few discrete faint clumps in the 5-GHz VLBA image. In our 5-GHz VLBA image, PG 0050+124 shows three discrete clumps within 15 mas east of the optical Gaia nucleus position, and their peak flux densities are about 5--6 times the image noise. The brightness of the clump closest to the optical nucleus is only 4 . Neither of these clumps could be identified as the AGN core from the current data.

sources.summary: The paper finds PG 0050+124 has diffuse east-west radio emission, VLBA-scale extended components, a steep-spectrum core, and polarization offset from the core suggesting jet-medium interaction.

quotes.quote: The uGMRT image of PG 0050+124 reveals double-sided diffuse emission in the east-west direction see Silpa20 . The 2-frequency uGMRT-VLA spectral index image exhibits a mean ultra-steep spectrum ( < -1 ) uGMRT core with a spectral index gradient detected at 3 level (based on the spectral index slices obtained using the AIPS task SLICE ) along the north-south direction. The VLA image of PG 0050+124 reveals a radio core surrounded by some diffuse emission. Recently, the 40  mas 1.4 GHz VLBA image of PG 0050+124 has revealed extended emission along the E-W direction Alhosani22 . The 4  mas 5 GHz VLBA image has also revealed weak discrete components located to the east of the optical nucleus in PG 0050+124 Alhosani22, Wang22a .

The steep spectrum core revealed in the in-band VLA spectral index image of PG 0050+124 is consistent with the sub-arcsec scale jet emission suggested in the literature. The uGMRT image of PG 0923+129 reveals a compact core surrounded by diffuse emission in the south-west direction Silpa20 while its VLA image detects diffuse emission in the north-east (NE) direction also. Both the 2-frequency uGMRT-VLA spectral index and the in-band VLA spectral index images reveal a steep spectrum core. PG 0934+013 appears compact in the uGMRT image while its VLA image exhibits a weak extension (a 2 feature) to the south. The 2-frequency uGMRT-VLA spectral index image shows a mean spectral index of 0.5 0.1 in the uGMRT core; this value is at the cusp of flat-steep division. The in-band VLA spectral index image of PG 0934+013 exhibits a steep spectrum core. PG 1011 040 shows a marginal elongation ( 1.8 feature) in the northwest direction in the VLA image, while it appears compact in the uGMRT image. Interestingly, the 2-frequency uGMRT-VLA spectral index image of PG 1011 040 exhibits a flat spectrum uGMRT core, whereas its in-band VLA spectral index image reveals a steep spectrum core. We note that the weak extensions in PG0934+013 and PG1011-040 must be treated with caution and need to be confirmed with deeper observations. Nevertheless, the spectral index properties of PG 0923+129, PG 0934+013, and PG 1011 040 suggest the presence of either a sub-arcsec scale jet or lobe emission or optically thin synchrotron emission from an AGN wind Hwang18 . Moreover, since these sources were found to lie on the radio-IR correlation in our uGMRT study Silpa20 , we cannot completely rule out the emission from stellar-related processes in them. PG 1119+120 appears compact in the uGMRT image. Its VLA image detects a triple radio structure comprising of a lobe-core-hotspot in the NE-SW direction.
