This is consistent with the spectral index gradient detected at 6 level (based on the spectral index slices) in the 2-frequency uGMRT-VLA spectral index image of its uGMRT core. The in-band VLA spectral index image of this source reveals a flat spectrum in the core. PG 1211+143 appears compact in both uGMRT and VLA images. We had concluded from our uGMRT study that the AGN rather than the stellar activity was the primary contributor of radio emission in this source Silpa20 . The 2-frequency uGMRT-VLA spectral index image reveals a marginally steep spectrum ( _ 2-freq = -0.59 0.07 ) uGMRT core, which could suggest either jet or lobe emission on sub-arcsec scales or optically thin synchrotron emission from an AGN-driven wind. Interestingly, the in-band VLA spectral index for the core is 0.5 0.1 which is at the cusp of flat-steep division. This could suggest that the steeper spectrum component associated with the radio outflow may have been missed out by the higher-resolution VLA observations and what is detected here is the synchrotron self-absorbed base of a low-power or `frustrated' jet. The uGMRT images of PG 1229+204 and PG 1244+026 reveal kpc-scale curved jets or lobe-like emission to the south. The VLA image of PG 1229+204 also detects extended emission in the same direction, resembling a curved jet. This extension is weak however, and should be treated with caution. The VLA image of PG 1244+026 reveals only a compact core. The 2-frequency uGMRT-VLA spectral index image of PG 1229+204 reveals a flat spectrum in the uGMRT core whereas its in-band VLA spectral index image reveals a steep spectrum VLA core. The 2-frequency uGMRT-VLA spectral index image detects a steep spectrum uGMRT core in PG 1244+026. PG 1426+015 appears compact in both the uGMRT and VLA images, and exhibits a flat spectrum uGMRT core in its 2-frequency uGMRT-VLA spectral index image. The in-band VLA spectral index images of PG 1244+026 and PG 1426+015 reveal flat spectrum in their cores, which could suggest synchrotron self-absorbed bases of small-scale jets (such as on sub-arcsec scales). Within errors, the possibility of flat spectrum ( _ in-band = -0.11 0.08 ) arising from the thermal free-free emission processes from AGN or HII regions cannot be ruled out in PG 1244+026. Radio Polarization Properties of Individual Sources Out of the 11 sources, we detect marginal linear polarization in four sources, viz., PG 0050+124, PG 0923+129, PG 0934+013, and PG 1229+204; this needs confirmation with deeper observations. The fractional polarization varies between 2 and 25 in them. The polarised emission in PG 0050+124 is found to be offset from the core; this could suggest jet-medium interaction. The in-band VLA spectral index image suggests this region to be optically thin. Therefore, the inferred B-fields are perpendicular to the vectors in this region. PG 0923+129 shows two distinctly polarized regions, one in the core and the other slightly offset from the core (annotated as C1 and C2 in Figure fig:2 , top right panel, respectively). The in-band VLA spectral index image reveals the C1 component to be optically thin and the C2 component to be optically thick. Since it is difficult to establish the origin of radio emission (among jet/AGN wind/starburst wind; see Section morphology ) in this source with the current data, we cannot as yet ascertain the relationship between the B-field structures and the direction of the radio outflow. Nevertheless, the polarization structures suggest the presence of a marginally resolved bent jet on the basis of (i) the drastically varying orientation of the EVPA vectors across the polarized knots, and (ii) the suggestion of jets in quasars being threaded by poloidal magnetic fields in the literature Miller12, MehdipourCostantini19 as well as from our previous polarization studies Silpa21a, Silpa21b, Silpa22 . Interestingly, the recent VLBA study by Chen23 has revealed three discrete components in PG 0923+129 at 1.4 and 4.9 GHz. The central component exhibits a flat spectrum, suggesting core emission while the extended components reveal a steep spectrum, most likely arising from a radio outflow. The polarized knots in PG 0934+013 and PG 1229+204 are slightly offset from their radio cores. From their in-band spectral index images, the polarized region is marginally steep in PG 0934+013 and very steep in PG 1229+204, suggesting these regions to be optically thin. The inferred B-fields in PG 0934+013 are transverse to the tentative jet feature to the south. Recent studies of RQ sources with the uGMRT and VLA have reported toroidal B-fields in the kpc-scale radio cores Silpa21a, Silpa21b, Silpa22 . In PG 1229+204, the inferred B fields are parallel to the direction of the southern jet. It is worth noting that poloidal B-fields in the jet have been observed in III Zw 2 Silpa21a and Mrk 231 Silpa21b as well as in the RL PG quasars Baghel2023 . Origin of Radio Emission and Jet - Medium Interaction Based on the multi-frequency, multi-resolution radio data from the uGMRT, VLA and VLBA, we deduce that the radio emission in PG 0003+199 and PG 0050+124 is most likely associated with sub-arcsec scale jets.

sources.summary: The paper uses IZw1 as a type-1 AGN fit example, finding the new GoMar23 model statistically preferred and large maximum dust grains required to reproduce its infrared spectrum.

quotes.quote: As an example of a typical type-1 AGN, Fig. fig:IZw1fit shows the resulting best fits for IZw1 using each of the tested models.

In the case of IZw1, it is clear that [ Nenkova08 ] , [ Hoenig10 ] , and [ Hoenig17 ] models cannot reproduce the observations. Significantly better fits ( ^2/dof <2 ) are obtained by [ Fritz06 ] , [ Stalev16 ] , and [ GoMar23 ] models, the last model being statistically preferred. The same conclusion is drawn from this object if no foreground extinction is included. Interestingly, [ Fritz06 ] , [ Stalev16 ] , and [ GoMar23 ] models converge to a thin torus with 10-30^ . In this case the maximum dust grain size is P_ size 10 m (

3.41 m ), well above the canonical 0.25 m (

0.097 m ) assumed in previous models.
Indeed, the maximum dust grain size is requested by the data to significantly improve the fit for IZw1.

sources.summary: The paper demonstrates fantasy on I Zw 1, fitting its optical spectrum with multicomponent AGN, Balmer, Fe II, and forbidden Fe II components that reproduce the observed spectrum.

quotes.quote: Case of I Zw 1 To illustrate the potential of the fantasy code, the multi-component fitting was done on the I Zw 1 observed spectrum in the 4000-6800 wavelength region (Fig. fig:fitZw ), which was corrected for Galactic extinction . We note that the I Zw 1 spectrum is pretty flat in the blue part, indicating that there might be significant intrinsic extinction by the host-galaxy see 2006ApJS..166..470R, 2022ApJS..258...38P . We have tested the host galaxy subtraction provided in fantasy (see Section 3.2), and seen no noticeable difference in the corrected spectrum in the region of interest (near H and H linea), and consequently in Fe II fittings, so we continued the analysis on the I Zw 1 spectrum not corrected for the host-galaxy contribution. The model (red line) consists of: broken power-law (black dashed line), narrow lines (green solid line), broad (blue solid line) and intermediate (blue dashed line) components of Balmer lines: H , H , H , He I lines (yellow line), intermediate components of [ O III ] (blue dashed line), and Fe II model (dark-red line) and [ Fe II ] lines (light-red line).

We note here that since I Zw 1 and other NLSy1 are known to have strong forbidden ion emission, the modeling also includes these lines, set to have the same width and shift as Fe II model. The list of [ Fe II ] lines is compiled from (Table tab:lines in the Appendix) . The model describes remarkably the observed spectrum, as seen through the residual spectrum (bottom panel, Fig. fig:fitZw ).

sources.summary: PG 0050+124 is a merging spiral quasar with tidal tails, super-Eddington accretion, multi-component outflows, and EVN evidence for a richer weak jet structure.

quotes.quote: The host spiral galaxy of PG 0050+124 (I Zwicky 001, Mrk 1502, UGC 00545, z=0.061 ) is a signature of galactic mergers, e.g., two tidal tails 1999A&A...349..735Z . 2019ApJ...876..102H formed a black hole mass of about 10^7 M_ for PG 0050+124 using the optical reverberation mapping technique and inferred that it is in a super-Eddington accretion state (similar to PG 0003+199 in our sample). Another study using X-ray reverberation also measured a similar black hole mass 2021Natur.595..657W . One of the most intriguing observational features of PG 0050+124 is the multi-component outflows, including ultra-high velocity outflows of up to 0.26 2022MNRAS.516.5171R .

In contrast, in our high-sensitivity EVN images, we detected a continuous core-jet structure extending eastward from the nucleus by 20 mas (projected size about 23 parsec). The brightness temperature of the core is 10^ 6 K. The flat spectrum of the core is ^ 4.8 _ 1.4 =0.00 0.29 see Fig. fig:0050 - panel 2022ApJ...936...73A .

sources.summary: The paper revisits I Zw 1 as a prototypical Type-1 NLS1, constructing a quasi-simultaneous UV-to-NIR SED and showing a stronger Big Blue Bump affects modeled low-ionization line equivalent widths.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we focus on the issue of the spectral energy distribution (SED) characteristic to a prototypical Type-1 Narrow-line Seyfert galaxy (NLS1) - I Zw 1. We extract the continuum from quasi-simultaneous spectroscopic measurements ranging from the near-UV ( 1200 ) to the near infrared ( 24000 ) to construct the SED and supplement it with archival X-ray measurements available for this source. Using the photoionization code CLOUDY , we assess and compare the contribution of the prominent ``Big Blue Bump'' seen in our SED versus the SED used in our previous work, wherein the latter was constructed from archival, multi-epoch photometric measurements. Following the prescription from our previous work, we constrain the physical parameter space to optimize the emission from these LILs and discuss the implication of the use of a ``better'' SED.

We show that the continuum extraction from the broad-band spectrum for I Zw 1 is able to highlight the prominence of the Big Blue Bump feature in the SED. This was not accounted for in the earlier works and led to interesting results, especially in the line EWs recovery where we see a marked increase in the recovered EWs compared to our previous works.

sources.summary: I Zw 1 is described as the canonical bright NLS1 iron-template object, but its broader lines, reddening, atypical continuum, and line ratios motivate using Mrk 493 instead.

quotes.quote: The most widely used empirical iron templates have been derived from observations of the bright narrow-line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) galaxy I Zw 1 ( z = 0.060 ), which is well known as a prototypical strong \ emitter Laor+1997 . With their broad emission-line widths of <2000 , NLS1 nuclei having strong Fe 2 emission are ideal targets for defining and testing iron emission templates because line blending is minimized, so that individual \ features can be clearly and reliably distinguished. In the optical, BorosonGreen1992 constructed an empirical template covering 3686-7484 \ that is perhaps the most widely used optical template. Their approach to template construction was to remove several non-iron emission lines and the underlying continuum from a ground-based spectrum of I Zw 1.

Despite the useful features of I Zw 1 as a very bright NLS1, it has some potential shortcomings as a template object: it exhibits an atypical narrow-line emission system Veron-Cetty2004 , an abnormal near-IR \ bump Garcia-Rissmann2012 , and a peculiar UV continuum shape, most likely due to substantial intrinsic reddening (see the overall continuum shape in fig:spec_STIS_IZw1 and also Laor+1997,ConstantinShields2003 ). I Zw 1 also exhibits atypical UV line ratios, specifically unusually weak \ emission and a high Si 3 1892 / C 3 ] 1909 line ratio (Fig.  fig:spec_STIS_IZw1 ), which are characteristic of extreme EV1 AGN Wills+1999 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes I Zwicky 1 as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy, modeling its complex X-ray variability, intrinsic outflows, accretion-disc reflection, and coronal emission.

quotes.quote: I Zwicky 1 (I Zw 1) is classified as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) galaxy gallo_nls1_2018 and is found at redshift z=0.06 . NLS1 galaxies are AGN characterised by comparatively high mass accretion rates onto relatively less massive supermassive black holes between 10^6 and 10^ 7 \ boller+96 . The mass of the black hole in I Zw 1 is estimated to be between 8 10^6 and 3 10^7 \ via the width of the H line and optical reverberation mapping vestergaard+06,huang+2019 . I Zw 1 is classed as a `complex' NLS1 galaxy, weaker in its X-ray emission compared to its optical and UV emission, relative to the `simple' NLS1 galaxies, but exhibiting extreme variability, showing sharp peaks and drops in its X-ray light curve gallo_nls1 .

The soft X-ray spectrum exhibits absorption, not just from the interstellar medium along the line of sight in our own Galaxy, but from a multi-component, variable outflow intrinsic to I Zw 1 costantini+07 . We model the absorption using the xstar photoionisation code xstar . Since the outflows in I Zw 1 have been observed to vary in time, xstar was used to generate grids of absorption models that span the ranges of column densities and ionisation parameters measured in previous observations of I Zw 1 silva_1zw1 .

We conclude that the model best-describing the observed spectra consists of the directly-observed continuum emission from the corona, as well as reflection from an accretion disc with a radial ionisation gradient, in addition to the two warm absorption components that were previously discovered in I Zw 1.

sources.summary: PG 0050+124 is detected at 1.4 GHz with a 4.8 GHz excess, requires two Gaussian radio components, and has a possible disk-corona source embedded in outflow emission.

quotes.quote: Since PG 1612 + 261 and PG 0050 + 124 were only detected at 1.4 GHz according these criteria, we examined their 4.8 GHz images for emission spatially coincident with that observed at the lower frequency. No such emission was detected in the 4.8 GHz image of PG 1612+261 (Fig. \ figure:1 ), but a 4-5 excess was detected in the 4.8 GHz image of PG 0050+124 (Fig. \ multi_comp ). While this does not the satisfy the detection criteria described above, the statistical significance of this spatial coincidence suggests it is 4.8 GHz emission from this RQQ, and is treated as such below.

A single 2D Gaussian was sufficient to model the observed emission for all RQQ detections but PG 0050+124, which required two separate Gaussian components at both 1.4 and 4.8 GHz (Fig. \ multi_comp ).

The diffuse 1.4 GHz emission (low compactness) from the second component in high Eddington RQQ PG 0050+124 (PG 0050+124-C2; Fig. \ multi_comp ) is characteristic of an outflow, but its compact (4.8 GHz) core has a flatter radio spectrum than expected for such emission. This suggests that C2 may be the location of the SMBH, with the flat-spectrum emission from the disk corona embedded within the more diffuse, steep-spectrum, radio emission generated by the outflow powered by this AGN (e.g., an10, pushkarev12 ).

sources.summary: The paper presents VLBI observations of I Zw 1, describing it as a highly accreting narrow-line Seyfert system with weak, compact, episodic radio jets.

quotes.quote: In this work, we conducted high-resolution very long baseline interferometry (VLBI) observations to explore the jets in the highly accreting narrow-line Seyfert I system . Our observations successfully revealed small-scale jets (with a linear size of 45 parsec) at both 1.5 and 5 GHz, based on the high radio brightness temperature, radio morphology, and spectral index distribution. Interestingly, the lack of a flat-spectrum radio core and knotty jet structures imply episodic ejections in , which resemble the ejection process in Galactic X-ray binaries that are in the canonical very high state.

The radiative efficiency of \ was estimated, as one of the Palomar–Green (PG) quasars PG 0050+124, to be =-2.21 or 1.18 0.04 based on the mass estimates with the broad emission line widths and the M- _* correlation, respectively, see 2011ApJ...728...98D . With such a high Eddington ratio and low radiative efficiency, therefore, the SMBH in \ must be growing with a mass accretion rate notably higher than the Eddington limit, i.e. the super-Eddington accretion rate. \ is also an extremely radio-quiet AGN with radio loudness parameter R =0.35 (fn. R =1.3 10^5(L_ 5 /L_ B ) ; L_5 is the radio luminosity at 5 GHz and L_ B is the optical luminosity of the nucleus at _B = 4400 ; radio-quiet AGNs are defined as R <10 . ) 2020ApJ...904..200Y .

sources.summary: The paper models optical and near-infrared iron emission in I Zw 1, a prototypical strong-emitting NLS1 galaxy, using observed line ratios and archival spectra to constrain BLR conditions.

quotes.quote: We compare our model predictions with the observed line flux ratios for I Zw 1 - a prototypical strong emitting active galactic nuclei (AGN). This allows putting constraints on the BLR cloud density and metal content that is optimal for the production of the emission, which can be extended to I Zw 1-like sources, by examining a broad parameter space.

The I Zwicky 1 (I Zw 1) is a nearby (z=0.061) Narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy (NLS1), considered a prototypical emitter, and widely studied in the literature wills1985broad, Rudy2000The1, vestergaardpirical, Veron-Cetty2004TheIZw, Huang2019ReverberationMass . NLS1s, a subclass of classical Type-I AGNs, display a narrower-to-broader range of H line widths compared to classical Seyfert-I galaxies, which typically feature FWHM(H ) < 2000 and a flux ratio, /H < 3 OSTERBROCK1985TheGalaxies . The I Zw 1 spectrum has a rich historical record of UV, optical, and NIR studies borosonission, vestergaardpirical,kovavcevic2010analysis, garcia2012near . Recent reverberation mapping estimated the BLR's distance of the line-emitting BLR from the central ionizing source, R _ BLR =37.2 light-days Huang2019ReverberationMass , a crucial parameter in our model that allows to minimize the degeneracies in our models. To perform the optical and NIR analysis, we use the reduced and available spectra of the I Zw 1 from previous works .

sources.summary: The paper treats I Zw 1 as a prototype narrow-line Seyfert 1 with decoupled line-emitting regions, a face-on accretion disk, and an axial outflow.

quotes.quote: The ``de-coupling'' is well seen in I Zw 1, the prototype Narrow Line Seyfert 1 Galaxy (NLSy1; see Fig. fig:rq ). The \ profile is very narrow, slightly blueward asymmetric and unshifted with respect to the rest frame defined by 21 cm observations, while the \ profile is almost totally blueshifted. At least in the case of I Zw 1 the distinction between LIL and HIL emitting regions appears to be observationally established (it was actually suggested because of the difficulty to explain the relative strengths of LIL and HIL emission using a photoionized ``single cloud;'' cs88 ). There is a very important zeroth-order result here: since I Zw 1 is a strong \ emitter, we have HIL \ and LIL \ in the same rest-frame wavelength range.

I Zw 1 can be considered as an extremum with an accretion disk seen face-on (i =0^ ), and an outflowing wind observed along the disk axis. We can infer that the opening angle of any \ outflow is probably large (i. e., the wind is quasi spherical) because \ profiles like I Zw 1 are rare.

sources.summary: The paper images I Zw 1, resolving its nucleus, outlying regions, tidal tail, elliptical isophotes, and a likely stellar bar that may feed nuclear activity and star formation.

quotes.quote: For example, in unexceptional conditions it was possible to reach 15 Strehl in the H-band correcting on the slightly extended V 14.1 nucleus of I Zw 1. An H-band image of I Zw 1 is presented in Fig. fig:izw1 . In addition to the bright nucleus, two outlying regions are visible.

We have not yet measured the flux density, nor estimated its stellar mass --- but the large size already suggests that it may also be a tidal dwarf galaxy, or possibly even the progenitor nucleus of the galaxy with which I Zw 1 is interacting. The inset in Fig. fig:izw1 reveals elliptical isophotes around the AGN. Such a strong deviation from axisymmetry implies the presence of a stellar bar on scales of 0.5--1 kpc, which may be the key to understanding how gas is fed into the nuclear region to fuel the AGN and star formation there.

sources.summary: The paper treats I Zw 1 as a Type 1 quasar with detected silicate emission, fitted 220 K silicate grains, and earlier spectra whose limited coverage and contrast missed the feature.

quotes.quote: Here, we report the first spectroscopic detection of both silicate features at high signal to noise in five PG quasars. Quasars are the high luminosity counterparts of Seyfert 1 galaxies, showing the same broad emission lines that define Type 1 AGNs. The five sources presented in this letter (PG 0804+761, PG 1211+143, PG 1351+640, I Zw 1 [ =PG 0050+124 ] and 3C 273 [ =PG 1226+023 ] ) were selected from a sample of 12 AGNs based upon their prominant silicate emission features at both 10 and 18 .
The resulting silicate grain temperatures (T _ sil ) range between 140 K for 3C 273 and 220 K for I Zw 1, see Table tab2 .

Given the existence of published mid-infrared photometric and spectroscopic data for these quasars, our finding may appear surprising at first. obtained 8--13 \ spectra of 3C 273 and I Zw 1, but the low contrast of the silicate feature to the strong continnum and the lack of coverage outside the N-band window prevented them from identifying silicate emission.

sources.summary: The paper presents XMM-Newton observations of I Zwicky 1, describing it as a prototypical NLS1 whose X-ray spectrum, absorption, continuum, and iron-line variability changed between 2002 and 2005.

quotes.quote: I Zwicky 1 ( ; z=0.0611 ) is considered the prototypical narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy (NLS1) mainly due to its distinct optical properties. At X-ray energies the active galactic nucleus (AGN) exhibits behaviour that is somewhat more modest than for typical NLS1s (e.g. Leighly 1999a, 1999b). However, a short ( 20 ) \ observation conducted in 2002 revealed a plethora of interesting characteristics, such as: hard X-ray flaring, unresolved transition array (UTA) absorption, and possibly multiple Fe  \ emission lines (Gallo 2004a, hereafter G04).

The main findings from this work are the following: (1) \ is 35 per cent dimmer in 2005 than it was in 2002. Between the two epochs the level of neutral column density intrinsic to \ changed, as well as the ionised absorber. In addition, subtle changes in the continuum shape are apparent.

sources.summary: The paper uses I Zw 1's well-studied, strong narrow Fe II spectrum to measure relative template-line intensities and notes that other AGN spectra can differ from its Fe II properties.

quotes.quote: We measured their relative intensities in I Zw 1 which has a well-studied spectrum b303 spectrum with strong and narrow Fe 2 lines. Relative intensities of these 15 lines were obtained by making the best fit of the I Zw 1 spectrum with the Fe 2 lines from the F , S , and G line groups. The extra lines are represented in Figure 1 (bottom), with a dotted line. Our template of Fe 2 is described by 7 free fitting parameters: width, shift, four parameters of intensity (for the F , S and G line groups and for the lines with relative intensities obtained from I Zw 1).

Also, a template which has more parameters may give a better fit, since relative intensities among Fe 2 lines can be different even in the similar AGN spectra, as is, for example, the case for spectra of I Zw 1 and Mrk 42 as e.g. bo1 . We applied this template to our sample of 302 AGNs from SDSS database, and we compare it with other theoretical and empirical models (see Appendix B). We found that the template fit Fe 2 emission very well (Figure 5 and Figure 6 ). In the cases when the Fe 2 emission of an object has different properties from I Zw 1, small disagreements are noticed in the lines for which the relative intensity was obtained from I Zw 1.

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 0050+124 as a quasar whose HI velocity exceeds the optical value, whose profile matches a rotating disk, and whose lower-velocity gas is polarized absorption.

quotes.quote: PG 0050+124 (I Zw 1) The heliocentric radial velocity listed in Table tab:sample for this source is from optical spectroscopy by ho09 . The optically-derived velocity is 700 km s ^ 1 lower than the HI-derived value of 18285 km s ^ 1 bothun84 . The optical emission lines may arise in part from outflowing gas, so the optically-derived value may is underestimate the actual systemic velocity of this object. The shape of the full intensity profile (Figure fig:specs ) at 18285 km s ^ 1 agrees with those previously published by bothun84 and hutchings87 , and matches expectations for a regularly rotating disk. However, at lower velocities, the profile becomes polarized and a broad absorption feature is centered at the optically-derived velocity (see Figure fig:diffspecs ).

sources.summary: The paper presents XMM-Newton timing of I Zwicky 1, finding frequency-dependent reverberation lags that indicate an extended low-height corona and a collimated core above the accretion disk.

quotes.quote: X-ray spectral timing analysis is presented of XMM-Newton observations of the narrow line Seyfert 1 galaxy I Zwicky 1 (I Zw 1) taken in 2015 January. After exploring the effect of background flaring on timing analyses, X-ray time lags between the reflection-dominated 0.3-1.0 \ energy and continuum-dominated 1.0-4.0 \ band are measured, indicative of reverberation off the inner accretion disc. The reverberation lag time is seen to vary as a step function in frequency; across lower frequency components of the variability, 3 10^ 4 to 1.2 10^ 3 \ a lag of 160 \ is measured, but the lag shortens to (59 4) \ above 1.2 10^ 3 . The lag-energy spectrum reveals differing profiles between these ranges with a change in the dip showing the earliest arriving photons. The low frequency signal indicates reverberation of X-rays emitted from a corona extended at low height over the disc while at high frequencies, variability is generated in a collimated core of the corona through which luminosity fluctuations propagate upwards.

sources.summary: ALMA CO(2--1) observations show compact central molecular gas plus outer spiral arms, disk-like rotation, a rotation curve traced to 8 kpc, and sharply rising velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: PG 0050+124: The CO(2--1) observation shows a compact central distribution along with two extended molecular gas outer spiral arm structures that match the morphology in the restframe I band HST/WFC3 image. The LOS velocity map clearly shows a disk-like rotational pattern with projected peak-to-peak rotational velocity v_ rot i 440 km s ^ 1 . The detection of the outer spiral structures allow us to trace the rotation velocities at R 8 kpc. At this radius, the rotation curve shows lower values than in the inner zone. The velocity dispersion increases sharply at R 0.8 and 2.8 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper traces I Zw 1’s 2020 Seyfert flare reverberation, inferring rapid coronal expansion and an increased scale height as the corona accelerates away from the disc.

quotes.quote: The reverberation lag increases rapidly in tandem with the rise of the first of a series of flares observed from the Seyfert 1 AGN I Zw 1 in 2020 January, then remains at the increased level during the subsequent flares. Accounting for spectral dilution of the time lags, the increasing reverberation lag is consistent with the corona increasing from a scale height of 2 \ to (12 4) \ above the disc as the flares are launched (assuming that the radial extent of the corona is small such that a point source approximation can be used to translate the measured time lag into a height). This measurement of the increase of the scale height falls alongside the observation that the corona accelerates to a velocity of 0.9 away from the disc during the flares, the measurement of the temperature dropping from 140_ 20 ^ +100 \ to 45_ 9 ^ +40 \ and the tentative measurement of the radial extent of the corona over the inner disc, increasing from 7_ 2 ^ +4 \ to 18_ 7 ^ +6 \ 1zw1_flare_paper .

During bright X-ray flares launched around the supermassive black hole in the AGN I Zw 1 in 2020, we observe how the reverberation lag time and scale height of the corona increases as the corona is accelerated away from the black hole and accretion disc. The behaviour giving rise to the continuous, stochastic variability of the corona is more complex.

sources.summary: The paper summarizes IZw 1 as variable and absorbed by two ionization-state gas components with high outflow velocities and accretion-disk-scale inferred distances.

quotes.quote: IZw 1 Ga2007, Cos2007 report on the spectral details and variability between two observations. They find evidence of absorption of two different ionization states. Si2018 worked on the same observation as we did and found a variable multi-phase ionized absorber by two gas components of similar column densities. The outflow velocity estimated for the low ionization component is of 1900 \ km/s and for the high ionization is 2500 \ km/s . By assuming that the outflow velocity is greater or equal to the escape velocity, they estimate the absorbers distance to the central source at 0.07, 0.04 \ pc respectively, placing them in the scale of the accretion disk.

sources.summary: The paper describes I ZW 1 as a nearby NLS1 with warm absorbers and hard- and soft-X-ray UFO components, including a secondary 2015 soft-X-ray UFO.

quotes.quote: I ZW 1 is a nearby ( z  0.06 ) NLS1 galaxy with a bright  10^ 44 erg/s , 2004Gallo and variable X-ray luminosity 2017Wilkins . Its X-ray spectrum reveals the presence of a strong Fe K emission and a weak soft excess 2007Gallo . 2017Wilkins unveiled a time lag of 160 s between the reflection-dominated energy and continuum-dominated band within 3 - 12  10^ 4 Hz.

Furthermore, the soft X-ray spectrum of I ZW 1 reveals the presence of two WAs, exhibiting a long-term variable nature 2007Costantini,2018Silva . It was attributed to the presence of either a two-phase WA originating from a shared clump or absorbing gas existing in a non-equilibrium state.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes I Zwicky 1 as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 with soft X-ray ionized absorbers and persistent UV/X-ray outflowing gas.

quotes.quote: I Zwicky 1 ( ) is a well-studied narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy NLS1; see and references therein vest_wilkes located at redshift z =0.06112 0.000015 condon . Since the time of ROSAT observations, \ has been known to host intrinsic absorption in the X-ray band boller96,lawrence97 . From moderate-resolution ASCA data, leighly99 could distinguish between a neutral component ( N_ H 2.6 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 ) and an ionised absorber, typically highlighted, in lower resolution spectra, by the \ and \ absorption edges. A similar spectral complexity was reported by luigi04 , using \ data. The presence of fast ( v_ out 1870 ) outflowing ionised gas was observed in the UV spectrum of \ using HST-FOS; laor97,crenshaw99 , through the analysis of the \ and \ absorption troughs. FUSE observed the \ absorption troughs, which displayed multiple components kriss02 . This paper is one in a series devoted to a long \ observation of . In the present study we focus on the soft X-ray absorbers, discussing the physical picture

sources.summary: The paper describes IZw1 as an NLS1 galaxy with unusually strong velocity-dependent neon ionization, no systemic [Ne v] component, and known optical outflow signatures.

quotes.quote: In some cases this can be substantial, like for IZw1, where the [ Ne v ] / [ Ne iii ] ratio increases from 0.21 at systemic velocity to 1.3 in the velocity bin centered at -1500 km s ^ 1 .

The sources with strong blue shifts in their [ Ne iii ] lines, all labeled in red, appear to define the upper envelope of this trend or a separate one. This is especially clear in the upper panel. The only obvious exception is the prototypical narrow-line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) galaxy IZw1.

The most extreme sources in this subsample do not even exhibit a [ Ne v ] component at systemic velocity. Examples are IZw1, IRAS 05189--2524, IRAS 07598+6508, IRAS 11119+3257 and IRAS 15462--0450. This strongly suggests that at least in these sources the observed [ Ne v ] emission cannot arise in NLR gas in keplerian rotation.

sources.summary: The paper treats I Zw 1 as an A3-bin FeII template, requiring higher turbulence and highly super-solar metallicity while noting consistency with prior metallicity findings.

quotes.quote: We also obtain the theoretical templates for the FeII pseudo-continuum using CLOUDY, which we have compared to observational templates for Mrk 335 and I Zw 1. These sources belong to the spectral bins A1 and A3 respectively. We found that the theoretical templates show best agreement if a reasonable turbulence is applied for Mrk 335: 0-10 km s ^ 1 ; I Zw 1: 40-50 km s ^ 1 with super-solar metallicities 1-2.5 Z _ and 4.8-5.4 Z _ , respectively. . The derived metallicities for I Zw 1 are consistent with the findings in literature suggesting highly super-solar values see e.g., negreteetal12 although they are lower if models with turbulence are considered.

sources.summary: The paper adopts I Zw 1 as the ionizing-continuum template, describing it as a nearby NLS1 and luminous radio-quiet PG QSO accreting near Eddington.

quotes.quote: I utilize the spectral energy distribution for the nearby (z= 0.0611) Narrow Line Seyfert 1 (NLS1), I Zwicky 1 (hereafter I Zw 1) (Also known by PG 0050+124 or Mrk 1502. The I Zw 1 ionizing continuum shape is obtained from http://vizier.u-strasbg.fr/vizier/sed/ http://vizier.u-strasbg.fr/vizier/sed/ ). I Zw 1 is the prototypical optical NLS1, with strong emission and unusually narrow permitted lines, e.g. H FWHM 1240 oster85 and it is also a luminous radio-quiet PG QSO (M _B =-23.5, pg83 ). The bolometric luminosity of I Zw 1 is L _ bol 3 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 porquet2004 , which for a black hole mass of 2.8 ^ +0.6 _ 0.7 10^7 M_ vester06 , implies that I Zw 1 accretes at close to the Eddington limit.

sources.summary: The paper includes I Zw 1 in its wind comparison, using reverberation black-hole mass, bolometric luminosity, X-ray wind properties, and a neutral outflow because CO wind is undetected.

quotes.quote: I Zw 1 . In this system M_ BH has been accurately measured by Huang+19 via a dedicated reverberation mapping campaign. We take L_ BOL from Veilleux+13 and the properties of the X-ray wind from ReevesBraito19 , who also determined a large value for the wind covering factor (here we assume /4 =0.5 ). A CO wind is undetected in this QSO. Instead, there is evidence for a neutral outflow Rupke+17 , which we use in our calculations.

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 0050+124 as a quasar host with extended clumpy spiral arms, star-forming spiral emission, AGN/shock-like interarm emission, and complex ionized-gas dynamics.

quotes.quote: PG 0050+124 : The host galaxy has two extended and clumpy spiral arms that extend out to the outskirts of the stellar disk. The emission coming from the spirals can be unambiguously associated with underlying star formation activity according to the BPT maps. On the other hand, the emission coming from the inter-arm regions are much fainter and likely produced by the AGN ionization and/or shocks. The LOS velocity map clearly shows a disk-like rotational pattern, while the velocity dispersion map suggests more complex ISM dynamics.

sources.summary: The paper notes I Zw 1 is barely resolved in CO, has a much lower CIGALE stellar mass than literature, and shows an elevated molecular gas fraction.

quotes.quote: I Zw 1 which is barely resolved in CO(1-0) with NOEMA Cicone_2014 ; Mrk 766 in which most of the CO(2-1) emission is concentrated in the inner 0.5 kpc Dominguez-Fernandez_2020 ;

On the contrary, for I Zw 1 the stellar mass from CIGALE is a factor of about 7 lower than the mass reported by . For consistency with the rest of our sample, we decided to use the estimation from CIGALE.
However, they tend to present small molecular gas fractions in comparison to the other surveys described above: f_ H_2 0.02-0.04 , except for Mrk 205 ( f_ H_2 0.45 ) and I Zw 1 ( f_ H_2 0.24 ).

sources.summary: The paper reports Fe-L reverberation evidence in IZw1 despite prior non-detection of Fe-K reverberation, with mostly sub-1000 s lags and a compact corona.

quotes.quote: In the case of IZw1, the Fe-K reverberation lags were probed using the lag-energy spectra, but no Fe-K reverberation feature was detected Kara2016 . Nevertheless, applied a different method to the standard Fourier technique and detected the Fe-K lag amplitude of 746 157 s due to the X-ray flares occurring near the black hole. Here, the evidence of the X-ray reverberation lags in IZw1 is also revealed but in the Fe-L band, with the lags varying mostly within 1000 s. The IZw1 mass is approximately 2.5 10^7 M_ Gonzalez2012 , so its corona is more compact than the IRAS 13224--3809 corona.

sources.summary: The paper identifies I Zw 1 as the best-fitting sample spectrum, with low residuals and a low chi-squared value likely reflecting overestimated flux uncertainties.

quotes.quote: As examples of the best fits obtained, Figure fig:best_fits_examples shows the best-fit models for the spectra of I Zw 1 and NGC 1052. We show the fits for these objects because I Zw 1 has the best fit with the lowest ^2_ value and NGC 1052 is a good example to show the behavior of some dust species, which is explained later. The residuals between the observed spectra and the model for both objects are within 5 at all wavelengths and their ^2_ are 0.12 and 0.68, respectively. In the case of I Zw 1, its low ^2_ value might indicate that the model has too many free parameters or that the errors are overestimated.

sources.summary: The paper describes I Zwicky 1 as a luminous radio-quiet AGN/QSO with strong silicate emission, weak emission-line equivalent widths, no PAH features, and no 20 micron spectral break.

quotes.quote: I Zwicky 1 is sometimes called a QSO (PG0050+124) and sometimes a "narrow line" Seyfert 1 galaxy and is a luminous but radio quiet AGN. The IRS spectrum in Figure 3 shows strong silicate emission already discussed hao05 . Emission line equivalent widths are weak, and there are no PAH features. The spectrum "break" at 20 is not present.

sources.summary: The review presents I Zw 1 as an extreme 4DE1 source whose high-ionization wind dominates, making it a prototype wind-dominated quasar and unsuitable for uncorrected virial black-hole mass estimates.

quotes.quote: I Zw 1 is in many ways not a peculiar source but an extreme in the 4DE1 sequence. Wind emission appears to be dominant for the HIL , and indeed I Zw 1 could be considered a prototype of ``wind-dominated'' quasars . Sources like I Zw 1 are clearly unsuitable for estimating the central black hole mass under the assumption of virial motions, unless a correction is applied .

sources.summary: The atlas gives I Zw 1's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: I Zw 1 is a compact spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0589 ( D 248 Mpc) with a Sy 1n nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 , and a nuclear starburst ring schinnerer_molecular_1998,sosa-brito_integral_2001 . Early MIR observation of this object were carried out by , , , , , , and . In addition, the source was monitored over the J to N bands with approximately yearly coverage from 1987 to 1998 and no significant variability was found neugebauer_variability_1999 .

sources.summary: The ALMA survey describes PG 0050+124 as one of the brightest sample quasars, compares its CO(2-1) detection with earlier work, and finds consistent line flux and width.

quotes.quote: PG 0050+124 is one of the brightest objects in our sample.
CO(2--1) was previously detected in PG 0050+124 using the JCMT (114 23 Jy km s ^ 1 ; Papadopoulos2008AA ) and in PG 1126 041 using IRAM 30 m (24.7 1.6 Jy km s ^ 1 ; Bertram2007AA ).
b The line flux and FWHM of PG 0050+124 from are entirely consistent with those reported recently by .

sources.summary: The appendix identifies IZW1 as a luminous near-Eddington NLSy1 galaxy, notes disagreement with literature outflow values, and attributes the discrepancy to coarser energy binning.

quotes.quote: IZW1 , alternatively UGC00545 , is a luminous NLSy1 galaxy, accreting at near-Eddington rates with literature outflow values > 0.25c from Reeves19 , which is in slight disagreement to our results. However, the energy binning had to be increased from optimal values to obtain physical fractional variance results. As a result,the less precisely binned variability spectra may be the reason for the discrepancy.

sources.summary: The host of PG 0050+124 is modeled as a grand-design spiral with a disk, free-index bulge, perturbed arm, and possible weak bar.

quotes.quote: PG 0050+124: The host features a grand-design spiral, with one of the arms mildly perturbed by a small, nearby companion, which as masked during the fit. We model the galaxy with a \ bulge (free n ) and an exponential ( n=1 ) disk. A weak, bar-like feature may be present, but adding a bar component does not significantly improve the residuals, and therefore we do not include it.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PG 0050+124 as a QSO host with spiral or tidal structure, a companion galaxy 16.5 kpc away, and ongoing star formation; it is also named I Zw 1.

quotes.quote: Most of the sources of Table tab:masses with < 200 show spiral structure or tidal tails (PG 0050+124, PG 1119+120, PG 1126-041, PG 1229+204, PG1426+015, PG 2130+099) in their NIR images ( surace01 ; veilleux06 ).
and spectroscopically confirmed that PG 0050+124 has a companion galaxy 16.5 kpc to the west of the QSO.
According to , one of the sources in our sample, PG 0050+124 (or I Zw 1) has ongoing star formation ( schinnerer98 ).

gmw_00000122.name: LEDA 13946
gmw_00000122.overview: LEDA 13946 is a spiral galaxy at a redshift of z=0.0365 that hosts a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770]. It is the smaller component of an interacting pair of galaxies, located south of its companion ESO 201-4 [arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The atlas gives LEDA 13946's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: LEDA 13946 is the smaller of a pair of two interacting spiral galaxies at a redshift of z= 0.0365 ( D 166 Mpc) with ESO 201-4 being the northern component (nuclear separation 28 ). It harbours a little-studied Sy 2 nucleus parisi_accurate_2009 that belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. LEDA 13946 has no reported MIR detection in the literature and appears as a compact source blended with ESO 201-4 in the images.

gmw_00000123.name: LEDA 170194
gmw_00000123.overview: LEDA 170194 is a low-inclination spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0367 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770] that features a Compton-thin torus and a weak reflection continuum [arXiv:1606.04941].

sources.summary: The atlas gives LEDA 170194's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: LEDA 170194 is a low-inclination spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0367 ( D 173 Mpc) with a little-studied Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 , which was discovered with RXTE and INTEGRAL in X-rays sazonov_identification_2005,masetti_unveiling_2006-1 . It also belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. No MIR observations are reported in the literature and no archival data are available for LEDA 170194.

sources.summary: The paper identifies LEDA 170194 as one of five objects with very large iron-K equivalent width because the reflection continuum is weak, interpreting its torus as Compton-thin and reproducible with a Monte-Carlo torus model.

quotes.quote: In the rest of objects (NGC 4388, NGC 5252, LEDA 170194, Mrk 520, and Mrk 915), the EW is very large ( >3 keV) because the reflection continuum (i.e., R ) is apparently very weak. We interpret that the tori in these objects are Compton-thin, thus producing a much weaker hump at 30 keV and absorption iron-K edge features than what the pexrav model predicts. In fact, we confirm that a Monte-Carlo based torus model by can well reproduce the spectra of these objects including the continuum and iron-K emission line, as done in , , and .

gmw_00000124.name: LEDA 178130
gmw_00000124.overview: LEDA 178130 is a morphologically undetermined galaxy at a redshift of z=0.0350 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1610.06243, arXiv:1310.2770] that exhibits complex X-ray absorption [arXiv:1610.06243, arXiv:1303.0009] and moderate variations in both intrinsic luminosity and column density [arXiv:1303.0009].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J05054575 2351139 as a nearby Seyfert 2 galaxy with complex X-ray absorption and narrow/intermediate near-infrared emission-line components.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J05054575 2351139 is a nearby galaxy hosting a Seyfert 2 nucleus. The optical spectrum analysed by shows very narrow H and H emission lines, confirming the Seyfert 2 classification. In the X-ray band, using XMM data, found a complex X-ray spectrum indicating a partially covered absorption.

The ISAAC J band LR spectrum shows an intense He I line, well separated from a faint Pa line. The Pa and [ Fe II ] 12570 \ lines have been also observed in MR mode. The Pa line shows a narrow component with FWHM = 145 ^ +30 _ 26 and a wider intermediate component with FWHM = 405 ^ +67 _ 49 . In contrast, the [ Fe II ] 12570 \ line can be modelled only by a single intermediate component with FWHM = 413 ^ +40 _ 38 (compatible with the width of the intermediate component of the Pa line). A narrow component of FWHM = 337 was also added to take into account for the [ Fe II ] 12791 \ blending to the Pa .

sources.summary: The paper classifies XSS J05054-2348 as a Seyfert 2 and finds a complex XMM/BAT spectrum, higher absorbing column, and luminosity variation.

quotes.quote: 6dF spectra for 2MASX J03565655-4041453 and XSS J05054-2348 reveal that these two objects can be classified as a starburst galaxy and a Seyfert 2 respectively (using the line ratio diagnostics of 1987ApJS...63..295V and 2006MNRAS.372..961K ).

XSS J05054-2348 A double power-law with an Iron line at 6.4 keV ( TBabs*zTBabs(powerlaw + zTBabs(powerlaw + zgauss)) ) is again the best-fitting model for the combined XMM and BAT data (see Table fitresults ) for details. We note the presence of some structure in the data below 2 keV, which can be well fit by a mekal model, improving the overall fit significantly ( ^ 2 30 ), but do not discuss this further here. The absorbing column found here is 9.9 0.3 10^ 22 cm^ 2 , representing an increase in a factor of 1.5 over the absorbing column found in W09 using an XRT spectrum. The intrinsic luminosity found here is about 75 per cent of that found in W09, suggesting both moderate intrinsic luminosity and column density variation. Here also, the XMM data reveal a complex spectrum (resembling partially-covered absorption) as opposed to the simple absorbed power-law shape found using the XRT data in W09.

sources.summary: The atlas gives LEDA 178130's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: LEDA 178130 is a morphologically-undetermined galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0350 ( D 160 Mpc) with a little-studied Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 discovered with RXTE in X-rays revnivtsev_identification_2006,parisi_accurate_2009 . It belongs as well to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. No MIR observations are reported but an archival /IRS LR staring-mode spectrum is available.

gmw_00000125.name: LEDA 549777
gmw_00000125.overview: LEDA 549777 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.061 [arXiv:1310.2770]. The galaxy was discovered to contain an active nucleus through Swift-BAT observations and is classified as a Seyfert 2.0 galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The atlas gives LEDA 549777's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: LEDA 549777 at a redshift of z= 0.061 ( D 258 Mpc) was discovered to contain an AGN with /BAT observations tueller_swift_2008 , which thus belongs to the 9 month BAT AGN sample. It was classified as a Sy 2.0 by . No observations have targeted LEDA 549777.

gmw_00000126.name: M51a
gmw_00000126.overview: M51a (NGC 5194) is a nearby, nearly face-on grand-design spiral galaxy [arXiv:1805.05643, arXiv:2108.10909]. It is strongly interacting with its companion, NGC 5195 [arXiv:1805.05643, arXiv:2404.09422], and hosts an active Seyfert 2 nucleus [arXiv:0705.4162, arXiv:1805.12140].

sources.summary: The paper describes M51a as an interacting grand-design spiral whose weaker bar and tidal perturbations are linked to the absence of a steady age sequence across its arms.

quotes.quote: M51a (NGC 5194) is a nearby, almost face--on (inclination = 22 ^ ) spiral galaxy located at a distance of 7.6 Mpc Tonry . It is a grand design spiral galaxy morphologically classified as SAc with strong spiral patterns Debra2 . M51a is interacting with a companion galaxy, M51b (NGC 5195). M51a has a star formation rate and a stellar mass of 6.9 M_ yr^ 1 and 2.4 10^ 10 M_ , respectively Lee, Both .

We find no age gradient across the spiral arms of M51a. This galaxy shows less strong arms and a weaker bar and hence a less self--gravitating disk. The absence of an age sequence in M51a indicates that the grand--design structures of this galaxy are not the result of a steady--state density wave, with a fixed pattern speed and shape, as in the early analytical models. More likely, the spiral is a density wave that is still changing its shape and amplitude with time in reaction to the recent tidal perturbations.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 5194 radial profiles, reporting a flatter central metallicity slope, characteristic ionization parameter, increasing softness-parameter slopes, and a flat stellar-temperature distribution.

quotes.quote: We also show in Figure grad_O the radial profiles of the total oxygen abundance in the three galaxies, taking the whole analysed sample and only for regions with a direct estimation of the electron temperature. Clear negative radial variations of the metallicity are observed in the three objects, with slopes larger than -0.2 dex/R _e , in agreement with the results obtained by the chaos collaboration. Only in the case of NGC 5194 do we obtain a much flatter slope when the \ regions in the central regions of this galaxy are considered using the values derived by HCM .

In this way NGC 628 has a characteristic log U value of -2.96, NGC 5194 has -3.11, and NGC 5457 has -2.56. Regarding T_* we observe radial variations consistent with those observed in the softness parameter, although the sign of the slope observed in cannot be always interpreted as radial variations of T_* in a determined sense. On one hand the two galaxies with clear decreasing slopes of the parameter, NGC 628 and NGC 5457, present at the same time very clear increasing slopes of T_* . Therefore in these two galaxies the observed variation of the softness parameter can be directly associated with a hardening of the stellar radiation across the galactic disc. On the other hand, NGC 5194, presenting increasing slopes of , has in average a flat radial distribution of the T_* values.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 5194 in M 51, finding its young and old stellar populations dominate dust heating while nearby NGC 5195 strongly affects adjacent regions.

quotes.quote: In this particular study, we examine how the radiation of an external heating source, like the less massive galaxy NGC 5195, in the M 51 interacting system, could affect the heating of the diffuse dust of its parent galaxy, NGC 5194, and vice versa. We use SKIRT , a state-of-the-art 3D Monte Carlo radiative transfer code, to construct the 3D model of the radiation field of M 51, following the methodology defined in the DustPedia framework. In the interest of modelling, the assumed centre-to-centre distance separation between the two galaxies is 10 kpc. Our model is able to reproduce the global spectral energy distribution of the system, and it matches the resolved optical and infrared images fairly well. In total, 40.7 of the intrinsic stellar radiation of the combined system is absorbed by dust.

Furthermore, we quantify the contribution of the various dust heating sources in the system, and find that the young stellar population of NGC 5194 is the predominant dust-heating agent, with a global heating fraction of 71.2 . Another 23 is provided by the older stellar population of the same galaxy, while the remaining 5.8 has its origin in NGC 5195. Locally, we find that the regions of NGC 5194 closer to NGC 5195 are significantly affected by the radiation field of the latter, with the absorbed energy fraction rising up to 38 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5194 as a nearly face-on grand-design spiral interacting with NGC 5195, comparing new VLA and GMRT radio fluxes with previous observations.

quotes.quote: (vii) NGC 5194: This is a Sbc type of grand design spiral galaxy seen nearly face on. It is also interacting with its smaller companion NGC 5195 at its North. This galaxy has been studied extensively in multiple wavebands and was observed as a control sample due to previous observations of this object at 0.33 GHz with GMRT Mulcahy2014A&A,Mulcahy2016A&A . Flux density of this galaxy as measured in L-band is 1490 mJy Condon1987ApJS . Our measured L-band flux density from the archival C and D array VLA data is 1.2 0.04 Jy.

Its flux density as estimated by us from the previous GMRT image is 4.8 Jy, while the one imaged by us provides the flux density for the same region as 3.7 0.2 Jy, which is about 30 lower than what is measured from the previous image. This discrepancy could not be due to a calibration error as the flux densities of a few compact source in the field matched within a few percents of each other in the previous map at the same frequency. The difference is also unlikely from missing zero spacing, as our map was made with short UV spacing above 150 which provides the necessary flux density for the size of NGC 5194 ( 10' ).

sources.summary: WiNDS treats NGC 5194 as the interacting M51a spiral, finding large disk-wide velocity perturbations, vertically perturbed disk signatures, out-of-plane motions, and an HI warp from NGC 5195.

quotes.quote: NGC 5194 (M51a) is a well-known nearby grand design spiral galaxy classified as non-barred by RC3, with a very low inclination angle i = 20^ . NGC 5194 is tidally interacting with its companion NGC 5195 (M51b), an early-type SB0 galaxy. NGC 5194 shows an intense star formation activity at its center and along its spiral arms.

It is interesting to note the large velocity perturbations throughout the disk, reaching amplitudes of 40 \ km \ s^ 1 . We recall the very low inclination angle of this galaxy, thus rendering it unlikely that these velocity perturbations are mainly due to in-plane velocity flow. 2007ApJ...665.1138S presented a deep kinematic study of NGC 5194 using full 2D velocity distributions from interferometric CO and Fabry-Perot H_ observations. They report a complex velocity field, with variations of the disk's PA and inclination along the galactic radius that strongly suggest a vertically perturbed disk and very significant out-of-plane motions. An HI warp has also been previously reported in NGC 5194 1990AJ....100..387R,2014PASJ...66...77O,2014MNRAS.443..186H , which has been attributed to the tidal interaction with NGC 5195.

sources.summary: The paper finds a steep outer-disk age rise and quenched ring in NGC5194, arguing ram pressure is unlikely because it is not cluster-associated.

quotes.quote: Strikingly, in two galaxies (NGC4321 and NGC5194), we found a steep increase in t MW that reached levels similar to those of the bulge. These old stellar populations in the very galaxy outskirts, which are also detectable as "quenched rings" below the spatially resolved MS, is unexpected.

This scenario is different from that for NGC5194, which shows a different behaviour with respect to the average trend of spiral galaxies within the disk (and the same might hold for NGC4321, which, unfortunately, is missing in the 18 galaxies sample). In this particular case, h_ FUV /h_ 3.6 m (NGC5194) = 1.03 (< 1.67) h_ NUV /h_ 3.6 m (NGC5194) = 0.91 (< 1.42) . The lower than the average values of this ratio for NGC5194 indicate that for this source, the stellar emission from the older populations (whose emission is traced by the 3.6 m light) has a flatter and more extended radial profile than the companion spirals. This is in line with our finding for NGC5194 (Fig. fig:mwa_all ).

However, NGC5194 is not associated with any cluster, and ram pressure stripping could not be invoked for its quenched ring. Alternatively, accretion of cold gas may have ceased or diminished in this case because the angular momentum of the remaining circumgalactic medium is too high to be incorporated into the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 5194/M51a's sub-kpc FIR-radio correlation, finding q70 enhancements along spiral arms and short smoothing scales for this more active star-forming disk.

quotes.quote: In this initial study, we examine the variations of the FIR-radio correlation on sub-kpc scales within four of the nearest face-on galaxies in the SINGS sample: NGC 2403, NGC 3031 (M81), NGC 5194 (M51a), and NGC 6946. Using high resolution Spitzer imaging, we are also able to test the smearing model of with greater accuracy, at higher spatial resolution, and in more galaxies, with the aim of gaining better insight into CR e^ diffusion and confinement within galaxy disks.

We find that elevated q_ 70 ratios are associated with bright structures in the infrared and radio images of each galaxy. The most obvious cases are seen for the bright spiral arms of NGC 3031, NGC 5194 and NGC 6946. All three galaxies show enhanced q_ 70 ratios along their arms, with local peaks centered on H II regions, and depressed ratios located in the quiescent inter-arm and outer-disk regions of each galaxy.

Figure res70 shows that scale-lengths < 1 kpc work best to improve the correlation for the more active star-forming galaxies (NGC 5194 and NGC 6946: SFR 4 M _ /yr), while scale-lengths > 1 kpc work best for the less active star-forming galaxies (NGC 2403 and NGC 3031: SFR < 1M _ /yr). We also find that by smearing the 70 m images, we are able to reduce the slope of the observed non-linearity in q_ 70 with 70 m surface brightness by > 25 for each galaxy. This suggests that the non-linearity may be due to the diffusion of CR e^ s and is not expected to be found in the global correlation when integrating the flux over entire galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper uses Spitzer observations of NGC 5194 to test RC-FIR behavior, finding active star-formation tracers and a monotonic spectral steepening as RC/FIR increases.

quotes.quote: NGC 5194 has been recently observed with Spitzer . found that the 24 m luminosity appears to be closely correlated to sites of active star formation; an initial look at the 24 m-RC correlation has been presented by . We use these new FIR high-resolution images to test the predictions of the model proposed in .

Using the 70 m Spitzer image of NGC 5194 it is now possible to study the point-by-point relation between the spectral index and the RC-FIR ratio. We measured the radio continuum spectral index between 1.4 and 4.9 GHz using the images by . These images have a resolution of 16 , comparable to that of the 70- m observations, and incorporate short-spacing single-dish observations. The relation between the radio continuum spectral index , defined as I _ ^ , and the RC/FIR ratio is shown in Fig. prova . As the RC/FIR ratio increases by an order of magnitude, a monotonic steepening of the spectral index is observed.

sources.summary: The paper studies M51a with AKARI mid-infrared images, finding smooth S7/S11 spiral-arm structure, L15/S11 and L24/S11 peaks at H II regions, and varying PAH ionization.

quotes.quote: M51 is a pair of the grand-design spiral galaxy, M51a (NGC 5194) and the companion galaxy, M51b (NGC 5195). With its proximity and prominent two-armed spiral structure, it has been a popular target to study ISM and its relationship to the spiral arm pattern. From optical and UV images, properties of H 2 regions and star clusters for the entire disk of M51a have been investigated, and their ages in the arms are found to be younger than those in interarm area LeeJ11, SanG11 .

The surface brightness ratio between the bands, i.e. \ color, is measured over the disk of the main galaxy, M51a, at 300 pc resolution. We find that the distribution of S7/S11 is smooth and well traces the global spiral arm pattern while L15/S11 and L24/S11 peak at individual H 2 regions. This result indicates that the ionization state of PAHs is related to the spiral structure. Comparison with observational data and dust models also supports the importance of the variation in the PAH ionization state within the M51a disk.

sources.summary: The paper models M51a's star-formation history, finding a viable 7 Gyr infall peak, inside-out disk formation, interaction-related recent infall, and active ongoing disk star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 5194 (M51a) is a grand-design spiral galaxy and undergoing interactions with its companion. Here we focus on investigating main properties of its star-formation history (SFH) by constructing a simple evolution model, which assumes that the disc builds up gradually by cold gas infall and the gas infall rate can be parameterizedly described by a Gaussian form. By comparing model predictions with the observed data, we discuss the probable range for free parameter in the model and then know more about the main properties of the evolution and SFH of M51a.

The model adopting a constant infall-peak time t_ p = 7.0 Gyr can nicely reproduce most of the observed constraints of M51a. We also introduce a 'toy' model, which allows a small amount of additional cold gas infall to the disc in recent time, to imitate the influence of the interaction between M51a and its companion. Our results show that the additional gas infall could be attributed to the observed small bumps on the radial profiles of cold gas surface density, SFR surface density and surface brightness in the UV-band. Although we adopt a constant infall-peak time, our model predictions still show evidence that the disc forms inside-out.

sources.summary: The paper compares AzTEC/SPITZER and Herschel-derived dust and gas masses for the NGC 5194 field, finding close agreement and little contribution from companion NGC 5195.

quotes.quote: A similar comparison between the HERSCHEL \ observations Cooper12 and those of AzTEC yield nearly identical masses ( <1 difference) for the Cooper12 NGC 5194 field, after adjusting to their adopted distance, to the low- case, and to their gas-to-dust mass ratio. Repeating this for their NGC 5195 field, however, yields a disagreement of a factor of 4. This can be at least partly attributed to the differences between their observed dust temperatures and ours. They find 35 K , whereas we find 22 K . These temperatures roughly correspond to the AzTEC/SPITZER data yielding a dust mass that is a factor of 4 higher.

Interestingly, this does not seem to be the case for the NGC 5194 field, where agreement is very tight. Also, based on the AzTEC/SPITZER data alone, the area covered by the Cooper12 map is sampling one-third the dust and gas mass of .

sources.summary: The paper reconstructs the resolved star formation history of M51a, finding early exponential growth, later decline, and a major burst after about 11.9 Gyr.

quotes.quote: We have obtained the time and space-resolved star formation history (SFH) of M51a (NGC 5194) by fitting GALEX , SDSS, and near infrared pixel-by-pixel photometry to a comprehensive library of stellar population synthesis models drawn from the Synthetic Spectral Atlas of Galaxies (SSAG). We fit for each space-resolved element (pixel) an independent model where the SFH is averaged in 137 age bins, each one 100 Myr wide.

By summing the contributions to the global star formation rate of each pixel, at each age bin, we have assembled the resolved star formation history of the whole galaxy. According to these results, the star formation rate of M51a was exponentially increasing for the first 10 Gyr after the Big Bang, and then turned into an exponentially decreasing function until the present day. Superimposed, we find a main burst of star formation at t 11.9 Gyr after the Big Bang.
We have included these wavelength ranges in our method and applied it to the Whirlpool galaxy, aka M51a. The RC3 type of M51a is SA(s)bc pec deV91 .

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 5194 using LEGUS and PAWS data, emphasizing its spiral structure, large young-cluster sample, and central molecular gas mapped at cloud-scale resolution.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we select M 51 (NGC 5194 with its smaller interacting companion, NGC 5195, collectively known as the Whirlpool galaxy) from the LEGUS survey due to the large number of cluster candidates over a large observed field with strong spiral features. NGC 5194 is relatively large in size (angular size of 11' 7' and stellar mass M_ = 2.4 10^ 10 M _ ), and combined with a star formation rate SFR(UV) = 3 M _ yr ^ 1 , this system provides a large and robust number of young clusters. NGC 5194 has a relatively low inclination 22 ^ ; colombo14b , mitigating projection effects, and a distance of 7.66 Mpc tonry01 , sufficiently nearby to lessen confusion between stellar systems and individual stars. The LEGUS dataset of NGC 5194 provides 5 pointings in the NUV (F275W) and U (F336W) broad-bands, observed with WFC3/UVIS.

We use the GMC catalogue colombo14a from the Plateau de Bure Interferometer Arcsecond Whirlpool Survey PAWS; schinnerer13,pety13 . PAWS mapped the molecular gas in the central 9 kpc of NGC 5194 using the ^ 12 CO(1--0) line emission at a cloud-scale resolution of 40 pc. The GMCs identified by Colombo et al. (2014) account for about half (54 ) of the flux in the PAWS map.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5194 (M51) as a full-disk dense-gas target with reduced central dense-gas efficiency, special EMPIRE observing setup, and a large HCN/CO decline.

quotes.quote: Similar results have been found by BIGIEL16 and CHEN15 across the full disk of NGC 5194 (M51): while there is an overall correlation between star formation rate tracers and HCN emission at kpc resolution, the efficiency of dense gas to form stars drops at small galactocentric radii (taking the observables at face value).

NGC 5194 (M51) was observed in July and August 2012, as a precursor program to EMPIRE. For this galaxy a slightly different E090 tuning was used, where the local oscillator frequency was set to 88.7 GHz. This configuration allowed to capture the isotopologues from the main dense gas tracers, H ^ 13 CN, H ^ 13 CO ^+ and HN ^ 13 C see JIMENEZDONAIRE17 , leaving ^ 13 CO and C ^ 18 O unobserved for this galaxy. The ^ 13 CO data for NGC 5194, however, was observed as part of the PAWS survey SCHINNERER13 .

We observe the largest HCN/CO declines in NGC 3627 ( 0.60dex), NGC 4254 ( 0.60dex), NGC 5194 ( 0.60dex) and NGC 6946 ( 0.55dex). Again, we see no strong morphological divide, NGC 3627 and NGC 6946 are strongly barred galaxies with prominent dense gas emission in their centers and bars, while NGC 4254 and NGC 5194 are unbarred galaxies rich in molecular gas.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC5194 with its companion M51b masked out, finding many accepted cells, a more star-forming bulge, and one of the largest main-sequence slope differences.

quotes.quote: The final sample includes 8 objects: NGC0628, NGC3184, NGC3938, NGC4254, NGC4321, NGC4535, NGC5194, NGC5457. Distances, sizes, inclinations and morphologies are those adopted by the DustPedia collaboration, based on the HyperLEDA database The HyperLEDA database is available at http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr/ 2014A&A...570A..13M , and are available on their website (see Tab. tab:sample ). The sample stellar masses are in the range between 10^ 10 10^ 11 M_ , and star formation rates between 1 and 4 M_ yr ^ 1 These sources represent the evolved descendants of high-redshift normal star-forming galaxies, that are rotationally supported systems, at least by z=2 e.g. 2015ApJ...799..209W, 2009ApJ...706.1364F, 2018ApJS..238...21F . Moreover, their regular dynamical properties suggest that they have quiescently assembled their stellar mass through cosmic times.

In NGC5194 the companion galaxy M51b has been removed by further rejecting each cell with a declination over 47.24052. On average, each galaxy has 4000 accepted points, with the exceptions of the closest ones, NGC5194 ( 10000 ) and NGC5457 ( 15000 ).

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 5194's central oval, a T_e-based N/O profile break without a matching O/H break, and strong-line methods yielding anomalous gradients including positive N2.

quotes.quote: In fact, several galaxies classified as SA in RC3 (NGC 2541, NGC 1068, NGC 5194, NGC 4395) contain a central oval distortion chapelon99,scoville88,zaritsky93,buta_atlas , while other galaxies classified as SAB (e.g. NGC 2903, NGC 925) have bars comparable in length and/or ellipticity to those in SB galaxies, or have no apparent bar, as is the case for NGC 2403.

It is also interesting to note that for the four galaxies in which a radial break is detected in the (N/O) profile derived from the T _e based abundances (NGC 628, NGC 3621, NGC 5194 and NGC 5457), such break is not detected in the T _e based 12+ (O/H) profile. Given the lower dispersion in the (N/O) profiles, the break seems to be more prominent and easily detected than in 12+ (O/H). However, a deeper analysis would be necessary.

NGC 5194 has a large deviation with HCM, N2 and R. In fact, all galaxies exhibit a negative oxygen abundance gradient with all methods, with the exception of this galaxy, for which a positive gradient is obtained with N2. This might be produced by the difficulty of strong-line methods in giving good estimates of metallicity for low-excitation \ regions bresolin2004 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5194/M51a as a cluster-rich, nearly face-on grand-design spiral whose interaction with NGC 5195 produces marked spiral geometry and a tidal tail.

quotes.quote: NGC 5194 NGC 5194 (M51a or the Whirlpool galaxy) is a well-studied spiral galaxy (morphology SAbc) due to its large size, relative proximity and almost face-on inclination. This galaxy contains the largest number of clusters in our sample. Its grand-design morphology, high star formation rate, rich star formation history, and numerous star-forming complexes and star clusters make it a benchmark for nearby extragalactic surveys e.g., PAWS Schinnerer_2013 .

NGC 5194 is known to be interacting with its companion galaxy (NGC 5195) resulting in a marked spiral geometry along with a tidal tail due to the interaction, and the two galaxies together are referred to as the M51 system. We note that the LEGUS field-of-view was obtained through multiple pointings, and the catalogued star clusters cover both members of the system. However, we found that removing the contribution from NGC 5195 star clusters does not change the TPCF, since it contains a very small fraction of the overall catalogue, and hence we keep the overall catalogue for completeness. We also refer to the system simply as NGC 5194 since this is the major contributor to the observed TPCF, and doing so maintains consistency in the format of names for the galaxies in this study.

sources.summary: The paper describes M51a as the face-on Sbc spiral NGC5194 with a weak Seyfert 2 nucleus, iconic spiral arms, and likely tidal-tail material.

quotes.quote: M51 is a one of well known interacting systems in the local Universe, consisting of a nearly face-on spiral galaxy M51a (NGC5194), and its companion M51b (NGC5195). Since the grand-design spiral arm of M51a was discovered by Lord Rosse in 1845, many works have focused on this interacting galaxy pair in both observation and theory to study galaxy evolution, tidal tails and star formation e.g. Toomre1981, Bastian2005, Calzetti2005, Kennicutt2007 . M51 multi-wavelength images from X-ray to radio have been observed to study its morphology and kinematics e.g. Rots1990, Dale2005, Watkins2015, Heyer2022 . M51a is a Sbc galaxy with a weak Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus (AGN) in its centre, while M51b is an early-type SB0 galaxy deVaucouleurs1995 . A deep X-ray image using Chandra shows the distribution of X-ray point sources. The luminosity function suggests that M51a is dominated by high-mass X-ray binaries, while M51b is dominated by low-mass X-ray binaries Kuntz2016 . In optical bands, the magnificent spiral arms of M51a, three-pronged structure of M51b and bridge between galaxies are system's iconic structures.

Due to the presence of optical and simulated counterparts, the Northwest cloud appears to be the tail of M51a, while the Northwest Plume is more likely a tidal tail of M51b. The large mass of the Northwest Plume suggests that M51b may have been as gas-rich as M51a before the interaction.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5194 as an interacting nearly face-on Seyfert spiral with a radio jet, biconical NLR, outflow bubble, ionisation cone, and mostly rotational gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 5194 is an almost face-on spiral galaxy in interaction with NGC 5195. Its nucleus is classified as a Seyfert type 2 Ho3 . Many studies have been made on the structure and the nucleus of this nearby galaxy.

A large outflow bubble extents up to 9 ( 42   pc) North of the nucleus and a bright cloud is located about 3 South of the nucleus. These structures have been observed at different wavelengths M51_Ford_bubblejet , and they are found to trace the biconical NLR along a PA of 163 . NGC 5194 is hosting a radio-jet which interacts with the surrounding interstellar medium.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5194 as M51's face-on Sbc spiral primary; selected clusters concentrate around its spiral arms, with red and faint fuzzy clusters extending across its disk.

quotes.quote: M51 is an interacting galaxy system that is composed of a face-on Sbc-type spiral galaxy NGC 5194 and a fainter companion, the SB0 galaxy NGC 5195.

The selected clusters are mostly found around the spiral arms of NGC 5194. Most of the star clusters are bluer than (B-V)=0.5 and (V-I)=0.8 , suggesting that they are young clusters with t 10^8 yrs. However, there are red clusters around both NGC 5194 and NGC 5195.

Among the large clusters, we found 27 faint fuzzy clusters that are distributed over the disk of NGC 5194 as well as around the body of NGC 5195. Interestingly, these faint fuzzy clusters are found to show an elongated spatial distribution along the northeast-southwest direction over the NGC 5194 disk.

sources.summary: The paper models M51A in the interacting Whirlpool system, emphasizing its grand-design spiral structure, overlap with M51B, deblending needs, and detailed two-arm/bulge decomposition.

quotes.quote: The classical Whirlpool galaxy is a beautiful system where a grand-design spiral, M51A, is interacting with another spiral, M51B (Figure m51 , Table m51-table ). In addition to there being obvious spiral structures for both galaxies, there are large tidal disturbances that emerge from M51B, as seen in the SDSS band image provided by D. Finkbeiner. Because they are closely overlapping, a desirable goal is to deblend M51A and B, as well as to model the spiral and tidal structures, simultaneously.

In the most detailed analysis of M51A, we use two spiral arm components and two components for the bulge. There is actually not a strong need to use two components for the bulge except to better capture the detailed profile shape, which has an inflection at 0 4 , as seen in Figure m51 i . On the other hand, the use of two spiral components is necessary because the spiral arm has a ``kink'' in the rotation that cannot be created by using a single smooth rotation function.

sources.summary: NGC 5194 is one of two inner-disc exceptions in the sample: high molecular-gas surface density and SFR make molecular gas drive instabilities, though uncertainties could move it toward star-driven regimes.

quotes.quote: Inner disc instabilities are now entirely driven by the stars at kpc scales ( _ 3 =5.9 2.3 kpc ), with two notable exceptions: the inner discs of NGC 5194 and NGC 6946 (2 of the data). These are the sample galaxies with highest surface density of molecular gas averaged over the inner disc ( _ CO 150 M _ pc ^ 2 for R 0.3 R_ 25 ), and highest star formation rate ( SFR 3 M _ yr ^ 1 ; see table 4 of L08, or table 1 of Walter et al. \ 2008). These are also active galaxies, where molecular gas plays a key role as fuel in the activity process (e.g., Krips et al. \ 2008).

The inner discs of NGC 5194 and NGC 6946 are again exceptions to the general rule. But instabilities in such discs are driven by the molecular gas (see discussion of Fig. \ 4), so they will naturally lead to its collapse or fragmentation. Are NGC 5194 and NGC 6946 true outliers? The condition that disc instabilities are driven by stars, rather than molecular gas, is T_ Q_ <T_ CO Q_ CO T_ _ / _ < T_ CO _ CO / _ CO (see Sect. \ 2.2 and remember that _ CO denotes the total surface density of molecular gas, helium included). As pointed out in Sect. \ 3, this condition is not fulfilled by the inner discs of NGC 5194 and NGC 6946.

sources.summary: The paper uses M51a as the main Whirlpool-galaxy test case, finding strong 0.1-5 Gyr star formation and IR emission dominated by 100 Myr-1 Gyr populations.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Region Characteristics & M51a & M51b & outskirts & all _ J2000 & 202.47065 & 202.49726 & & _ J2000 & 47.19517 & 47.26531

quotes.quote: We recover, for M51a, a peak star formation rate (SFR) between 0.1 and 5 Gyr ago, with much lower star formation activity over the last 100 Myr. For M51b, we find a declining SFR toward the present day. In the outskirt regions of M51a, which includes regions between M51a and M51b, we recover a SFR peak between 0.1 and 1 Gyr ago, which corresponds to the effects of the interaction between M51a and M51b.

In the long term, we will apply our method widely; however, here we initially apply our method to data in the Whirlpool galaxy (M51a or NGC5194) and its companion (M51b or NGC5195).
