We interpret the broad lines arising from an accretion disk the inner edge of which is restricted to large radii ( r_ in 100 r_g ).

M 81 The PN and MOS2 spectra of M81 were also fit by a power-law modified by photoelectric absorption. The best-fit absorption column density is consistent with the Galactic value ( =4.2 10^ 20  cm^ 2 ). This simple fit is statistically unacceptable ( 2 of 397.5 for 303 dof) due to significant spectral features in the iron K region (see Fig.  f1 ).

sources.summary: The paper presents M81* as a nearby low-luminosity AGN with variable circular polarization, no detected linear polarization, a one-sided jet, and similarities to Sgr A*.

quotes.quote: M81* is unpolarized in the linear sense at a frequency as high as 86 GHz and shows variable circular polarization at a frequency as high as 15 GHz. The spectrum of the fractional circular polarization is inverted in most of our observations. The sign of circular polarization is constant over frequency and time.

M81* has been studied extensively using Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) in the past. BietenholzBartelRupen2000 resolved M81* into a stationary core with a one sided jet. Multi-wavelength ( HoFilippenkoSargent1996 ) and sub-millimeter observations ( ReuterLesch1996 ) showed many similarities between M81* and Sgr A*, the central radio source in our Milky Way ( MeliaFalcke2001 ).

sources.summary: The paper links GRB 051103 to the M81 region, finding UV-bright knots in M81's northern arm that support an SGR-flare interpretation.

quotes.quote: The IPN 3- error box, totaling 260 square arcminutes, includes the outskirts of the nearby galaxies M81 and M82. At first glance, the error region seems to exclude the main body of M81 or M82 (see however Obs ). In this paper, we present new optical and radio observations of M81 and M82 region which cover the entire IPN error quadrilateral, taken several days after the burst, and use them to put limits on any afterglow emission related to this GRB.

A region containing several UV-bright knots, in an extension of the northern arm of M81, is clearly seen within the error quadrilateral. Several of the brightest UV sources, found within the error quadrilateral, are marked by arrows and their UV and optical fluxes are listed in Table Tab-UV . Some of these sources are extended (in the POSS images) and blue, and may be a young star forming regions in the outskirts of M81.

sources.summary: The paper studies Messier 81's central molecular gas, finding no CO emission inside 300 pc, a weak clump around 460 pc, and a low CO line ratio implying diffuse, weakly excited gas.

quotes.quote: We investigate the molecular gas content and the excitation and fragmentation properties in the central region of the spiral galaxy Messier 81 in both the ^ 12 CO(1-0) and ^ 12 CO(2-1) transitions. We have recently observed the two transitions of CO in the M 81 center with A, B, and HERA receivers of the IRAM 30-m telescope. We find no CO emission in the inner 300 pc and a weak molecular gas clump structure at a distance of around 460 pc from the nucleus. Observations of the first two CO transitions allowed us to compute the line ratio, and the average I_ 21 /I_ 10 ratio is 0.68 for the M 81 center. This low value, atypical both of the galactic nuclei of spiral galaxies and of interacting systems, is probably associated to diffuse gas with molecular hydrogen density that is not high enough to excite the CO molecules.

sources.summary: The paper derives center estimates and harmonic-decomposition results for NGC 3031, describing its disk morphology, non-circular motions, and whether the inferred potential is consistent with being round.

quotes.quote: NGC 3031 NGC 3031 (Fig. fig:ngc-3031 ) a) Center estimates [ 1.5ex ] NGC 3031 (better known as M 81) is a grand design spiral. Its two well-defined spiral arms are easily visible in the IRAC 3.6 m image as well as in the \ intensity map. NGC 3031 contains a strong central radio source. The 3.6 m image also shows a well-defined central component with a central minimum which coincides with the position of the central radio-continuum source ( = 202 7, = 371 9).

sources.summary: The paper studies the nearby galaxy M 81 through its low-luminosity AGN M 81*, describing radio brightness, jet structure, source size, and high-frequency VLBI detection.

quotes.quote: At the heart of the nearby galaxy M 81 (B0951+693, J0955+6903, NGC 3031; D = 3.63 Mpc), the low-luminosity (10 ^ 37.5 erg s ^ 1 ) radio source M 81 ^* is one of the closest active galactic nuclei (AGN). This object is four orders of magnitude brighter than Sgr A ^* , but has similar shape and polarization properties. M 81 ^* shows a stationary feature in its structure with a jet to the northeast (seen at 3.6 cm; ). Its size follows the law ^ 0.8 between 2.3 GHz and 22 GHz.
Our observations have resulted in the detection of M 81 ^* at the shortest wavelength performed with VLBI so far.

sources.summary: The survey finds NGC 3031/M81 has a steeper, fainter 20 kpc profile on one side, asymmetric counts possibly tied to interactions or substructure, and high halo metallicity.

quotes.quote: NGC 3031/M81 has a steeper profile, while at 20 kpc NGC 5236 is still dominated by the (face-on) disk.

Whilst M31 also fits this trend, the Sab galaxy NGC 3031/M81 does not, as a steeper and fainter profile is evident at 20 kpc on one side of the galaxy. Then again, the higher counts in another field at 20 kpc, albeit on the other side of M81, is consistent with the general trend. This observed asymmetry may be due to the multiple interactions M81 is undergoing or due to halo substructure.
The more massive galaxies (NGC 891, NGC 3031, NGC 4565, NGC 7814) have significantly higher metallicities than the lower mass galaxies (IC5052, NGC4244, NGC7793) as has been seen before e.g., Mou07 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes M 81's infrared central region, finding a compact probable Seyfert-related core plus spiral-arm structure that shapes its surface-brightness profile.

quotes.quote: M 81 - This is the closest galaxy and thus its central region is observable with a high level of detail see bendoSI . The core is compact, possibly the IR counterpart of the Seyfert nucleus, on which a bright arc, presumably the start of one of the spiral arms, connects. Moving away from the center, the brightness of the arm diminishes until it increases again to form a bright, almost ring-like structure. Thus all profiles, except the 24 \ one, show a first local minimum at 80'' radius (1.4 kpc at 3.65 Mpc). With high enough resolution, the double nature of the core component (central unresolved source and spiral arm) is visible in the profile as a break in the slope of the core profile.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Chandra observations of M81, building a 265-source X-ray catalog, measuring spectra and variability, and deriving luminosity functions for its bulge and disk regions.

quotes.quote: We present an analysis of 15 Chandra observations of the nearby spiral galaxy M81 taken over the course of six weeks in May--July 2005. Each observation reaches a sensitivity of 10^ 37 erg s ^ 1 . With these observations and one previous deeper Chandra observation, we compile a master source list of 265 point sources, extract and fit their spectra, and differentiate basic populations of sources through their colors. We also carry out variability analyses of individual point sources and of X-ray luminosity functions in multiple regions of M81 on timescales of days, months, and years. We find that, despite measuring significant variability in a considerable fraction of sources, snapshot observations provide a consistent determination of the X-ray luminosity function of M81.

sources.summary: The paper presents M81 dwB as the smallest sample galaxy, with a BEC mass near the observed mass, little non-circular motion, and a rotation curve poorly reproduced by NFW.

quotes.quote: M81 dwB: The BEC model gives a very good description of the rotation curve of the galaxy. This galaxy is the smallest in the sample, having a mass of only M=0.3 10^9M_ . With the adopted values for radius and central density the mass of the galaxy is M_ M81dwB ^ BEC =0.33 10^ 9 M_ . The velocity field shows no significant non-circular motions in the galaxy, except perhaps in the very outer regions. The NFW fit cannot reproduce the main features of this rotation curve.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3031's outer profile as II.o-OLR and describes bar evidence, rings, profile breaks shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3031 (II.o-OLR; SA(s)ab) : This galaxy is the nearest in our sample, at 3.6 Mpc. We found a bar of 17 (300 pc) radius which coincides with a maximum in the ellipticity; this is the small bar previously reported by elmegreen95 . Inspection and ellipse-fitting of Spitzer IRAC images Program ID 1035; willner04 provides evidence for a bar with 136 (2.4 kpc) as the semi-major axis of maximum isophotal ellipticity ( ) and 216 (3.8 kpc) as an upper limit on the bar size ( ), with the latter estimated from the apparent inner ring in GALEX images gdp07 . (The boxy morphology in this region also suggests a bar; see Erwin Debattista, in prep.) Two outer arms open up quickly from a radius of 250 (4.4 kpc), forming an incomplete outer pseudoring. We measured the outer pseudoring on both a VLA 21 cm image adler96 and a GALEX NUV image, both available from NED. Since the SE arm of the outer pseudoring is more symmetric, and can be seen to wrap around onto the NW arm, we measured from the nucleus to the center of the SE arm at its maximum extent.

sources.summary: The paper tests density-wave predictions in M81 and finds no star-formation propagation across its spiral arm, favoring kinematic spiral patterns driven by tidal interactions with companions.

quotes.quote: Stationary density waves rotating at a constant pattern speed _ P would produce age gradients across spiral arms. We test whether such age gradients are present in M81 by deriving the recent star formation histories (SFHs) of 20 regions around one of M81's grand-design spiral arms. For each region, we use resolved stellar populations to determine the SFH by modeling the observed color-magnitude diagram (CMD) constructed from archival Hubble Space Telescope (HST) F435W and F606W imaging.

Although we should be able to detect systematic time delays in our spatially-resolved SFHs, we find no evidence of star formation propagation across the spiral arm. Our data therefore provide no convincing evidence for a stationary density wave with a single pattern speed in M81, and instead favor the scenario of kinematic spiral patterns that are likely driven by tidal interactions with the companion galaxies M82 and NGC 3077.

sources.summary: M81 is one of three non-reverberating dwarf Seyferts whose STIS line-profile modeling yields a giant BLR far off the reverberating AGN size-luminosity relation.

quotes.quote: In the present contribution, high quality spectra obtained with the Space Telescope Imaging Spectrograph (STIS) are used to constrain the size of the BLR in the dwarf Seyfert nuclei of M81, NGC 3998, NGC 4203, NGC 3227, NGC 4051, and NGC 3516.

There are presently six LLAGNs whose BLR size has been deduced by modeling the shape of the H Balmer lines including three non-reverberating dwarf Seyferts (M81, NGC 3998 and NGC 4203), and three reverberating ones (NGC 3227, NGC 3516 and NGC 4051). Some basic observables are listed in Table 1, and their BLR size-luminosity relationship is illustrated in Figure 1. The three non-reverberating dwarf Seyferts lie far from the correlation defined by reverberating AGNs. The other three LLAGN are reverberating, and their inner radii consistently lie on the correlation defined by other reverberating AGN. This latter result is important as it shows that the line profile fitting method does yield a size that agrees with the reverberation size.
The angular size of the BLR in NGC 3998 and M81 is so large that it is partially obscured by the 0.1 slit employed for the STIS observations.

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 3031's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 3031: The SDSS i- band decomposed bulge profile comes from Beifiori2012 , which gives R_e=41.4'' . Sani2011 quote R_e=100.2'' , probably overestimating the size of the bulge by including the bar of the galaxy as part of the bulge in the fit. Fisher2010 are in better agreement with our adopted solution. The M/L is determined using the distance of KormendyHo2013 and by fitting the kinematics of Fabricius2012 (see Appendix C and Fig. fig_kinprofile ), summing the disk and the bulge profiles. We classify the galaxy as barred following Gutierrez2011 and Erwin2013 .

sources.summary: The paper reports that MOND overpredicts NGC 3031 outer velocities and cannot reproduce an inner bump; adding the EFE only slightly reduces the rotation-curve mismatch.

quotes.quote: (4) NGC 3031 : The MOND curve predicts significantly higher velocities in the outer regions of this galaxy. Even including the EFE, NGC 3031 still exhibits a large discrepancy between the rotation curve predicted by MOND and the observed rotation curve. This is because of an inner bump in the observed data (at about R=7.5 kpc) of this galaxy which is not possible to reproduce in MOND. This could be accompanied by non-circular motions. As given in Table 2, the reduced ^2 is slightly decreased from 4.3 to 3.3 when the EFE is taken as a free parameter.

sources.summary: The paper describes M81 as a low-SFR grand design spiral with a LINER nucleus, extensive X-ray source catalogs, notable variability, and mixed LMXB, HMXB, and SNR classifications.

quotes.quote: M81 (NGC 3031) is a nearby grand design spiral galaxy with low SFR and a low-ionization nuclear emission region (LINER). The X-ray population of M81 has been studied by \ fabbiano02-88 , \ immler06-01 , and \ tennant03-01,swartz02-03 . The most comprehensive analysis was completed by sell07-11 , who used 220 ks of \ data from 16 observations to classify and investigate the variability of 265 sources detected above 10^ 37 . They found significant variability in 36-60 of their sources but concluded that snapshot observations provided a consistent determination of the XLF of M81. Color diagnostics identified large numbers of many different source types such as LMXBs, HMXBs, and SNRs.

sources.summary: The paper maps H I around the M81 group, describing M81 as the dominant grand-design spiral whose disk and surrounding group gas shape the large-scale rotation.

quotes.quote: Being the most massive galaxy in the group, M81 (NGC 3031) is a prototypical grand design spiral galaxy. Optical HST observations of the galaxy Chandar2001 have revealed the presence of a young, blue cluster population, whose formation appeared to have begun about 600 Myr ago as a result of the galaxy's interaction with its counterparts. Later, Davidge2009 found that the specific SFR in the M81 disk is consistent with that expected for an isolated galaxy of a similar mass, indicating that the SFR in M81 has not significantly increased in the past few tens of millions of years.

We observe a flat trend for the rotation velocity of the overall system from 20 kpc out to 80 kpc, well beyond the outskirts of the M81 disk, although with asymmetries like a wiggle at the vicinity of M82. This supports the assumption that intergalactic gas and galaxies in the system participate to a large-scale ordered rotation motion which is dominated by M81.

sources.summary: M81 is presented as a nearby large spiral where follow-up surveys have found three preliminary B[e] supergiant candidates, all with optical B[e] features but lacking dust confirmation.

quotes.quote: Most promising for dedicated searches are the nearby large spiral galaxies M81, M101, and NGC 2403, for which pioneering ground-based surveys have been conducted already in the eighties and nineties of the last century ( ) and which are nowadays extended to fainter objects thanks to the capabilities of the Hubble Space Telescope (e.g., ). Spectroscopic follow-up investigations revealed a number of variable luminous objects ( ), but only in one of these galaxies, M81, three B [ e ] SG candidates have been found so far ( ). They all display the typical B [ e ] features in their optical spectra, but without complementary information about the presence of (warm) circumstellar dust, their classification remains preliminary. These objects will certainly not remain the only of their kind, because the search for more candidates has just begun. The B [ e ] SG candidates in M81 are listed in Table tab:M81 .

sources.summary: The paper treats M81 as the central massive spiral of the group, involved in strong interactions with M82 and NGC3077 and surrounded by extended halos and tidal debris.

quotes.quote: It contains more than 40 member galaxies, including the large Sb spiral galaxy M81, the peculiar galaxies M82 and NGC3077, 9 late-type galaxies, at least 20 low-luminosity early-type dwarfs and a variety of stellar debris features, some of which are tidal dwarf galaxy candidates. Most of the member galaxies cluster around either M81 or NGC2403, the low mass spiral galaxy that lies 14 degrees to the southwest of M81 and 1 Mpc in the foreground.

While the binary sub-group structure and the total number of member galaxies are similar to those of the Local Group, the recent strong gravitational interactions amongst M81, M82 and NGC3077 provide a quite different environment to that found locally. These interactions were first revealed through studies of the emission line of neutral hydrogen (HI) which showed an extremely disturbed HI gas distribution with tidal bridges connecting the three galaxies e.g. 1975ApJ...195...23G, 1981MNRAS.195..327A, 1994Natur.372..530Y, 2008AJ....135.1983C, 2018ApJ...865...26D .

Early highlights from this survey include the discovery of very extended (and rather perturbed) stellar halos around M81, M82 and NGC3077 as well as an enormous tidal stream which links the three systems 2015ApJ...809L...1O .

sources.summary: The paper uses M81 as a well-studied host galaxy with a large surveyed satellite population, excluding uncertain tidal dwarfs and treating disturbed M82 as its satellite.

quotes.quote: M81: using the Canada-France-Hawaii Telescope (CFHT) and Hubble Space Telescope (HST) observations, Chiboucas2009 and Chiboucas_2013 have discovered and confirmed 14 new dwarf galaxies in the M81 group. Their survey covers at least 300 kpc projected distance around M81 and in some directions more than twice that, see figure 27 of Chiboucas_2013 . They increased the number of known members of this group to 36 with its faintest dwarf having an -band absolute magnitude of M_ =-6.8 M_ V 8 , Chiboucas_2013 . See their Table 4 for a full list of information. It is worth noting that we do not include two tidal dwarf galaxy candidates of M81 namely A0952 and Garlnd, Karachentsev2002, Makarova2002 in our analysis due to uncertainties on their nature.

sources.summary: The paper uses M81 as a small-group host with published satellites, likely completeness to M_V 9 within 250 kpc, and a radial profile close to simulations.

quotes.quote: The satellites of M81 are taken from chiboucas2013 . M81 is likely complete to better than M_V 9 within the inner projected 250 kpc. We refer to the more massive hosts as `small group' hosts and include M81, CenA, NGC 1023, and M104.

Indeed, both M81 and CenA closely follow the median simulated profile. A few of the observed hosts, particularly the more massive (`small-group') hosts such as M81 and CenA, have similar radial profiles to the simulated hosts, but none of the observed are less concentrated than the average simulated profile.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3031 as the massive spiral of the M 81 group, with related sources and HI excluded from SZ extraction and a notable individual correlation signal.

quotes.quote: NGC 3031 (M 81): This is the second closest spiral, nearly at the same distance as NGC 253. It is the most massive spiral (Sab) in the M 81 group, which contains the starburst galaxy M 82. M 82 and other galaxies were excluded in the SZ extraction, as were the extended HI regions connecting several group galaxies. Within the 500 kpc radius region (7.7 ^ ), there are noticeable residuals, presumably from Galactic dust, which were excluded.

The six objects with the most significant correlations are NGC 253 (chance probability or p-value of 5.9 ), NGC 3031 (p = 9.2 ), NGC 5236 (p = 1.3 ), and three objects with p < 0.2 : NGC 891, NGC 4826, and NGC 5055.

sources.summary: The paper treats M81 as the FRB 200120E host, reporting its atomic gas mass, star-formation rate, stellar mass, GBT spectrum, and membership in a well known interacting group.

quotes.quote: M81, the host of , has an atomic gas mass =2.79 10^9 deblok18 , SFR of 0.6 gordon04 and stellar mass of =7.2 10^ 10 deblok08 .
For M81, the host of , I used the spectrum from rots80 , also with GBT. Most of the optical disk of the galaxy is included within the beam. The spectral resolution is 22 .
M81 is a member of a well known interacting group e.g. deblok18 .

sources.summary: The paper treats M81 as the nearby host of FRB 20200120E, using whole-galaxy values and finding low gas fraction, short depletion time, and outlier behavior.

quotes.quote: We report the properties of molecular gas in a sample of six host galaxies of fast radio bursts (FRBs) obtained from CO observations with the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (FRBs 20180924B, 20190102C, and 20190711A) and results of one non-detection in a dwarf galaxy (FRB 20121102A) and two events detected in M81 (FRB 20200120E) and the Milky Way (FRB 20200428A).

FRB 20200120E is the closest known extragalactic FRB, localized to a globular cluster associated with M81 bhar21, kirs22 . FRB 20200428A was identified as the Galactic magnetar SGR 1935+2154 the20, boch20a . Since they occupy different positions on the phase space of luminosity and duration of FRBs, it is actively debated whether they are the same populations as other FRBs e.g., nimm22 . Although the positions of the two nearby FRBs within the host galaxies are localized, we use the values for the entire host galaxies to treat them the same way as other FRBs.

The FRB 20200120E host has a smaller gas fraction and a shorter gas depletion time and is largely offset from the distributions of other star-forming galaxies. Those FRB hosts are offset from the scaling relations of other star-forming galaxies, which is in contrast to the majority of the hosts of core-collapse SNe, SLSNe hats20 , and LGRBs hats20a , suggesting different origins of FRBs.

sources.summary: The paper fits spatially resolved UV-to-FIR photometry of M81, mapping stellar properties and showing younger star-forming outer regions versus an older, massive bulge.

quotes.quote: To show the power and versatility of Lightning when applied to a nearby galaxy, we fit the spatially resolved UV-to-FIR photometry (SED map) of the nearby spiral galaxy, M81 (NGC 3031). To generate the SED map, we gathered 23 publicly-available photometric images ranging in wavelength from GALEX FUV to Herschel 350 m. We then preprocessed these images for fitting by (1) subtracting foreground stars, (2) convolving each image to a common 25 ^ PSF, (3) re-binning them to a common 10 ^ pixel scale, (4) estimating the background to update the photometric uncertainties, (5) correcting each bandpass for Galactic extinction, and (6) combining the images into a data cube which contains the pixel-by-pixel SEDs.

One stand out result from these property maps is that the younger, star-forming population, which is more highly obscured, is concentrated in the spiral arms as seen in the SFR (SFR of the last 100 Myr) and V band optical depth maps. Additionally, the older, more massive population can clearly been seen to reside in the bulge region, where the stellar mass is high and SFR is low, resulting in a low specific star formation rate (sSFR; sSFR = SFR /M_ ).

sources.summary: The paper presents M81 as a nearby LINER with nonstellar activity, extended LINER emission, likely composite nature, possible shock or post-AGB ionization, and ROSAT evidence for mixed compact and extended emission.

quotes.quote: A good example is M81, one of the nearest bright LINERs. Ho (1998) stressed the incontrovertible, nonstellar nature of M81, though the stellar contribution cannot be ruled out (Maoz et al. 1998). Recently, Hameed Devereux (1999) discussed the extended nuclear emission-line regions having a LINER spectrum in M81, and argued that M81 is likely a composite object. They also proposed that shock heating or UV-photons from post asymptotic giant branch stars are probably responsible for the extended LINER emission. Further evidence for the ``composite'' nature for M81 is provided by the ROSAT HRI data (Colbert Mushotzky 1999), in which the intensity ratio of the point-like component to the extended one is about 3/2 (Colbert, private communication).

sources.summary: The paper treats M81 as a low-luminosity AGN/LINER with truncated-disk evidence: no Big Blue Bump, a red bump, and an ADAF-like inner accretion flow at low Eddington ratio.

quotes.quote: Low-Ionization Nuclear Emission Region (LINER) galaxies, and more generally low luminosity AGN (LLAGN), have unusual spectral properties . It has been proposed that the observations could be understood if accretion proceeds via an ADAF in these galactic nuclei , . A feature of the proposed models is that the accretion flow consists of two zones: an outer thin disk that extends from some large radius down to a transition radius R_ tr and an inner ADAF that extends from R_ tr to the BH horizon. There is some direct evidence for such a geometry of the accretion flow in NGC 4258 , M81 and NGC 4579 . M81 and NGC 4579, in particular, lack the Big Blue Bump that is seen in AGN. Instead, they have a red bump, which can be fitted only with a truncated disk .

Finally, in the previous subsection, we discussed intermediate cases like M81, with L_ acc 10^ 4 10^ 5 L_ Edd . We argued that the accretion occurs via a thin disk at large radii ( R>R_ tr 10^2R_S ) and vai an ADAF at smaller radii ( R<R_ tr ).

sources.summary: The paper treats M81 as a star-forming late-type comparison where mixed bulge and disk X-ray source populations explain luminosity-function differences near spiral arms.

quotes.quote: These deviations are mostly apparent for M81 and M101, having disk star formation at the level of 1 M _ /year. The bulge size corresponds to the aperture of 100 150 for M101 m101bulge ) and 450 240 ellipsoid in the case of M81 m81bulge .

This might explain a variety of the shapes of the luminosity functions observed by Chandra in different regions of nearby late type galaxies e.g. m31b , as illustrated by right panel of Fig. fig:xlf_m101 , showing the luminosity distribution in a large region of M81, including both bulge and disk population. Due to rather large value of the M_*/ SFR 4.6 10^ 10 yrs, comparable fractions of low and high mass X-ray binaries are expected. The total luminosity function of all sources can be presented as a sum of LMXB and HMXB contributions (Fig. fig:xlf_m101 ), in agreement with different luminosity distributions of the sources near the spiral arms and in the inter-arm regions observed by Chandra m81 .

sources.summary: NGC 3031 is identified as M81 and described as a well-studied spiral with low radio continuum brightness, recognizable spiral structure, low star formation activity, and a low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: This is the well known galaxy M 81, the dominant galaxy of the M 81 Group, and it is one of the best-studied spiral galaxies in all spectral regimes. Compared to other grand-design spirals, its radio continuum brightness is rather low. The overall spiral structure is readily recognisable in the radio continuum. The low radio continuum brightness and corresponding low star formation activity is in accord with the low CO surface brightness throughout M 81 (Brouillet et al. ; ). The central point source is a low-luminosity AGN (e.g. de Bruyn , Filippenko ).

sources.summary: The paper places M 81 at the center of a tidal-interaction scenario, with nearby low-mass HI clouds possibly linked to HVC analogues or debris from the M 81 triplet.

quotes.quote: Our observations show that the new population of low--mass HI clouds appears to be confined to a region toward the south--east of the central triplet (at distances of 100 kpc from M 81). Possible explanations for these free--floating HI clouds are that they are related to the dSphs found to the South--East of M 81, that they belong to the galaxies of the M 81 triplet (equivalent to HVCs), that they are of primordial nature and provide fresh, unenriched material falling into the M 81 group, or that they are tidal debris from the 3--body interaction involving M 81--M 82--NGC 3077.

using the 76--m Lowell telescope and later mapped the large--scale HI distribution in the group, tracing the complex tidal tails and bridges resulting from the 3--body interaction between M 81, M 82, and NGC 3077.
The numerical simulation published by shows material from M 81 being dragged out by the passage of NGC 3077 and spread out towards the South--East.

sources.summary: The paper uses M 81 as a nearby-galaxy analogue, citing VLA evidence for an organized nuclear magnetic arc and a compact central source analogous to Sgr A*.

quotes.quote: The study of extended, polarized features in M 81 illustrates that the nuclear regions of other normal galaxies may be rich in magnetized and filamentary features similar to those found in our own Galactic center(Kaufman et al 1996). These authors used the VLA in all its configurations at both 1.4 and 4.9 GHz to obtain 1 ^ and better spatial resolution. Kaufman et al (1996) identify a large-scale (up to 1 kpc) magnetized ``arc" within the inner 1 kpc of the galaxy, indicating that the interstellar magnetic field in this region is well-organized. In addition, the compact source in the center of M 81 (M 81 ^* ; Brunthaler et al 2001) has been found to be one of the best analogs to the GC compact source Sgr A ^* .

sources.summary: The paper uses M 81 as a nearby spiral comparison, adopting its H i gas mass and AKARI dust mass while noting Herschel gives a consistent dust estimate.

quotes.quote: For the uniformity of data, we select the samples observed by AKARI : blue compact dwarf galaxies in hirashita09b and spiral galaxies (M 81 from sun11 and M 101 from suzuki07 ).

For the gas mass of the dwarf galaxies and M 81, we adopt the H i mass, since the molecular mass is negligible or not detected. The H i masses of M 81 and of the dwarf galaxies are taken from walter08 and in hirashita09b , respectively.
The dust mass in M 81 derived by the Herschel observation ( 3.4 10^7 M _ ; bendo10 ) is similar to that estimated by the AKARI observation adopted in this paper ( 3.2 10^7 M _ ; sun11 ).

sources.summary: The paper places this THINGS low-surface-brightness galaxy in Group A, finding fitted BDM cores with r_s exceeding r_c, typical transition energy near 0.11 eV, and BDM fits preferred over other cored profiles.

quotes.quote: We obtain seven galaxies (DDO 154 (A1), NGC 2841 (A2), NGC 3031 (A3), NGC 3621 (A4), NGC 4736 (A5), NGC 6946 (A6), and NGC 7793 (A7)) (we call Group A) with fitted values r_s > r_c 0 that are shown in Table tab:onlydm_bdm . For these galaxies we obtain average values for: the core r_c 40 pc , r_s 5 kpc , and the energy E_c 0.11 \ eV for the min.disk analysis, that are typical values for a galaxy, except for DDO 154 which has a larger core, r_c = 1.35 kpc . We notice that for all these galaxies the ^2 in BDM is smaller than NFW's but the extra parameter ( r_c ) in BDM makes the reduced ^2_ red equivalent for both profiles. When comparing the BDM profile with the other two cored profiles, Burkert and ISO (Ref. ) we conclude that BDM is still better.

sources.summary: The paper describes M 81 as a giant, highly organized spiral whose optical arms nearly terminate in a pseudoring while infrared imaging shows more complex inner spiral structure.

quotes.quote: M 81 is a giant spiral having a B band absolute magnitude of 21.1 and shows extremely organised and well-developed high-surface brightness structure.

In optical images, the spiral structure of M 81 appears to nearly terminate in a large pseudoring in the outer parts of the bright bulge. Spiral structure inside this pseudoring appears mainly in near-side extinction arcs. In contrast, at 8 m there is complex spiral structure inside the apparent pseudoring that continuously winds outward.

sources.summary: The paper identifies M 81 as SN 1993J's host galaxy and says its high declination and radio-loud AGN enabled VLBI calibration, with prior work reporting AGN core-jet structure and jet precession.

quotes.quote: SN 1993J in the galaxy M 81 is, by far, the best studied young radio-luminous supernova in the Northern Hemisphere. The high declination and moderate radio-loud AGN activity of the host galaxy, M 81, allowed us to perform an intensive VLBI monitoring of the supernova with a high-quality phase calibration, using the closeby AGN in M 81 as calibrator source.

The strong and spherical radio emission of SN 1993J and the high quality of the VLBI observations, also allowed us to use the supernova shell as an astrometry reference to study the AGN in M 81. Results on the core-jet structure of the AGN and its frequency core-shift can be found in Bietenholz et al. ( ) and Mart i-Vidal et al. ( ); in the latter, strong evidence of jet precession, coupled to changes in the flux density of the jet, have also been reported.

sources.summary: The paper adds M 81 to the forbidden-line velocity comparison, flags its ionizing-photon estimate as highly uncertain, and notes it as an exception to the expected relation.

quotes.quote: We add also M 81, which has measurements of v of 18 forbidden lines in .
The uncertainty in in the Seyferts is small compared to the uncertainty in the estimate, however the estimate of in the LINERs (M 81, PKS 1716-649 and NGC 7213) is highly uncertain.
With the exception of M 81, the actual observed are generally consistent with eq. eq: FWHM vs ncrit and the and estimates. In M 81 the estimate of is based on gas and stellar dynamics.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 3031 infrared SED data with ADAF+disk+jet models, noting competing predictions for 10 micron emission and that the Y11 model contributes mainly near 3-4 microns.

quotes.quote: Figures f1 f3 show the comparison between the observed SEDs from M12 and references therein, plus Herschel far-IR data for NGC 3031 from Bendo12 and published ADAF+disk+jet models for these objects.
In NGC 3031 the Y11 JD model accounts for very little of the IR luminosity, whereas the N13 AD model predicts that most of the emission at 10 m comes from the truncated disk.

In the ADAF+disk+jet models considered for NGC 3031, the largest contribution to the observed IR emission arises in the Y11 model around 3 -- 4 m, where the model would account for 30 of the observed luminosity. At longer wavelengths the ADAF+disk+jet contribution would be within the photometric errors.

sources.summary: The paper identifies M81 as the richest main-disturber suite, describes nearby tidal dwarf companions in HI filaments, and notes distinctive M81-group features absent from the Local Group.

quotes.quote: The suites around the MDs themselves were ranked according to the number of suite members n_s from the maximum of n_s=53 for the suite around M81 to n_s=1 .

This way, four dwarf galaxies are close to the M81 and all of them are young stellar systems formed in the tidal HI-filaments connecting M81 with M82 and NGC 3077 (Yun 1999, Makarova et al. 2002, Karachentsev et al. 2011).

According to _1, M_B diagram the suite around M81 is most similar to our Galaxy with its neighbors. However, the M81 group has its essential features: the presence of HI-filaments (Yun 1999), young ``tidal'' Holm IX- type dwarfs (Makarova et al. 2002), and also BCD galaxies (Chiboucas et al. 2009), which all are absent in the Local Group.

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 3031's low-luminosity AGN, excludes its central bins, and finds its far-infrared colors strongly tied to older stellar emission and suppressed central ratios.

quotes.quote: NGC 3031 contains a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus that potentially produces nonthermal emission in these wavebands.

Regardless of the bin size used, the 160/250 and 250/350 m ratios for NGC 3031 and the 250/350 m ratios for NGC 5457 appear much better correlated with the 3.6 m surface brightness, while the 160/250 m ratios for NGC 5457 still appear better correlated with the H emission.
The data in Figure f_multfit clearly show that the 250/350 m ratio is unusually suppressed for NGC 3031, but the results are not as clear for NGC 4725.

sources.summary: The comparison identifies NGC 3031 as strongly tidally affected, with systematic gas-stellar arm offsets used to explain discrepant pitch angles in star-formation tracers.

quotes.quote: The largest crosses denote the optical data for the three galaxies (NGC 3031,NGC 4321 and NGC 5194) which are apparently undergoing the strongest tidal interactions (i.e. with P > 10^ 3 (equation 1); see Table 4 and Figure 17).

This is most clearly demonstrated in the case of NGC 3031 (M81) where the azimuthal offset increased systematically with radius and whose behaviour was the subject of detailed analysis in Kendall et al. 2008.

In the most discrepant galaxies (NGC 3031 and NGC 4579) this difference in pitch angles can be explained by our previous demonstration (Kendall et al. 2008, 2011) that in these galaxies the spiral pattern traced at 8 m in shocked gas is significantly displaced upstream with respect to the NIR (stellar) arms. In a differentially rotating galaxy it would then be expected that the spiral traced by star formation indicators should be more tightly wound than the stellar arms.

sources.summary: The paper includes M81 dwB among dwarf-galaxy rotation curves and reports that it prefers the smallest SIDM cross section unless higher halo concentration scatter is allowed.

quotes.quote: To measure DM self-interactions at small-to-intermediate scales, we consider rotation curves of five dwarf galaxies (IC 2574, NGC 2366, Ho II, M81 dwB, DDO 154) in the THINGS sample and seven LSB galaxies (UGC 4325, F563-V2, F563-1, F568-3, UGC 5750, F583-4, F583-1) from Kuzio de Naray, et al. . Two galaxies have been omitted from each of these samples for which was not well-determined. To model these galaxies, we include the contributions to the rotation curve from DM, gas, and stars, with _* allowed to vary uniformly by 0.3 dex from the quoted population synthesis values .

If we fix the _s r_s relation to its median in CDM cosmology in our analysis, the galaxies UGC 5750 and IC 2574 prefer the largest cross sections, /m 10 cm^2/g , while M81 dwB prefers the smallest cross sections, /m 0.1 cm^2/g . However, if UGC 5750 and IC 2574 halos are 2 less concentrated and M81 dwB halo 2 more concentrated than the median halo, the inferred /m become consistent, within errors, with 1 cm^2/g .

sources.summary: The paper describes M81 as the grand-design spiral whose encounter with M82 may have triggered M82’s starburst, mass loss, and altered magnetic-field morphology.

quotes.quote: This starburst could have been triggered by an interaction with the grand-design spiral galaxy M81 2008AJ....135.1983C . While the first interaction between these two galaxies happened around 100 Myrs ago, the last starburst periods of M82 are known to have occured 5-10 Myrs ago 2003ApJ...599..193F .

2005ApJ...628L..33M could even find spiral arms connected to the tips of this stellar bar and claim that M82 has lost a significant part of its mass due to its encounter with M81 hinting at a former small spiral morphology.

Even though the regular field morphology in some parts looks more poloidal, following the main outflow direction, the shearing forces from the interaction with M81 and outflow might have modified the X-shape, to the extent that it can no longer be recognised.

sources.summary: The paper maps M81 with Planck data, finding frequency-independent temperature asymmetries interpreted as Doppler effects from halo rotation or M81 Group interactions.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: J2000 coordinates R.A.: 09 ^h 55 ^m 33.1730 ^s , Dec: +69 3 55.061 (Galactic Longitude l=142.0918406 , Galactic Latitude b=40.9001409 )

quotes.quote: As in the case of M82, so also for the M81 galaxy a substantial North-South and East-West temperature asymmetry is found, extending up to galactocentric distances of about 1.5 . The temperature asymmetry is almost frequency independent and can be interpreted as a Doppler-induced effect related to the M81 halo rotation and/or triggered by the gravitational interaction of the galaxies within the M81 Group. Along with the analogous study of several nearby edge-on spiral galaxies, the CMB temperature asymmetry method thus is shown to act as a direct tool to map the galactic haloes and/or the intergalactic bridges, invisible in other bands or by other methods.

M81, also known as Bode's galaxy (or NGC 3031), at J2000 coordinates R.A.: 09 ^h 55 ^m 33.1730 ^s , Dec: +69 3 55.061 (Galactic Longitude l=142.0918406 , Galactic Latitude b=40.9001409 ) is a SA(s)ab type galaxy at a distance of 3.6 0.2 Mpc from us (see, e.g., Gerke2011 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3031/M81 as a nearby spiral with a distinctive nuclear dust spectrum; standard templates make its weak modeled AGN contribution unreliable.

quotes.quote: NGC 3031 : NGC 3031, also known as M81, is a nearby spiral galaxy. CIGALE modeled the galaxy with f_ AGN 0.01 , although L13 reported a total IR AGN luminosity fraction of 4 and a maximum of 16 in the 8--35 \ range. The galaxy nucleus has a unique dust spectrum smi10 , and modeling based on standard templates is unreliable. However, nothing in the total-galaxy SED requires an AGN.

sources.summary: The paper describes M81's spiral arms as tidally induced by companion passages, producing bifurcations, kinks, and star-formation chronology that challenges steady Lin-Shu density-wave expectations.

quotes.quote: The spiral arms in M81 (whose magnetic properties are presented by Sokolov:1992aa ) also arise from tidal interactions. Modelling of the interaction Yun:1999aa indicates that tidal structures were induced by the passages of the two companions M82 around 200 Myr ago and NGC 2077 around 280 Myr ago. As before, the interactions between the two sets of spiral arms thus induced produce the observed bifurcations and kinks in the current arms. Here again the evidence for the lack of monotonic star formation chronology across the arms Choi:2015aa suggests that the traditional idea of Lin--Shu density wave theory of a steady spiral pattern with gas flowing steadily through the arms at each radius is not tenable.

sources.summary: The paper targets M81 as a nearby face-on spiral in an interacting group, using its size, stellar mass, SFR, and proximity for a VLA transient survey.

quotes.quote: We therefore embarked on a radio monitoring campaign of the nearby spiral galaxy M81 to search for transients. M81 is one of largest nearby galaxies observable in the Northern Hemisphere, lying at a distance of 3.6 0.2 Mpc gerke11 in an interactive group that includes M82, with a SFR between 0.3 and 0.9 M_ yr ^ 1 depending on the SFR indicator; gordon04 . Its large size, and therefore high stellar mass predicts a high number of low mass X-ray binaries grimm02 , while being nearly face-on ensures that we are not viewing through a large absorbing column. These properties, along with its close proximity makes it an ideal galaxy to target for this monitoring survey.

sources.summary: The paper says M 81 has a compact core-like VLBI structure, a confirmed parsec-scale jet likely powering the radio emission, and H I evidence for gas exchange with nearby galaxies.

quotes.quote: Meanwhile, the remaining 3 targets are found with a compact core-like structure (M 81, NGC 3998, and Arp 102B). Among these, a pc-scale jet has been confirmed in M 81 in the previous VLBI study at 1.7 8.4 GHz and 43 GHz Marti-Vidal2011, Ros2012 , which is also likely the origin of the radio emission.

M 81 is found in relatively busy environments with a number of close neighbouring galaxies. The gas exchange with neighbors has been confirmed by H i imaging data, indicating that they are in the early to advanced merging state e.g., Yun1994 .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3031 as a low-accretion LLAGN where infall dominates losses, the accretion rate is too low for photomeson neutrinos, and the predicted neutrino flux is too faint for IceCube-Gen2.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:time shows the loss and acceleration rates as a function of proton energy for NGC 3516, NGC 4258, and NGC3031, which have m 0.9 m_ cr , m 0.3 m_ cr , and m 0.04 m_ cr respectively. For NGC 3516, t_ fall and t_ pp are comparable in the entire energy range. The photomeson production is effective above _p 30 PeV. The synchrotron and Bethe-Heitler losses are always subdominant in the range of our interest. On the other hand, for NGC 4258 and NGC 3031, the infall timescale is always dominant below the cutoff energy due to lower m .
For NGC 3031, m is too low to effectively create neutrinos via photomeson production.
The neutrino emission from NGC 3031 is too faint to be detected even with IceCube-Gen2.

sources.summary: The paper uses M81 as a low-luminosity AGN comparison, noting its ADAF-linked X-ray emission, black-hole mass, one-day variability, larger radiation region, and modeled SED.

quotes.quote: It is believed that X-ray emission of low luminosity AGN like M81 comes from ADAF. The mass of the supermassive black hole in M81 was 7.0^ +2 _ 1 10^ 7 M_ Schodel07 , and the X-ray variability time scale was confirmed to be about one day Parola2003 . Therefore, in the case of M81, the size of the radiation region is 25 R_ s , which is much larger than R_ s of M87 estimated in this study. The origin of X-ray emission of M81 was investigated by who modeled the multiwavelength SED.

sources.summary: The paper presents M81 as part of a nearby candidate cosmic-ray source pair, describing it as a weakly active giant spiral that interacted with M82 and may merge later.

quotes.quote: The galaxy M81 is a giant spiral, with a central black hole mass of 7 10^7 M_ , being a Seyfert I, although with weak activity at present. It interacted with the M82 galaxy some few hundred million years ago, what probably induced the strong starburst activity of this last, and the two galaxies are deemed to eventually merge into a single one in future interactions (the smaller galaxy NGC 3077 contributes to actually form a trio). M82 has some AGN activity, with a central black hole of 3 10^7 M_ . Note that while Cen A is almost at the center of the hot-spot hinted by Auger observations above 40 EeV , the M81/M82 complex is not far from the location of the Telescope Array reported hot-spot above about 57 EeV .

sources.summary: The paper treats M81 as a nearby galaxy with Planck halo temperature asymmetry and estimates a halo rotational velocity of 250-450 km s^1.

quotes.quote: Planck data has shown the existence of a temperature asymmetry towards the halo of several nearby galaxies, such as M31, NGC 5128, M33, M81 and M82. However, the cause of this asymmetry is an open problem. A possibility to explain the observed effect relies on the presence of ``cold gas clouds'' populating the galactic halos, which may be the answer to the so-called missing baryon problem.
For the M81 galaxy v_ rot turns out to be in the range 250-450 km s^ 1 while for M82 it is between 100 km s^ 1 and 345 km s^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies M81 as one of the three brightest ELVES central galaxies, hosting 24 confirmed satellites and being excluded from the main MW-analog analysis as part of a higher-mass small-group subset.

quotes.quote: The three brightest central galaxies are NGC3379, NGC3627, and M81, which are also the first three in the K_s band magnitude-ranked figure legend. They host the largest number of confirmed satellites -- 57, 27, and 24, respectively. Interestingly, these three groups have the largest number of massive satellites and as mentioned in Carlsten:2022 , their group luminosity is significantly larger than that of the primary host. As explained below, we exclude these three `small groups' from the analysis as their higher mass, and likely different formation history from bonafide MW-analogs may bias the results.

sources.summary: The paper reports an outer-disk q spike for M81dfwA and includes it among less-massive gas-rich dwarf galaxies with low, oblate halo q values.

quotes.quote: A clear trend is that q does not vary significantly beyond the R_ 25 radius (Figure 1). For the 6 large galaxies that have M(*)>10^ 9 M_ , q is close to constant in the outer disk region, except for NGC 3184 which shows a spike in q at large radii. However, this spike maybe due to errors in _ H i measurement as the column density decreases at the disk edge. A similar trend is present for the less massive galaxies ( M(*)<10^ 9 M_ ), where M81dfwA shows a similar spike in q at the disk edge. The dwarf galaxy IC1613 definitely has q 1 around R_ 25 and 0.5 < q < 1.0 beyond that. This is an exceptional case for the dwarfs. The gas rich dwarf DDO46 is the only galaxy that shows a significant decrease in q from q=0.5 at R_ 25 to 0.3 at 2R_ 25 , which indicates a fairly rapid change in halo shape with increasing radius.

Figure 2 and Table 2 show that galaxies with more massive stellar masses (M(*) > 10 ^9 M _ ) have q values close to q 1 at R_ 25 and beyond (e.g. NGC 628 and NGC 6946). The exceptions are NGC 4214 and NGC 5236. Of these two galaxies, NGC 4214 is a relatively small galaxy and close to being a dwarf olling.1996 . In fact, based on its rotation velocity, stellar mass, and absolute B-band magnitude values, NGC 4214 has been classified as a dwarf galaxy by 2008AJ....136.2782L . But NGC 5236 is quite massive ( M(*) 6.9 10^ 10 M_ and so its low q value is quite surprising. For the less massive galaxies, a large fraction of which are LSB dwarfs, q is much smaller (Figure 2 and table 2). Six of them have q=0.2-0.3 (Holmberg1, M81dwfA, IC10, DDO75, DDO187 and UGC8833) and 3 of them have q=0.4-0.5 (Holmberg2, F564-V3, DDO46). The exception is IC1613 which has a relatively large q 1 value at R_ 25 .

It is interesting to note that the gas rich galaxies (9 of them) all have oblate halos, with q<0.6 but the stellar dominated galaxies have a range of q values with q 0.2 (very oblate) to q 1.25 (prolate). Perhaps the most important result from this study is that the gas rich galaxies all have oblate halos at R_ 25 (Fig. 3). There are 9 gas rich galaxies that have M(gas)/M(baryon>0.5 and they are all dwarf galaxies : Holmberg1, Holmberg2, M81dwfA, DDO46, DDO53, DDO75, DDO187, F564-V3 and UGC8833. The q varies between 0.11 in DDO187 to 0.55 in Holmberg2. The stellar dominated galaxies, however, have a variety of q values ranging from q=0.21 in IC10 to q=1.25 for NGC5194 at R_ 25 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3031 as M81/Bode's galaxy, a slightly inclined grand-design spiral whose fitted attenuation curve is steeper than the Milky Way law and has a small bump.

quotes.quote: NGC 3031 (M81), also known as Bode's galaxy, is a slightly inclined grand design spiral. hoversten finds M81 is best fit with a MW-like dust law, which in the Noll_09 formalism corresponds to 0.1 and E_b 3 2020ARA&A..58..529S . We find the attenuation curve is steeper than the MW law ( between -0.28 and -0.37) with a small bump ( E_b between 0.61 and 2.94). There is good agreement on the slope fo the attenuation law between fits with different SFHs, with the piecewise SFH preferring the steepest attenuation curve with the strongest bump.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 3031 as a grand-design spiral in an interacting system, with quiet inner rotation and outer gas motions dominated by group tidal interactions.

quotes.quote: NGC 3031 NGC 3031 is a grand-design spiral and together with NGC 3034 and NGC 3077 forms an interacting system. The global inclination angle of i=59^ deBlok_etal_2008 approximately corresponds to the inclination angle measured by the TRM in the middle region of the disk, i.e. 400''<R<1000'' (where the optical radius is R_ 25 660'' ). Some fluctuations of the order of 20^ are present both at small radii where the determination of the angles is more problematic. Moreover, the TRM finds that the P.A. is 150^ with a small increasing trend in the outermost periphery of the galaxy (see Fig.

sources.summary: The paper includes M81 as the nearby host of FRB 20200120E, compiles extensive Chandra data, and notes that its known LLAGN is one of two AGN among seven FRB hosts.

quotes.quote: The z=0 source corresponds to FRB 20200120E located in the nearby M81 galaxy at d=3.6 Mpc Kirsten2022 .

In the case of FRB 20200120E (located in M81, which has been observed extensively at X-ray wavelengths), we separately compile and merge all existing Chandra ACIS observations that cover the host and FRB position Details of the observations included in each merged event file are given in the Facilities statement at the end of the paper. using the python code superchandra.py superchandra , which allows us to easily query and analyze all Chandra observations at a given set of input coordinates.

Based on the Chandra X-ray observations presented here and compiled from the literature, we find evidence for AGN in two FRB host galaxies: the host of FRB 20190608B (Section frb190608_agn ) and the host of FRB 20200120E (M81), which was already known to harbor an LLAGN Miller2010 . We note that X-ray emission is also detected from the host of FRB 20201124A but is consistent with a star formation origin Piro2021 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses M81 in historical dark-matter and rotation-curve contexts, emphasizing uncertain early measurements and asymmetric HI-envelope kinematics within its group.

quotes.quote: For M81 he used data based on a spectrum by Max Wolf, but the scatter is very large when compared with modern data by . The value for the luminosity of M81 is different in Lundmark's two papers, which is very confusing.

In retrospect, these data are not very convincing, since M81 is the main galaxy in a group with M82 and NGC 3077, and the kinematics of the HI envelope is rather asymmetric. The rotation curve declines with radius in the SE part, while it increases with radius in the NW part.

sources.summary: The paper treats M81 as a nearby galaxy with an observed precessing nuclear jet; its nuclear core has a very small redshift, a 7 yr timescale, and a high modeled LISA SNR.

quotes.quote: Alternatively, if the AGN is within sufficient proximity from us, the precession of an off-the-line-of-sight jet can also be directly observed; this is the case of the nearby galaxy M81, whose jet precesses over a timescale of 7 - 12 yrs .

M81*, the nuclear core of the M81 galaxy, has distance z < 0.001 , binary BH mass M = , and t_ obs 7 yrs , implying that a primary BH spin aligned from accretion provides t_ tid 5 yrs for a binary separation = r_ decoup 0.006 pc. The corresponding LISA SNR is remarkably high, 10^6 , due to the very small redshift and modest binary mass. These examples show that a broad range of observed jet precession timescales can be explained without tuning the disk migration parameters.

sources.summary: The paper cites M 81 as a comparison case whose mid-infrared silicate emission is better reproduced by olivine optical constants than by astronomical silicates.

quotes.quote: Another similar work is the one performed by Smith2010 . They attempt to reproduce the MIR silicate emission of M 81 in order to study its dust composition. They find that the best-fit model is obtained using the optical constants of olivine measured by Dorschner1995 (the same used in this work). They also tried to fit the MIR silicate emission of M 81 using the dielectric functions of astronomical silicates measured by DraineLi1984 , but they found that such a model was not as good as the one based on olivine.

sources.summary: The paper treats M81 as a nearby spiral hosting a low-luminosity AGN jet with detected circular polarization, flat or inverted CP spectrum, and little linear polarization.

quotes.quote: Low-luminosity AGN -- M81 ^ * Circular polarization was also detected in the compact radio jet of the nearby spiral galaxy M81 . Their estimated values of CP were 0.27 0.06 0.07 at 4.8 GHz and 0.54 0.06 0.07 at 8.4 GHz, where errors are separated into statistical and systematic terms (see also Bower et al. 2002 for more results on LLAGN). This suggests that the CP spectrum is flat or possibly inverted. They also detected no linear polarization at a level of 0.1 , indicating that the source has a high circular-to-linear polarization ratio.

sources.summary: The review cites M81 nova observations as consistent with two nova populations, estimating that no more than 60 percent of its novae belong to the bulge.

quotes.quote: The analysis of 78 plates obtained in the 1950-55 Palomar campaign for the discovery of novae in M81 has been recently published by Shara, Sandage and Zurek . These authors find that the spatial distribution of novae in M81 is fully consistent with the two nova populations hypothesis (see their Fig. 1). Particularly they estimate that the fraction of novae belonging to the M81 bulge is not larger than 60 .
The analysis of the spatial distributions suggests that nova populations in M31 and M81 are a mixture of disk and bulge novae ( 30 70 and 40 60 respectively).

sources.summary: The paper uses M81 to illustrate spiral-arm offsets and corotation constraints, noting tighter dust and star-forming arms and a literature spread in pattern speeds.

quotes.quote: Under both of these circumstances, the pitch angles of the arms are expected to follow i P > i D > i SF . visser80a makes the point that a constant is required for the P-arms and D-arms to have the same pitch angle, and comments that in M81 the tendency is for both the `gas and dust' (D-arms) and ` \ regions and young stars' (SF-arms) to be tighter than the potential arms.
This approach was taken by visser80b in an analysis of M81, and more recently for several galaxies kranz03 .

Of course, values determined by different methods rarely agree exactly; table M81table lists pattern speeds derived for the spiral pattern in M81 from a variety of sources in the literature. This indicates a spread of some 25 in one of the most studied spiral galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper finds M81 has an extended, partly metal-rich globular-cluster system resembling M31 and the Milky Way, with blue clusters consistent with a universal halo specific frequency.

quotes.quote: M81 globulars have intrinsic color distributions which are very similar to those in the Milky Way and M31, with 40 of sample clusters having colors expected for a metal-rich population.

If we consider only the blue, metal-poor clusters in the early-type spiral M81, this galaxy is also consistent with having formed a ``universal'' specific frequency of halo GC population, with a value of S_N 0.6 .

Approximately 40 of our M81 GC sample appears to be metal-rich. Based on the total number of GCs estimated for M81, we calculate S_N=1.0^ +0.4 _ 0.3 for the bulge normalized specific frequency, assuming the B/T ratio given in Table 9 and that 40 of the total M81 GC population is metal-rich.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3031 as a declining-rotation-curve galaxy omitted from one relation but recovered when its maximum rotation velocity is used.

quotes.quote: Also, galaxies with significant non-circular motion in the HI gas such as NGC 3031 were omitted in fer4 .
NGC 3031 has a declining RC, R_ 25 11 arcmin (from the LEDA database), v_ (R_ 25 ) 190 km s ^ 1 , and _ = 174 17 km s ^ 1 .
NGC 3031 is not on the plotted line of fer4 . However, if v_ = v_ rmax = 243 14 km s ^ 1 is used, NGC 3031 does fit on the plotted line.

sources.summary: The paper treats M81 as the threshold-case exception: its UV profile ends near the H-alpha threshold because of GALEX field and infrared-background limits, while FUV imaging still shows outer spiral arms.

quotes.quote: The last one is M81, for which our UV profiles ends at the position of the threshold. Note that we are limited for this galaxy by its large angular size: the south outer part of the galaxy is getting close to the limit of the GALEX field of view. The infrared background is also relatively bad so that we cannot extend the profiles very far out in the disk. However, an inspection of the FUV image (at full resolution) clearly reveals two spiral arms extending in the area between R/R_ threshold equals 1 and 1.5.

sources.summary: The paper maps neutral-hydrogen clouds around the interacting M81/M82 group, centering observations on M81 and associating a nearby cloud with M81 by velocity.

quotes.quote: The group contains optically bright galaxies M81, M82, NGC 3077, and NGC 2976, which show the remnants of strong interactions wal02 , as well as over 40 dwarf galaxies.

The full group extends over a 40 20 degree area (right ascension range 7 to 11 hours, declination range 58^ 78^ ), but the brightest galaxies (M81, M82 and NGC3077) are all within a 2^ 2^ region centered on M81, which was where we concentrated our observations.
Although Cloud 4 is located equidistant from M81 and M82 at a projected distance of 33 kpc from both galaxies, it is most likely associated with M81, as it is much closer to M81 in velocity.

sources.summary: The paper reports deep ACS imaging of M81's outer disk, isolates Cepheids in that field, and derives a distance modulus of 27.78 corresponding to 3.60 Mpc.

quotes.quote: Dolphin 3 1 Astronomy Department, University of Washington, Box 351580, Seattle, WA 98195; lmccomma@astro.washington.edu, jd@astro.washington.edu, ben@astro.washington.edu, mrdavis@astro.washington.edu 2 Department of Astronomy and McDonald Observatory, University of Texas, Austin, TX 78712; yoachim@astro.as.utexas.edu 3 Raytheon Corporation, 870 Winter Street Waltham, MA 02451; adolphin@ratheon.com The ACS Nearby Galaxy Survey Treasury (ANGST) has acquired deep ACS imaging of a field in the outer disk of the large spiral galaxy M81.
We have isolated 11 fundamental mode and 2 first overtone Cepheid variables in an M81 deep field consisting of 9 V-band and 11 I-band images.

We calculate a distance modulus for M81 of 27.78 0.05_r 0.14_s \ with a corresponding distance of 3.60 0.23 \ Mpc, after removing one of the confirmed Cepheid variables, due to its obvious deviation from the mean distance modulus.

sources.summary: The paper reports M 81 X-ray variability, hints of Fe K emission, host-galaxy UVOT complications, and possible weak-jet contribution from radio evidence.

quotes.quote: The historical flux of M 81 decreases between 2005 April and 2006 June almost achromatically and then increases by 2007 February by a factor of 2 in the soft X-rays and slightly less in hard X-rays.

The spectra show no significant absorption features superimposed on the power-law continuum; however, M 81 shows hints of an Fe K emission line (6.4 keV, fixed), with an equivalent width EW =640_ 410 ^ +650 eV.

Because of its proximity (3.6 Mpc), the host galaxy of M 81 occupies most of the field-of-view of UVOT, making it difficult to estimate the sky background to be subtracted from the nucleus and the galaxy light.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3031 as a classical-bulge galaxy and reports its central morphology, Sersic index, and mid-infrared star-formation state.

quotes.quote: NGC 3031 .SAS2../Sb() Classical Bulge. There is a dust lane that extends to near the center of the galaxy, however most of the light is dominated by smooth isophotes that are resemble an E galaxy. The decomposition yields n_b=3.9 , and the bulges is not active with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =-0.5 mag.

sources.summary: The paper maps M81 in CO J=3-2, confirms weak molecular emission, detects nine CO peaks, and links molecular gas statistically with star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: A'ohoku Pl., University Park, Hilo, HI 96720, USA Netherlands Organisation for Scientific Research, Laan van Nieuw Oost-Indie 300, NL-2509 AC The Hague, the Netherlands Received May 04, 2010; accepted November 12, 2010 We present the first complete ^ 12 CO J=3-2 map of M81, observed as part of the Nearby Galaxies Legacy Survey being carried out at the James Clerk Maxwell Telescope.

We combine these data with a new H map obtained using the Isaac Newton Telescope and archival , 24 m , and FUV images to uncover a correlation between the molecular gas and star forming regions in M81.
M81 is a nearby (3.63 Mpc, Freedman1994 ) nearly face-on SA(s)a b Buta2007 spiral galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies M81 as the nearby host galaxy of SN 1993J and cites a VLBI expanding-shock-front distance estimate of 3.96 0.29 Mpc, consistent with Cepheid measurements.

quotes.quote: SN 1993J, in the nearby galaxy M81, was one of the nearest as well as one of the radio-brightest core-collapse supernovae observed in recent history e.g., SN93J-Sci, SN93J-1, SNVLBI-Bologna .

One of the results of our VLBI campaign was in fact a direct, geometrical estimation of the distance to SN 1993J and M81 using the expanding shock front method, which resulted in a value of 3.96 0.29 Mpc. This value is consistent with that obtained from Cepheid measurements by Huterer et al. \ HutererSS1995 , but 9 13 larger than the more commonly cited value of Freedman et al. \ Freedman+2001 , (see SN93J-4 for a fuller discussion).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3031/M81 as a bright, nearby LLAGN with extensive monitoring data, making its accretion-jet SED model comparatively well constrained.

quotes.quote: NGC 3031 This source is among the brightest LLAGNs known since it is the nearest AGN besides Centaurus A and has been the subject of a broadband multiwavelength monitoring campaign markoff08, miller10 . Due to the many available observational constraints, accretion-jet models can be well constrained for this LLAGN. For illustrative purposes, here we will report our modelling results using the data compiled by EHF10.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3031/M81 as the closest-system spiral in the sample, with multiwavelength spiral and filamentary structure, a LINER nucleus, and a small fitted AGN infrared contribution.

quotes.quote: NGC 3031: Also known as M81, this galaxy is in our closest system and has very clear spiral arms and filamentary structure seen in all the images across our whole filter range. Its nucleus is a LINER. The best-fit decomposition of the IR spectrum performed with DECOMPIR has a 4 AGN contribution to the total IR emission and a 16 contribution in the 8 35 range.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3031 as a type 1.5 Seyfert whose VLBI flux exceeds VLA likely through variability, with unusually strong radio variability and a weak precessing one-sided jet.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, NGC 3031 (type 1.5, Marti-Vidal et al. 2011) and NGC 4565 (type 1.9, Falcke et al. 2000) have a VLBI flux density larger than the VLA, likely due to intrinsic variability.

Indeed, the brightest sources in the sample show strong X-ray variability (see C06), while the radio flux densities of most LLAGN show moderate variability (Mundell et a. 2009), except for a few examples such as NGC 3031.
In the case of NGC 3031, a weak one-side jet is observed and its extreme variability has been interpreted as caused by a precessing jet (Marti-Vidal et al. 2011).

sources.summary: The atlas gives M81's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: M81 is a nearby grand-design spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 3,6 0.5 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with one of the intrinsically weakest known AGN, which is also a flat-spectrum radio source (e.g., ho_radio_2001 ). It exhibits both LINER and Sy 1.8 characteristics and extraordinary low obscuration ( 5 10^ 20 ^ 2 ; page_x-ray_2003 ). Motivated by its similarity to Sgr A ^* , an extensive multiwavelength campaign was performed on M 81 by in order to compare and characterize SED and the short-term (daily) variability that occurs in this object (in addition to the yearly variations).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3031 as M 81, a group spiral with a low-luminosity Seyfert/LINER nucleus, compact radio variability, and an old central stellar population.

quotes.quote: NGC 3031 (S1.5) Also known as M 81, this spiral galaxy is part of an interacting galaxy group that also includes M 82 and NGC 3077 Yun94 . Although the active nucleus is classified as a low-luminosity Seyfert 1.5 by Ho97 , it is often referred to as a LINER, and Ho96 note that its ionization parameter is low relative to typical Seyferts. The nucleus appears unresolved in HST optical images Devereux97 .

sources.summary: The paper searches M81 globular clusters for accreting intermediate-mass black holes, using VLA observations of 206 probable clusters and finding no individual, stacked, or HLX-1-like detections.

quotes.quote: Nantais et al. \ used the Hubble Space Telescope to localize probable globular clusters (GCs) in M81, a spiral galaxy at a distance of 3.63 Mpc.
We used the NRAO Karl G. \ Jansky Very Large Array (VLA) to search for the radiative signatures of IMBH accretion from 206 probable GCs in a mosaic of M81.
Finally, no analog of HLX-1, a strong IMBH candidate in an extragalactic star cluster, occurs in any individual GC in M81.

sources.summary: The paper highlights M81 as a massive classical-bulge galaxy with an anemic stellar halo, arguing it shows no evidence for the expected past major or minor merger activity.

quotes.quote: Three (M81, NGC 4258, NGC 7814) have less massive stellar halos. NGC 4258 has a low mass classical bulge and a relatively uninformative limit on its stellar halo mass. NGC 7814 has a very large bulge five times more massive than its stellar halo. Most notable among these is M81, with a large classical bulge and an anemic stellar halo containing only 2 0.9 of its total stellar mass. M81 shows no sign of any significant past major or minor merging activity that was expected to drive the formation of its classical bulge .

sources.summary: M 81 is described as a spiral with a prominent classical bulge, a large bulge-plus-halo fraction, and a substantial bulge paired with a more modest stellar halo.

quotes.quote: The Andromeda Nebula M 31 and the spiral M 81 have relatively prominent classical bulges.
The galaxies M 31 and M 81 have large bulge plus stellar halo fractions, (B+H)/T = 0.46 and 0.36.
But M 81 has a substantial classical bulge and a much more modest stellar halo.

sources.summary: The paper summarizes radiative-transfer modeling of early-spiral M 81, highlighting that old stars provide about half its dust heating and exceed old-star heating in barred-galaxy cases.

quotes.quote: Within the DustPedia http://dustpedia.astro.noa.gr framework the methodology of constructing an RT model was optimised and applied to the early-spiral galaxy M 81 , to a sample of face-on barred galaxies , to NGC 1068 which hosts an active galactic nucleus (AGN) , and to the M 51 interacting system .

Throughout this series of radiative transfer papers new insights on the dust heating processes were presented. showed that the contribution of the old stellar population to the dust heating of M 81 is approximately 50 , highlighting the importance of old stars to the FIR emission.
The estimated contribution of the old stellar population in those galaxies remains considerably high, yet not as not as significant as in M 81.

sources.summary: The paper highlights M81-dw J1004+6835 as the most diffuse candidate, projected near NGC 3077, overlaid on tidal debris, and analogous to Canes Venatici I/Andromeda IX.

quotes.quote: The blue `stars' in M81-dw J1004+6835's CMD are likely to be real, from young stars in M81's Hi tidal field Okamoto2015 .

In contrast, our most diffuse ( _V _ <r_e 29.5 mag arcsec ^ 2 ) candidate is M81-dw J1004+6835 --- it is just 10 kpc south of NGC 3077 and is superimposed on a rich stellar population from NGC 3077 itself.
In their relatively high luminosities and large sizes, M81-dw J1004+6835 and M81-dw J1000+6841 are most similar to Canes Venatici I/Andromeda IX and Hercules/Andromeda XXIV, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper models M81's UV morphology with a shorter star-formation e-folding time, treats its AGN contribution as unimportant, and emphasizes its unusually low UV concentration.

quotes.quote: Therefore, we assumed some evolution (scenario 2): we adopted an exponential decrease of the star formation rate with an e-folding rate of 8 Gyrs except for M81 which has a e-folding rate of 3 Gyrs.
M51 is classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy and M81 as an AGN galaxy but the contribution from the nucleus to the overall UV emission is not important.
M81 appears very extreme: this quiescent early-type spiral has a very low concentration.

sources.summary: The paper reports both short- and long-term X-ray variability in NGC 3031 and models it with shock-dominated clouds close to the nucleus heated by a high-velocity shock.

quotes.quote: Short term variability ( 1 day) was found in NGC 3031, NGC 4258 and NGC 5033. Long-term variability is observed in NGC 3031, NGC 4258 and NGC 4579, while no significant variability was found in NGC 404, NGC 4111, NGC 4438, NGC 4579 and NGC 4639.
NGC 3031 has shock dominated clouds close to the nucleus (=2.4 pc) heated by high velocity shock ( =700 ).

sources.summary: The paper argues the molecular complex may lie in M 81’s perturbed outer disk, possibly cospatial with Concentration i rather than in a detached tidal arm.

quotes.quote: Also, re-inspecting N-body simulations of the M 81 group and the H i data we find that it might be located inside the extreme outer disk of M 81 and be cospatial with the H i feature known as Concentration i .
The simulations show two components overlapping at the location of the complex as well as an overdensity at the edge of the disk of M 81 similar to Concentration i (Fig.
Thus, instead of being inside a well detached tidal arm it could be located in a region of the outer disk of M 81 which was strongly perturbed by the interaction with M 82.

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes M81 as a LINER, citing its compact radio core, multi-epoch HST UV measurements, and variable UV flux limits for the AGN component.

quotes.quote: M81 (NGC 3031) - Bietenholz et al. (2000) used VLBI to measure in this galaxy a radio core with 132 mJy at 3.6 cm, and Nagar et al. (2002) obtained 165 mJy at 2 cm with the VLBA. M81 was imaged by M05 at five epochs. The mean level at 2500 , 200 10^ 17 , was similar to the one measured by Maoz et al. (1998) at 1500 in the 1993 HST/FOS spectrum of Ho et al. (1996) -- 150 10^ 17 .

gmw_00000128.name: MCG-1-5-47
gmw_00000128.overview: MCG-1-5-47 is an edge-on spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0172 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus that was first discovered in hard X-rays [arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The atlas gives MCG-1-5-47's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: MCG-1-5-47 is an edge-on spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0172 ( D 67.7 Mpc) with a Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 , which was first discovered in hardest X-rays with INTEGRAL bird_third_2007,bodaghee_description_2007,masetti_unveiling_2008 . /IRAC and IRS observations of this object are available, and a compact nuclear source embedded in the narrow host emission is visible in the IRAC images. The IRS LR/HR staring-mode PBCD spectrum is very noisy and partly flagged in the N band.

gmw_00000129.name: MCG-1-13-25
gmw_00000129.overview: MCG-1-13-25 is a spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0159 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts a Seyfert 1.2 active galactic nucleus that appears nearly unresolved in infrared imaging [arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The atlas gives MCG-1-13-25's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: MCG-1-13-25 is a spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0159 ( D 65.7 Mpc) with a Sy 1.2 nucleus pietsch_new_1998 , which was first discovered with HEAO brissenden_multiwaveband_1987 . It is a member of the nine-month BAT AGN sample. /IRAC, IRS and MIPS observations of MCG-1-13-25 are available and show a nearly unresolved source in the corresponding images.

gmw_00000130.name: MCG-1-24-12
gmw_00000130.overview: MCG-1-24-12 is a nearby spiral Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:2101.08793, arXiv:1310.2770] that hosts an active galactic nucleus with variable X-ray flux [arXiv:2101.08793]. Its extended narrow-line region is surrounded by a faint ring of H II regions [arXiv:1501.02022].

sources.summary: The paper models MCG-01-24-12 as a nearby Seyfert 2 galaxy with variable X-ray flux, a stable absorbed power-law continuum, hot corona constraints, and possible fast ionized outflows.

quotes.quote: We present a detailed X-ray spectral analysis of the nearby Seyfert 2 galaxy MCG-01-24-12 based on a multi-epoch data set. Data have been taken with different X-ray satellites, namely XMM-Newton , NuSTAR , Swift and Chandra and cover different time intervals, from years down to a few days. From 2006 to 2013 the source had a 2-10 keV flux of 1.5 10 ^ 11 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , consistent with archival observations based on HEAO and BeppoSAX data, though a 2019 Chandra snapshot caught the source in an extreme low flux state, a factor of 10 fainter than its historical one. Based on phenomenological and physically motivated models, we find the X-ray spectrum of MCG-01-24-12 to be best modelled by a power-law continuum emission with =1.76 0.09 with a high energy cut-off at E _ =70^ +21 _ 14 keV that is absorbed by a fairly constant column density of N _ H =(6.3 0.5) 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 .

These quantities allowed us to estimate the properties of the hot corona in MCG-01-24-12 for the cases of a spherical or slab-like hot Comptonising plasma to be kT _ e =27 ^ +8 _ 4 keV, _ e =5.5 1.3 and kT _ e =28 ^ +7 _ 5 keV, =3.2 0.8, respectively. Finally, despite the short duration of the exposures, possible evidence of the presence of outflows is discussed.

sources.summary: The paper adopts MCG -1-24-12 as the true HEAO-1 source counterpart after Beppo-SAX/NFI showed this Seyfert 2 galaxy dominated the field X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: Malizia2002 pointed out that the z=0.167 AGN which was listed in the on-line A2PIC catalog was a misidentification of the X-ray source H 0917-074.
